titleZh: 第一讲：罗马的百年灾难

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, welcome back to our 4th series on World History. For the next 20 lectures we are going to learn about the MiddleAges. In European history, the Middle Ages really begin with the fall of the Western Roman Empire. So to understand the origin of the Middle Ages, we need to go back to the decline of the Roman Empire. Today we will be focusing on the years between 180 and 376AD. During these two centuries, the Roman Empire was still the strongest power in Europe, but it had begun a long slow decline which would result in the collapse of the Western Empire in the 5th century.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列课 程的第四辑。在这 20 节课当中， 我们将学习中世纪的历史。 在欧洲历史上，中世纪真正开始于 西罗马帝国的灭亡。为了理解中世 纪的起源，我们需要先回到罗马帝 国的衰落时期。 今天，我们会把注意力集中在公元 180 年到 376 年之间。 在这两个世纪里，罗马帝国仍然是 欧洲最强大的力量，但它已经开始 了漫长而缓慢的衰落。最终，西罗 马帝国在公元 5 世纪走向奔溃。

paragraphs.en: Today we will study that decline. The peak of Roman power occurred during the 84 years that Rome was ruled by the "Five Good Emperors": Nerva, Trajan, Hadrian,Antonius Pius, and MarcusAurelius. During their rule Europe experienced its longest period of internal peace. Historians call this time the "Pax Romana" or "Roman Peace". The Empire reached its greatest extent in 117AD, when its territory even reached Mesopotamia. Here is a map of the empire at that time. Unfortunately, this golden age ended in 180AD, when the emperor MarcusAurelius made a mistake. Instead of picking the most competent man to be his
paragraphs.zh: 今天我们将学习这段衰落的过程。 罗马的权力巅峰出现在由“五贤 帝”统治的 84 年间，他们是:涅尔 瓦、图拉真、哈德良、安东尼·庇 护和马可·奥勒留。 在他们统治期间，欧洲经历了历时 最长的内部和平时期。历史学家称 之为“罗马和平”。 帝国的版图在公元 117 年到达顶 峰，当时它的领土甚至达到了美索 不达米亚。这是当时帝国的地图。 不幸的是，这个黄金时代在公元 180 年结束了，当时的皇帝马可·奥 勒留犯了一个错误。他没有选择最 有能力的人作为他的继任者(正如 他的前任所做的那样)，而是选择

paragraphs.en: successor(as his predecessors had done), he chose his son Commodus. This was a disaster because Commodus was a terrible ruler. He was more interested in luxury and games, than in ruling the empire. He was also a megalomaniac and claimed to be a living god. In 192 AD, he was assassinated. This assassination began a terrible period in Roman history which lasted for 104 years. In the century before 192, the Empire had 6 emperors. In the century afterwards, it had 26. Historians call this time "The Crisis of the 3rd Century" and this is when the decline of Rome began. Here I have posted the years that each of those 26 emperors were in
paragraphs.zh: 了他的儿子康茂德。 这是一场灾难，因为康茂德是一个 可怕的统治者。他对奢侈品和游戏 ——而不是统治帝国——更感兴 趣。他也是一个自大狂，自称是一 个活神。公元 192 年，他遭到暗杀。 这次暗杀后，罗马历史上一段长达 104 年的可怕时期开始了。 在 192 年以前的一个世纪里，这个 帝国有过 6 位皇帝。而这之后的一 个世纪里，这个数字是 26。 历史学家称这一时期为“三世纪危 机”，也就是罗马开始衰落的时候。 我在这里列出了这 26 位皇帝在位 的年份。如你所见，他们中的大多

paragraphs.en: power.As you can see most of them did not rule for very long, the average reign was only about 4 years. And that's because emperors were constantly being betrayed, assassinated or overthrown by the army.As you can see, of those 26 emperors, only 3 died a natural death. The rest were murdered by their political enemies. This constant political instability weakened the empire. And of course, many of these emperors died fighting civil wars with other men trying to replace them. During the 3rd century, the empire experienced near-constant civil war, as Roman fought Roman. And during this century, the
paragraphs.zh: 数并没有统治很长时间，平均统治 时间只有 4 年左右。 这是因为皇帝经常被军队背叛、暗 杀或推翻。如你所见，在这 26 位 皇帝中,只有 3 人死于自然死亡。 其余的人都被政敌谋杀了。 这种持续的政治不稳定削弱了帝 国的力量。 当然，这些皇帝中还有很多死于与 夺位者的内战。在公元 3 世纪，罗 马帝国经历了几乎持续不断的内 战——罗马人打罗马人。 在这个世纪，波斯的帕提亚王朝被

paragraphs.en: Parthian dynasty in Persia was overthrown and a new more aggressive family took over the Persian empire: The House of Sasan.After 224AD, they became Rome's most powerful enemy. The Persians were dangerous because they were equal to the Romans in wealth and sophistication.And the Sassanid dynasty was more energetic and aggressive than the Parthians had been. As these wars continued, fewer and fewer citizens wanted to serve in the army. So the Romans became more dependent on recruiting Germanic soldiers. After a while, fewer and fewer of the people who fought for Rome were actually Romans themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 推翻，一个新的更具侵略性的家族 接管了波斯帝国: 萨珊家族。公元 224 年后，他们成为罗马最强大的 敌人。 波斯人非常具有威胁性，因为他们 的财富与复杂程度与罗马人不相 上下。萨珊王朝比帕提亚王朝更有 活力和侵略性。 随着这些战争的继续，公民们越来 越不愿意在军队服役。于是，罗马 人开始更加依赖于招募日耳曼士 兵。 过了一段时间，为罗马而战的罗马 人越来越少了。

paragraphs.en: These emperors were also constantly short on money to pay their armies. So they tried debasing their currency in order to make their silver supplies go farther. "Debasement" means mixing a greater percentage of cheap metal like copper in with more valuable metals like silver or gold, and then pretending the coins are just as valuable as they were before. You can see this debasement in the declining quality of Roman coins from this period. But of course, people figured out what was happening and the coins dropped in value. This caused massive inflation which badly damaged the economy. And then the Romans also had to
paragraphs.zh: 这些皇帝也经常缺钱供养军队。因 此，他们试图让货币贬值，以便让 白银储备能够坚持更长时间。 “贬值”意味着将更大比例的廉价 金属(如铜)与更有价值的金属(如 银或金)混合在一起，然后假装这 些硬币和以前一样值钱。 你可以看到这一时期罗马硬币质 量的下降。 当然，人们知道发生了什么，硬币 的价值下降了。这造成了严重损害 经济的大规模通货膨胀。 罗马人还必须应对历史上最严重

paragraphs.en: deal with the worst epidemic in their history. During the reign of MarcusAurelius a disease killed millions of people, including the emperor's second in command: Lucius Verus. This may be why MarcuAurelius was forced to choose his son Commodus as his successor. Scientists think this same disease spread across Eurasia by following the silk road and reached China. (Or maybe it went the other way.) However it got there, this disease also caused turmoil in China, which eventually led to the collapse of the Han dynasty. And then 70 years later, another plague hit the empire. This one lasted from 249 to 262AD. Historians call this one the Plague
paragraphs.zh: 的流行病。在马可·奥勒留统治期 间，一种疾病夺去了数百万人的生 命，其中包括皇帝的副手卢修斯·维 鲁斯。也许这就是为什么马库·奥 勒留不得不选择他的儿子康茂德 作为继承人。 科学家们认为，这种疾病通过丝绸 之路在欧亚大陆传播，最终到达中 国。(也可能是反过来。) 无论传播方向如何，这种疾病在中 国也引起了动荡，并最终导致了汉 朝的灭亡。 70 年后，另一场 瘟疫席卷了罗马 帝国。它从公元 249 年持续到 262 年。历史学家称这是塞浦路斯瘟 疫，以一位基督教主教的名字命

paragraphs.en: of Cyprian, after the Christian bishop who wrote about it. We think that these two epidemics from 165-180 and from 249-262 might have marked the emergence of smallpox in human history. And since Eurasian populations had not yet developed immunity to the disease, it may have killed 25% of the population during both epidemics. Modern medicine has defeated smallpox and driven the virus extinct in 1980. But before that happened, this disease killed hundreds of millions of people across the centuries and changed the course of human history. The crisis of the 3rd century eventually ended with the emperor
paragraphs.zh: 名。 我们认为这两次公元165-180年和 249-262 年的疫病可能标志着天花 出现在了人类历史上。 由于欧亚地区的人们还没有对这 种疾病产生免疫力，因此很有可能 在这两次时疫中损失了四分之一 的人口。 现代医学打败了天花，并在 1980 年使这种病毒灭绝。但在此之前， 这种疾病在几个世纪内夺去了数 亿人的生命，改变了人类历史的进 程。 罗马皇帝戴克里先最终解决了三 世纪危机。他意识到这个帝国太大

paragraphs.en: Diocletian. He realized that the empire was too big for one man to rule. So he created a system with two senior emperors, called Augusti, who ruled the eastern and western halves, and two junior emperors, called Caesars, who helped them. This system was called the Tetrarchy and it helped stabilize Rome. Diocletian also moved his capital out of the city of Rome, north to the city of Milan (then called "Mediolanum") This made it faster for the emperor to get information from the rest of the empire and to respond to threats. And then Diocletian retired in 305 AD, and lived out the rest of his life in peace.
paragraphs.zh: 了，一个人无法统治。因此，他建 立了一个体系，有两位皇帝，称为 “亚古士督”，分别统治东罗马和 西罗马，其下还有两位统帅，称为 “凯撒”，辅助他们。这个体系被 称为“四帝共治制”，它帮助稳定 了罗马。 戴克里先还把他的首都从罗马搬 到了北部的米兰(当时称为“中奥 拉南”)。 这使得皇帝能够更快地从帝国其 他地方获取信息，并对威胁做出反 应。 戴克里先于公元 305 年退休，平静 地度过了余生。

paragraphs.en: After Diocletian, the next strong emperor was named Constantine. He came to power in 313AD. Unfortunately, this transition of power from Diocletian to Constantine was not peaceful. Instead a short civil war was fought between Diocletian's potential successors. According to legend, before a key battle in that civil war, Constantine looked up and saw the Greek Letters "Chi" and "Ro" in the Sun, and heard a voice say, "in my name you will conquer." "Chi" and "Ro" are the first two Greek letters in the word "Christ," as in "Jesus Christ". Constantine told his soldiers to paint those letters on their shields,
paragraphs.zh: 继戴克里先之后，下一位强大的皇 帝是君士坦丁。他于公元 313 年开 始掌权。 不幸的是，这场由戴里克先到君士 坦丁的权力交接并不和平。戴克里 先的其他潜在继任者之间发生了 短暂的内战。 根据传说,在内战的关键战役之 前,戴克里先抬起头,在阳光中看 到了希腊字母“Chi”和“Ro”,并 听到一个声音说：“你将以我的名 义去征服。” “Chi”和“Ro”是希腊语中“基 督”一词的前两个字母，也就“耶 稣·基督”的“基督”。 君士坦丁让士兵们把这些字母写 在盾牌上，然后他们赢得了这场战

paragraphs.en: and then they won the battle. This battle is called the Battle of the Milvian Bridge, and it is a key turning point in Roman history. According to legend, Constantine then converted to Christianity and became Rome's first Christian emperor. But more skeptical historians have another explanation for his conversion. They point out that Constantine's mother was Christian. And they argue that the Christian religion had grown enough by the point to become a strong power-base for a man looking to become emperor. Whatever the real reason,
paragraphs.zh: 役。 这场战役被称为米尔维安大桥战 役，它是罗马历史上的一个关键转 折点。 据传说，君士坦丁后来皈依基督 教，成为罗马第一位基督教皇帝。 但持怀疑态度的历史学家对他的 皈依有另一番解释。 他们指出君士坦丁的母亲是一名 基督徒。 他们认为基督教已经发展到足以 成为一个想成为皇帝的人的强大 的权力基础。 不管真正的原因是什么，君士坦丁

paragraphs.en: Constantine's conversion changed the course of World History. After him Christianity became the official religion of the Roman Empire, and then the dominant religion of Europe. Constantine had an impact on Roman history in a second way. He moved the capital city from Milan to a new city in the east. The original name of this city had been "Byzantium" but he renamed it "Constantinople" after himself. Constantinople was built on a peninsula jutting out into the Bosphorus strait. This was a wise strategic position for a capital city, which we will
paragraphs.zh: 的皈依改变了世界历史的进程 在他之后，基督教成为罗马帝国的 官方宗教，然后成为了统治欧洲的 宗教。 君士坦丁还以另一种方式影响了 罗马历史。 他把首都从米兰搬到东部的一座 新城市。 这座城市最初的名字是“拜占庭”， 但他以自己的名字重新命名为“君 士坦丁堡”。 君士坦丁堡建在一个伸入博斯普 鲁斯海峡的半岛上。 对于首都来说，这是一个明智的战 略位置，我们将在后面的课程中更

paragraphs.en: discuss in more detail in later lessons. But for now, here is a picture of what Constantinople looked like in Roman times. Constantine ruled the empire for 24 years, a very long reign for a Roman emperor. Then, in 337 AD, Constantine fell ill. He was baptised on his death-bed, and died on May 22nd, 337AD. After Constantine, the imperial crown passed to 3 of his sons: Constantine II, Constans, Constantius II and then on to his nephew Julian. The reason the crown passed from Constantine's sons to his nephew, is
paragraphs.zh: 详细地讨论这一点。 但现在，我们先来看一张展现古罗 马时期君士坦丁堡风貌的照片。 君士坦丁统治帝国 24 年，对罗马 皇帝来说是一个很长的统治时期。 后来，在公元 337 年，君士坦丁病 倒了。 他在临终时受洗，并于公元 337 年 5 月 22 日去世。 君士坦丁之后，皇位传给了他的三 个儿子:君士坦丁二世、康斯坦斯、 君士坦丢二世，然后又传给了他的 侄子尤里安。 王位之所以传给了君士坦丁的侄 子，是因为君士坦丁的儿子们邪恶

paragraphs.en: that his sons were vicious and treacherous and killed each other fighting over power. Constantine and his family may have been Christian, but when they fought each other over power, their behavior was very unlike Jesus Christ. And then in 360, when the crown passed to Julian, he surprised everyone. Julian declared that he was not a Christian like his cousins and uncle. Instead he was a pagan like the classical Romans had been. He banned Christians from working as teachers and re-opened the old pagan temples to gods like Jupiter and Minerva. Pagan Romans cheered. Christian
paragraphs.zh: 奸诈，他们为了争夺权力而自相残 杀。 君士坦丁和他的家族或许曾经是 基督徒，但当他们为权力而斗争 时，他们的行为已与耶稣基督完全 背离。 360 年，王位传给了尤里安，他让 所有人都大吃一惊。 尤里安宣称他不像他的堂兄弟和 叔伯那样是基督徒。相反，他是一 个异教徒，就像古典罗马人一样。 他禁止基督徒从事教师工作，并重 新开放了古老的异教徒神庙，供奉 朱庇特和密涅瓦等神。 罗马的异教徒们欢呼雀跃，而基督

paragraphs.en: Romans were furious. But Julian was emperor, so his decisions were law. Julian also grew a beard, which was different from his predecessors. He loved to read about and discuss philosophy, and he was willing to talk to people from all social classes. Julian is really a fascinating figure, and he might have changed Roman history, but in 362AD, after just 2 years on the throne, he made the foolish decision to invade Persia. After spending the year in eastern Anatolia, Julian led his army into Persian territory on March 5th, 363 AD. For the next 3 months, they fought their way deeper and deeper into
paragraphs.zh: 徒则怒不可遏。 但尤里安是皇帝，因此他的决定就 是法律。 与前几任皇帝不同，尤里安还留了 胡子。他喜欢阅读和讨论哲学，他 愿意与来自各个社会阶层的人交 谈。 尤里安确实是一个令人着迷的人 物，他原本非常有可能改变罗马历 史，但在公元 362 年，仅仅在位两 年，他就做出了入侵波斯的愚蠢决 定。 在安纳托利亚东部度过了一年之 后，尤里安于公元 363 年 3 月 5 日 率领他的军队进入波斯领土。 在接下来的 3 个月里，他们越打越 深入波斯领土。到了六月底，他们

paragraphs.en: Persian territory. By late June they were deep into Mesopotamia. But then came the battle of Samarra on June 26th, 363AD. This was a small, unimportant battle, except for one reason. Julian was injured in the fighting.Aspear struck him in the side and gave him a mortal wound. The wound became infected, and Julian died three days later on June 29th, 363AD. With the death of their leader, the Roman army decided to retreat.And on the way back they elected a man named Jovian to be the new emperor. Jovian led the army out of Persia, but then died suddenly just 8 months later. After Jovian's sudden death, another
paragraphs.zh: 已经深入了美索不达米亚。 但是在公元 363 年 6 月 26 日，萨 马拉战役爆发了。 这原本是一场很小，也不太重要的 战役，但是尤里安在这场战役中受 了伤。一根长矛射中了他的腰部， 使他受了致命伤。 伤口感染了，三天后，尤里安于公 元 363 年 6 月 29 日去世。 领袖死了，罗马军队决定撤退。在 回去的路上，他们选了约维安做新 皇帝。约维安率领军队离开了波 斯，但 8 个月后突然死亡。 约维安暴毙后，另一位将军瓦伦提

paragraphs.en: general named Valentinian was chosen as emperor. Both Jovian and Valentinian were Christians, so the official religion went back to being Christianity during their rule. Valentinian would rule for the next 9 years, and in many ways, he was the last of the great Roman emperors. But of course Valentinian didn't know that at the time. What he did know was that the empire was too big to rule alone, and he needed to choose a co-emperor. But this was a dangerous choice, whoever he chose might rebel against him and start another civil war.
paragraphs.zh: 尼安被选为皇帝。 约维安和瓦伦提尼安都是基督徒， 所以在他们统治期间，基督教被恢 复为官方宗教。 瓦伦提尼安在位九年。从很多方面 来说，他是最后一位伟大的罗马皇 帝。 但当然，瓦伦提尼安当时并不知道 这一点。 他所知道的是，帝国太大了，不能 仅由一个人独立统治，他需要选择 一个共治皇帝。 但这是一个危险的选择，无论他选 择谁，都可能反抗他，引发另一场 内战。

paragraphs.en: So Valentinian chose the man who he trusted most in the world, his younger brother Valens. Valentinian also had a son named Gratian, but in 364AD, Gratian was only 5 years old. In his official duties Valentinian did a good job, but his private life was not as upstanding. In 370AD he fell in love with another woman named Justina, and divorced his first wife, Marina Severa. Then Valentinian had two children with Justina.Adaughter named Flavia Galla, (born in 370) and a son named Valentinian II (born the next year in 371AD) The descendants of this royal family
paragraphs.zh: 所以瓦伦提尼安选择了他在这个 世界上最信任的人：他的弟弟瓦伦 斯。 瓦伦提尼安也有一个儿子，名 为 格拉提安。但在 364 年， 格 拉提安只有 5 岁。 瓦伦提尼安在公务上做得很好，但 他的私生活却不那么正直。 公元 370 年，他爱上了另一位名叫 贾丝蒂娜的女子，并与第一任妻子 玛丽娜·塞维拉离婚。 然后瓦伦提尼安和贾丝蒂娜生了 两个孩子。一个女儿叫弗蕾维亚·加 拉(生于 370 年)，另一个儿子叫瓦 伦提尼安二世(生于公元 371 年) 随着罗马帝国的瓦解，这个皇室的

paragraphs.en: would rule Rome for the next 75 years as the empire fell apart. But for now everything seemed stable. And then in 375AD, Valentinian died suddenly at the age of 54. He suffered a stroke while angrily yelling at some ambassadors from a Germanic tribe. Authority now passed to his 16-year old son Gratian and younger brother Valens. Valentinian's death happened at a terrible time for the empire, because at that point, a new menace was just beginning to emerge: a powerful new people called the Huns. The Huns had been living on the steppe grasslands north of the
paragraphs.zh: 后裔将统治罗马 75 年。 但就目前而言，一切似乎都很稳 定。 然后在公元 375 年，瓦伦提尼安突 然去世，享年 54 岁。 他在愤怒地向日耳曼部落的一些 大使大喊大叫时中风了。 现在，他的权力传给了 16 岁的儿 子格拉提安，和他的弟弟瓦伦斯。 瓦伦提尼安死得不是时候。因为在 那个时候，一个强大的新民族—— 匈奴人，出现了。 几个世纪以来，匈奴人一直生活在 里海北部的大草原上。但在 3 世纪

paragraphs.en: Caspian sea for centuries. But in the late 300s, they began moving west. They crossed the Volga river around 360, and arrived north of the Black Sea by 375, the year Valentinian died. The Huns were usually disorganized and fought among themselves. But when united by a strong king, their skills fighting with arrows on horseback made them an almost unstoppable force. In this way they were very similar to the Mongols who came 800 years later. Now they were moving west, pushing into land controlled by their neighbors the Goths. They defeated the Goths in battle, and many Gothic tribes began moving south
paragraphs.zh: 后期，他们开始向西迁移。 他们在公元360年左右渡过伏尔加 河，在瓦伦提尼安去世那年，也就 是公元 375 年，他们到达了黑海以 北。 匈奴人通常组织混乱，相互争斗。 但是当他们在一个强大的国王的 带领下团结在一起时，他们在马背 上射箭的技巧使他们几乎成为一 股不可阻挡的力量。 在这一点上，他们与 800 年后来到 这里的蒙古人非常相似。 现在他们向西进发，进入他们的邻 居哥特人控制的土地。他们在战斗 中打败了哥特人，为了逃离匈奴人 的进攻，许多哥特部落开始向南部 和西部迁移。

paragraphs.en: and west, in order to flee the Hunnic advance. Of course, the only thing south and west was the Roman empire, so the Goths and other Germanic tribes began pushing against the Roman border. So, like a row of dominoes, the Hunnic migrations began a chain of events that would eventually lead to the fall of the Western Roman Empire. But the details of that story must wait for our next lesson, as we discuss the Goths. I hope you will join me next time for the next part of this story. Thank you all very much. Okay everyone I hope you enjoyed this lesson and want to learn more.
paragraphs.zh: 当然，南部和西部就是罗马帝国， 所以哥特人和其他日耳曼部落开 始向罗马边境逼近。 因此，就像多米诺骨牌一样，匈奴 人的迁徙引发了一连串的事件，最 终导致了西罗马帝国的灭亡。 但是这个故事的细节必须等到我 们下节课，我们讨论哥特人时再详 细解说。 我希望你们下次能和我一起看这 个故事的下一部分。非常感谢大 家。 好了，同学们，我希望你们喜欢这 节课，希望你们有兴趣了解更多。

paragraphs.en: If you do, I have a good recommendation for you. That is The History of Rome Podcast by Mike Duncan. You can download it for free, and it will take you all the way from the legendary founding of Rome by Romulus until the fall of the Western Empire in 476Ad. I recommend it quite highly. It has been one of my main sources in creating this lesson. Also, if you found the emperor Julian to be an interesting Character, you might enjoy the novel Julian written by the famous American author, Gore Vidal. The novel is a fictional account of Julian's life, but it is based upon the historical sources.
paragraphs.zh: 我有一些很好的资源可以推荐给 你们。 那就是麦克·邓肯的《罗马历史》 播客。你可以免费下载它，它将带 你领略传奇的罗马创始人罗穆卢 斯，一直到西元 476 年罗马帝国灭 亡。 我强烈推荐这个播客，它是我研发 课程时的重要参考资料之一。 此外，如果你觉得尤里安皇帝是个 有趣的人物，你可能会喜欢美国著 名作家戈尔·维达尔的小说《尤里 安》。 这部小说是对尤里安生活的虚构 描述，但它是以历史资料为基础而 写作的。

titleZh: 第二讲：哥特人|一场发生在 1600 年前的难民危机

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on the Middle Ages. Today we will continue learning about the Fall of Rome. In order to truly understand how Rome fell we need to introduce as very important player in this story: The Goths. This lesson will focus on a short period of only 19 years. But in these two decades, a lot happened. This is when the fall of the Western Roman empire really began. Let's continue where we left the story last time. It is 375AD, the Roman Emperor Valentinian I has just died, leaving the empire under the control of his 16-year old son Gratian and his younger brother
paragraphs.zh: 1600 年前的难民危机 大家好，欢迎回到我们的中世纪系 列讲座。今天，我们来继续学习罗 马帝国的衰落。为了真正了解罗马 帝国是怎么衰落的，我们需要了解 这个故事中一个很重要的角色— —哥特人。 本课会聚焦于一段只有 19 年的短 暂时期。但是在近二十年间，发生 了很多事情。 这是西罗马帝国真正开始衰落的 时间。 我们从上次课的结尾说起。 公元 375 年，罗马皇帝瓦伦提尼安 一世去世，帝国的统治权落到了他 16 岁的儿子格雷西恩和他的弟弟 瓦伦斯手里。

paragraphs.en: Valens. The death of Valentinian I came at terrible time, because the Huns were beginning to move westward. In the same year of 375AD, the Huns defeated a Germanic people called the Goths. Many Gothic tribes began moving south and west in order to escape the Huns. But of course this pushed them against the borders of the Roman Empire. The next year in 376AD, a Gothic tribe led by a King named Frittigern reached the Danube river, the northern border of the Roman Empire. Frittigern and his people were
paragraphs.zh: 瓦伦提尼安一世死的很不是时候， 因为这时匈人正朝着西方进发。 同样在公元 375 年，匈人击败了一 个日耳曼民族——哥特人。 为了躲避匈人，很多哥特人部落开 始向南边和西边迁徙。 但是，这也把他们推向了罗马帝国 的边境。 公元 376 年，由国王弗里蒂杰恩率 领的一个哥特人部落到达了位于 罗马帝国北部边境的多瑙河。 弗里蒂杰恩和他的人民是在躲避

paragraphs.en: running away from the Huns. But instead of attacking the border, Frittigern did something interesting. He asked for permission to enter the empire and settle his people within its borders. At that point the eastern emperor Valens had most of his army in the Middle East fighting the Sassanid Persians. Valens didn't have a large army on the Danube since most of his troops were in Syria. And since Frittigern was asking permission, Valens thought that maybe he could recruit Frittigern's men to work as soldiers in his war against Persia. This was not such a strange idea. Ever since the crisis of the 3rd
paragraphs.zh: 匈人。 但是，弗里蒂杰恩没有直接攻打边 境，而是做了一件很有趣的事情。 他请求获得进入帝国，并且在边境 内安置人民的许可。 当时，东罗马皇帝瓦伦斯的大部分 军队都在中东对抗萨珊波斯人。 瓦伦斯在多瑙河周边没有大量的 士兵，因为他的军队都在叙利亚。 而且，因为弗里蒂杰恩请求进入的 许可，瓦伦斯觉得也许能把他的人 收作士兵，与他一起攻打波斯。 这并不是什么新鲜的想法。自从三 世纪的危机以来，罗马人就一直在

paragraphs.en: century, the Romans had been hiring Germanic tribes to work as soldiers in the Roman army. In fact, the Romans even had a special term for these allied Germans, they called them "Federatti" So Valens agreed to led Frittigern and his Goths cross the Danube, and promised to supply them with food if Frittigern would fight for him. Frittigern agreed. And so, in the summer of 376AD, about 100,000 Goths crossed the Danube. This was way more than the Romans had been expecting, and pretty soon this migration turned into a crisis.
paragraphs.zh: 雇佣日耳曼部落人民到罗马军队 中充当士兵。 事实上，罗马人甚至还有一个特殊 的词来称呼这帮日耳曼盟军—— 所以，瓦伦斯同意带领着弗里蒂杰 恩和他的哥特人们穿过多瑙河，也 承诺如果弗里蒂杰恩为他们打仗 的话，他们就会为之提供食物。 弗里蒂杰恩同意了。 因此，在公元 376 年的夏天，大约 有十万名哥特人穿越了多瑙河。 这比罗马人设想的可要多多了，很 快，这次大规模的移民就变为了危 机。

paragraphs.en: Valens sent supplies of food, but corrupt officials sold the food and stole the money instead of giving it to the Goths. The Romans were also supposed to collect the Gothic weapons as they crossed the border, but corrupt officials accepted bribes from the Goths instead of doing this. So now there were 100,000 armed and hungry Goths inside the border of the Empire. As the Goths began to run out of food, local Romans sold it to them at very high prices. In fact, the Goths became so hungry that some families traded their children as slaves to their Roman neighbors in exchange for dogs to eat.
paragraphs.zh: 瓦伦斯送来了食物供给，但是腐败 的官员却把食物卖掉换钱，并没有 交给哥特人。 罗马人本来也应该在哥特人越过 边境的时候收缴他们的兵器，但是 接受了哥特人贿赂的腐败官员并 没有这样做。 也就是说，这时有十万名饥肠辘辘 而且全副武装的哥特人在帝国的 边境内！ 当哥特人的食物消耗殆尽的时候， 罗马当地人以很高的价钱卖食物 给他们。 事实上，哥特人实在太饥饿了，甚 至有的家庭将孩子卖给他们的罗 马邻居当奴隶，以此换狗来吃。

paragraphs.en: The price was one child for one dog. The situation on the border was now a crisis and the Goths were hungry and desperate. But Valens was in the south-east fighting the Persians, so he wasn't paying any attention to this problem. Then the local Roman governor Lupicinus tried to kidnap Frittigern to prevent any sort of rebellion, but this backfired. His trap failed and Frittigern escaped. The attempted kidnapping and shortage of food pushed the Goths too far. In the winter of 376 the hungry, desperate Goths rebelled.
paragraphs.zh: 价钱就是一个孩子换一条狗。 边境上的现状就是危机四伏，哥特 人已经饿到绝望了。 但是瓦伦斯还在东南边跟波斯人 打仗，他根本注意不到这个问题。 这时，罗马本地执政长官卢皮奇努 斯试图绑架弗里蒂杰恩以避免暴 乱发生，但是却事与愿违。 他的计划失败了，弗里蒂杰恩跑 了。 这次未遂的绑架和长期的食物短 缺把哥特人逼急了。在 376 年的冬 天，饥饿绝望的哥特人叛乱了。

paragraphs.en: Now things were really looking bad. Valens realized that he couldn't continue his war with Persia, so he sent ambassadors to the Persian emperor Shapur II asking for peace. Shapur agreed, but the Romans had to give up territory inArmenia to end the war. It took a while to work out the peace treaty, so Valens and his army had to stay in Syria for the next 18 months establishing peace with Persia. This meant that no Roman armies were available to stop the Goths, and for more than a year they rampaged around Thrace stealing, killing, and plundering the Romans. The Romans retreated into their walled cities, which the Goths were
paragraphs.zh: 至此事情变得很糟。瓦伦斯意识到 他不能继续与波斯人打仗了，所以 他派遣大使去往波斯帝国沙普尔 二世那里谈和。 莎普尔同意了，但是想要终止战 争，罗马人必须放弃亚美尼亚的领 土。 制定出全部的和平条约花费了很 长时间，所以为了与波斯建立友好 关系，瓦伦斯和他的军队不得不在 叙利亚继续停留了 18 个月。 这就意味着，没有罗马的军队能阻 止哥特人，长达一年多的时间里， 哥特人在色雷斯制造混乱，偷窃， 杀害，抢劫罗马人。 罗马人撤退回到围城里面，那里是 哥特人攻不到的地方。但是哥特人

paragraphs.en: unable to attack. But the Goths controlled the countryside around the cities. This horrible situation continued for all of 377 while Valens was trying to make Peace with Persia. Finally, in early 378 a treaty was signed and Valens could move North to stop the Goths. The western Emperor couldn't stop the Goths either, because all of his soldiers were needed to defend the Rhine and Danube rivers. So he needed a year to organize a new army. Then, in 378, another large Germanic tribe called theAlemmani attacked the Empire by crossing the Rhine river. This tribe was also running away
paragraphs.zh: 控制着城市周边的乡村。 这个可怕的境况持续了整个公元 377 年，而这一年瓦伦斯一直都在 试图与波斯讲和。 最终，在 378 年初，合约签订了， 瓦伦斯可以向北出发去制止哥特 人了。 但是西部皇帝也没办法阻止哥特 人，因为他全部的士兵都要去保护 莱茵河和多瑙河。因此他需要一年 的时间来筹备一个新的队伍。 之后在 378 年，另外一个叫做阿勒 曼尼的日耳曼大部落越过莱茵河 攻打了帝国。 这个部落也是在躲避匈人。

paragraphs.en: from the Huns. So Gratian had to send his army north to fight theAlemmani just as Valens’army was reaching Constantinople. In spring of 378 Gratian sent letters to his uncle Valens telling him that he needed a few months to defeat theAlemanni and then he would come east and reinforce him. Together they would defeat the Goths. Then in late May Gratian attacked theAlemmani and defeated them. Gratian drove them back across the Rhine, and by June of 378, the Alemmani threat had been defeated. But Valens was getting impatient.
paragraphs.zh: 这样，当瓦伦斯的军队才刚到达君 士坦丁堡的时候，格雷西恩就必须 得把他的军队派到北部去跟阿勒 曼尼人打仗。 在公元 378 年的春天，格雷西恩给 他的叔叔瓦伦斯写信，告诉他自己 这里需要几个月的时间来对抗阿 勒曼尼人，之后他就会去到东边帮 助瓦伦斯。 他们会一起战胜哥特人的。 五月底的时候，格雷西恩攻打了阿 勒曼尼人并且将其击败。 格雷西恩将他们驱赶回莱茵河对 面，也就是说到 378 年的六月前为 止，阿勒曼尼人的威胁终于被消除 了。 但是瓦伦斯等不了了。哥特人已经

paragraphs.en: The Goths had been pillaging the Roman countryside for almost 2 years. The people of Constantinople were protesting against his inaction. And Valens was also beginning to feel jealous of his nephew's victories. Everybody was praising the young emperor Gratian while he sat around doing nothing. By July of 378 the Goths had moved south and were threatening the countryside around the Roman city ofAdrianople. Valens received intelligence reports that the Gothic army was only about 10,000 men, much smaller than his Roman army of 15,000. Valens’impatience got the better of him, and he decided to attack.
paragraphs.zh: 在罗马的乡村抢夺了两年了。君士 坦丁堡的人民都在抗议他的不作 为。 而且瓦伦斯对于他的侄子的胜利 也感到很嫉妒。 所有人都在称赞年轻的格雷西恩 国王，然而他却一直无所事事。 378 年七月，哥特人转移到南边， 开始对罗马城市阿德里安堡周边 的乡村形成威胁。 瓦伦斯得到情报称哥特人的军队 只有大概一万人，远远少于他自己 一万五千人的罗马军队。 瓦伦斯的不耐烦战胜了他的理智， 他决定要开始攻打。

paragraphs.en: He marched his army toAdrianople and then sent them out in pursuit of the Goths. But it took his men 7 hours of marching to find the Goths. By this time they were all tired and dehydrated. When the Romans reached the Goths, the Goths began burning the grass and smoke blew into the Roman lines, confusing them and making them even more thirsty. But the Romans were confident of victory and even though they were hot, thirsty and tired, they charged into battle. Fierce fighting began between the Gothic and Roman infantry, but the superior discipline of the Roman troops began to prevail.
paragraphs.zh: 他行军到阿德里安堡，派出士兵去 追赶哥特人。 但是他的人花了七个小时去找哥 特人。这时他们都非常疲惫而且身 体有些脱水了。 当罗马人到达哥特人所在的地方 时，哥特人开始放火烧草，产生的 烟雾顺着风飘向罗马人的路上，不 仅是他们无法辨别方向，还使他们 的口渴加剧。 但是罗马人对于胜利很有信心，即 使他们又热又渴又累，他们还是继 续战斗。 哥特人和罗马步兵之间展开了激 烈的战斗，但是罗马军队卓越的纪 律性开始占上风了。

paragraphs.en: The Goths were pushed back and the Romans began winning the battle. But just at that moment the Romans heard the thundering of hooves, and Valens realized his terrible mistake. Yes, his 15,000 infantry outnumbered the 10,000 Gothic infantry, but Roman intelligence had messed up. The Goths also had 10,000 cavalry who had been out searching for supplies.And now they were charging into battle, attacking the surprised Romans from behind! The Roman infantry were hit from behind, and a massacre began of Valens's exhausted and dehydrated troops. The Roman army was utterly
paragraphs.zh: 哥特人被压制，罗马人开始有赢的 迹象了。 但是就在这时，罗马人听到了铁蹄 的雷鸣声，瓦伦斯意识到他犯了严 重的错误。 是，没错，他的一万五步兵远超过 哥特人一万步兵，但是罗马人糊涂 了！哥特人还有一万的骑兵出去搜 寻供给了。然而现在他们都回来参 与战斗了，正在从后方攻击猝不及 防的罗马人。 罗马人腹背受敌，瓦伦斯的军队已 经体力不支，严重脱水，无力抵挡 敌人的大屠杀。 罗马军队阿德里安堡全军覆没，瓦

paragraphs.en: destroyed at Adrianople, and Valens himself was killed on the battlefield. It was one of the greatest Roman defeats in their entire history. Afew days later Gratian arrived to reinforce Valens. But now there was no more Valens and no more army to reinforce. With Gratian's reinforcements, the Romans would have beaten the Goths, but his army was too small to fight them alone. So Gratian retreated west and the Gothic war continued. Gratian now needed to find a good leader to replace his uncle as emperor of the East, so he chose one of his most reliable generals, a man named Theodosius.
paragraphs.zh: 伦斯也在战场上被杀。 这是罗马整个历史上最大的失败 之一。 几天后，格雷西恩过来帮助瓦伦 斯。但是这时，已没有瓦伦斯，也 没有需要帮助的军队了。 如果有了格雷西恩的帮助，罗马本 可以打败哥特的，但是只剩格雷西 恩的军队独自应战，完全没有胜 算。 格雷西恩于是只好向西边撤退， 哥特的战争还在继续。 现在，格雷西恩需要的是一个能代 替他叔叔去统治东部的领导者，所 以他从他最信任的将军中挑选了 一位，叫做狄奥多西。

paragraphs.en: The assignment was clear: if you can stop the Goths, I will make you co-emperor. Theodosius accepted the job. The only problem was Theodosius had no army to fight them with. Every soldier the empire had was already needed to defend the borders. If he took any soldiers away from border defense, the empire would be invaded by either Persians or Germanic tribes. So Theodosius set to work recruiting men and building a new army from scratch. Unfortunately, Theodosius knew that his fresh recruits would be no match for the veteran Gothic army. So he avoided direct battle and chased the Goths around Thrace and
paragraphs.zh: 任务很明确：如果你能阻止哥特 人，我就任命你为共治皇帝。 狄奥多西接受了这个任务。 唯一的问题就是狄奥多西手下没 有能为他打仗的队伍。帝国所拥有 的每个士兵都被派去保卫边境了。 如果他从边境战士中调走任何一 队，帝国就会受到来自波斯或者是 日耳曼部落的侵略。 所以狄奥多西开始着手招兵买马， 白手起家组建队伍。 不幸的是，狄奥多西知道他新招的 士兵根本不可能是哥特的老兵部 队的对手。 所以他避免直接战斗，围着色雷斯 和马其顿追赶哥特人长达 5 年。他

paragraphs.en: Macedonia for five years, harassing their forces and launching hit and run attacks, but avoiding a direct battle. This was not satisfying for the Romans, but it was a wise strategy. The Gothic war of 378-383 was a stalemate. The Goths did not have the equipment to attack the walled Roman cities, but the Romans did not have the soldiers to defeat the Goths. After 5 years of fighting, both the Goths and the Romans were exhausted and ready for peace. (By the way, I'm using Frittigern's picture to represent the Goths, but he had died by 383 and they were being led by other men at that point.)
paragraphs.zh: 们骚扰哥特军队，展开游击战，就 是不与哥特展开正面对战。罗马当 然不满足于此，但这是一个很明智 的战略。 378 年到 383 年，哥特之战陷入僵 局。哥特人没有能攻打罗马城墙的 装备，罗马也没有能战胜哥特的士 兵。 五年的战斗之后，哥特人和罗马人 都精疲力尽了，都想谈和。 （顺便说一下，我用弗里蒂杰恩的 照片来代表哥特人，但是他在 383 年的时候早已去世了，哥特军队是 由其他人在带领的。）

paragraphs.en: This was the agreement. 1) The Goths and Romans would stop fighting, and the Goths would stop attacking Roman settlements. 2) The Goths would be allowed to settle in the Empire. But unlike other Federatti tribes, they would be allowed to keep their own leaders and laws and live together as a group. Basically, they would be an independent nation within the borders of the empire. 3) The Goths would provide soldiers for Theodosius's army. So now the war was over, but there was a nation of Goths living inside the borders of Rome. Perhaps the Romans could have
paragraphs.zh: 以下是和平条约： 1）哥特人和罗马人将停止战争， 哥特人会停止攻打罗马人的住处。 2）哥特人可以在帝国定居。但是 不像其他的盟军部落，他们还可以 继续效忠自己的领导，按照自己的 法律生活，可以继续在一起群居。 基本上他们就是一个坐落于帝国 领土上的独立国家。 3）哥特人要为狄奥多西提供士兵。 至此战争告一段落，但是在罗马的 领土上，哥特人作为一个独立的国 家生活着。 也许罗马人本可以改变这种状况，

paragraphs.en: changed this situation, but Theodosius soon needed those Gothic soldiers, because in 383, the governor of Britain - Magnus Maximus - rebelled. Gratian rushed north to stop Maximus but he lost the battle and was killed onAugust 25th, 383AD. With the death of Gratian, his younger half-brother Valentinian II became the Western Emperor, but he was just 16 years old! For the next 4 years there was an uneasy peace as Valentinian ruled in Italy and NorthAfrica. Magnus Maximus ruled Britian, Gaul and Spain. And Theodosius ruled the east. Magnus Maximus was an old friend
paragraphs.zh: 但是狄奥多西很快就需要这些哥 特士兵了，因为 383 年，不列颠的 统治者——马格努斯.马克西穆斯 谋反了。 格雷西恩赶往北边去制止马克西 穆斯，但是他战败了，公元 383 年 8 月 25 日被杀。 因为格雷西恩死了，他的同父异母 的弟弟瓦伦提尼安二世继承了西 部帝国的王位，但是他此时只有 16 岁。 之后的四年是难得的平静。 瓦伦提尼安统治着意大利和北非。 马格努斯 马克西穆斯统治着不列 颠，高卢和西班牙。 狄奥多西统治着东部。 马格努斯马克西穆斯是狄奥多西

paragraphs.en: of Theodosius and probably thought that his friend would support his rebellion. But Theodosius decided to stay loyal to the family that had made him emperor. Finally, in 387, Magnus Maximus got tired of waiting and decided to attack Italy. His army was stronger and so Valentinian and the Imperial family fled east to the protection of Theodosius in Constantinople. Theodosius's wife had died the previous year, so Justina proposed a marriage between him and her daughter Flavia Galla. This marriage would guarantee that Theodosius continued to protect the Imperial family, and for Theodosius it would ensure his legitimacy.
paragraphs.zh: 的老朋友，他可能认为他的老朋友 会支持他的谋反。 但是狄奥多西决定要对授予他王 位的皇室家庭保持忠诚。 终于，在公元 387 年，马格努斯 马 克西穆斯等得不耐烦了，决定攻打 意大利，于是瓦伦提尼安和整个皇 室都逃往东边去君士坦丁堡寻求 狄奥多西的庇护。 狄奥多西的妻子前一年去世了，所 以贾斯汀娜提出将自己的女儿弗 拉维亚加拉嫁给他。 这个联姻能够确保狄奥多西继续 保护皇室家庭，对于狄奥多西来 说，也能保证他王位的合法性。

paragraphs.en: So Theodosius agreed. The next year Theodosius went west with his army and his Gothic allies and he defeated Magnus Maximus at the Battle of the Save river. The odosius restored Valentinian II to power in the west and left his generalArbogastes behind to lead the military. Arbogastes, like much of the Roman army, was a German. For four years the empire had peace. But the young emperor Valentinian II was now basically a puppet of Arbogastes, the real leader in the west. By 392 Valentinian II was 21 years
paragraphs.zh: 所以狄奥多西同意了。 第二年狄奥多西带领着军队和哥 特盟军去往西边，在萨韦河之战中 击败了马格努斯马克西穆斯。 狄奥多西把东部的统治权还给了 瓦伦提尼安二世并且留下了他的 将军，阿波加斯特，来领导军队。 就像很多的罗马军人一样，阿波加 斯特是一个日尔曼人。 帝国拥有了四年的和平。 但是，年轻的国王，瓦伦提尼安二 世现在基本上就是阿波加斯特的 木偶，阿波加斯特才是西方真正的 王。 到 392 年，瓦伦提尼安二世 21 岁

paragraphs.en: old, and he began to resent the power of this German general Arbogastes. In May, the two men began fighting. Valentinian tried to fireArbogastes, butArbogasted told him that Theodosius not Valentinian was his boss. The next day Valentinian II was found hanged to death in his room. Arbogastes claimed it was a suicide. But back in Constantinople people began to suspect thatArbogastes had murdered Valentinian. Arbogastes sent a message to Theodosius explaining the situation, but he got no instructions. Arbogastes realized that Theodosius might have him arrested and executed for murder, so he decided
paragraphs.zh: 了，他开始憎恨阿波加斯特这名日 耳曼将军所拥有的权力。 五月，这两个人打起来了。 瓦伦提尼安想要罢免阿波加斯特， 但是阿波加斯特却说狄奥多西才 是他的上司，并不是瓦伦提尼安。 第二天，瓦伦提尼安被发现吊死在 自己的房间。 阿波加斯特声称这是一起自杀，但 是君士坦丁堡的人民都开始怀疑 是阿波加斯特谋杀了瓦伦提尼安。 阿波加斯特给狄奥西多解释这个 情况，但是他没有收到任何回复和 指示。 阿波加斯特意识到狄奥多西可能 会以谋杀罪逮捕他并处决他，于是 他决定谋反。

paragraphs.en: to rebel. He appointed his old teacher Eugenius as the new Western Roman Emperor. Back in Constantinople, Theodosius's new wife, Flavia Galla, was convinced that her brother had been murdered, and she began putting pressure on her husband to bringArbogastes to justice. Theodosius began organizing another army, and two years later in 394 he marched west with his Gothic allies to met Arbogastes's army at the battle of the Frigidus river on September 5th, 394AD. The Frigidus river is in northeastern Italy, just on the other side of the Alps.
paragraphs.zh: 他任命他的老师尤金尼厄斯作为 西罗马帝国的新皇帝。 在君士坦丁堡，狄奥多西的妻子， 弗拉维亚加拉坚信自己的兄弟是 被谋杀的，她开始向她的丈夫施 压，要他制裁阿波加斯特。 狄奥多西开始组建另一支队伍，两 年后的 394 年，他带着哥特盟军向 西进军去会阿波加斯特的军队。 394 年 9 月 5 日，他们在弗里吉都 斯河展开战斗。 弗里吉都斯河在意大利东北部，在 阿尔卑斯的另一边。

paragraphs.en: As Theodosius's army reached the other side of the mountains, they came up against the army of Arbogastes, which was waiting for them, and had already seized all the high ground. Theodosius decided to attack anyway. His army was half Roman and half Gothic. The Roman part was led by his general Stilicho, the Gothic part was led by a Gothic general named Alaric. The first day of fighting happened on September 5th. Theodosius sent his Gothic troops ahead, and kept his Romans in reserve, so the Goths took most of the casualties. By the end of the day it looked like Theodosius would lose.
paragraphs.zh: 当狄奥多西的部队到达山的另一 边时，他们迎面遇到早早等在那 里，而且已经占领所有有利高地的 阿波加斯特军队。 狄奥西多决定无论如何都要进攻。 他的军队是由一半罗马人一半哥 特人组成的。 罗马人的部分是由斯提里科将军 率领的，哥特部分是由哥特将军亚 拉里克率领。 9 月 5 日开战，狄奥多西派哥特部 落在前面冲锋陷阵，将罗马人留在 后方，所以哥特人伤亡惨重。 这一天打下来，狄奥多西似乎要输 了。

paragraphs.en: But the second day of fighting went better andArbogastes was defeated on September 6th, 383AD. However,Alaric and the Goths felt betrayed. Alaric began to suspect that Theodosius had wanted the Goths to take such heavy casualties and used the battle to weaken them. Alaric decided that he could not trust the Romans. After the battle of the Frigidus, Theodosius was the undisputed sole Roman Emperor. He was the last man to ever rule a united Roman empire by himself. But even though Theodosius was only 48 years old, his reign would last just another 4 months.
paragraphs.zh: 但是第二天战况好转，在公元 383 年 9 月 6 日，阿波加斯特战败了。 然而，亚拉里克和所有哥特人都觉 得被背叛了。 亚拉里克开始怀疑狄奥多西故意 想要哥特人伤亡如此惨重，他是在 利用战争来削弱哥特人。 亚拉里克决定不能再相信罗马人 了。 弗里吉都斯河之战以后，狄奥多西 是罗马帝国毋庸置疑的唯一的皇 帝。 他是最后一个独自统治罗马联合 王国的人。 但是尽管狄奥多西只有 48 岁，他 的统治也只过了四个月就到头了。

paragraphs.en: In the first days of 395AD Theodosius fell ill, and died on January 17th. He had 3 surviving children. His two sons from his first marriage: Honorius andArcadius. And a 7-year old daughter named Galla Placidia from his marriage to Flavia Galla. In 395 the imperial crown passed to his two sons: Honorius and Arcadius. Arcadius was made emperor of the East, and Honorius was made emperor of the West. Unfortunately, neither of Theodosius's sons were capable leaders, especially Honorius who was just 11 years old.
paragraphs.zh: 公元 395 年初，狄奥多西抱病，1 月 17 日就去世了。 他有三个孩子。两个儿子是与第一 位妻子所生：霍诺留和阿卡狄奥 斯。 还有一个同弗拉维亚加拉所生的 七岁女儿叫做加拉·普拉西提阿. 在 395 年，皇室的王冠传到给了他 的两个儿子：霍诺留和阿卡狄奥 斯。 阿卡狄奥斯被封为东部的王，霍诺 留统治西部。 不幸的是，狄奥多西的两个儿子都 不是有能力的统治者，尤其是只有 11 岁的霍诺留。

paragraphs.en: So just as the Western Empire was about to enter its period of greatest crisis, a child was put in charge. Of course, Honorius wasn't the real leader in the West. That job was held by Stilicho, Theodosius' Roman commander from the Battle of the Frigidus. But at this point, Theodosius's other generalAlaric saw an opportunity. Alaric had decided that he no longer could trust the Romans and was afraid that they would soon launch a surprise attack against the Goths. So he wanted to be made a general in the Roman army. This way he would have access to Roman intelligence networks and know if someone was planning a surprise attack against his people.
paragraphs.zh: 就在西部帝国即将爆发严重的危 机时，这个孩子被任命为皇帝了。 当然，霍诺留不是西部真正的领导 者。这个工作实际上是在由斯提里 科在领导，就是那个在弗里吉都斯 河之战中担任罗马指挥的将军。 但是在这时，狄奥多西的另一个将 军亚拉里克看到了机会。 亚拉里克早就决定不再相信罗马 人，并且他担心罗马人很快就会向 哥特人发动突袭。 所以他想在罗马军队中担任一个 将军的角色。这样他就能接触到罗 马的情报网，如果有人想偷袭他的 人民他就会得到消息。

paragraphs.en: Also as a general, he could use the military to keep his people supplied and fed. WhenAlaric's request for an official position was refused, he launched a rebellion in northern Greece, just a few months after Theodosius died. ButAlaric wasn't Rome's only problem. Because at that time a great movement of people was beginning on the other side of the empire's borders. By the time it was all over, the western Roman empire would be gone for good. But that is a story for our next class. Okay everyone I hope you enjoyed that lesson. If you want to learn more, my main recommendation is the same as last time.
paragraphs.zh: 而且，作为一个将军，他也可以使 用军队的资源来给自己的人民提 供补给和食物。 当亚拉里克主动请缨被拒绝后，他 在北希腊发动了谋反，就在狄奥多 西去世几个月之后。 但是亚拉里克并不是罗马面临的 唯一问题。 因为此时，在帝国国境的另一边有 一大群人开始迁移了。在这次迁移 结束的时候，西部罗马帝国就完全 湮没了。 我们会在下次课讲这个事情。 好了，这节课的内容就到这里了。 希望你们都很享受这节课。如果你 想学习更多关于罗马的故事，我的 建议跟上节课一样！

paragraphs.en: Check out The History of Rome podcast by Mike Duncan. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 那就是去听听麦克邓肯的罗马历 史广播。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第三讲：劫掠罗马|罗马的第一次陷落

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on the Middle Ages, today we are going to learn about the Sack of Rome. This lecture is focused on a very short period of time, the 15 years between 395 and 410AD. Our story begins in 395AD with the death of Theodosius, the last man to rule the entire Roman empire.After he died he passed his throne on to his two sons: Honorius and Arcadius. But Honorius andArcadius were both very young, and both weak rulers. So their ministers were the real rulers. In the west the empire was led by
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到中世纪系列讲 座，今天我们继续学习罗马的陷 落。 这节课会聚焦在很短的一段时间， 从公元 395 年到 410 年的 15 年间。 我们的故事从公元395年狄奥多西 ——最后一个统治罗马帝国的人 ——去世说起。他去世之后，王位 传到了他的两个儿子：霍诺留和阿 卡狄奥斯手中。 但是霍诺留和阿卡狄奥斯不仅年 纪都很小，而且都是很懦弱的统治 者。所以实际上他们的大臣才是真 正的统治者。 在西部，这个帝国是在狄奥多西的

paragraphs.en: Theodosius's old general Stilicho. In the east it was led by the consul Rufinius. Trouble began immediately upon Theodosius's death, Stilicho claimed that Theodosius had appointed him regent for the entire empire. Rufinius thought he was lying. Both Rufinius and Stilicho hated each other, which was a terrible situation for Rome's leadership as the empire was heading into a crisis. At this point, Theodosius's gothic generalAlaric saw an opportunity with a child on the Western throne and the chief ministers busy fighting each other. SoAlaric led a Gothic rebellion in 395AD, the same year that
paragraphs.zh: 旧将军斯提里科统帅之下的。 而东部是由执政官鲁芬统治。 狄奥多西一死，麻烦立刻就来了。 斯提里科宣称狄奥多西任命他为 罗马帝国的摄政王。 鲁芬认为他是在撒谎。 鲁芬跟斯提里科互相看不顺眼，这 种情况对于正在面临危机的罗马 帝国的统治来讲不是件好事。 这时，在西部皇帝与首席大臣的争 斗中狄奥多西的哥特将军亚拉里 克看到了机会。 所以，公元 395 年，也就是狄奥多 西去世的同一年亚拉里克发起了

paragraphs.en: Theodosius died. His goal was to force the Romans to give him an official position in the new administration that he could use to protect his people from possible Roman treachery. Alaric began his campaign by ravaging Greece and Macedonia. Stilicho sailed east and defeated Alaric in battle but this made Rufinus nervous that he was getting too powerful. So he started manipulatingAracadius. Under the influence of his minister, Arcadius told Stilicho to leave Greece, soAlaric escaped. But this backfired on Rufinius who was assassinated by other members of the eastern court. BecauseArcadius was such a weak
paragraphs.zh: 叛变。他的目的是要逼迫罗马人在 新的执政党中给他一个长官的位 置，这样如果罗马人出尔反尔，他 也有能力保护自己的人民。 亚拉里克从摧毁希腊和马其顿王 国开始他战役。 斯提里科出海向东打败了亚拉里 克，但是这也使得鲁芬担心他越来 越强。所以他开始控制阿卡狄奥斯 了。 在这位大臣的影响下，阿卡狄奥斯 让斯提里科离开希腊，所以亚拉里 克也逃走了。 但是这对鲁芬却产生了事与愿违 的影响——他被东部宫廷的其他 成员暗杀了。 因为阿卡狄奥斯是一个很懦弱的

paragraphs.en: leader, the real leadership of the empire then passed on to his wife Eudoxia and the court euncuh Eutropius. Aelia Eudoxia was famously corrupt and spent huge sums of money on living a lavish lifestyle. For this reasons she was frequently criticized by John Chrysotum the bishop of Constantinople for her wasteful lifestyle and ignoring the poor. But while all that intrigue was happening in Constantinople, nobody was attending toAlaric, who continued his rampage through the Balkans for the next 4 years. Then in 402,Alaric launched an invasion of Italy and attacked the capital of Milan.
paragraphs.zh: 领导者，帝国的管理权又转移到了 他的妻子尤多西娅和他的宦官欧 特罗庇厄斯。 艾丽娅·尤多西娅以生活堕落闻 名，她一生花费了大量的金钱，过 着骄奢淫逸的生活。 因此她经常被君士坦丁堡的主教 批评，浪费奢侈的生活方式，忽略 对穷苦大众的照顾。 但是就在这些龌龊事情发生之时， 没有人关注亚拉里克，任由他在巴 尔干地区胡作非为了四年。 然后在 402 年，亚拉里克发动了对 意大利的侵略，攻打首都米兰。

paragraphs.en: But Stilicho defeated him in two battles. AndAlaric was forced to retreat, with a much weakened army. This attack on Milan convinced Stilicho that he needed to move the boy emperor Honorius to a safer place, so he chose the city of Ravenna. Ravenna was a Roman fortress inside a swamp, so it was almost impossible for an enemy to attack. There Honorius would spend the rest of his life hiding from the world as his empire collapsed. Four years later, there was another invasion of Italy. We often describe the gothic leaders likeAlaric as barbarians, but that's false.Alaric spoke Latin, was a
paragraphs.zh: 但是斯提里科在两场战役中都打 败了他。 亚拉里克被逼撤兵，军队实力也削 弱了很多。 这次对于米兰的攻打使得斯提里 科坚信他需要把年轻的皇帝霍诺 留转移到一个安全的地方，所以他 选择了拉文纳。 拉文纳是地处沼泽的罗马要塞，所 以基本上不可能受到敌人的攻击。 在这里，霍诺留在逃避帝国陷落中 度过了余生。 四年之后，意大利再一次遭到侵 略。 我们经常把哥特人的首领，比如亚 拉里克形容为野蛮人，其实这是错 误的。亚拉里克说拉丁语，是基督

paragraphs.en: Christian, and admired Roman culture.Alaric's goal was to get a position of power within the empire. But the invasion of 406 was led by a true Barbarian. His name was Radagaisus and he was a pagan who vowed to burn Rome to the ground and sacrifice the senators to his gods. Stilicho did not have enough men to stop Radagaisus, so he removed all of the solders on the Rhine frontier, and brought them south to reinforce his army. With these reinforcements he was able to defeat Radagaisus who was trying to capture Florence. Radagaisus was killed onAugust 23rd, 406 and his people were either
paragraphs.zh: 教徒，并且很崇拜罗马文化。亚拉 里克的目的只是想在这个帝国内 部找到自己的统治位置。 但是发动406年这次侵略战争的是 真正的野蛮人。他的名字叫拉多加 苏斯, 他是个异教徒，他宣称要把 罗马烧为灰烬，把议员都杀了献祭 他自己的上帝。 斯提里科没有足够的人手来阻止 拉多加苏斯，所以他把所有在莱茵 边疆的士兵都带到南边去壮大自 己的队伍。 在这个帮助下，他就可以去抵御正 在试图占领弗洛伦萨的拉多加苏 斯了。 拉多加苏斯在 406 年 8 月 23 日被 烧，他的人民要么被杀，要么被奴

paragraphs.en: killed, enslaved, or joined the Roman army. Now it was September 406AD, but Stilicho was beginning to worry about his enemies in the Eastern empire, and he began to suspect that a civil war might begin between the Eastern and Western empires. So he decided to keep the Rhine troops in Italy, and he left the border undefended. This was Stilicho's big mistake, because in December of 406, three large Germanic tribes, who spoke a similar language to the Goths, realized that the Rhine was undefended. These were the Suebi, Alan and Vandal tribes. So on December 31st, 406Ad, hundreds of thousands of them just walked across the frozen river.
paragraphs.zh: 役，要么加入了罗马人的军队。 现在是公元 406 年的九月，但是斯 提里科开始担心他在东部帝国的 敌人了，他开始怀疑东部帝国和西 部帝国之间要爆发内战了。 他决定把莱茵部落留在意大利，但 那样的话北部边境就毫无防守了。 斯提里科犯了一个大错误，因为 406 年的 12 月，三个跟哥特人说着 同样语言的日耳曼大部落——苏 维汇，阿兰聊，汪达尔——意识到 了莱茵边境没有守卫。 公元 406 年是 12 月 31 日，数以十 万计的人从结冰的河面上走了过 来。

paragraphs.en: This New Years Eve invasion in 406 is a major milestone in the collapse of the Western Empire. Hundreds of thousands of Germanic people had entered the Roman empire, and they would never leave. For the next decade these tribes rampaged across Gaul and Hispania, fighting the Romans and pillaging the countryside. To make things even worse, the governor of Britain, Constantine III, saw this invasion as proof of the emperor's weakness. So in 407 he launched a rebellion and declared himself the new emperor who would restore order to Gaul. And then Constantine pulled all the
paragraphs.zh: 这一次发生在406年最后一天的侵 略是罗马西部帝国陷落的里程碑 式的事件。 数以十万计的日耳曼人民进入到 罗马帝国，而且他们根本就不准备 离开。 在之后的十年里，这几个部落在高 卢和伊比利亚半岛横冲直撞，攻击 罗马人，掠夺乡村。 雪上加霜的是，不列颠的长官，康 斯坦丁三世，把这个当作是帝国软 弱的证明。 所以 407 年他叛乱了，宣称自己是 能使高卢恢复秩序的新皇帝。 接着，康斯坦丁把剩下的士兵带出

paragraphs.en: remaining soldiers out of Britain and crossed the channel to begin his rebellion. And then, while all this chaos was going on, the eastern emperor Arcadius died the next year in 408 AD, even though he was just 31 years old. Arcadius's throne passed on to his 7 year-old son Theodosius II. And perhaps the arrival of a boy emperor would have spelled the doom of the eastern empire as well. But the eastern empire got lucky. Instead of a scheming, treacherous and egotistical maniac, the regent turned out to be a smart, capable, and hardworking man named Anthemius. Unlike so many other leaders at this
paragraphs.zh: 了不列颠，穿过海峡，开始谋反。 然后，就在这些混乱发生的时候， 东部皇帝阿卡狄奥斯第二年也就 是 408 年去世了，享年 31 岁。 皇位传到了他的7岁儿子狄奥多西 二世手上。 而稚子皇帝的登基同样也可能导 致东部帝国的灭亡。 但是东部帝国很幸运。摄政王并不 是一个诡计多端的阴险小人，反而 是一个思维敏捷，能力卓越，努力 工作的人，他叫做安提迈乌斯。 不像罗马历史上处在这个位置的

paragraphs.en: point in Roman History,Anthemius seemed only to care about the well-being of the empire. Under his leadership the great Theodosian walls were constructed. These mighty walls would save Constantinople again and again when her leaders and armies failed. These walls would keep the capital city safe for the next 1000 years. Since a new emperor was coming to the throne, Stilicho decided that he needed to go east to represent the interests of the Western Empire. So in the summer of 408, Stilicho left Honorius in Ravenna and began travelling east to Constantinople. But this was a bad mistake. Honorius was weak-willed and
paragraphs.zh: 其他领导者，安提迈乌斯是真正关 心帝国的福祉的。 在他的领导之下，狄奥多西城墙建 了起来。 这道坚固的城墙在君士坦丁堡的 领导人和军队失败的时候一次次 的保护了自己。 这道城墙在接下来的 1000 年里保 护首都安然无恙。 既然新的皇帝登上宝座，斯提里科 决定他要去到东部以展示西部帝 国对东部很感兴趣。 所以在 408 年的夏天，斯提里科把 霍诺留留在拉文纳就启程向东去 往君士坦丁堡。 但是这是个错误的决定，霍诺留是 个意志薄弱的笨蛋。他玩物丧志，

paragraphs.en: unintelligent. He spent most of his time playing with his pet birds. He was easy to manipulate, and with Stilicho gone, his ministers pounced. They gathered around Honorius and began telling him that Stilicho was to blame for the rebellion and barbarian invasions now happening in Gaul. They were able to turn Honorius against Stillicho. And then, once they had Honorius's backing, they struck and had Stilicho assassinated. The western empire had just lost its best leader. Now a new minister came to power named Olympias. He was a hateful, xenophobic man, and he stirred up the Roman population against all the Gothic people who were living
paragraphs.zh: 一直在玩宠物鸟。 控制他太容易了，斯提里科一走， 他的大臣们就开始恶虎扑食了。 他们聚在霍诺留身边开始告诉他 发生在高卢的叛变和野蛮的侵略 都是斯提里科的错。他们成功地挑 拨了霍诺留和斯提里科的关系。 接着，他们一旦获得了霍诺留的支 持，就开始攻击并暗杀了斯提里 科。西部帝国失去了最棒的领导 者。 现在，一个新的大臣上任了，叫做 奥林匹亚斯，他一个充满仇恨，恐 惧异族人的人，挑起罗马人民对抗 所有生活在他们周围的哥特人。

paragraphs.en: among them. He told the Romans that they had to kill every Goth in Italy to be safe. And so terrible riots broke out all across Italy, and thousands and thousands of Goths began to be massacred by angry Roman mobs. These were mainly the wives and children of Gothic men who were fighting in Roman armies!!! So of course thousands of gothic soldiers and their families began to flee Italy.And where did they go? Well right into the arms ofAlaric, who used these refugees to rebuild his army. So a month later, the newly strong Alaric began negotiations with Honorius. He demanded a position in the Roman army, and regular
paragraphs.zh: 他告诉罗马人必须要杀掉所有在 意大利的哥特人，他们才能安全。 于是，暴乱发生并且蔓延到整个意 大利，数以千计的哥特人被愤怒的 罗马暴徒屠杀。 被杀害的大多是正在前线为了罗 马在拼杀的哥特男人的妻子和孩 子。 自然而然，数以千计的哥特士兵和 他们的家庭开始逃离意大利。他们 去了哪里呢？他们逃到了亚拉里 克的军队，他正在招募难民来重组 队伍。 一个月之后，重获新生且更加强大 的亚拉里克开始跟霍诺留谈判了。 他想在罗马的队伍中寻求一个官 职，定期的食物供给和给自己人民

paragraphs.en: supplies of food and money for his people. In return he would take his people north to the Danube border and defend Rome's frontier. It was actually a very reasonable deal. But Honorius was stupid, and his ministers were xenephobic, so they refused. Very well, if the Romans refused to give his people what he needed, thenAlaric would come and take it. In September of 408 he marched into Italy, the Romans had no army or leaders to stop him. Those soldiers they did have were protecting the emperor in Ravenna, butAlaric just ignored Honorius and marched around Ravenna.
paragraphs.zh: 一些钱。作为回报，他会把他的人 都带到多瑙河边去守护罗马的边 境。 这其实是一个非常合理的交易。 但是霍诺留很愚蠢，他的大臣们很 排外，所以就拒绝了这个提议。 很好，如果罗马人拒绝提供这些东 西的话，那么，亚拉里克就会亲自 过来拿。 在 408 年的九月，他进军意大利， 罗马人根本就没有军队或者统帅 来制止他。 他们所拥有的士兵都在拉瓦纳保 护着帝国，但是亚拉里克直接忽略 掉霍诺留进军拉瓦纳。

paragraphs.en: He had a much bigger goal in mind. For several daysAlaric's army marched south across the Italian countryside. And then they arrived in front of the Gates of Rome. The Romans begged their emperor to send help. But since Honorius himself was safe in Ravenna, he refused to do anything. So it fell to the Senate to negotiate withAlaric. They tried bluffing and tried to scare him by saying there were 800,000 people in Rome, all prepared to fight.Alaric laughed and said: "The thicker the grass the easier the cutting."
paragraphs.zh: 他脑子里有一个更大的目标。 几天以来，亚拉里克的军队向南穿 过意大利村庄。 然后他们就达到了罗马城门前。 罗马人祈求他们的帝国派人来帮 忙。 但是因为霍诺留自己在拉瓦纳很 安好，他就拒绝出兵。 所以与亚拉里克谈判的任务就落 在了参议院的头上。 他们企图虚张声势吓唬他，骗他说 罗马有 80 万士兵在，都准备好迎 战了。亚拉里克笑着说：“越粗的 草越好割。”

paragraphs.en: They askedAlaric what he wanted and he told them, all of the non-Roman slaves in the city, and all of the gold and silver. The senate asked what would then be left for the Romans? Alaric's reply: "Their lives." The Romans complied. They gave Alaric 15,000 kilograms of silver, 4000 silk tunics, 2500 kilograms of gold, 3000 hides, and 40,000 slaves, who then joinedAlaric's army. Then, having got what he wanted, Alaric left. AgainAlaric tried negotiating with Honorius for a position in the Roman government, and again Honorius stupidly refused. Realizing how easy things had been the last time,Alaric decided to
paragraphs.zh: 他们问亚拉里克想要什么，他回答 说，他想要城里所有非罗马人奴隶 还有所有的金银珠宝。 参议院问罗马能留下什么呢？ 亚拉里克回答说：“你们的命。” 罗马人服了。他们给了亚拉里克 1 万 5 千千克的白银，4 千件丝绸上 衣，2 千 5 百千克黄金，3000 张兽 皮，还有后来加入到亚拉里克军队 的 4000 个奴隶。 就这样，拿到东西的亚拉里克撤兵 了。 亚拉里克再次与霍诺留谈判，想要 一个罗马政府中的职位，霍诺留再 一次，十分愚蠢地拒绝了。 亚拉里克从上一次的经历中意识 到这非常容易，两年后的 410 年，

paragraphs.en: return to Rome two years later in 410. Alaric and his men were back in front of the Gates of Rome in Summer of 410. And this time, they put the city under siege.And onAugust 24th, 410AD, they broke through the walls. According to some sources, a traitor inside the city opened a gate for them. And the Goths entered the city and began to sack Rome. The sack was fairly orderly.Alaric was a Christian, so he left church property alone, and anyone who hid in a church would be safe. But otherwise his army had full
paragraphs.zh: 他决定要回到罗马。 亚拉里克和他的人在公元410年再 次回到罗马的城门前。 这一次，他们围攻了城池。在公元 410年8月24日，他们攻陷了城墙。 据一些史料记载，一个叛徒从里面 为他们打开了城门。 哥特人进入到城里开始攻陷罗马。 劫掠罗马的过程非常有序。亚拉里 克是个基督徒，所以他把教堂完完 整整地留下了，只要是躲在教堂里 都能保命。 除此之外，他的军队被全权赋权在

paragraphs.en: permission to pillage Rome to their heart's content. Some of these Goths had been slaves or had their families killed just 2 years before, so there was also a desire for revenge among some elements ofAlaric's army. For many historians, this sack of Rome in 410AD marks the fall of the western Roman empire. But the Western empire would limp onwards for another 66 years until the last Roman emperor was deposed in 476AD. In its place would rise Germanic Kingdoms.And that is the story for our next lesson. Thank you very much! Okay everyone I hope you enjoyed that lesson. If you want to learn
paragraphs.zh: 罗马城里烧杀抢掠。 这些哥特人有的曾经是奴隶，有的 是在两年前家人被杀害了，所以他 们都想要报仇。 一些历史学家认为，公元 410 年罗 马的这次陷落标志着西部罗马帝 国的衰落。 但是西部帝国还是踉踉跄跄地继 续走了 66 年直到公元 476 最后一 任罗马皇帝被废黜。 取而代之的是日耳曼王国。这个部 分的故事且听下回分解。非常感 谢。 希望大家喜欢这节课。如果你们想 了解更多，那我再一次推荐大家去

paragraphs.en: more I will once again recommend my main source for this series of lessons. And that source is The History of Rome podcast by Mike Duncan. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 听我这个系列讲座的资料来源。 那就是麦克邓肯的罗马历史广播。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第四讲：日耳曼诸王国|罗马的第二次陷落

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on the Middle Ages, today we will learn about the Germanic Kingdoms that replaced the Western Roman Empire. We will be focused on the 90 years between 410 and 500AD. As you might remember from last time, the real invasion of Germanic tribes began on New Years eve 406 AD. The Vandal, Suebi andAlani tribes poured across the now undefended Rhine frontier. In response to this, the governor of Britain rebelled and brought his soldiers across the channel to stake his claim to Gaul.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的中世纪系 列讲座，今天我们将会讲到在西罗 马帝国废墟中出现的日耳曼诸王 国。 我们将聚焦于公元 410 年到 500 年 的这 90 年间。 正如你可能还记得的那样，上一次 我们说到日耳曼部落真正的入侵 行动开始于公元 406 年的新年前 夜。 汪达尔人，苏维汇人和阿兰聊人涌 入了现在毫无防御的莱茵河边境。 与此同时，不列颠统治者起兵叛 乱，让他的士兵渡过了大运河并宣 布占领高卢。

paragraphs.en: And Stilicho's enemies used this crisis to turn the emperor against him and have him murdered. Then with Stilicho gone,Alaric was able to invade Italy unopposed. Alaric came once in 408AD and shook the city down for tribute, then he came again in 410 and sacked Rome for real. For the first time in 800 years an enemy army was inside the walls of Rome, sacking the city. After pillaging Rome, Alaric and his men moved south, hoping to capture ships and sail across the Mediterranean to NorthAfrica. But that autumn while the Goths were in southern Italy,Alaric got sick with malaria.
paragraphs.zh: 斯提里科的敌人利用这次危机使 得皇帝开始与他为敌，并成功地谋 杀了斯提里科。 在斯提里科死后，亚拉里克得以毫 无阻碍地入侵意大利。 公元408年亚拉里克再次入侵意大 利，并迫使罗马屈身纳贡，410 年， 他再次率军入侵，这一次真正劫掠 了罗马城。 八百年来首次，敌人的军队进入了 罗马城墙之内，洗劫了这座城市。 在抢劫完罗马以后，亚拉里克带着 他的士兵转而向南，希望能够夺得 一些船只，由此横渡地中海抵达北 非。 但那年秋天，当哥特人驻扎在意大 利南部的时候，亚拉里克患上了疟 疾。

paragraphs.en: And he died. AfterAlaric's death, his crown passed to his brother-in-lawAtaulf. Ataulf then led the Goths out of Italy and into Gaul where they joined in the general mayhem being caused by the other Germanic tribes at that point. And even worse, the Goths had a prisoner. They had captured the emperor's half-sister Galla Placidia. She was the daughter of Theodosius and the grand-daughter of Valentinian I. And then around 414AD,Ataulf married her. Now the Gothic kings had bigger ambitions, the no longer wanted to just raid the empire, they were hoping to join their family to the
paragraphs.zh: 然后他就死了。 在亚拉里克死后，他的王位传给了 他的妹夫阿陶尔夫。 阿陶尔夫于是率领着哥特人离开 意大利进入高卢，在那里他们与其 他日耳曼部落一起，参与了在当地 的大肆破坏。 更糟糕的是，哥特人还抓到了一个 俘虏。他们擒获了皇帝的异母姐妹 加拉·普拉西提阿，她是狄奥多西 一世和瓦伦提尼安一世孙女的女 儿。 大约在公元 414 年前后，阿陶尔夫 迎娶了她。 现在哥特国王们有了更大的野心， 他们想要的不再是劫掠这个帝国， 他们希望让自己的家族成为帝国 皇族的一员。

paragraphs.en: imperial family. Ataulf said as much declaring that before he had just wanted to turn Rome into Gothia. But he had decided that his people were too unruly and wild. SoAtaulf declared that he would revive the Roman Empire with Gothic vigor. It was around this time, 411AD, that all of these various tribes began to settle down and form kingdoms of their own. Constantine III was still trying to organize Roman resistance up north in Gaul, so the tribes settled down in Spain where they could be more secure. At around the same time, thing began to go better for the Romans
paragraphs.zh: 阿陶尔夫此前曾经声称他只是想 要把罗马变成哥特王国的一部分， 但现在他觉得他的人民太过桀骜 不驯、充满野性。 于是阿陶尔夫宣布他将用哥特人 的活力复兴罗马帝国。 也正是在这一时间前后，公元 411 年，所有这些不同的部落都开始定 居下来，并形成他们自己的王国。 君士坦丁三世仍在试图组织罗马 人在高卢北部进行抵抗，因此这些 部落选择了在西班牙定居，在那里 他们更加安全。 大约在同一时间，形势开始有利于 罗马人，当时霍诺留为斯提里科选

paragraphs.en: when Honorius chose a competent replacement for Stilicho, the general Constantius III. Constantius III marched north and defeated the rebel Constantine III. (and yes, I know the two men had similar sounding names!) And so from 411 - 421 Gaul was temporarily returned to the Roman Empire. But the island of Britian was lost for good. Let’s take a moment to look at what happened to Britain after the Romans left. When the Romans withdrew their troops from Britain, Germanic tribes living in what is now Denmark and North Germany
paragraphs.zh: 择了强有力的替代者，将军君士坦 迪乌斯三世。 君士坦提乌斯三世挥军向北，击败 了叛乱的君士坦丁三世。 （以及没有错，我知道这两个人的 名字听起来非常相似！） 因此，从 411 年到 421 年，高卢暂 时回到了罗马帝国的统治之下。 但不列颠岛已经永久地丢掉了。 让我们花一点时间来看看在罗马 人离开后，不列颠岛上发生了些什 么。 在罗马人从不列颠撤军之时，生活 在今天的丹麦和德国北部的日耳 曼诸部落意识到，这个岛屿如今已 经全无防御。

paragraphs.en: realized that the island was undefended. There were three major tribes that responded to this change. The Angles, the Saxons and the Jutes. In the 150 years after the Roman withdrawal they began attacking the island. Since these three tribes were similar in terms of language and ethnicity, we can call them theAnglo-Saxons as a group. At first theAnglo Saxons came as raiders and pirates. The local Romano-British tried to resist them, but without the support of the Imperial armies, they were not strong enough. Eventually theAnglo-Saxons
paragraphs.zh: 对这一变化作出反应的是三个大 部落，盎格鲁人，撒克逊人以及朱 特人。 在罗马人撤走后的一百五十年里， 他们开始攻打这个岛。 由于这三个部落在语言和种族血 缘方面十分相似，我们可以把他们 作为一个群体来看待，称他们为盎 格鲁-撒克逊人。 起初盎格鲁-撒克逊人是劫匪和海 盗。 当地的罗马不列颠人曾试图抵御 他们，但没有帝国军队的支持，他 们没有足够的军力。 最终盎格鲁-撒克逊人停止了劫

paragraphs.en: stopped raiding and came to settle down. They set up farms and villages all along the east coast of the island. After 560AD, these Germanic tribes began calling their new country "Angle-Land" or "England" TheAnglo-Saxons were roughly divided into 7 kingdoms: Northumbria, Mercia, EastAnglia, Kent, East Saxon (or Essex), South Saxon (or Sussex) and West Saxon (or Wessex). The Romano-British were mostly Celts by ethnicity, and they fought theAnglo-Saxons, but slowly got pushed west. TheAnglo-Saxons called them "Welsh" which is theAnglo-Saxon word for "foreigner."
paragraphs.zh: 掠，开始定居下来，他们沿着不列 颠岛的东海岸建立了农场和村庄。 在公元 560 年以后，这些日耳曼部 落开始将他们的新家园称作“盎格 鲁之地”，或者是“英格兰“。 盎格鲁-撒克逊人大致分成了七个 小王国：诺森布里亚、麦西亚、东 盎格利亚、肯特、埃塞克斯、萨塞 克斯和威塞克斯。 从种族来说，罗马不列颠人主要是 凯尔特人，他们与盎格鲁-撒克逊 人持续作战，但逐渐被赶向了西 边。 盎格鲁-撒克逊人称呼他们“威尔 士“，在盎格鲁-撒克逊语言中这 个词意为”外来者“。

paragraphs.en: Since the "Welsh" were strongest in the west, theAnglo-Saxons called that part of the country "Wales." If you look at a county-map of the United Kingdom, you can see that many of these place names still exist today. But now, let’s return to the continent. After defeating the rebel Constantine III, the Roman general Constantius turned his attention south to the Goths, and he attacked them in 416AD. The Goths were living in northern Spain by this point. By 416Ataulf had died and the Goths were being led by a new King named Wallia.Ataulf and Galla Placidia had had a son named
paragraphs.zh: 因为“威尔士“在西方最为强大， 盎格鲁-撒克逊人因此将这个国家 的西部称为”威尔士“。 如果你查阅今天英国的郡属地图 的话，会发现这其中的很多地名直 到今天依然在沿用。 不过现在，让我们回到欧洲大陆上 来。 在击败了君士坦丁三世的叛乱以 后，罗马将军君士坦提乌斯将他的 注意力转向了南方的哥特人，并在 公元 416 年攻打了他们。 哥特人生活在西班牙北部的这一 点上。 416 年，阿陶尔夫去世，哥特人如 今被一位名叫瓦利亚的新国王所 领导。阿陶尔夫与加拉·普拉西提 阿曾有过一个名叫狄奥多西的儿

paragraphs.en: Theodosius, but the baby had died the next year, and thenAtaulf died shortly afterwards. Now in 416, Constantine was making demands. 1) Return Galla Placidia to Italy. 2)Attack theAlani tribe in Spain. If they did that, the Romans would give the Goths permission to settle in theAquitaine region of Gaul. King Wallia agreed, and Galla Placidia was returned. Honorius quickly forced Placidia to marry Constantius III in order to ensure the loyalty of his general. The next year Galla gave birth to a daughter named Justa, and then the following year in 419, she gave
paragraphs.zh: 子，但那孩子第二年就不幸夭折， 阿陶尔夫在不久后也撒手人寰。 现在，在公元 416 年，君士坦丁提 出了要求。 1）让加拉·普拉西提阿回到意大 利。 2）攻打西班牙的阿兰聊部落。 如果他们做到了这些，罗马人就要 允许哥特人在高卢的阿基坦地区 定居。 国王瓦利亚同意了，加拉·普拉西 提阿被放了回去。 霍诺留很快强迫普拉西提阿嫁给 了君士坦提乌斯三世，以确保他的 将军的忠诚。 第二年，加拉生下了女儿胡斯塔， 又接下来的一年，也就是公元 419 年，她又生下了一个儿子，名叫瓦

paragraphs.en: birth to a son named Valentinian III. The western Roman Empire now had an heir. In 418 the Goths honored the terms of their alliance and invaded Hispania. They attacked theAlani tribe and defeated them. ThenAlani fled south and asked to join the Vandal tribe. The Vandal king agreed. Central Spain returned to Roman control. But the Vandals had become a much stronger tribe. Then in 421 Constantius III died. And Honorius died just two years later in 423AD. The imperial crown now passed on to Valentinian III, who was just 4 years-old.
paragraphs.zh: 伦提尼安三世。西罗马帝国现在有 了继承人。 418 年，哥特人以他们同盟的名义 起兵攻占了罗马行省西班牙。他们 攻击了阿兰聊部落并击败了他们。 阿兰聊人逃往南方，并要求加入汪 达尔人部落。 汪达尔国王同意了。西班牙中部回 到了罗马控制下，但现在汪达尔成 了强势得多的部落。 然后在 421 年，君士坦提乌斯三世 死了。 仅仅两年以后，也就是公元423年， 霍诺留也死了。帝国皇位现在传给 了瓦伦提尼安三世，当时他只有四 岁。

paragraphs.en: So as you can imagine, it was Valentinian's mother, Galla Placidia who really ruled the empire when Valentinian was a child. Actually this begins a 50 year period where the emperors were mostly puppers, and other people actually ruled the empire. On this slide I have made a timeline with the final 10 Roman emperors at the bottom, and the real rulers of the empire at the top. For a brief period the emperor Majorian did actually rule the empire and try to put the west back together. But he was betrayed by his friend Ricimer. Many historians consider Majorian (seen here in green) as the last "true" Roman Emperor.
paragraphs.zh: 所以你可以想象，在瓦伦提尼安长 大成人之前，是他的母亲、加拉·普 拉西提阿实际统治着帝国。 事实上，这开启了长达五十年的一 段漫长时期，其中罗马皇帝基本上 只是傀儡，统治帝国的另有其人。 在这张幻灯片上我做了一个时间 线，其中图片下半部是最后的十个 罗马皇帝，上面则是当时帝国的真 正统治者们。 有一段很短暂的时间里，皇帝马约 里安的确做过帝国的实质统治者， 并且试图重新征服西部。但他被他 的朋友里希默背叛了。很多历史学 家认为马约里安（图上我用绿色标 注）是最后一位“真正的”罗马皇 帝。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile, while all this was going on, the Vandal King Genseric saw an opportunity. He took his people across the straits of Gibraltar and invaded northAfrica in 429AD. He then fought the Romans for the next 10 years. And by 439, he had captured Carthage and all of Roman north Africa. From there the Vandals began raiding Roman shipping all across the Mediterranean. Then in 455, in the chaos that followed the death of Valentinian III, Genseric attacked Rome directly. For the second time in 50 years, Rome was sacked by a Germanic tribe. This second sack of Rome in
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，随着形势的变化，汪达 尔国王盖萨里克看到了机会。公元 429 年，他带领着他的人马渡过了 直布罗陀海峡，入侵了北非。 在接下来的十年里他都在与罗马 人作战。 到 439 年，他占领了迦太基，夺取 了罗马帝国在北非的所有领土。 从此汪达尔人开始抢劫罗马帝国 在地中海上的所有船只。 然后在 455 年，在瓦伦提尼安三世 的死亡所带来的混乱当中，盖萨里 克直接攻打了罗马。 五十年以来第二次，罗马被一个日 耳曼部落所劫夺。455 年这次对罗 马的第二次洗劫同时也标志着西

paragraphs.en: 455 is another common date for the end of the Western Roman Empire. The Vandals did so much damage to Rome, that they became famous for this over the next 1500 years. As a result, the English language has a word "vandalize" which means to deliberately destroy or damage property. But the Empire couldn't do much to stop the Vandals, because from 443 to 453 they were dealing with a much bigger problem: The Huns. For all of this story, the Huns have been lurking in the background, a scary menace that was pushing the Germanic tribes into the Empire, but not attacking Rome directly. But all that changed in 443 when a strong new King named Attila came
paragraphs.zh: 罗马帝国的另一个末日。 汪达尔人对罗马的破坏是如此严 重，以至于他们在未来的一千五百 年里都因此事而闻名。 结 果 之 一 是 英 语 中 出 现 了 “vandalize”一词，意思是彻底 摧毁和破坏财产。 但帝国没有为了阻止汪达尔人做 出太多努力，因为从 443 年到 453 年，他们有个更大的麻烦要处理， 这就是匈人。 在整个这个故事当中，匈人一直在 背景当中若隐若现，他们是将日耳 曼诸部落推往罗马帝国的恐怖威 胁，但没有直接侵袭过罗马。 但在 443 年，一切都改变了，因为 强有力的新国王阿提拉登上了王

paragraphs.en: to power. He organized the different Hunnic tribes into a powerful empire. In this way,Attila is very similar to Genghis Khan, who lived 800 years after him. The Huns, like the later Mongols, were people who lived on the Eurasian steppe.As a result they were excellent horsemen and archers. This made them very fast and powerful on the battlefield. In 443Attila launched his first invasion of the Roman empire. He attacked the eastern Empire first. The Theodosian walls were too strong to overcome, so Constantinople survived. ButAttila destroyed every Roman army that was sent against him, and captured every other Roman town. Then rich
paragraphs.zh: 位。他将不同的匈人部落整合成了 一个强盛的帝国。 在这方面，阿提拉与成吉思汗非常 相似，后者生活在其后八百年的时 代中。 与后来的蒙古人一样，匈人是生活 在亚欧大草原上的部族。因此他们 都是杰出的骑手和弓箭手，这让他 们在战场上既行动迅速，又战力强 盛。 443 年，阿提拉发动了他对罗马帝 国的第一次入侵。他首先攻击了帝 国的东部。由于狄奥多西城墙过于 坚固难以攻克，君士坦丁堡得以幸 存。但阿提拉击溃了被派去与他对 抗的所有罗马军队，并夺下了其他 所有罗马城池。匈人满载战利品回 到了北方。

paragraphs.en: with plunder, the Huns returned north. The Huns came again in 447, destroying every Roman army sent against them again and pillaging the center of the Empire. Then in 451,Attila turned his attention west and attacked the western Roman Empire. He began to burn his way across northern Gaul. But this time the Romans and the Goths made an alliance and a combined Roman-Gothic army foughtAtilla at the Battle of the Catalaunian Plains. This was a tremendous battle. The Huns were led byAttila, and the allies were led by the Roman general FlaviusAetius and the
paragraphs.zh: 447 年，匈人再次来袭，又一次击 溃了前往阻击他们的所有罗马军 队，并劫掠了帝国的中心。 而后在 451 年，阿提拉的注意力转 而向西，攻打了罗马帝国的西部。 他开始清理他去往高卢北部的道 路。 但这一次，罗马人和哥特人结成了 同盟，罗马-哥特联军在卡塔隆平 原战役与阿提拉展开了战斗。 这是一次惨烈的战斗。匈人由阿提 拉率领，联军的统帅则是罗马将军 弗拉维乌斯·艾提乌斯和哥特国王 狄奥多里克一世。

paragraphs.en: Gothic king Theodoric. The Battle occurred on June 24th, 451AD, and the Huns were defeated for the first time ever! Gaul was saved andAttila retreated. But the victory came at a heavy cost, the great Gothic king Theodoric was killed in the fighting. Attila was not done however. The Battle of the Catalaunian Plains had been a setback, not a crippling defeat, and so he invaded the empire again in 452, but this time he focused on Italy. Now that their land was not threatened, the Goths refused to help and the Romans could not stop Attila on their own. Attila pillaged northern Italy.
paragraphs.zh: 这场战役发生于公元 451 年的 6 月 24 日，匈人有史以来第一次被击败 了！ 高卢被拯救了，阿提拉撤了军，但 这场胜利伴随着沉重代价，伟大的 哥特国王狄奥多里克一世死于战 场。 然而，阿提拉的问题没有被解决。 卡塔隆平原战役是一次挫折，但不 是严重的失败，因此他在 452 年再 次入侵了帝国，不过这一次，他的 目标是意大利。 由于自己的领地已经不再受到威 胁，哥特人现在拒绝再提供帮助， 而罗马人无法依靠自己的力量阻 止阿提拉。 阿提拉劫掠了北意大利。最终他的

paragraphs.en: Eventually his army became weighed down with all that plunder, and his men started dying of Malaria. So he agreed to accept payment from Rome and then return home in late 452. Attila would probably have invaded Rome again and again for the rest of his life. But the next year the Romans got lucky. In 453Attila married a Gothic princess, and then got very drunk at his wedding. Then while he was passed out from all the alcohol he got a nose-bleed. ButAttila was too drunk to wake up, and he choked to death in his sleep. The next morning, his friends went into his room only to find his new
paragraphs.zh: 军队被沉重的战利品所压垮，而士 兵开始因为疟疾死去。他因此决定 接受罗马的赎金，然后在 452 年年 底撤军北归。 阿提拉本来可能会在他的余生中 一次又一次地重复入侵罗马，但第 二年，罗马人交了好运。 453 年，阿提拉迎娶了一位哥特公 主，并在婚礼上喝得酩酊大醉。然 后他因为过量酒精而失去了意识， 鼻腔血管破裂。 但阿提拉醉得太严重，没有醒过 来，就这样在睡梦中窒息而死。 第二天早上，他的朋友进入他的房 间时，只看到他的新娘在阿提拉的

paragraphs.en: bride sobbing over Attila's dead body. Attila's powerful personality and leadership skills had been the only thing holding the Hunnic empire together.After he died the different tribes all began to fight each other and the empire collapsed. But the damage had been done. Valentinian III died the next year in 455AD, and after him there were 9 emperors in 20 years as Germanic generals placed puppet emperor after puppet emperor on the throne. Eventually in 476AD, a Romano-Germanic King named Flavius Odoacer decided he would no longer bother with choosing a puppet emperor. He overthrew the last Western
paragraphs.zh: 尸体边痛哭。 阿提拉强有力的个人意志和领导 才能是将匈人帝国维系在一起的 唯一因素。在他死后，不同的部落 开始彼此争斗，帝国由是崩溃。 但伤害已经铸成。瓦伦提尼安三世 在第二年死去，也即公元 455 年， 在他死后的二十年里罗马先后有 过九位皇帝，因为日耳曼将军们在 将一个又一个的傀儡皇帝推上皇 位。 最终，在公元 476 年，一位名叫弗 拉维乌斯·奥多亚塞的罗马-日耳 曼国王决定他不会再费心去选择 傀儡皇帝。 他推翻了最后一位西罗马帝国皇

paragraphs.en: Roman emperor, a boy named RomulusAugustulus and declared that he would rule Italy directly as its King. This moment when Odoacer deposed the last Roman Emperor in 476AD, is viewed by historians as the official end of the Western Roman empire, but as we have seen, the empire had been collapsing for the last 100 years. In European history, this year of 476AD is often chosen as the end of the Classical Era and the beginning of the MiddleAges. The Western Empire was gone and in its place were a dozen Germanic Kingdoms. The Western Goths in Spain and southern Gaul. The Suebi in western
paragraphs.zh: 帝，一个名叫罗慕路斯·奥古斯都 路斯的男孩，并宣布他将直接作为 意大利国王统治意大利。 奥多亚塞在公元476年废黜最后一 位罗马皇帝的这一时刻被历史学 家视为西罗马帝国的正式灭亡，但 正如我们所见，帝国的崩溃持续了 一百年。 在欧洲历史上，公元 476 年通常被 认为是古典时代的终结，中世纪的 开始。 西罗马帝国消逝了，在它原本的疆 域内如今存在着数十个日耳曼王 国。 西哥特人在西班牙和南高卢地区， 苏维汇人在西班牙西部，法兰克人

paragraphs.en: Spain. The Franks in Northern Gaul, the Burgundians eastern Gaul, the Eastern Goths in Italy, the Vandals in NorthAfrica, and the Anglo-Saxons in Britain.And there were many other smaller kingdoms beside those. Europe would never again be unified into a single country. But look on the map, the Eastern Roman empire continued. With its capital at Constantinople, the Eastern Empire was still the strongest single power in Europe. And in fact, the Eastern Roman Empire would last another 1000 years before it fell to the Ottoman Turks in 1453AD. So join me next time as we take a look at that Eastern Empire, or as
paragraphs.zh: 在北高卢地区，勃艮第人在高卢东 部，东哥特人在意大利，汪达尔人 在北非，而盎格鲁-撒克逊人在不 列颠。除此之外，还有很多其他的 小王国散落其间。 欧洲再未被统一为一个单一国家。 但看看地图，东罗马帝国的历史仍 在继续。以君士坦丁堡为首都的东 罗马帝国，仍旧是欧洲最强大的那 个政权。 事实上，东罗马帝国还将持续存在 一千年之久，直到它公元 1453 年 陷落于奥斯曼土耳其人之手。 所以下一次我们将来看看东罗马 帝国，或者用历史学家在西罗马帝

paragraphs.en: historians call it after the fall of the west, the Byzantine Empire. Thank you very much! Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do I have one major recommendation for you. And that recommendation is The History of the Decline and Fall of the Roman Empire. This is the great classic work on Roman history. First published in 1776, this is the most famous history book on the fall of the Roman Empire. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 国崩溃后采用的名字来称呼它：拜 占庭帝国。 非常感谢！ 好了大家，我希望你们喜欢这个课 程并且想要了解更多。如果你们想 要的话，我有一个主要的推荐。 这就是《罗马帝国衰亡史》。这是 一部关于罗马历史的经典著作，首 次出版于 1776 年，它是关于罗马 帝国衰亡的最著名的历史著作。 非常感谢。

titleZh: 第五讲：拜占庭帝国|会建墙的不止有中国

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. This is the fourth series in our course, and it will focus on the middle ages. For today's lesson we are going to be learning about the Byzantine Empire. Today's lesson will focus on the years between 330AD, when Constantinople became the capital of the Roman Empire, and 565 when Justinian the Great died. So this is a period of about 230 years. This is what that looks like on our timeline. The story of of the Byzantine Empire really begins in 306AD,
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。这是我们课程的第四 季，将围绕中世纪展开。 今天的课程中我们将会了解的是 拜占庭帝国。 今天的课程主要围绕着从公元 330 年——当时君士坦丁堡成为了罗 马帝国首都——到公元 565 年的这 段时期，这一年查士丁尼大帝去 世。 所以这是一段约为 230 年的时期， 可以在我们的时间线上看到。 实际上拜占庭帝国的故事开始于 公元 306 年，当时君士坦丁一世登

paragraphs.en: when Constantine the First became the Roman Emperor. In so many ways Constantine marks a major turning point in Roman history. First, because he was the first Christian emperor. And second because he moved the capital city of the Roman Empire from Rome to Constantinople. Constantinople was a great place for a capital city because it sat on the crossroads of all the north-south trade between the Black Sea and Mediterranean, and on the east-west trade between Asia and Europe. And so Constantinople could tax all of the trade and become fabulously wealthy. If we zoom in a little closer, we can see that there are two straits that connect the Mediterranean Sea to
paragraphs.zh: 基成为罗马皇帝。在很多方面君士 坦丁都标志着罗马历史上的重要 转折点。首先，这是因为他是第一 位基督教皇帝。 其次，是因为他将罗马帝国的首都 从罗马迁到了君士坦丁堡。 作为首都，君士坦丁堡是一个绝佳 地点，因为它坐落于所有黑海与地 中海之间的南北商路，以及亚洲与 欧洲之间的东西商路的交叉口。因 此，君士坦丁堡能够对所有过路商 旅课税，并由此变得极其富有。 如果我们凑近一点，可以看到连接 地中海和黑海的有两个海峡，达达 尼尔海峡，和博斯普鲁斯海峡。

paragraphs.en: the Black Sea: the Dardanelles strait and the Bosphorus strait. Constantinople was built on the Bosphorus. Zooming in a little closer, here is what the Bosphorus looks like. On the left I have an aerial view, and on the right I have included a satellite view. At the southern part of the Bosphorus there is a peninsula that pokes out into the channel. It is called "The Golden Horn". Constantinople was built on that peninsula. Here is a map of the Golden Horn peninsula, but zoomed in a little closer. And here is a map of the city of
paragraphs.zh: 君士坦丁堡就建在博斯普鲁斯海 峡边。 放大一点，这是博斯普鲁斯海峡的 样子。 右边我放了一张航拍图，左边则是 卫星图片。 在博斯普鲁斯海峡的南端，是一个 延伸到海峡之中的半岛，它被称为 “金角”。 君士坦丁堡就建在这个半岛上。 这是一张金角半岛地图，不过放大 了一点。 这里是罗马时代君士坦丁堡城的

paragraphs.en: Constantinople as it appeared in the Roman era. This is an artist's depiction of the Peninsula during the Byzantine Era. Agreat benefit of putting a city on a peninsula is that the enemy armies can only attack it from one side. This makes the city much easier to defend. After Constantine there were 5 emperors who ruled a united Roman Empire from Constantinople. And then from 379 until 395, the Empire was ruled by Theodosius 1: the last Emperor to rule a united Eastern and Western Roman Empire. After Theodosius died in 395AD, he divided the empire between his
paragraphs.zh: 地图。 这是拜占庭时代一位艺术家绘制 的半岛的样子。 在半岛上建城的一大好处是敌军 只能从一个方向攻城。 这使得城市易于守卫。 在君士坦丁大帝以后，又有五位皇 帝在君士坦丁堡统治过尚且统一 的罗马帝国。 从 379 年到 395 年，统治帝国的是 狄奥多西：他是最后一位统治着统 一的东西罗马的帝国皇帝。 在狄奥多西于公元 395 年逝世以 后，他将帝国分成两部分，分别传

paragraphs.en: two sons: Honorius andArcadius. Honorius ruled the western part of the empire from Rome, while Aracadius ruled the richer eastern half from Constantinople. But then, as we learned in previous lessons, the Germanic invasions began. The Romans were frequently divided by civil-wars and betrayal. So the western Empire was unable to keep the barbarians out. Eventually the Western part of the Roman empire completely collapsed. In 476, the last Western Roman emperor was deposed and the Germanic tribes set up their own Kingdoms in former Roman territory.
paragraphs.zh: 给了他的两个儿子：霍诺留和阿卡 狄奥斯。 霍诺留以罗马为首都，统治着帝国 的西部，而阿卡狄奥斯则以君士坦 丁堡为首都，统治着帝国较富庶的 东部。 但正如我们在此前几课里已经了 解到的，正在那个时候，日耳曼蛮 族的入侵开始了。 罗马人因内战和背叛而不断分裂， 因此西罗马帝国未能抵抗蛮族。 最终西罗马帝国完全崩溃了。476 年，最后一任西罗马帝国皇帝被废 黜，日耳曼部落在前罗马帝国的领 土范围内建立了他们自己的王国。

paragraphs.en: But the Eastern Empire survived. The people living in the East still thought of themselves as "Romans" but they no longer controlled the city of Rome itself. So after 476, historians start calling the Eastern Roman Empire by a new name. They call it the Byzantine Empire. During those years of crisis the Eastern Empire was led by three emperors. Theodosius I, his son, Arcadius, and then his grandson, Theodosius II. One reason that the East survived was that Theodosius II, built up Constantinople's defensive walls. These walls became known as the "Theodosian Walls" and here is a photo of a reconstructed part of those walls to give you a sense of what they looked like.
paragraphs.zh: 但东罗马帝国幸存了下来。生活在 东罗马帝国的人依然认为自己是 “罗马人”，但他们已经不再拥有 罗马城本身的控制权。所以在 476 年以后，历史学家开始使用一个新 的名字来称呼东罗马帝国，这就是 拜占庭帝国。 在危机年代，东罗马帝国先后有三 位皇帝在位，分别是狄奥多西一 世，他的儿子阿卡狄奥斯，以及他 的孙子狄奥多西二世。 东罗马帝国得以幸存的原因之一， 是狄奥多西二世建造了君士坦丁 堡的防御城墙。 这些城墙被称为“狄奥多西城墙”， 这是一张这些城墙被重建以后的 部分的照片，为了给你们一些关于 城墙外观的认知。

paragraphs.en: Constantinople's walls were massive. First there was a moat that could be quickly filled with water during an enemy attack. Then came a low wall on the other side of the moat which could be used for fighting enemies trying to get across. If the enemies got past the moat and low wall, then there was the outer wall and towers. And if those were breached, there were the massive inner walls.An army trying to take Constantinople by force had to overcome four lines of defense before they could enter the city. These defenses were so good that Constantinople was unconquered for over 1000 years. Only the development of cannons in
paragraphs.zh: 君士坦丁堡的城墙规模宏大，首先 是一道护城河，可以在受到敌人攻 击的时候迅速灌满水。 在河的另一边是一道较矮的城墙， 可以被用来与试图渡河的敌人展 开战斗。如果敌人渡过了护城河和 矮墙，后面还有外墙和塔楼。 如果这些都被攻破，后面还有宏伟 的内墙。试图用武力攻打君士坦丁 堡的军队必须首先攻克四道防线， 才能进入城市。 这些防御设施是如此坚不可摧，以 至于君士坦丁堡在超过一千年的 时间里从未被攻克过。 直到 15 世纪，依赖火炮的发展才

paragraphs.en: the 1400s finally let an enemy army break through those mighty walls. This period of weakness and collapse continued after the reign of Theodosius II for another 77 years until a strong emperor finally came to power in 527AD. His name was Justinian the Great. In 527, when Justinian took the throne, he began to turn things around. With the help of his strong wife Theodora, Justinian set to work trying to rebuild the Roman Empire. First he fought a war against the Vandal tribe from 533 to 534. This war was a success and the Eastern Romans captured Sicily and Carthage. The next year, they began a much
paragraphs.zh: 让敌方军队得以攻破这些无所不 能的城墙 在狄奥多西二世之后，衰弱和崩溃 的时期又持续了 77 年之久，直到 公元 527 年，终于有一位强大的皇 帝接过了权柄。他的名字是查士丁 尼大帝。 527 年，查士丁尼接掌皇位，他开 始逐渐扭转形势。 在他强大的妻子狄奥多拉的帮助 下，查士丁尼着手试图重建罗马帝 国。 他首先在 533 到 534 年发动了与汪 达尔部落的战争。这次战争取得了 胜利，东罗马军队攻占了西西里与 迦太基。 第二年，他们又向哥特人发动了远

paragraphs.en: longer and more difficult war against the Goths. to re-take Italy. The war lasted for 19 years and was much harder, but in the end, Justinian was able to re-conquer the peninsula and capture the city of Rome itself, which was an important symbolic victory. Under Justinian, the Byzantine Empire nearly succeeded in putting the Roman Empire back together again. Here is the Byzantine Empire at the peak of its strength. Or to look at it another way. Here is the Byzantine Empire in 476AD when the West finally fell, and 6 years before Justinian was born. And here is the Byzantine Empire in 550, after Justinian's wars of re-conquest.
paragraphs.zh: 为漫长也远为艰难的战争，为了夺 回意大利。 这场战争持续了十九年，过程极为 艰苦，但最终查士丁尼得以重新征 服了亚平宁半岛，并攻占了罗马 城，这是一场重要的、具有象征性 意义的胜利。 在查士丁尼的领导下，拜占庭帝国 几乎成功地将罗马帝国重新统合 到了一起。这里是巅峰时期的拜占 庭帝国。 或者我们也可以从另一种角度来 看待它。这是公元 476 年西罗马帝 国最终灭亡时的拜占庭帝国，此时 距离查士丁尼出生还有 6 年。 这是 550 年的拜占庭帝国，在查士 丁尼的重征战争以后。

paragraphs.en: Now Constantinople, the capital of this second Roman Empire, was the biggest and richest city in Europe. Here is an artist's depiction of the city at its peak. Here we can zoom in on the Royal harbor which connected to the palace. And here is another image of that harbor. The Byzantine Empire was Christian, so the church forbade gladiator fights. BUT chariot races were still very popular, and in fact, they became the MOST popular entertainment in the city. The two most powerful teams: the greens and the blues had so many
