paragraphs.zh: 现在君士坦丁堡，第二个罗马帝国 的首都，是欧洲最大也最富饶的城 市了。 这是一位艺术家笔下描绘的这座 城市的全盛时期。 现在我们放大一点，来看连接皇宫 的皇家港口。 这是港口的另一张图。 拜占庭帝国信奉基督教，因此教会 禁止角斗士角斗。 但是战车比赛依然非常流行，实际 上，它们是城内最流行的娱乐活 动。 两支最强的队伍分别是绿军与蓝 军，它们拥有如此之多的支持者，

paragraphs.en: fans that they even became a powerful faction in Byzantine politics. And this is also the time when Christianity completed its conquest of the Mediterranean world. Here is the extent of Christianity in 310AD, when Constantine was in power. Here it is 80 years later in 390AD under Theodosius. And here is Christianity under Justinian, 170 years later in 560 AD. To show off his power and religious devotion, Justinian ordered the creation of the biggest church in the Christian World. Here it is the Hagia Sophia, which
paragraphs.zh: 乃至于它们甚至成了拜占庭政治 中的一个强大的派别。 这也是基督教完成它征服地中海 世界的征程的时代。 这是公元 310 年基督教的扩张，当 时正值君士坦丁在位。 这是 80 年后的形势，公元 390 年， 当时狄奥多西在位。 这是 170 年后的基督教传播图，公 元 560 年，查士丁尼在位时期。 为了显示其力量以及对宗教的虔 诚，查士丁尼下令建造了基督教世 界中最大的教堂。 这就是圣索菲亚大教堂，这个名字

paragraphs.en: means "holy wisdom". It was completed in 537AD, ten years into Justinian's reign. At that time, it was the world's largest building. (This definition excludes the pyramids since those are tombs not buildings.) And here is what the interior looks like. The interior of the church was decorated with beautiful mosaics like this one showing the emperors Justinian and Constantine offering gifts to Jesus and Mary. Constantine offers the city of Constantinople, and Justinian offers the Hagia Sophia. These Byzantine mosaics showed us a change in thinking that
paragraphs.zh: 意为“神圣的智慧”。 它完工于公元 537 年，也即查士丁 尼执政十年以后。 当时这是世界上最大的建筑（这一 定义没有将金字塔计算在内，因为 金字塔是陵墓而非建筑。） 这是内部的情形。 教堂的内部装饰着美丽的镶嵌画， 比如这一幅，描绘了查士丁尼和君 士坦丁两位皇帝向耶稣和玛丽亚 敬献礼物。 君士坦丁献上了君士坦丁堡城，而 查士丁尼献上的就是圣索菲亚大 教堂。 这些拜占庭镶嵌画向我们显示了 中世纪在思考方式上发生的变化。

paragraphs.en: occurred in the MiddleAges. In the Bronze and Classical eras, kings would claim to be living gods on earth, which was the source of authority. But in Christianity it is terrible sin to claim to be a god. So instead we see Emperors showing off their submission and worship of Jesus. This showed that the emperor was a good Christian, and also God's servant on Earth. This was the new way that king's claimed power, as God's representative on earth. Byzantine art also gives us the first depictions of Jesus Christ as a man with long hair, a full beard and delicate features.
paragraphs.zh: 在青铜时代和古典时代，国王会被 宣布为地上的，活着的神明，这成 为他们的权威的来源。 但在基督教内，自称神明是一项重 罪。所以我们看到，取而代之的是 皇帝们开始显示他们对于耶稣的 恭顺和崇拜。 这显示了皇帝是一名好的基督徒， 是上帝在地上的仆人。 这成了一种新的国王宣示权力的 方式，他们成了上帝在地上的代 表。 拜占庭艺术还第一次向我们描绘 了作为一个长头发、蓄满胡子而容 貌柔和的男人的耶稣基督形象。

paragraphs.en: The New Testament never describes Jesus' appearance. So this popular image of Jesus actually goes back to Byzantine artwork. This image here is from the 500s AD, and it is the oldest image of Jesus which depicts him in this way. If you look closely, you can see that the two sides of Jesus' face are drawn differently. This was probably done to show how the Christians thought that Jesus was both fully man and fully God. By drawing the two sides of this face differently, the artist is showing the two sides of Jesus's nature. Justinian also reorganized the Roman law codes to make the legal system more rational and fair. He
paragraphs.zh: 新约从未描述过耶稣的外貌，所以 耶稣的这一流行形象事实上正是 来自拜占庭艺术。 这里的这副画完成于公元 6 世纪， 这是以这种方式描绘的最为古老 的耶稣画像。 如果仔细看的话，你会看到画中耶 稣的两侧脸颊是不同的。 这样做可能是为了显示当时的基 督徒对于耶稣既作为一个完全的 人又作为一个完全的神的认知。 通过对于两边脸颊的不同画法，画 家显示了耶稣所具有的两面性。 查士丁尼还重新编撰了罗马法典， 以使法律系统更加理性化和公平 化。他下令创设的法律书和法典未

paragraphs.en: ordered the creation of a law-book and law-code which would be the basis of all future Byzantine laws. In fact, many legal systems in different modern European countries trace their laws back to this code. This painting shows Justinian and his wife Theodora receiving the finished law code. So by 540AD it must have seemed to Justinian that the Empire was about to begin a new golden age. New territory was being conquered, his great church was complete, his power was unchallenged. But then, the next year, it all began to fall apart... In 541AD a strange new disease
paragraphs.zh: 来将会成为所有拜占庭法律的基 础。 事实上，现代许多欧洲国家的法律 系统都可以将他们的法律追溯到 这部法典。 这幅画显示了查士丁尼和他的妻 子狄奥多拉接受最终完稿的法典 的情形。 所以到公元 540 年，对于查士丁尼 来说帝国显然即将开启一段新的 黄金时代。 新的领土已被征服，他的宏伟教堂 也已完工，他的权力已不容置疑。 但随后，在第二年，一切开始分崩 离析…… 公元 541 年，一种新的陌生疾病开

paragraphs.en: began to spread across Europe. It first appeared in Egypt and then travelled across the Mediterranean by following the trade routes. It was the Black Plague, a disease caused by the bacteria Yersina pestis and carried in rodents like rats. Then it spread from rat to rat and then to people through flea bites. Victims of the disease will swell up at their lymph nodes as you can see in the picture on the right. And in the late stages of the disease the extremities of the body will die and turn black with gangrene, as you can see on the left. In this first appearance of the Black Plague, the Mediterranean world was devastated. At its peak, the disease was killing
paragraphs.zh: 始流行于欧洲大陆。它首先出现于 埃及，然后随着商路被扩散到整个 地中海沿岸。 这就是黑死病，一种由鼠疫耶尔森 氏菌导致、由老鼠等啮齿类动物传 播的疾病。起初它在老鼠之间传 播，然后通过跳蚤叮咬又传染给了 人。 正如你在右边图片上看到的一样， 这种病的感染者将出现淋巴结肿 大。 在病程末期，他们的肢体末端都会 坏死，并因坏疽而发黑，如你在左 边图片上所看到的。 黑死病第一次出现之际，地中海世 界遭到了毁灭 在其爆发期，这种疾病在一天内夺

paragraphs.en: 5000 people a day in Constantinople, and it may have killed 1/4th of the population of the Byzantine Empire. The Byzantine Empire survived the plague, but this epidemic damaged their economy and weakened their ability to wage war. And the Byzantines had another major weakness that would affect them for their entire history. As you can see from this map, the Classical Roman empire only had 1 powerful neighbor, the Persians to their east. To their west was theAtlantic Ocean, to their south was the Sahara desert, and to their north were only disorganized barbarian tribes. But as you can see from this map,
paragraphs.zh: 走了君士坦丁堡 5000 人的生命， 因它而死的人数可能占到了拜占 庭帝国总人口的四分之一。 拜占庭帝国从瘟疫中幸存了下来， 但这种流行病重创了其经济，削弱 了他们发动战争的能力。 拜占庭还有另一个在他们的全部 历史中都影响了他们的关键弱点。 正如你从这张地图上看到的，古典 时代的罗马帝国只有一个强盛的 邻国，这就是东边的波斯人。 他们的西边是大西洋，南边是撒哈 拉沙漠，北边则只有一盘散沙的野 蛮人部落。 但正如你能从这张地图上看到的，

paragraphs.en: the Byzantines had a more difficult strategic position. They also had the Persian empire to their east, but unlike the classical Romans they had powerful neighbors to their west as well: the Germanic Kingdoms that had replaced the Western Empire. This meant that the Byzantines had to fight a two-front war for their entire history. But, by far, the Byzantine Empire's most powerful enemy was the Persians. At this point Persia was ruled by the Sasan dynasty, so we call this period of Persian history the Sasanian Empire.And they will be the focus of our next class. Okay everyone, I hoped you
paragraphs.zh: 拜占庭处在一个远为困难的战略 地位上。 他们的东边同样是波斯帝国，但与 古典时代的罗马人不同的是，他们 的西边同样是强大的邻居：取代了 西罗马帝国的日耳曼诸王国。 这意味着在他们的全部历史中，拜 占庭都必须双线作战。 但是，直到目前为止，拜占庭帝国 最强大的敌人仍是波斯人。 此时统治着波斯的是萨珊王朝，因 此我们称这段时期的波斯历史为 萨珊帝国，这将是我们下一次课的 重点。 好了，我希望你们喜欢这次课程并

paragraphs.en: enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do, I have some resources to share with you. First off, there is the primary source: The Secret History by the historian Procopius. There is the series Justin & Theodora by the Youtube channel Extra Credit. Finally, there is a great TedEd video about the walls of Constantinople by the historian Lars Brownworth. Okay everyone, that's all for now. See you next time and thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 且愿意了解更多。如果你们确实想 的话，我有一些资源可以与你们分 享。 首先是一份第一手资料：历史学家 普罗科匹厄斯所写的《秘史》。 这是一个关于查士丁尼和狄奥多 拉的系列剧，来自 Youtube 频道 最后，还有一个有关君士坦丁堡城 墙的出色的 TedEd 介绍视频，作者 是历史学家 Lars Brownworth。 好了，这就是今天的全部内容，下 次见，谢谢你们！

titleZh: 第六讲：萨珊王朝的波斯人|罗马的东方劲敌

paragraphs.en: Hello, and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to learn about the Sassanid Persians: Rome's Great Rival to the east. For this lesson, we will be focusing on the years between 224 and 651 AD. As you guys might remember from our last series, the first great Persian empire was theAchaemenid empire which lasted from 550 until 330 BC. TheAchaemenids were conquered byAlexander the Great, and his empire lasted from 330 until 312 BC. AfterAlexander's empire fell apart,
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列课程。今天我们将学习萨珊王 朝的波斯人：罗马在东方的强大对 手。 这节课，我们将集中学习公元 224 年到 651 年之间的历史。 你们可能还记得，上一辑我们讲到， 第一个伟大的波斯帝国是阿契美尼 德帝国，从公元前 550 年持续到公 元前 330 年。 阿契美尼德人被亚历山大大帝征服 后，他的帝国从公元前 330 年持续 到公元前 312 年。 亚历山大帝国瓦解后，波斯由他的

paragraphs.en: the Persian part was controlled by his general Seleucus. The Seleucids lasted from 312 until about 129 BC. Around 129 BC, the Greek Seleucids were overthrown by a native Persian rebellion. These new Persian rulers were called the Parthians. The Parthian empire lasted for about 350 years until it fell in the year 224AD. Here is a map of Parthian empire at its greatest extent, around 77AD. But by 224, the Parthian empire was in steep decline. Roman attacks from the West had greatly weakened it. In that year a Persian noble named Ardashir launched a rebellion. Over the next 2 years he won battle after battle and conquered the entire
paragraphs.zh: 西流基将军控制。西流基王朝从公 元前 312 年持续到公元前 129 年。 大约在公元前 129 年，希腊西流基 人在当地波斯人的叛乱中被推翻。 这些新的波斯统治者被称为帕提亚 人。帕提亚帝国持续了大约 350 年， 直到公元 224 年灭亡。 大约在公元 77 年，帕提亚帝国的版 图到达了最大值，这是当时的地图。 但到了 224 年，帕提亚帝国急剧衰 落。罗马人从西方的进攻大大削弱 了国家实力。 那一年，波斯贵族阿尔达希尔发动 了一场叛乱。 在接下来的两年里，他赢得了一场 又一场的战争，征服了整个帕提亚

paragraphs.en: Parthian kingdom. Then he pushed its borders outwards.Anew dynasty known to history as the Sassanid persians was born. Down in southern Iran there is a mountain called Naqsh-e Rustam. This site is famous for its rock carvings and the tombs of many Achaemenid kings. For example, the tomb of Darius and Xerxes can be found there. On the side of that mountain there is a carving that shows the Zoroastrian GodAhura Mazda handing the ring of power toArdashir, symbolically anointing him as the new King of Persia. Ardashir's son Shapur I was also a
paragraphs.zh: 王国。 然后他把边界向外拓展。历史上一 个新的王朝——萨珊王朝诞生了。 在伊朗南部有一座山叫纳克什-鲁 斯塔姆。 这个地方以岩石雕刻和许多阿契美 尼德国王的墓穴而闻名。 那里埋葬着大流士和薛西斯。 在那座山的一侧有一尊雕刻，琐罗 亚斯德（拜火教）神阿胡拉·马兹 达将权力之环交给阿尔达希尔，象 征性地膏他为波斯的新国王。  阿尔达希尔的儿子沙普尔一世也是

paragraphs.en: great leader and pushed the boundaries of the empire even further outwards, re-establishing the borders of the old Parthian empire. At this point Rome was in the middle of the Crisis of the Third Century. So the Romans were in no position to really stop the Sassanids. And Shapur reigned for 30 years, while there were 8 different Roman Emperors at that same time. One of these emperors, Valerian, did make an effort to stop Shapur. In 260AD, Valerian launched an invasion of the Sassanid empire, and met the Persians at the battle of Edessa. But this battle turned into a disaster for the Romans. The Roman army was destroyed and Valerian was
paragraphs.zh: 一位伟大的领袖，将帝国的疆界推 向了更远的地方，重建了古帕提亚 帝国的疆域。 此时，罗马正处于三世纪危机之中。 所以罗马人没有能力阻止萨珊人。 沙普尔统治了 30 年，同时期罗马经 历了 8 代皇帝。 其中一个皇帝，瓦勒良，曾努力地 阻挡沙普尔。 公元 260 年，瓦勒良入侵萨珊帝国， 并在埃德萨战役中与波斯人交战。 但对罗马人来说，这场战争变成了 一场灾难。罗马军队被摧毁，瓦勒 良被俘。

paragraphs.en: captured. At Naqsh-e Rustam Shapur commemorates that victory with this rock carving. It shows Valerian bowing to him after being taken prisoner. According to legend, Shapur then humiliated Valerian by using him as a footstool when getting on his horse. But after Shapur, the Sassanids experienced their own period of instability and had 7 emperors in 39 years. The next great Sassanid emperor was probably Shapur II who was born the same year that his father died, so he was technically emperor for his entire 70-year life, from 309 to 379AD.
paragraphs.zh: 在纳克什-鲁斯塔姆 ，沙普尔一块 石雕记录了这场战役的胜利。石雕 展示了瓦勒良在被俘后向他鞠躬。 传说中，沙普尔骑在瓦勒良的马上， 把他当作脚凳来羞辱他。 但在沙普尔之后，萨珊王朝经历了 一段不稳定的时期，在 39 年内出现 了 7 位君主。 下一个伟大的萨珊王朝君主是沙普 尔二世，他出生在他父亲去世的同 一年，所以严格来说，从公元 309-379 年，他在世的 70 年间，一 直是君主。

paragraphs.en: Shapur II was a strong emperor and he pushed the borders of the empire westward again. This brought him into conflict with the Romans, so in 362 the emperor Julian decided to attack the Persians. You might remember this from our first lesson on the decline of Rome. In March of 363AD, Julian launched an invasion of the Parthian empire. Things went well until June 26th, 363AD, when the Romans fought the Persians at the Battle of Samarra. The battle was not particularly important, except that Julian was injured in the fighting. He died a few days later, and the Romans were forced to retreat.
paragraphs.zh: 沙普尔二世是一个强大的君主，他 再次把帝国的边界向西推进。 这使他与罗马人发生了冲突，所以 在 362 年，尤里安决定进攻波斯人。 你可能还记得我们的第一课：“罗马 的衰落”。 公元 363 年 3 月，尤里安入侵帕提 亚帝国。 原本一切都进展顺利，直到公元 363 年 6 月 26 日，罗马人在萨马拉 战役中与波斯人作战。 这场战斗原本并不特别重要，但是 尤里安在战斗中受伤了。几天后他 死了，罗马人被迫撤退。

paragraphs.en: The Battle ofAdrianople happened just 15 years later, and the long Roman decline began. But Shapur was not just a military leader. The Holy Book of Zoroastrianism: the Avestas was compiled and organized under him. So those were the main accomplishments of Shapur II, the 10th Sassanid King, and one of the greatest. The next interesting king was the 14th, Yazdegerd I. Here is an image of Yazdegerd, it shows him hunting deer with a spear, and here is a map of the Persian empire during his rule. Yazdegerd was notable mainly for being a peace-loving king, which is a valuable and rare trait among
paragraphs.zh: 15 年后，阿德里安堡战役爆发，罗 马开始了漫长的衰落。 但沙普尔不仅仅是一位军事领袖。 索罗亚斯德教（拜火教）的圣书《波 斯古经》就是在他的领导下组织编 纂而成的。 这就是沙普尔二世的主要成就。他 是萨珊王朝的第十任统治者，也是 最伟大的统治者之一。 下一个有意思的国王是第 14 任君 主：伊嗣埃一世。 这是伊嗣埃一世的形象，画面中他 正在用长矛猎鹿；右边是他统治时 期波斯帝国的地图。 伊嗣埃一世以爱好和平著称，这在 古代统治者中非常少见。

paragraphs.en: ancient rulers. Interestingly, when the Roman emperorArcadius died, his will involved a gift of 500 kilograms of gold and the request that Yazdegerd symbolically "adopt" his son Theodosius. This request and gift deeply flattered Yazdegerd and he sent ambassadors to the Roman Senate declaring his full support for the 4 year old Theodosius as Roman Emperor. He threatened war if the boy was not chosen as the next emperor. This support worked, and there was a smooth transition of power from Arcadius to Theodosius II. Another good effect of this decision was that there was peace between
paragraphs.zh: 有趣的是，罗马皇帝阿卡狄奥斯死 后，他的遗嘱中包括了 500 公斤黄 金的礼物，并且要求伊嗣埃一世象 征性地“收养”他的儿子狄奥多西。 这个请求和礼物令伊嗣埃一世非常 高兴，他派大使到罗马元老院，宣 布全力支持 4 岁的狄奥多西担任罗 马皇帝。他威胁说，如果这个男孩 不被选为下一任皇帝，就要开战。 这种支持起了作用，权力顺利地从 阿卡狄奥斯过渡到了狄奥多西二世 手中。 这个决定的另一个好处是，在公元 408-420 年伊嗣埃一世统治的剩余

paragraphs.en: Rome and Persia for the rest of Yazdegerd's reign from 408 to 420 AD. After Yazdegerd I, his son Bahram V was also a strong king, but more warlike than his father. After Bahram, the Sassanids went into decline for the next 50 years until a strong king named Kavadh I restored their power in the late 400s and early 500s. Kavadh I was a great king and ruled for over 40 years, but his son Khosrow I was even greater and ruled from 531 to 579AD. Under Khosrow the empire was large and her borders were secure. Khosrow reformed the tax system to make tax collection more efficient and he also reformed the army to
paragraphs.zh: 时间里，罗马和波斯之间保持了和 平。 伊嗣埃一世之后，他的儿子巴赫拉 姆五世也是位优秀的国王，但比他 父亲更好战。 巴赫拉姆五世之后，萨珊王朝在接 下来的 50 年里逐渐衰落，直到 4 世纪末、5 世纪初，卡瓦德一世恢 复了国家实力。 卡瓦德一世是位伟大的国王，在位 40 多年，但他的儿子霍斯罗夫一世 更了不起，于公元 531-579 年间在 位。 在霍斯罗夫一世的统治下，帝国疆 域辽阔，边境也很安全。 霍斯罗夫一世改革了税收制度，提 高了征税效率。他还改革了军队， 使之在战斗中更加有效。

paragraphs.en: make it more effective in battle. Khosrow also founded theAcademy of Gundeshapur a great center of learning and culture. And he ruled, like most Persian emperors in those days, from the city of Ctesiphon. Here is a picture of Taq Kasra the Sassanid palace at Ctesiphon. This shows us what it might have looked like around 500AD. Today Ctesiphon is a ruin, it was replaced by the city of Baghdad which was founded a few miles north on the Tigris river in 762AD. But part of the original Sassanid palace still remains. Here is what it looks like today. The site is called Taq Kasra today.
paragraphs.zh: 霍斯罗夫一世还建立了冈德沙普尔 学院，这是一个伟大的学术文化中 心。 和当时大多数波斯皇帝一样，他也 统治着泰西封。 这是泰西封中萨珊王朝的 Taq Kasra 宫殿的图片，向我们展示了 它在公元 500 年左右的样子。 今天的泰西封已经是一片废墟，取 而代之的是巴格达，这座城市于公 元 762 年建立于底格里斯河以北几 英里处。 但是萨珊宫殿的一部分仍然保留 着。这是它今天的样子。 这个遗址如今被称为 Taq Kasra。

paragraphs.en: In fact the arch at Taq Kasra is the largest unreinforced brick arch in the world, and it has been standing for 1500 years. I think Khosrow is an especially interesting emperor, because he was the ruler at the same time that Justinian was the ruler of the Byzantine empire. So the greatest Sassanid emperor and the greatest Byzantine emperor overlapped with each other. Of course they fought several wars. First came the Iberian war from 526-531, which was happening just as both men were taking power. After making peace Justinian launched several wars in the west to reconquer the original Roman empire. He was able to recapture
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，Taq Kasra 的拱顶是世界 上最大的无钢筋砖拱顶，它已经在 这里矗立了 1500 年。 在我看来，霍斯罗夫是一位非常有 意思的君主，因为他在位期间，查 士丁尼是拜占庭帝国的统治者。 也就是说，最伟大的萨珊君主和最 伟大的拜占庭君主同时在位。 当然，他们之间爆发了好几场战争。 第一次是 526-531 年的伊比利亚战 争，当时两人都刚刚获取政权。 讲和后，查士丁尼在西方发动了几 场战争，以重新征服原来的罗马帝 国。他重新夺回了意大利、北非和 西班牙南部。

paragraphs.en: Italy, NorthAfrica and southern Spain. But then the next big Roman-Sassanid war began. This one lasted from 541 to 562AD. For the next 70 years the Byzantines and the Sassanids were often at war with each other. This period saw the rule of 3 Persian emperors: Khosrow I, Hormzid IV and Khosrow II. It also saw the rule of 6 Byzantine emperors: Justinian, Justin II, Tiberius II, Maurice, Phocas, and Heraclitus. Justinian and Heraclitus were good leaders, but the other Byzantine emperors were mediocre. First came the Roman-Persian war
paragraphs.zh: 但接着，另一场大规模的罗马-萨珊 战争开始了。它从公元 541 年持续 到 562 年。 在接下来的 70 年里，拜占庭和萨珊 之间经常发生战争。 这段时间，波斯经历了三任君主： 霍斯罗夫一世，霍尔木兹四世和霍 斯罗夫二世。 而拜占庭则经历了 6 任：查士丁尼、 查士丁二世、提比略二世、莫里斯、 福卡斯和赫拉克利特。 查士丁尼和赫拉克利特是优秀的领 导者，但其他拜占庭皇帝都很平庸。 首先是公元 572- 591 年的罗马-波

paragraphs.en: of 572- 591. Then came 10 years of peace under the last period of Emperor Maurice's rule.As you can see from the map, the Byzantines were over-extended and had begun losing control of the new territory conquered by Justinian. But even though both empires really needed a longer period of peace, the fighting began again in 602AD. This would be the last great Roman-Persian war, and it would go on for 26 years. The Romans would win eventually, but not before both empires had been deeply weakened by a generation of warfare. This turned out to be most unwise, because while the last
paragraphs.zh: 斯战争。 在莫里斯统治的最后一段时期，帝 国实现了 10 年的和平。从地图上可 以看出，拜占庭帝国扩张过度，渐 渐开始不能掌控查士丁尼征服的新 领土。 尽管两个帝国都需要更长的和平时 期，战争依然在公元 602 年爆发了。 这将是最后一场重要的罗马-波斯 战争，这场战争将持续 26 年。 罗马人最终会取得胜利，但在此之 前，持续了一代人的战乱已经大大 削弱了双方国力。 事实证明这是极不明智的，因为当 最后一场波斯-罗马战争正在进行

paragraphs.en: Persian-Roman war was being waged, a new power was beginning to grow in strength, down to the south and across theArabian desert. Apeople who have been marginal for most of human history, would suddenly become one of the strongest armies in the world, and would complete one of greatest conquests that history has ever seen. And these people, theArabs, would be motivated by a new religion that was about to change the world forever. So please join me next time for that story! Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do I have some recommendations for you.
paragraphs.zh: 的时候，一个新的力量开始壮大， 并一直延伸到了南部和整个阿拉伯 沙漠地区。 一个在人类历史上大部分时间处于 边缘地位的民族，将突然成为世界 上最强大的军事力量之一，并完成 历史上最伟大的征服之一。 这个民族：阿拉伯人，会被一种即 将永远改变世界的新宗教所激励。 预知后事如何，且听下回分解！ 好了，各位，希望你们喜欢这节课。 如果你们有兴趣了解更多，我有一 些很好的资源可以推荐给你们。

paragraphs.en: First there is the podcast In Our Time by the BBC. It is a great podcast where the host, Marvin Bragg will lead a discussion on a new interesting topic every week. They have a good one on the Sassanid Persians. Also, if you ever make a trip to Iran, you must go see the carvings and tombs at Naqsh-e-Rustam. And at Kazerun, you should go see the colossal statue of Shapur I. And if Iraq ever gets safe enough in my lifetime for tourists to visit, I think the ruins of Ctesiphon and the arch at Taq Kasra would be worth seeing. Thank you all very much!
paragraphs.zh: 首先是 BBC 的播客节目 In Our Time. 这是一档很棒的播客节目， 主持人马文·布拉格每周都会就一 个有趣的新话题进行讨论。他们对 萨珊王朝波斯人的见解很值得一 听。 此外，如果你有机会去伊朗，一定 要去看纳克什-鲁斯塔姆的雕刻和 坟墓。 在卡泽伦，你应该去看沙普尔一世 的巨型雕像。 如果在我有生之年，伊拉克能安全 到可以让游客参观的程度，那么我 认为泰西封遗址和 Taq Kasra 拱门 也非常值得一看。 谢谢大家！

paragraphs.en: Lecture Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on the MiddleAges, today we will learn about the beginning of the religion of Islam. Our lesson will focus on the life of the prophet Muhammad, who lived from 570 to 632AD. Our story begins in theArabian city of Mecca which sat on an important trade route between Syria and the Indian Ocean. At that point in history, theArabs had no political unity and were divided into many different tribes. Here is a map of some of the many different tribes inArabia in 570AD. One of these tribes were called the Quraysh and they controlled the city
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们关于中世纪 的世界历史系列课程，今天我们将 学习伊斯兰教的起源。 这节课，我们将聚焦先知穆罕默德 的一生。他生活在公元570年到632 年。 我们的故事从阿拉伯城市麦加开 始，这座城市位于叙利亚和印度洋 之间重要的贸易路线上。 当时，阿拉伯人在政治上没有达成 统一，分裂成了许多不同的部落。 这是公元 570 年阿拉伯半岛诸多不 同部落的地图。 其中一个名为“古莱什族”的部落 控制了麦加城，和一个重要的圣地

paragraphs.en: of Mecca as well as an important shrine in the city called the "Kaaba". In 570, a baby boy named Mohammed was born in Mecca. His mother's name wasAminah and his father's name was AbdAllah. Sadly, both of Muhammad's parents died before he was seven years old, and he was orphaned. So he was raised by his grandfather Abd-al Muttalib and then his uncleAbu Talib. Before I go any further, I'd like to make a note that for many Muslims, drawing pictures of Mohammed or his close companions is strongly forbidden. Out of respect for this belief, I will use anArabic logo of each person's name to represent them instead of a
paragraphs.zh: “克尔白圣殿”。 公元 570 年的麦加，一个名叫穆罕 默德的男婴诞生了。他母亲名叫阿 米娜，父亲名叫亚伯拉罕。 可悲的是，穆罕默德的父母在他七 岁之前就去世了，他成了孤儿。所 以他由祖父阿卜杜勒-阿尔穆塔利 布(Abd-al Muttalib)和叔父阿布 塔利布(Abu Talib)抚养长大。 在我进一步阐述之前，我想指出， 伊斯兰教严令禁止画穆罕默德或其 亲密伙伴的画像。 出于对这一信仰的尊重，我将用每 个人的阿拉伯语名来代表他们，而 不是用图片。

paragraphs.en: picture. Muhammad grew up and married a wealthy widow named Khadija when he was 25 and she was 40. Muhammad had developed a good reputation for trustworthiness and honesty and this attracted Khadija. They had 6 children. Two sons who died as children, 3 adopted daughters, and a daughter who lived to adulthood named Fatima. The family also took care of a younger cousin of Mohammed's named Ali. Ali and Muhammad were cousins, but Muhammad was 31 years older, soAli was like a son to Muhammad. Muhammad also developed a strong interest in spiritual matters, so he often went alone to a nearby cave
paragraphs.zh: 穆罕默德长大后娶了一个有钱的寡 妇，名叫哈蒂嘉，当时穆罕默德 25 岁，哈蒂嘉 40 岁。 穆罕默德在可靠和诚实方面建立了 良好的声誉，这吸引了哈蒂嘉。 他们一共养育了 6 个孩子。两个儿 子在孩童时期就死了，三个养女， 还有一个女儿活到了成年，名叫法 蒂玛。 这家人还照顾穆罕默德的表弟阿 里。 阿里和穆罕默德是表兄弟，但穆罕 默德比阿里大 31 岁，所以阿里就像 穆罕默德的儿子一样。 穆罕默德还对精神方面的事物产生 了浓厚的兴趣，所以他经常独自前 往附近的 Jabal-al Nour 山，在洞

paragraphs.en: on a mountain called Jabal-al Nour, where he would sit alone and pray. But then, according to Muslim tradition, onAugust 10th, 610AD, an angel appeared to Muhammad with a message from God. Here is an English translation of that first message from God. After the vision was over Mohammed was terrified and ran down the mountain and back to his house. When Muhammad reached his house he was afraid that he might be going crazy, but his beloved wife Khadija encouraged him by telling him that God would not punish a good man like him. For the next few years, Mohammed kept his visions and revelations
paragraphs.zh: 穴中坐着祈祷。 根据伊斯兰教的传说，公元 610 年 8 月 10 日，天使带着上帝的启示出 现在穆罕默德面前。 这是第一条上帝启示的英文翻译。 启示结束后，穆罕默德吓坏了，他 跑下山，回到家里。 穆罕默德回家后，他害怕自己会发 疯。但深爱他的妻子哈蒂嘉鼓励他， 告诉他上帝不会惩罚像他这样的好 人。 在接下来的几年里，默罕默德没有 将他遇到神迹和启示的事外传，只

paragraphs.en: quiet, and only the people in his family knew about them. The core message of Mohammed's revelations was submission to God, so this new religion was called "Islam" which means "submission" inArabic. The people who practiced this new religion were known as "Muslims" which means "submitter" or "one who surrenders". Mohammed was the first Muslim, his wife Khadija was the second, and his younger cousinAli was the third. Around 613AD, Mohammed began going public with his message and sharing these revelations with his followers. His first follower outside of his
paragraphs.zh: 告诉了他的家人。 穆罕默德启示的核心是臣服于上 帝，所以这个新的宗教被称为“伊 斯兰教”，在阿拉伯语中意思是“臣 服”。 践行这种新宗教的人被称为“穆斯 林”，意思是“服从者”或“投降 者”。 穆罕默德是第一个穆斯林，他的妻 子哈蒂嘉是第二个，他的表弟阿里 是第三个。 公元 613 年左右，穆罕默德决定公 开并与他的追随者分享他所获得的 启示。 他家族之外的第一个追随者是他的

paragraphs.en: family was a friend of his named Abu Bakr. Many people in Mecca were attracted by the poetry of the revelations and the content of the messages. But many other people in Mecca were also made angry by this new religion. Since the people of Mecca were polytheists who worshipped many gods, the belief in Islam that there is only one God went against their religion. People were worried that this new religion would keep people from making pilgrimages to the Kaaba in Mecca. And many people did not like how the Muslims were forming a new
paragraphs.zh: 朋友阿布·巴克尔。 麦加的许多居民被《启示录》所展 现的诗意和启示的内容所吸引。 但是还有很多人对这个新宗教感到 愤怒。 因为麦加人是多神论者，所以伊斯 兰教中只有一个神的信仰违背了他 们的宗教。 人们担心这种新宗教会让人们不再 去麦加的克尔白朝圣。 而且，许多人对穆斯林在家庭或部 落之外以新的方式组织社区的行为

paragraphs.en: community, outside of the control of family or tribe. So the Muslims began to make many enemies in Mecca. Then in 619, nine years after his first revelation, came Mohammed's "Year of Sorrow." His beloved wife Khadija died, followed by his uncle and adoptive fatherAbu Talib. At the same time, tension between the Muslims and Pagans in Mecca continued to grow. By 622,Anger between the Muslims and the Pagans had become so strong, that the pagans, led byAbu Sufyan,Amr ibn Hisham (Abu Jahl) and Umayyah ibn Khalaf decided to murder Mohammed.
paragraphs.zh: 感到反感。 于是，穆斯林逐渐在麦加树敌。 到了 619 年，公开启示的第九年， 穆罕默德迎来了他的“悲伤之年”。 他深爱的妻子哈蒂嘉去世了，紧随 其后的是他的叔父，也是养父：阿 布塔利布。 与此同时，麦加的穆斯林和异教徒 之间的紧张关系继续加剧。 到 622 年，穆斯林和异教徒之间的 矛盾变得如此强烈，以至于阿 布·苏富扬、阿姆尔·伊本·希沙 姆和乌马耶·伊本·哈拉夫领导的 异教徒决定谋杀穆罕默德。

paragraphs.en: So a group of assassins gathered to kill Muhammad, and they arrived at his house on the morning of June 17th, 622AD. But by the time the killers had arrived, Muhammad was gone. He andAbu Bakr, his closest friend and one of the earliest converts to Islam, had fled north to the city of Medina where a second Muslim community was developing. In Medina, Islam had allowed the Arab tribes of the BanuAws and Banu Khazraj to live in peace with each other. Now to allow everyone to live together peacefully and to defend themselves against attacks from Mecca, Mohammed created the "Constitution of Medina."
paragraphs.zh: 于是一群刺客聚集在一起，计划刺 杀穆罕默德。他们在公元 622 年 6 月 17 日早晨到达了他的住所。 但当杀手到达时，穆罕默德已经离 开了。 他和他最亲密的朋友、最早皈依伊 斯兰教的阿布·巴克尔逃到了北部 的麦地那市，那里正在发展第二个 穆斯林社区。 在麦地那，伊斯兰教维护了阿拉伯 部落巴努阿瓦族和巴努哈扎尔族之 间的和平。 现在为了让所有人都能和平相处， 保护自己免受麦加袭击，穆罕默德 创立了“麦地那宪法”。

paragraphs.en: Two years later, in 624, Muhammad told his followers that it was time to fight back. The plan was to launch a raid on the Meccan trade caravan being led by Abu Bakr. They set up an ambush near the wells of Badr. The caravan heard about the attack and avoided the ambush, but a Meccan army came out to fight the Muslims at the Battle of Badr wells on March 13th, 624AD. The Muslims won this battle and two of their main enemies-Amr ibn Hisham (Abu Jahl) and Umayyah ibn Khalaf - were both killed that day in the battle. The war between the Muslims in Medina and the pagans in Mecca
paragraphs.zh: 两年后的 624 年，穆罕默德告诉他 的追随者：是时候反击了。 他们的计划是突袭由阿布·巴克尔 领导的麦加贸易商队。 他们在巴德尔井附近设下埋伏。 商队听说了这次袭击，避开了埋伏。 但在公元 624 年 3 月 13 日，一支麦 加军队和穆斯林爆发了巴德尔井战 争。 穆斯林赢得了这场战斗，他们的两 个主要敌人——阿姆尔·伊本·希 沙姆和乌马耶·伊本·哈拉夫—— 都在当天的战斗中丧生。 麦地那穆斯林和麦加异教徒之间的 战争又持续了 3 年，但随着时间的

paragraphs.en: continued for 3 more years, but as time went on more and more of the pagans began converting to Islam. Abu Sufyan realized that he would lose this war and needed to make peace. Abu Sufyan travelled to Medina under the protection of one of Mohammed's uncles:Al Abbas. There in MedinaAbu Sufyan was required to convert to Islam and was given the terms of surrender: 1)All of the Qurayish tribe would convert to Islam. 2) The idols and statues in the Kaaba would be smashed and the building would be dedicated to Allah alone. Abu Sufyan accepted this and The
paragraphs.zh: 推移，越来越多的异教徒开始皈依 伊斯兰教。 阿布·苏富扬意识到他迟早会输掉 这场战争，并企图讲和。 阿布·苏法扬在穆罕默德的叔叔阿 尔·阿巴斯的保护下前往麦地那。 在麦地那，他被要求皈依伊斯兰教， 并收到了投降条件。 1） 古拉伊什部落的所有人都必须 皈依伊斯兰教。 2）摧毁克尔白圣殿中所有的神像和 雕像，将这座建筑献给真主阿拉。 阿布·苏富扬接受了这些条款，于

paragraphs.en: Muslims went south to Mecca. The Muslims peacefully entered Mecca; nobody was killed. The idols in the Kabba were smashed and everyone assembled before Mohammed and converted to Islam as a group. Then Mohammed gave his cousin Abu Sufyan large gifts, put him in charge of Mecca and went home to Medina. Now the Muslims were strong! They had thousands of followers and controlled both Mecca and Medina. Over the next two years, many moreArabs converted to the new religion and their strength grew. By 632, they had conquered about
paragraphs.zh: 是穆斯林南下来到了麦加。 穆斯林和平地进入了麦加，没有人 被杀害。 克尔白的神像被砸碎，所有人聚集 在穆罕默德面前，共同皈依伊斯兰 教。 然后穆罕默德给了他的叔叔阿布苏 法扬很多礼物，让他掌管麦加，然 后自己回到了麦地那。 现在，穆斯林已经变得非常强大! 他们有成千上万的追随者，控制着 麦加和麦地那。在接下来的两年里， 更多的阿拉伯人皈依了这种新的宗 教，伊斯兰教的力量也随之增长。 到 632 年，他们已经征服了半个阿

paragraphs.en: half ofArabia, and were destroying the old pagan religion and idols all across the country. During the 22 years from 610 to 632 Mohammed continued to have more revelations from God. His followers treasured these revelations as the direct word of God, and the early Muslims memorized them. Over time these revelations were written down into a book, which Muslims call the Quran. The Quran is divided into 114 chapters of different length. Muslims call each of these chapters a "Surah". For Muslims, the Quran is the direct word of God.
paragraphs.zh: 拉伯半岛，并摧毁了全国各地的古 老异教及其圣像。 在 610-632 的 22 年间，穆罕默德继 续从上帝那里得到更多启示。 他的追随者将这些启示视为上帝真 言，早期的穆斯林则将其铭记在心。 随着时间的推移，这些启示被写进 了一本书，穆斯林称之为《古兰经》。 《古兰经》分为 114 章，每一章长 度 不 同 。 穆 斯 林 称 这 些 章 节 对穆斯林来说，《古兰经》就是上帝 真言。

paragraphs.en: The first chapter of the Quran is also the 7th revelation that Muhammed ever received. It is called "The Opening" or "Al-Fatihah" inArabic, because it summarizes the relationship of humans to God in the Muslim tradition. This revelation is also recited every time Muslims pray. It goes like this: In the name ofAllah, the most Gracious the most Merciful. All praise and thanks toAllah, the Lord of the Universe. The Most Merciful, the Most Gracious, Master of the Day of Judgment. You alone we worship, and you alone we ask for help.
paragraphs.zh: 《古兰经》的第一章是穆罕默德得 到的第七次启示。 第一章被称为“开端”，它总结了穆 斯林传统中人类与上帝的关系。穆 斯林每次祈祷时，都会背诵这一启 示。 内容如下： 奉至仁至慈的真主之名 一切赞颂，全归真主，全世界的主 至仁至慈的主 报应日的主 我们只崇拜你，只求你佑助

paragraphs.en: Guide us to the straight path. The path of those on whom you have bestowed your favors. Not the path of those who have gone astray and earned your wrath. Muslims feel a deep reverence for this prayer and revelation. On this slide I have included a picture of Muslims at prayer on the left and a picture of Al-Fatihah in the originalArabic on the right. Muslims also developed a set of five practices, which became the core of the religion. These are known as the "Five Pillars of Islam." The first pillar is called "Shahada" . This is a declaration of faith that goes like this: "There is no God but God, and
paragraphs.zh: 求你引导我们上正路 你所佑助者的路 不是受谴怒者的路，也不是迷误者 的路 穆斯林对这种祷告和启示深感崇 敬。 在这张幻灯片上，我在左边放了一 张穆斯林祈祷的照片，在右边放了 一张阿拉伯文《开端》的照片。 穆斯林发展了一套每个穆斯林应履 行的五项基本功课，成为宗教的核 心。这些被称为“五功”。 第一项功课叫做“念功”。这是一种 信仰宣言，内容如下: “万物非主，惟有真主！穆罕默德

paragraphs.en: Mohammed is his prophet." Here is what that looks like in Arabic: And here is the transliteration of that in English. If you say these words and mean them, then you are a Muslim. The second pillar is called "Salah" which means "Prayer" AMuslim must pray five times a day. Each prayer has a different name according to the time of day when you pray it. They are: 1) Fajr (Dawn) 2) Dhuhr (Noon) 3)Asr (afternoon) 4) Maghrib (evening) 5) Isha (night) The third pillar is called Zakat.
paragraphs.zh: 是真主的使者！” 阿拉伯语如下： 英文直译如下： 如果你说出了这些话，并且是认真 的，那么你就是一个穆斯林。 第二项功课叫做“拜功”，意思是礼 拜。 穆斯林每天必须祷告五次。每次祷 告都有不同的名字，根据你祷告的 时间而定。它们是: 1）晨礼（拂晓） 2）晌礼（正午） 3）晡礼（下午） 4）昏礼（傍晚） 5）宵礼（夜晚） 第三项功课叫做“课功”。穆斯林必

paragraphs.en: Muslims must give a portion of their income back to their community in the form of charity. The fourth pillar is called Sawm. This means fasting. For one month a year, during the Holy Month of Ramadan, Muslims are not allowed to eat or drink while the sun is in the sky. Fasting is required of all adults, but it is forbidden to children and people who have a medical condition which would make it dangerous. Its also important to know that the month of Ramadan, like the entire Muslim Calendar, is based on the lunar calendar. Since the lunar calendar and solar calendar don't match up, the Islamic
paragraphs.zh: 须把他们收入的一部分以慈善的形 式回馈给社会。 第四项功课叫做“斋功”，意思是禁 食。 每年斋月，穆斯林都不能在太阳升 起时吃喝。 所有成年人都需要禁食，但儿童和 身体状况不佳的人不能禁食。 斋月和整个穆斯林历法一样，都是 以阴历为基础的。 由于阴历和阳历并不完全匹配，伊 斯兰教历法会随着季节前后移动。

paragraphs.en: months move through the seasons. Here for instance are the start dates of Ramadan from 1938 until 2038. The fifth and final pillar is called the Hajj. This occurs during the Islamic month of Dhu al Hijjah. If a Muslim can afford it, once in his or her life, he or she must make a pilgrimage to Mecca. This tradition, and the direction of prayer connects all Muslims in the world to the city of Mecca. Together these five practices are the core of being a good Muslim and of the religion of Islam. Mohammed himself made his own true Hajj in 632, and then on March 6th, 632AD, he gave his followers a farewell sermon on MountArafat.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，这里是 1938 年到 2038 年斋 月的开始日期。 第五项，也是最后一项功课叫做“朝 功”。发生在伊斯兰历的 12 月。 穆斯林的一生中，如条件允许，应 到麦加的克尔白朝拜一次。 这一传统和祈祷的方向将世界上所 有的穆斯林与麦加联系在一起。 以上五功构成了伊斯兰教和成为一 个优秀穆斯林和核心要素。 穆罕默德在 632 年去了麦加朝圣， 当年 3 月 6 日，他在阿拉法特山上 给他的追随者们做了一次告别布 道。随后他回到了麦地那。

paragraphs.en: Then he returned to Medina. Afew months later, in summer of 632AD, Mohammed became sick with fever, head pain, and weakness, and then on June 8th, 632AD he died. His final words were: "Oh God, highest friend in heaven." He was buried that same day in Medina, in the mosque he had established in that city. His mosque and tomb are still there today. So what would happen now? The Prophet Mohammed had died, but the Muslim community continued. This religion had united theArabs for the first time in history, and given them a strong sense of purpose.
paragraphs.zh: 几个月后，公元 632 年夏天，穆罕 默德开始发烧、头痛，逐渐虚弱， 于公元 632 年 6 月 8 日去世。 他的临终遗言是：“真主啊，至高的 朋友。” 当天，他被埋葬在麦地那他亲自建 立的清真寺里。 他的清真寺和坟墓至今仍在那里。 那么接下来会发生什么呢?先知穆 罕默德已经去世，但穆斯林社区仍 在继续繁衍。这种宗教在历史上第 一次团结了阿拉伯人，并赋予他们 强烈的使命感。

paragraphs.en: But who would lead the Muslims now that the prophet was gone?And what would the Muslim community do with their new unity and strength? That is our story for next time. Thank you very much! I hope you enjoyed that lesson, and want to learn more, if you do I have a recommendation for you. My recommendation is the documentary The Life of Muhammad by the BBC. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 但是现在先知已经不在了，谁来领 导穆斯林呢?穆斯林社区将如何利 用他们的团结和力量？ 那是我们下节课要讲的内容了。谢 谢大家！ 希望你们喜欢这节课，希望你们有 兴趣了解更多。我有一些很好的资 源可以推荐给你们。 我的推荐是 BBC 的纪录片《穆罕默 德的一生》。非常感谢。

titleZh: 第八讲：逊尼派和什叶派|中东乱局的起源

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on the MiddleAges, today we will learn about what happened in the generation immediately following Muhammad's death. We will focus on the 48 years between 632 and 680AD. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. This time period corresponds to the first generation of Muslims after the death of the Prophet Muhammad. When the Prophet Muhammad died in June of 632AD, the question became, who would succeed him as leader of the Muslim community. There were two men who seemed most suitable for the job. The first
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史中世纪 系列课程。今天我们将学习穆罕默 德死后一代人的故事。 我们将关注公元 632-680 年之间的 48 年。在我们的时间轴上是这样 的。 这个时期相当于先知穆罕默德死后 的第一代穆斯林。 公元 632 年 6 月先知穆罕默德去世 后，问题来了：谁将接替他成为众 多穆斯林的领袖？ 有两个人最适合做这项工作。第一 个是阿布·巴克尔，他是穆罕默德

paragraphs.en: was Abu Bakr, Muhammad's closest friend, and father in law through Muhammad's third wifeAmina. But there was also the third person ever to convert to Islam, Muhammad's cousin and adopted sonAli.Ali's claim to leadership was strengthened by the fact that he was married to Fatima, the only surviving child of Muhammad and of his first wife Khadija. Not only that, but Ali and Khadija had two sons, Husayn ibnAli, and Hasan ibnAli, both grandsons of Muhammad. However, on the day that Muhammad died, things happened quickly. WhileAli was preparing Muhammad's body for burial,Abu
paragraphs.zh: 最亲密的朋友，也是穆罕默德的第 三任妻子阿米娜的父亲。 另一人选是皈依伊斯兰教的第三 人：穆罕默德的表弟，也是养子： 阿里。阿里娶了穆罕默德与第一任 妻子哈蒂嘉唯一幸存的女儿——法 蒂玛，这一事实巩固了他的继承权。 而且阿里和法蒂玛还养育了两个儿 子：侯赛因·伊本·阿里和哈桑·伊 本·阿里，他们都是穆罕默德的外 孙。 然而，穆罕默德过世的那天，一切 都发生得很快。 当阿里收拾穆罕默德的遗体准备下 葬时，阿布·巴克尔和另一位重要

paragraphs.en: Bakr and another important Muslim leader named Umar rushed over to a meeting of the Medina tribes, and argued thatAbu Bakr should be chosen as the next leader of the Muslims. WhenAli heard thatAbu Bakr had been chosen as the next leader, he decided to accept this rather than split the community. SoAbu Bakr became the first caliph, the religious and political leader of all Muslims. Abu Bakr immediately faced problems as tribes acrossArabia rebelled against the rule from Medina. At the same time, other people also claiming to be prophets began to rise up all acrossArabia.
paragraphs.zh: 的穆斯林领袖乌玛尔冲到麦地那部 落的一个会议上，主张阿布·巴克 尔应该当选穆斯林的下一任领袖。 当阿里听说阿布·巴克尔被选为下 一任领导人时，他决定接受现实， 而不是分裂这个团体。 因此阿布·巴克尔成为了第一任哈 里发（伊斯兰教主），所有穆斯林的 宗教和政治领袖。 随着阿拉伯各地的部落纷纷反抗麦 地那的统治，阿布·巴克尔的统治 很快遇到了问题。 与此同时，阿拉伯各地也逐渐有人 自称是先知。

paragraphs.en: It tookAbu Bakr a year, but he eventually put down all of these rebellions and re-established Muslim control ofArabia under his leadership. NextAbu Bakr decided to move north and attack the Persian Empire, which had been weakened by 20 years of war with the Byzantines. First the Muslims struck north-east and won a rapid series of victories fromApril-December of 633. By the end of that year the Muslim-Arabas were in control of Mesopotamia. This victory against the Persians was so fast thatAbu Bakr could send those armies west the next year and attack Byzantine-Syria in 634. InAugust the Muslims defeated the Romans at the battle ofAjnadayn
paragraphs.zh: 阿布·巴克尔花了一年时间，最终 镇压了这些叛乱，并在他的领导下 重新建立了穆斯林对阿拉伯的控 制。 接下来阿布·巴克尔决定向北进攻 波斯帝国，这个帝国在与拜占庭 20 年的战争中被大大削弱了。 首先，穆斯林袭击了东北地区，并 在633年4月至12月间迅速取得了 一系列胜利。到年底，穆斯林阿拉 伯人控制了美索不达米亚。 这场对波斯人的胜利来得相当快， 阿布·巴克尔于是在第二年将这些 军队派往西部，并在 634 年攻击拜 占庭-叙利亚。 当年 8 月，穆斯林在阿加那丹战役 中击败了罗马人，赢得了叙利亚农

paragraphs.en: and won control of the Syrian countryside, although the major cities were still in Roman hands. But that same month onAugust 23rd, 634,Abu Bakr died of fever. Before his death, however, he had chosen Umar as the next caliph, so there was a smooth transition of power. ButAli was passed over again. Umar was also an effective caliph. Under him the Muslims defeated the Romans at the battle of Yarmouk on August 636. The major cities were all captured over the next 3 years and the Muslims were in control of all Syria by 640. The Muslims were so strong by this point that they could fight a
paragraphs.zh: 村的控制权，而主要城市仍掌控在 罗马人手中。 但就在这个8月：634年8月23日， 阿布·巴克尔死于发烧。 然而，在他死前，他选择了乌玛尔 作为下一个哈里发，所以权力的过 渡比较平稳。 但是阿里又被略过了。 乌玛尔也是一个不错的哈里发。在 他的领导下，穆斯林于 636 年 8 月 在耶尔雅莫科河战役中击败了罗马 人。 在接下来的 3 年里，穆斯林逐步占 领主要城市；到 640 年，他们控制 了整个叙利亚。 那时的穆斯林非常强大，甚至可以 两线作战。在摧毁东部萨珊帝国的

paragraphs.en: two-front war. While they were destroying the Sassanid empire in the east, they launched an invasion of Egypt in 639. In 642 they won a huge victory against the Persians at the Battle of Navahand. And they conquered Egypt that same year. By 644 the Muslims controlled all of this territory in green. In just 10 years the Muslims had completely destroyed the Sassanid empire. The Persian emperor Yazdegerd I died in 651, but his son Peroz III and his court fled all the way to Tang China. Umar's conquests were astonishing.
paragraphs.zh: 同时，又于 639 年入侵埃及。 642 年，他们在纳瓦汉德战役中取 得了巨大的胜利，战胜了波斯人。 同年他们征服了埃及。 到 644 年，穆斯林控制了图片中所 有显示绿色的这些领土。 仅仅 10 年，穆斯林就彻底摧毁了萨 珊帝国。 波斯皇帝伊嗣埃一世死于 651 年， 但他的儿子卑路斯三世和他的朝廷 逃到了中国唐朝。 乌玛尔取得了惊人的胜利。

paragraphs.en: But then, on October 31st, 644, a Persian slave named Piruz Nahavandi attacked Umar while he was leading the prayers at the main mosque in Medina. Umar was stabbed seven times in the belly before the other people in the mosque could help him. Piruz then killed himself before he could be taken captive. Umar did not die immediately, he succumbed to his injuries 4 days later on November 4th, 644. There were five men who had been close companions of Muhammad and were considered suitable as the next caliph. Ali,Uthman ibnAffan,Zubayr ibn al Awwam,Abd al Rahman,Sad ibn Abi Waqqas
paragraphs.zh: 但随后，644 年 10 月 31 日，一名 名叫皮鲁兹·纳哈万迪的波斯奴隶 在麦地那一座大清真寺主持祈祷时 袭击了乌玛尔。 在清真寺内人员能够上前营救时， 乌玛尔腹部已经被捅了 7 刀。 皮鲁兹在被捕之前自杀了。 乌玛尔没有立即死亡，而是在 4 天 后——644 年 11 月 4 日，他因伤去 世。 有五个人是穆罕默德的亲密伙伴， 被认为是下一个哈里发的合适人 选。 阿里、奥斯曼·伊本·阿凡、祖拜 尔·伊本·阿瓦姆、阿布德·阿拉 赫曼、萨德·伊本·艾比·瓦卡斯

paragraphs.en: As Umar was dying, he asked them to choose a successor from among themselves. At this point a division was beginning to develop, among Muslims. Some thought that any of these men could be the next caliph, according to the traditional beliefs and practices that were developing. These beliefs and practice are called the "Sunnah" inArabic, so this faction became known as the Sunni Muslims. Another faction believed thatAli had the strongest claim to leadership, since he was the 3rd Muslim ever and Muhammad's adopted son,AND son-in law. This group became known as the
paragraphs.zh: 乌玛尔临终前，要这五人自己从中 选出一位领袖。 这时，穆斯林内部开始出现分歧。  根据当时正在发展的传统信仰和实 践，一些人认为这些人中的任何一 个都有可能成为下一个哈里发；这 些信仰和实践在阿拉伯语中被称为 “圣行”，因此这个派别被称为逊 尼派穆斯林。 另一派则认为，阿里最有资格担任 领导人。他是有史以来第三位穆斯 林，也是穆罕默德的养子和女婿。 这个组织后来被称为“什叶派阿

paragraphs.en: "Shi'atuAli" or "Party ofAli." These people believe that the caliph should beAli, or a member of his family. But in this election,Ali was outvoted. Zubayr supported Ali, but Sad Ibn Abi Waqqas andAbd al-Rahman supported Uthman. Not wanting to cause division, and outvoted 3 against 2,Ali accepted the decision. SoAli was passed over for leadership a 3rd time. Uthman became the 3rd Caliph. In many ways Uthman was a good leader. He ruled from 644 until 656. He consolidated earlier conquests and reformed the economy of the
paragraphs.zh: 里”或“阿里党”。这些人认为哈 里发应该是阿里，或者他的家族成 员。 在这次选举中，阿里的票数屈居人 下。 祖拜尔支持阿里，但阿布德·阿拉 赫曼和萨德·伊本·艾比·瓦卡斯 支持奥斯曼。 由于不想引起分裂，阿里以 2 票对 3 票的结果败选。他接受了这个结 果。 于是乎，阿里三次都没能当选。 奥斯曼成为第三位哈里发。 从很多方面来看，奥斯曼都是一个 好领导。他于 644-656 年统治穆斯 林，巩固对了早期征服领土的统治， 并改革了哈里发国的经济。

paragraphs.en: Caliphate. The final version of the Quran was compiled under him, and he made important reforms to taxation and the economy. But Uthman also had weaknesses as a ruler, and his biggest weakness was favoring his own family. Uthman's family descended from an ancestor called Umayyah, so they were called the Umayyad family. Umar had a strong rule against putting his family members in government posts. He felt that would be unfair and cause division in the Muslim community. But Uthman ruled in a more nepotistic way, and so he chose his cousin Muawiya as the governor of Syria.
paragraphs.zh: 《古兰经》的最终版本就是在他的 领导下编纂的，同时，他还对税收 和经济进行了重要的改革。 但作为统治者，奥斯曼也有缺点。 他最大的缺点就是偏袒自己的家 族。 奥斯曼家族出身于倭玛亚，因此他 们被称作倭玛亚家族。 乌玛尔明令禁止家族成员担任政府 职务。他认为这样做不公平，会导 致穆斯林社会的分裂。 但是奥斯曼的统治方式更加任人唯 亲，他选择了他的堂兄穆阿维亚作 为叙利亚的统治者。

paragraphs.en: (Muawiya was also the son ofAbu Sufyan.) Uthman also came under more and more control by his cousin Marwan, who served as his secretary. Some of Uthman's family members were greedy, especially in Egypt. This made people angry. In the summer of 656 a large group of protestors came to Medina from Egypt. The situation escalated and they put Uthman's house under siege for 20 days. Then, on June 17th 656AD, some of the protesters climbed the wall of a nearby house, jumped into Uthman's garden and entered his house.
paragraphs.zh: （穆阿维亚也是阿布·苏富扬的儿 子。） 奥斯曼还越来越受到他堂兄弟马尔 万的影响。马尔万是他的秘书。 奥斯曼的一些家族成员贪得无厌， 尤其是在埃及，使得人民怨声载道。 656 年夏天，一大群抗议者从埃及 来到麦地那。 他们包围了奥斯曼家 20 天，局势就 此升级。 然后，在公元 656 年 6 月 17 日，几 个抗议者爬上附近一所房子的围 墙，跳进奥斯曼的花园，进入他的 房子。

paragraphs.en: There they found the elderly Uthman reading his Quran in his garden.And they killed him there in his own garden. After Uthman's death, the leadership of the Muslim community finally passed toAli. But this was not a smooth transition of power. Ali did not feel strong enough to immediately arrest Uthman's killers. So at this point Uthman's family, the Ummayads, began to accuseAli of organizing Uthman's murder. The strongest Ummayad leader was Uthman's cousin Muawiyah, who was also governor of Syria. Muawiyah refused to recognizeAli's authority and demanded that
paragraphs.zh: 他们发现年老的奥斯曼正在花园里 读《古兰经》，便将奥斯曼杀死在自 家花园里。 奥斯曼死后，穆斯林社区的领导权 最终到了阿里手中。 但权力的交接并不十分顺畅。 阿里在逮捕杀死奥斯曼的凶手时感 到力不从心。 于是，倭玛亚家族开始指控阿里谋 杀了奥斯曼。 倭玛亚家族最强大的领导人是奥斯 曼的堂兄穆阿维耶，他也是叙利亚 的统治者。 穆阿维耶拒绝承认阿里作为领导 者，并要求立即将杀害奥斯曼的凶

paragraphs.en: Uthman's killer be brought to justice immediately. This caused the the first Muslim civil war, called the First Fitnah. This disagreement between Ali and Muawiyah turned violent and both men rallied their armies. The two Muslim armies fought each other at the Battle of Siffin on July 26th, 657AD. After a bloody day of fighting and thousands of deaths, both sides felt sick from killing their fellow Muslims. So Muawiyah suggested they submit the problem to arbitration instead of fighting. Ali agreed. Each side nominated their own
paragraphs.zh: 手绳之以法。 这导致了第一次穆斯林内战。 阿里和穆维耶之间的分歧演变成暴 力冲突，双方都集结了军队。 公元 657 年 7 月 26 日，两支穆斯林 军队之间爆发了隋芬之战。 经过一天的血腥战斗和数千人的死 亡，双方都不愿再杀害穆斯林同胞。 于是穆阿维耶提议将问题提交仲 裁，而不再诉诸武力。 阿里同意了。 双方都指定自己的法律专家作为谈

paragraphs.en: Islamic law expert as a negotiator. But the arbitrators came back on February of 658 and suggested that bothAli and Muawiyah should step down. This stunnedAli because it put him on the same level as Muawiyah. SoAli refused to accept the decision, which technically placed him in violation of theArbitration. This causedAli's support to begin breaking apart. One group ofAli's supporters were religious extremists called Kharajites. The Kharijites believed thatAli should have fought instead of talking. So they split away and became Ali's enemy.
paragraphs.zh: 判代表。 658 年 2 月仲裁员们回来后，却建 议阿里和穆阿维耶都应该下台。 这让阿里很震惊，因为这让他和穆 阿维耶处于同一级别。 于是，阿里拒绝接受仲裁结果，严 格地说，他违背了仲裁。 这使得支持阿里的势力开始瓦解。 阿里的支持者之一是一群宗教极端 分子：哈瓦利吉派。 哈瓦利吉派认为阿里应该坚持战斗 而非对话。于是他们分道扬镳，成 了阿里的敌人。

paragraphs.en: In the meantime, Syria and Egypt stayed independent and the Muslim world became divided. Then on January 26th, 661AD a Kharijite extremist named Abdul Rahman attacked Ali with a poisoned sword while he was praying at the Great Moque in Kufa Iraq. Ali was mortally wounded and died soon after. This is a picture of the Great Mosque whereAli was assassinated. Now thatAli was dead, Muawiyah had emerged as the natural leader of the Muslim world, since he already controlled Egypt and Syria and had the largest army. The only real threat to his rule, was Ali's oldest son, the 35 year-old
paragraphs.zh: 同时，叙利亚和埃及保持独立，穆 斯林世界开始分裂。 然后在公元 661 年 1 月 26 日，一位 名叫阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼的哈瓦利吉 派极端分子在伊拉克的大清真寺祈 祷时用一把有毒的剑袭击了阿里。 阿里受了致命伤，不久就去世了。 阿里就是在图中的这座大清真寺被 暗杀的。 阿里死了，穆阿维耶自然成为了穆 斯林世界的领袖，因为他已经控制 了埃及和叙利亚，拥有最大的军队。 唯一真正威胁他统治的是阿里的大 儿子：35 岁的哈桑。

paragraphs.en: Hasan. So Muawiyah signed a treaty with Hasan. Hasan accepted Muawiyah's rule, but Muawiyah promised not to choose his successor and let it be chosen by the Islamic world instead. Muawiyah ruled as Caliph for the next 19 years before he died in 680 AD. Because Muawiyah's center of power was in Syria, the capital of the Caliphate was moved from Medina to Damascus. But trouble began again in 680 when Muawiyah died. Instead of honoring his treaty with Hasan, Muawiyah chose his son Yazid as his successor. Hasan had died in 670AD, but his
paragraphs.zh: 于是穆阿维耶和哈桑签订了一项协 议。 哈桑接受穆阿维耶作为统治者，同 时穆阿维耶也承诺不指定继任者， 而是由穆斯林人民来选择。 在穆阿维耶于公元 680 年去世之 前，他作为哈里发统治了 19 年。 穆阿维耶的权力中心在叙利亚，哈 里发国的首都便从麦地那搬到了大 马士革。 680 年，动乱又出现了。这一年， 穆阿维耶去世，他没有遵守与哈桑 的协定，而是选择了自己的儿子耶 齐德作为继任。 哈桑于公元 670 年去世，但他的弟

paragraphs.en: younger brother Husayn still lived. Husayn refused to swear allegiance to Yazid and viewed him as a usurper and treaty-breaker. At the time, Husayn was staying in Mecca with his family. Then Husayn received letters from the people of Kufa in Mesopotamia. The people of Kufa promised to support Husayn. So he began a journey from Mecca to Kufa with a small group of his supporters. But on October 10th, 680AD, Yazid's army ambushed Husayn and his company near the Iraqi city of Karbala. This event is called the Battle of Karbala, but Husayn and the Shia had only 72 warriors, while the Umayyad general had an army of
paragraphs.zh: 弟侯赛因还活着。侯赛因拒绝宣誓 效忠耶齐德，视他为篡夺者和协议 破坏者。 当时，侯赛因和家人住在麦加。 接着，侯赛因收到了美索不达米亚 的库法人的来信。 库法人承诺支持侯赛因。于是，他 和一小群支持者开始了从麦加去往 库法的旅程。 但是在公元 680 年 10 月 10 日，耶 齐德的军队在伊拉克的卡尔巴拉附 近伏击了侯赛因及其同伴。 这一事件被称为卡尔巴拉之战，但 侯赛因和什叶派只有 72 名战士，而 倭玛亚的将军则有一支大约 5000 人的军队。

paragraphs.en: about 5000 soldiers. The Shia were also travelling with their women and children. This picture of the battle should give you an idea of how unfair the fight was. Husayn, his family, and his men were all massacred. Husayn was buried in Karabala and his later followers built a shrine to him on that spot. This killing of Husayn marked a final split of the Muslim Community into the Sunni Muslims and Shiite Muslims. Today the Muslim world is still divided between the Sunni Muslims and the Shiite Muslims. Here is a map of where the Shia and
paragraphs.zh: 什叶派还带着妇女儿童。 这张图片应该能让你明白这场战斗 是多么的不公平。 侯赛因，他的家人和他的战士都被 屠杀了。 侯赛因被埋葬在了卡拉巴拉，他后 来的追随者在那里为他建造了一座 神殿。 侯赛因被杀，标志着穆斯林最终分 裂为逊尼派穆斯林和什叶派穆斯 林。 今天的穆斯林世界仍然分为逊尼派 穆斯林和什叶派穆斯林。 这是一张今天什叶派和逊尼派长居

paragraphs.en: Sunni live today. As you can see, the Sunni Muslims are the majority and are 90% of the world's Muslim population. The Shi'ites are only about 10% of the population, but they form a majority in Iran and southern Iraq. And there is a cold-war going on in the Muslim World between Saudi Arabia, the major Sunni power, and Iran, the major Shi'ite power. There are wars going on in Yemen, Iraq, and Syria that are today being fueled by the Sunni-Shia conflict. And there is lower-level conflict going on in Pakistan,Afghanistan, Bahrain, Lebanon and Nigeria as well. So the history I just taught you is
paragraphs.zh: 地的地图。 如你所见，逊尼派穆斯林是多数派， 占世界穆斯林人口的 90%。 什叶派人口仅占总人口的 10%，但 在伊朗和伊拉克南部占多数。 在穆斯林世界，逊尼派大国沙特阿 拉伯和什叶派大国伊朗之间正在进 行冷战。 也门、伊拉克和叙利亚之间目前正 在进行的战争，也是由逊尼派和什 叶派的冲突引发的。 巴基斯坦、阿富汗、巴林、黎巴嫩 和尼日利亚之间也有小规模冲突。 所以我刚才教你们的历史仍然影响

paragraphs.en: still affecting the world we live in today. But now, lets return to history. Now leadership of the Muslim world would be passed along families instead of among the prophet's closest companions. The first of these families were Muawiya's family. Under them a united Caliphate would reach the height of its territory, and a Muslim GoldenAge would begin. But that is a story for next time. Okay everyone I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do I recommend The Cartoon History of The Universe Book 3, which covers the middle ages
paragraphs.zh: 着我们今天的世界。 但现在，让我们回到历史中。 现在，穆斯林世界的领导权将通过 家族传承，而不是在先知最亲密的 伙伴之间交接。 首先就是穆阿维耶的家族。 在他们的领导下，一个统一的哈里 发王国将达到其领土的顶峰，穆斯 林的黄金时代将开始。 但这是下节课的内容。 好了，希望大家喜欢这节课，并有 兴趣了解更多。 如果你有的话，我推荐《漫画宇宙 史》第三册，它涵盖了中世纪，包 括逊尼派和什叶派的分裂。

paragraphs.en: including the Sunni-Shia split. And I also recommend The Oxford Encyclopedia of the Islamic World. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 我还推荐《牛津伊斯兰世界百科全 书》。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第九讲：穆斯林的黄金时代

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to learn about the Islamic Golden Age. Our class will focus on the years from 661 to 811AD. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. The year 661AD marks a major turning point in Islamic history. In that year Ali was assassinated and Muawiyah became the 5th Caliph. After Muawiya died, his son succeeded him. From now on the position of Caliph would be passed on through families. Muawiya's family were known as
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们世界历史系 列讲座。今天我们将要学习穆斯林 的黄金年代。 我们的课程会聚焦于公元 661 年到 811 年。这里是这段时间在时间线 上的样子。 公元 661 年是穆斯林历史上的一个 重要转折点。 那一年，阿里被暗杀。穆阿维耶成 为了第五任哈里发。 在穆阿维耶死后，他的儿子继承了 王位。 从这以后，哈里发便成为了世袭制 穆阿维耶也被叫做倭马亚，因为他

paragraphs.en: the Umayyads in honor of an ancestor named Umayyah. Here you can see all the Umayyad kings on this family tree. And here they are in the order in which they reigned. As you can see, the Umayyad Dynasty was relatively short, it lasted for only 89 years and most Umayyad caliphs ruled for only a few years. The two greatest Umayyad Caliphs wereAbd al-Malik ibn Marwan and his sonAl-Walid ibnAbd al-Malik. Together they expanded a unified caliphate to its largest extent ever, and their combined reigns added up to 30 years of power and stability. Let's look at their accomplishments
paragraphs.zh: 们有个祖先叫做 Umayyah。 这里你可以看到家族谱上所有的 倭马亚国王。 这里是按先后次序排列的。 你可以看到，倭马亚王朝相当短 暂，只存在了 89 年，大部分倭马 亚哈里发只统治了几年时间。 两个最伟大的倭马亚哈里发是 Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan 和他的儿子 他们一起把一个统一的哈里发国 扩张到有史以来最大的程度，他们 的联合统治加起来，带来了长达 30 年的权力和稳定。 让我们来详细了解一下他们的成

paragraphs.en: in more detail. Abd al-Malik ibn Marwan was caliph from 685 until 705.And during that time he had several accomplishments. First, he madeArabic the official language of the caliphate. Second, he reorganized the postal service. Third, he gave the Caliphate its own currency. The silver dirham which weighed 3 grams and the golden dinar which weighed 4.25 grams. Abd al-Malik also made a decision which still affects our world today. When Israel was an independent Kingdom, the Jews had built a temple to God under King Solomon. This first temple existed from 970 -
paragraphs.zh: 就。 685 年到 705 年间担任哈里发，在 此期间，他取得了几项成就。 首先，他使阿拉伯语成为哈里发国 的官方语言。 其次，他重组了邮政系统。 第三，他制定了哈里发自己的货 币。重 3 克的银迪拉姆和重 4.25 克的金第纳尔。 Abd al-Malik还做了一个影响我们 至今的决定。 当以色列是一个独立国家的时候， 犹太人在所罗门王的统治下为神 建造了一座圣殿。第一个神殿存在 于公元前 970 - 587 年，直到被巴

paragraphs.en: 587 BC until it was destroyed by the Babylonians. Later, in 516 BC, the Jews built a second temple that lasted untilAD 70. The second temple was destroyed by the Romans in 70AD because the Jews had rebelled against Roman rule. When the Roman's retook Jerusalem, they sold the entire population into slavery and destroyed the city. The only thing that remained of the second temple was a small segment of an exterior retaining wall on the western side. This became known as the "Western Wall."
paragraphs.zh: 比伦人摧毁。 后来，在公元前 516 年，犹太人建 造了第二座圣殿，一直持续到公元 70 年。 第二圣殿在公元 70 年被罗马人摧 毁，因为犹太人反抗罗马人的统 治。 当罗马人重新占领耶路撒冷时，他 们把所有人都卖为奴隶，摧毁了这 座城市。 第二座寺庙仅存的东西是西侧的 一小段外墙。 这就是著名的“西墙”。（在中国我 们叫它哭墙）

paragraphs.en: And it is a holy place for Jews because of its connection to the Second Temple. But in 691,Abd al-Malik built a mosque on the spot where the Jewish temple had been. This mosque is called the Dome of the Rock. But this created a conflict. Now BOTH the Muslims and the Jews were claiming the same exact spot as a Holy place! The Jews cannot rebuild their temple because a Muslim mosque sits at that exact location. Conflict over the whole area, known as the Temple Mount, continues to this very day! Abd al-Malik’s other great accomplishment was continuing the
paragraphs.zh: 它是犹太人的圣地，因为它与第二 圣殿相连。 但是在 691 年，Abd al-Malik 在犹 太圣殿的原址上建造了一座清真 寺。这座清真寺被称为圆顶清真 寺。 但这造成了冲突。现在，穆斯林和 犹太人都声称拥有同样的圣地! 犹太人不能重建他们的寺庙，因为 一个穆斯林清真寺就坐落在那里。 冲突蔓延了整个圣殿山，一直延续 到今天。 Abd al-Malik的另一项伟大成就是 继续将哈里发国扩张到北非。

paragraphs.en: expansion of the Caliphate into NorthAfrica. And capturing Carthage in 695AD. After this,Arabic became the main language of NorthAfrica. Abd al-Malik died in 705AD, and his sonAl-Walid I took power after him. Al-Walid built the great Umayyad Mosque in Damascus. UnderAl-Walid, Muslim forces invaded Spain in 711AD. They defeated the Goths and had mostly completed the conquest by 715AD. And Muslim armies pushed eastward too. By 712 they had reached the Indus river. These Muslim conquests were so
paragraphs.zh: 并在公元 695 年攻占迦太基。 此后，阿拉伯语成为北非的主要语 言。 公元 705 年，Abd al-Malik 去世， 他的儿子 Al-Walid 一世继位。 AI-Walid 在大马士革建造了大乌 马亚德清真寺。 公元 711 年，在 Al-Walid 的领导 下，穆斯林军队入侵西班牙。 他们打败了哥特人，并在公元 715 年完成了大部分的征服。 穆斯林军队也向东推进。到了 712 年，他们到达了印度河。 这些穆斯林的征服是如此广泛，我

paragraphs.en: extensive, that I think we need to take a moment to look at the big picture. In 624 Muhammad and the Muslims controlled only the city of Medina. In that year they began their war against Mecca. By 628 they controlled the cities north of Medina. And when Muhammad died in 632, they controlled half ofArabia. By 634,Abu Bakr had re-conquered theArabian peninsula and was ready to push north. By 640 the Muslims had conquered all of Mesopotamia and Syria. By 661, the Muslims had added Egypt, Cyprus, and Persia to their territory.
paragraphs.zh: 认为我们需要花一点时间来看看 穆斯林发展的始末。 624 年，穆罕默德和穆斯林只控制 了麦地那市。那一年他们开始了对 麦加的战争。 628 年，他们控制了麦地那北部的 城市。 穆罕默德死于 632 年，他们控制了 阿拉伯半岛的一半。 到 634 年，阿布·巴克尔重新征服 了阿拉伯半岛，并准备向北推进。 到 640 年，穆斯林征服了美索不达 米亚和叙利亚 到 661 年，穆斯林已经把埃及、塞 浦路斯和波斯添加到他们的领土 上。

paragraphs.en: By 710, they had conquered all of NorthAfrica and Central Asia. And Spain was theirs by 733! All of this had been accomplished in just 110 years. You can certainly understand why many Muslims felt that God was on their side. In just over a century they had conquered the largest empire in history. But these conquests had limits. The Nubians drove them off in 652 at the Second Battle of Dongola. In 732 an Umayyad army was defeated by the Franks at the Battle of Tours. This was their farthest advance into Western Europe. From 674-678 theArabs tried to capture Constantinople. But they
paragraphs.zh: 到 710 年，他们征服了整个北非和 中亚。 到 733 年，西班牙已经是倭马亚的。 这一切都是在 110 年里完成的。 这样你就可以理解为什么许多穆 斯林认为上帝站在他们一边。仅仅 一个多世纪，他们就征服了历史上 最大的帝国。 但这些征服是有限度的。 在 652 年的东古拉第二战役中，努 比亚人把他们赶走了。 732 年，一支倭马亚军队在图尔战 役中被法兰克人击败。这是他们进 入西欧最远的地方。 674-678 年间，阿拉伯人试图占领 君士坦丁堡。但他们失败了。

paragraphs.en: failed. They tried again in 717, and were defeated again. TheArab's learned the same lesson that almost all armies learned for 1000 years, the mighty Theodosian walls of Constantinople were just too strong. In addition, the Byzantines had developed a new secret weapon: Greek fire! This was a chemical substance that could be set on fire and used as a flamethrower. Even more terrible, the Greek fire burned on water and could therefore be used against theArab ships. For the next 736 years the walls of Constantinople functioned as the
paragraphs.zh: 717 年他们又试了一次，又失败了。 阿拉伯人学到了几乎所有军队 1000 年来所吸取的教训，君士坦丁 堡强大的狄奥多西城墙实在是太 强大了。 此外，拜占庭人还发明了一种新的 秘密武器:希腊火药! 这是一种化学物质，可以被点燃， 用作喷火器。 更可怕的是，希腊火药可以在水上 燃烧，因此可以用来对付阿拉伯的 船只。 在接下来的 736 年里，君士坦丁堡 的城墙一直充当着“欧洲的盾牌”，

paragraphs.en: "Shield of Europe", no invading army could break through them. It was only the development of gunpowder that finally allowed to Turks to knock them down in 1453. But that's a story for another day. The Umayyad CaliphAl-Walid died in 715.After his death the Umayyad's went into decline. One caliph, Hisham, would have a long reign of 19-years, but all the others ruled for only a few years at a time. In 747, a group ofArabs claiming descend from Muhammad's uncle Al-Abbas rebelled against Umayyad rule. The Rebellion started in Persia and moved west. TheAbbasid leader, As-Saffah defeated the Umayyad
paragraphs.zh: 入侵的军队无法突破。 直到 1453 年，火药的发展才最终 使土耳其人将其打垮。但这是另一 个故事。 倭马亚哈里发 Al-Walid 死于 715 年。他死后，倭马亚开始衰落。 一个哈里发 Hisham 将拥有长达 19 年的统治，但其他所有哈里发一次 只统治了几年。 在 747 年，一群阿拉伯人声称自己 是穆罕默德的叔叔阿巴斯的后裔， 反对倭马亚统治。 叛乱开始于波斯并向西移动。阿巴 斯的领袖，As-Saffah 在几场战役 中击败了倭马亚军队。

paragraphs.en: army in several battles. After the last battle in 750, the Battle of the Zab, Umayyad power was destroyed. The entire Umayyad family was massacred by theAbbasids, but one prince managed to escape the killing. His name was Abdul Rahman I. For the next 5 years he and a servant named Bedr made their way through Mesopotamia, Palestine, the Sinai and Egypt. Then he crossed all of northAfrica. Finally he made it to Spain where his family still had supporters. Once he reached SpainAbdul Rahman I launched a rebellion and broke away from theAbassids. Spain became an independent
paragraphs.zh: 在 750 年的最后一场战役，扎布战 役之后，倭马亚政权被摧毁。 整个家族都被阿巴斯家族屠杀，但 有一位王子设法逃脱了杀戮。他的 名字是阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼一世。 在接下来的五年里，他和一个名叫 Bedr 的仆人穿越了美索不达米亚、 巴勒斯坦、西奈和埃及。然后他穿 越了整个北非。 最后他来到了西班牙，在那里他的 家族仍然有支持者。 他一到西班牙，阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼 一世就发动叛乱，脱离阿巴西德家 族。西班牙成为一个独立的穆斯林 王国:科尔多巴酋长国。

paragraphs.en: Muslim kingdom: The Emirate of Cordoba. The entire Muslim world would never again be unified under a single Muslim ruler. But aside from Spain, theAbbasid's maintained control of the Umayyad caliphate's territory at first. TheAbbasid Dynasty was founded byAs-Saffah whose major accomplishment was to overthrow the Umayyads. He ruled for four years from 750-754. ButAs-Saffah died of smallpox in 754 and was succeeded by his brotherAl-Mansur. And it was Al-Mansur who really made theAbassids great. Al Mansur decided to move the capital away from Damascus closer
paragraphs.zh: 整个穆斯林世界再也不会在一个 穆斯林统治者的统治下统一。 但除了西班牙，阿巴斯家族最初一 直控制着倭马亚哈里发国的领土。 阿巴斯王朝是由阿萨法建立的，他 的主要成就是推翻了倭马亚王朝。 从 750 年到 754 年，他统治了四年。 但是阿萨法在 754 年死于天花，王 位由他的弟弟 Al-Mansur 继承。 正是 Al-Mansur 使得阿巴斯真正强 大起来。 Al-Mansur 决定把首都从大马士革 搬到离他在美索不达米亚的权力

paragraphs.en: to his power base in Mesopotamia. Instead of choosing an existing city for his capital,Al-Mansur decided to build a brand new city: Baghdad. So in 762 Baghdad was born and it soon became the biggest and greatest city of Mesopotamia. The old Persian capital of Ctesiphon was abandoned as people move from it to go live in the new capital. Many of its stones were even taken to build Baghdad. Al Mansur chose the place where the Euphrates and Tigris rivers came closest together as the location for his new capital. He said these words about it: "Indeed, this island between the Tigris in the East and the Euphrates in the West is the harbor of the
paragraphs.zh: 基地更近的地方。 Al-Mansur 没有选择现有的城市作 为首都，而是决定建立一个全新的 城市:巴格达。 所以在 762 年巴格达诞生了，它很 快就成为了美索不达米亚最大最 伟大的城市。 当人们从波斯首都泰西封搬到新 首都后，泰西封被废弃了。它的许 多石头甚至被用来建造巴格达。 Al-Mansur 选择了幼发拉底河和底 格里斯河最接近的地方作为他的 新首都。他是这样说的: 的确，这个位于东部底格里斯河和 西部幼发拉底河之间的岛屿是世 界的港口。所有在底格里斯河上航

paragraphs.en: world.All the ships that sail up the Tigris...and the adjacent areas will anchor here... it will indeed be the most flourishing city in the world." Meanwhile, theAbassids were continuing to push their territory and religion further east. In July of 751 an Abassid army came up against a Tang Chinese army at the Battle of the Talas river. You see, in 751 the Tang Dynasty was at the height of its power, its territory stretched all the way into the central Asia Tarim Basin. The battle was going well for the Chinese at first. But halfway through the battle, the Karluk mercenaries, switched sides and helped theArabs.
paragraphs.zh: 行的船只……以及在相邻的区域 行驶的船只会在这里抛锚……它 将成为世界上最繁华的城市。 与此同时。阿巴斯家族继续把他们 的领土和宗教推向更东边。 751 年 7 月，一支阿巴斯军队在塔 拉斯河战役中与一支唐朝军队交 战。 你看，在 751 年唐朝正处于权力的 巅峰，它的领土一直延伸到中亚的 塔里木盆地。 这场战役一开始对中国人来说很 顺利。 但在战斗进行到一半时，卡鲁克雇 佣军改变了立场，帮助了阿拉伯 人。

paragraphs.en: The Tang soldiers were massacred, and the battle turned into a rout. The Battle of Talas is a turning point in Chinese history. Before the battle, the Tang Empire controlled the area of Transoxiana. After the battle they lost control of that region. Perhaps the Tang could have re-taken the area, but just four years later An-Lushan launched a rebellion in 755. This war was devastating and lasted for 8 years until 763. The Tang dynasty survived for another 144 years, but it was never as powerful again. Another consequence of the Battle of Talas, was that theAbbasids got paper-making technology from the
paragraphs.zh: 唐朝的士兵被屠杀，战争变成了溃 败。 塔拉斯战役是中国历史上的一个 转折点。在战争爆发前，唐朝控制 了河中地区。 战斗结束后，他们失去了对那个地 区的控制。 也许唐朝本可以重新占领这一地 区，但仅仅四年之后，安禄山就在 755 年发动了叛乱。这场战争是毁 灭性的，持续了 8 年，直到 763 年。 唐朝又延续了 144 年，但它再也没 有那么强大过。 塔拉斯战役的另一个结果是，阿巴 斯家族从中国人那里得到了造纸 技术。

paragraphs.en: Chinese. In 794, a paper making factory was opened in Baghdad and by 850 there were over 100 paper-making workshops. This new supply of paper also helped fuel a great period of learning. Al-Mansur founded a school and library called "The House of Wisdom". He did this in imitation of the great PersianAcademy of Gondishapur. AndAl-Mansur began an effort to translate all the classics of Greek, Indian and Persian literature into Arabic. This effort, called the translation movement, preserved many works
paragraphs.zh: 794 年，巴格达开办了一家造纸厂， 到 850 年，已有 100 多家造纸厂。 在纸张方面的供应也促进了学习 的发展。 Al-Mansur 建立了一所学校和图书 馆，名为“智慧之屋” 他这样做是在模仿波斯的贡迪沙 普尔学院。 Al-Mansur 开始努力将所有希腊、 印度和波斯文学的经典著作翻译 成阿拉伯语。 这种努力被称为“翻译运动”，它 将许多古典的作品保留到了现代。

paragraphs.en: of classical antiquity into the modern era. Probably the single most valuable advance that theArabs gained from this effort was the Indian number system. TheArabs learned about the Hindu Numeral System and developed the Arabic Numerals that the whole world uses today. This number system was vital to the development of higher mathematics. For example, there was the great mathematicianAl-Khwarism who discovered algebra. There was the scholar Jabir ibn Hayyan who developed many tools of chemistry. There was Al-Kindi who admired
paragraphs.zh: 阿拉伯人从这一努力中获得的最 有价值的进步可能是印度的数字 系统。 阿拉伯人了解了印度数字系统，并 发展出了今天全世界使用的阿拉 伯数字。 这个数字系统对高等数学的发展 至关重要。 例如，伟大的数学家 Al-Khwarism 发明了代数。 有一位学者 Jabir ibn Hayyan 多 化学工具。 Al-Kindi 崇拜亚里斯多德，他被认

paragraphs.en: Aristotle and is considered the father ofArab Philosophy. And much of this learning was made possible by tireless translators like Hunayn Ibn Ishaq who translated 116 books in his lifetime. By 800AD, the Muslim world was experiencing a golden age, and the Abbasid caliphate could claim to be the center of world civilization. But now we will leave this flourishing society behind and turn our attention west to Spain. Please join me next time for that story.
paragraphs.zh: 为是阿拉伯哲学之父。 这些知识大部分都是由像 Hunayn Ib Ishaq这样孜孜不倦的译者完成 的，他一生翻译了 116 本书。 公元 800 年，穆斯林世界经历了一 个黄金时代，阿巴斯哈里发国可以 说是世界文明的中心。 但现在我们将把这个繁荣的社会 抛在身后，把注意力转向西方的西 班牙。 欲知后事如何，且听下回分解

titleZh: 第十讲：阿里乌派，哥特人和伊比利亚|信仰真主的西班牙

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture on World History, today we are going to look at what was happening in the Iberian peninsula during the Early MiddleAges. This lesson will focus on the 4 centuries between 313 and 732AD. Our story begins in 313AD, when the Roman Empire was still the dominant power in Europe. On that year (according to legend) Constantine the Great saw the Chi Rho sign in the sun. And then he went on to win the Battle of the Milvan bridge. Whatever the real reason, Constantine became the first Christian Roman Emperor.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列，今天我们将要看看在中世纪 早期的伊比利亚半岛发生了些什 么。 这一课将会关注从公元 313 年到 732 年之间的四个世纪。 我们的故事开始于公元 313 年，那 时罗马帝国仍主宰着欧洲。 根据传说，在那一年君士坦丁大帝 在太阳中看见了 Chi 和 Rho(代表 耶稣)符号。 然后他在米尔维安大桥战役中获 得了胜利。 无论真实原因为何，君士坦丁成了 第一位信仰基督的罗马皇帝。

paragraphs.en: But by the time Constantine converted, Christianity was already about 300 years old, and there were disagreements within the church about what Christian believed exactly. So Constantine called a meeting to sort it all out. He chose the town of Nicea inAsia Minor, a short distance from Constantinople. The meeting lasted about a month from May 20th to June 19th, 325 AD.And eventually the bishops agreed on a statement of faith. This statement of faith is known as the Nicean Creed, and even today it stands as a summary of what orthodox Christians believe. Here is the English version.
paragraphs.zh: 但到君士坦丁皈依之时，基督教已 有大约三百年历史，教会内部关于 基督徒究竟在信仰什么的问题异 议丛生，因此，君士坦丁召集了一 次会议，以厘清这些问题。 他选择了小亚细亚的尼西亚城，距 离君士坦丁堡不远。 会议持续了大约一个月，从公元 325 年的五月二十日到六月十九 日。最终，主教们达成了一份信仰 声明。 这份信仰声明被称为尼西亚信经， 直到今天它依然代表了对于正统 基督徒信仰的全面总结。 这里是其英文版。

paragraphs.en: And here is the Chinese version. But not everyone agreed with the Nicean Creed. An influential priest fromAlexandria named Arius disagreed about the nature of Jesus. Christians believe that there is one God in three persons: The Father, The Son, and the Holy Ghost. This is called the Trinity. The Nicene Creed stated that Jesus (God the Son) was eternal like God the Father, butArius thought that God the Father must have existed before Jesus at some point, if Jesus was "begotten" of the father. These sorts of arguments about unprovable mysteries might seem silly, but in the Byzantine Empire, people argued about the trinity and the nature of Jesus all the time, and
paragraphs.zh: 这是它的中文版。 但并非每个人都认同尼西亚信经。 一位来自亚历山大港的很有影响 力的牧师叫做阿里乌斯，他不同意 其中对于基督本质的说法。 基督徒相信在三个人当中存在着 同一的神：圣父、圣子和圣灵。这 就是三位一体。 尼西亚信经声称，耶稣（神子）是 永恒的，一如圣父上帝，但阿里乌 斯认为圣父上帝必须在耶稣之前 就已存在，如果说耶稣是由其父 “产生”的话。 这些关于无法证明的谜团的不同 观点可能看起来有点傻，但是在拜 占庭帝国，人们因为三位一体和基 督本质的问题终日争吵，这些分歧 甚至削弱了帝国。

paragraphs.en: these divisions weakened the empire. Some Christians agreed withArius, and this caused a split in the Christianity between the Nicene Catholics Christians and theArian Christians. This disagreement might have been an unimportant detail in history, except that Arius had a big influence on two important people, a Bishop named Eusebius (who was a friend ofArius) and a priest named Ulfilas who was Eusebius's student. In the 340s, Eusebius sent Ulfilias into Gothic territory to convert them to Christianity. To fulfil his mission, Ulfilas created the GothicAlphabet in order to translate the Bible. Since the goths
paragraphs.zh: 有些基督徒认同阿里乌斯的观点， 这导致了教内尼西亚正统派和阿 里乌派的分裂。 这一分歧在历史上可能会是一个 无关紧要的细节，但事实上，阿里 乌斯对两个重要人物产生了强大 影响：一位名叫尤西比乌的主教， 他是阿里乌斯的朋友，还有一位名 叫乌尔菲拉的牧师，他是尤西比乌 的学生。 在公元四世纪四十年代，尤西比乌 将乌尔菲拉派往哥特人的领地，去 向他们传教。 为了完成使命，乌尔菲拉创设了哥 特字母表以翻译圣经。因为当时的 哥特人是没有书写系统的一伙文

paragraphs.en: were an illiterate people without a writing system. Modern German, (and English) evolved from Old Gothic, so you could say that Ulfias's Gothic bible is the very beginning of German and English literature. Ulfilas had some success as a Christian missionary and converted many Gothic chiefs. But he converted them toArian Christianity, not traditional Nicene Catholicism. This meant that when the Goths invaded the Roman Empire in 376, many of their chiefs like Frittigern andAlaric were already Christians. Then came the crossing of the Danube on the last day of 406, and the sack of Rome in 410 and 455.
paragraphs.zh: 盲。 现代德语（以及英语）是从古哥特 语演化而来，所以你可以说乌尔菲 拉的哥特语圣经就是德语和英语 文学的起源。 作为基督教传教士，乌尔菲拉取得 了相当的成功，他使得一些哥特官 员皈依了基督教，但他们所接受的 是阿里乌派基督教，而非传统的尼 西亚正教。 这意味着，当哥特人在公元 376 年 入侵罗马帝国的时候，其中的许多 将领，例如弗里蒂杰恩和亚拉里 克，已经是基督徒了。 他们在公元 406 年的最后一天渡过 了多瑙河，并在 410 年和 455 年两 次劫掠了罗马。

paragraphs.en: As we've already seen, the Western Empire collapsed under this invasion of different Germanic tribes. The three most important tribes from this period were the Goths, Franks and Vandals. The Vandals eventually settled in NorthAfrica under the leadership of their king Genseric. And their Kingdom survived for 99 years, until they were conquered by the Byzantine Romans under Emperor Justinian. NorthAfrica became Roman territory again, and remained that way for over 150 years, until it was conquered by theArabs in 698. The other two major Germanic tribes were the Goths who settled in
paragraphs.zh: 正如我们已经看见的，西罗马帝国 在多个日耳曼部落的侵袭之下崩 溃了。 这一时期最重要的三个部落是哥 特人、法兰克人和汪达尔人。 汪达尔人最终在他们的国王盖萨 里克的领导下定居北非。 他们的王国存在了 99 年，最终被 查士丁尼大帝领导的拜占庭罗马 军队所征服。 北非再次成为罗马领土，并持续了 超过一百五十年，直到公元 698 年 它被阿拉伯人攻占为止。 另外两个主要的日耳曼部落当中， 哥特人定居在了西班牙，而法兰克

paragraphs.en: Spain, and the Franks, who settled in Northern Gaul. As you might have guessed, the Franks built up their Kingdom which became the ancient origin for the modern country of France. We will learn more about them next class. For now, lets focus on the Goths. Here is what the Mediterranean world looked like in 476 when the Western Roman Empire finally fell. The first King of the Goths was Alaric, who led them to the first sack of Rome in 410AD. ButAlaric died later that year from malaria, and his brother in law Ataulf became the second Gothic King.
paragraphs.zh: 人在高卢北部安了家。 你可能已经猜到了，法兰克人所建 立的王国就是现代法国的始祖。 我们将在下一次课中了解到关于 这些的更多信息。 现在我们还是聚焦于哥特人。这是 476 年的地中海世界，西罗马帝国 就在这个时候最终陷落。 哥特人的第一位国王是亚拉里克， 他领导他们进行了公元 410 年对罗 马的第一次劫掠。 但亚拉里克在同一年晚些时候死 于疟疾，他的妹夫阿陶尔夫成为了 第二任哥特国王。

paragraphs.en: Ataulf led them to Southern Gaul where they settled down. Alaric andAtaulf were the beginning of a line of 34 kings who ruled the Goths from 395 to 714. After them the next really important King was the 5th King, Theodoric the First. It was Theodoric I who made the alliance with the Roman general FlaviusAetius and defeated Atilla the Hun at the Battle of the Catalaunian Plains. Unfortunately, Theodoric I died in battle, defending his people against the Huns. After Theodoric the next really important King wasAlaric II, who was the 9th King.
paragraphs.zh: 阿陶尔夫带领他们到了高卢南部， 然后定居在那里。 亚拉里克和阿陶尔夫是哥特多达 34 位的国王谱系的开端，这 34 位 国王从公元 395 年到公元 714 年一 直统治着哥特人。 在他们之后，下一位真正重要的国 王是第五位国王，狄奥多里克一 世。 正是狄奥多里克一世与罗马将军 艾提乌斯结成了同盟，并在卡塔隆 平原战役中击败了匈人统治者阿 提拉。 不幸的是，狄奥多里克一世在这场 战役中阵亡，面对匈人保卫了他的 族民。 在狄奥多里克死后，下一位真正重 要的国王是亚拉里克二世，他是第 九位皇帝。

paragraphs.en: Alaric was important for his failure. He was defeated by the Franks and killed in the fighting at the Battle of Vouille in the summer of 507. As a result of this battle, the Franks pushed the Goths out of Gaul entirely.After 511 their Kingdom would be limited to the Iberian peninsula. The next really important King was Recarred I, who was 17th King of the Goths. During Recarred's life, the Byzantines had recaptured southern Spain for the Roman Empire. To help him re-conquer this territory, Recarred switched his religion fromArian Christianity to Nicene Catholicism.
paragraphs.zh: 使得亚拉里克格外重要的是他的 败绩。他被法兰克人所击败，并在 507 年夏天的武伊勒战役中被杀。 这次战役导致法兰克人完全将哥 特人从高卢驱逐了出去。在 511 年 以后，他们的王国疆域已经被限制 在了伊比利亚半岛之内。 下一位真正重要的国王是哥特人 的第十七位国王，雷卡里德一世。 在雷卡里德生活的年代，拜占庭人 已经重新攻占了西班牙南部，使之 重回罗马帝国统治。 为了重新夺回这块领土，雷卡里德 把自己的宗教信仰从阿里乌派基 督教改成了尼西亚天主派。

paragraphs.en: After his conversion in 587, the Nicene Catholics in southern Spain were willing to support him, and Recarred was able to drive the Byzantines out of Iberia. Under Recarred, the Goths became Catholic, which is an important event in the history of the Catholic Church. The Franks had converted a few decades earlier, so Recarred's conversion made Nicene Catholicism the strongest religion in Europe. After Recarred, the next really important Gothic Kings were Wittiza and Roderic, the 32nd and 33rd Kings. They are important because Roderic overthrew Wittiza in 710AD.
paragraphs.zh: 在他于 587 年皈依以后，西班牙南 部的尼西亚天主教开始支持他，而 雷卡里德由此得以将拜占庭人赶 出了伊比利亚半岛。 在雷卡里德的统治下，哥特人成为 了天主教徒，这是天主教会历史上 的一个重要事件。 法兰克人已经在几十年前皈依了 天主教，所以，雷卡里德的皈依使 得尼西亚天主教成了欧洲最强大 的宗教。 在雷卡里德之后，接下来真正重要 的哥特国王是威特萨和罗德里克， 分别是第 32 位和第 33 位国王。 他们之所以重要，是因为罗德里克 在公元 710 年推翻了威特萨。

paragraphs.en: This badly divided the Goths and started a civil war. This was terrible timing, because the Umayyad Caliphate was completing its conquest of North Africa in 710Ad. The Umayyads saw an opportunity and offered to help Wittiza's enemies, and with their support, the Arabs invaded Spain in 710. The Muslim army was led by a general named Tariq ib Ziyad.After he crossed into Iberia, Roderic rushed south to meet him in Battle. This battle, known to history as the Battle of Guadalete was a major turning point in Spanish history. Half of Roderic's army was still mad at him for overthrowing Wittiza and they abandoned him in the middle
paragraphs.zh: 这严重分裂了哥特人，并导致了内 战的爆发。 战争发生在一个糟糕的时间，因为 倭马亚王朝在公元 710 年正将完成 它对于北非的征服战争。 倭马亚人在其间看到了机会，并向 威特萨的敌人提供了帮助，在他们 的帮助下，阿拉伯人在 710 年入侵 了西班牙。 穆斯林军队由一位名叫塔里克·伊 本·齐亚德的将军率领，在他进入 伊比利亚半岛以后，罗德里克赶往 南方与他交战。 这场战役史称瓜达雷特战役，是西 班牙历史上的一个重要转折点。 有一半的罗德里克的军队仍对他 推翻威特萨一事感到恼怒，他们在 战役中背弃了他。

paragraphs.en: of the Battle. Roderic was then overwhelmed by theArabs and was killed along with him men. However, it was a mistake for Roderic's Gothic enemies to betray him, because theArabs turned around and crushed them afterwards. Within a short time, theArabs had conquered the Gothic capital of Toledo and then began their conquest of Iberia. Divided and weakened by Civil War, the Goths were overwhelmed and crushed. The Gothic Kingdom came to an end, and Spain now became another province of the Umayyad caliphate. By 732, the conquest of Spain was
paragraphs.zh: 罗德里克随即被阿拉伯人推翻，并 与他的亲信一同被杀。 然而，罗德里克在哥特的国内敌人 选择背叛他是一个严重错误，因为 阿拉伯人很快转过身来，也摧毁了 他们。 在很短的时间里，阿拉伯人攻占了 哥特人首都托雷多，开始了他们对 于伊比利亚的征服行动。 因内战而四分五裂且虚弱不堪的 哥特人一败涂地。哥特王国因此灭 亡，而西班牙现在成了倭马亚王朝 的一个省。 到 732 年，西班牙已经大体被征服，

paragraphs.en: largely complete, except for resistance in the northern mountains ofAsturias. At this point, theArabs began thinking about pushing the Caliphate even deeper into Europe. In 732 the generalAbdul Rahman al Ghafi led an army across the Pyrenees mountains. His goal was to conquer the Franks. The Battle fought that year would become one of history's turning points. But that is a story for another day... Okay everyone I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do, I have a recommendation for you. My recommendation is the Youtube
paragraphs.zh: 只除了北部的阿斯图里亚斯山区 还有一些抵抗力量。 此时，阿拉伯人开始考虑将哈里发 国疆域进一步推向欧洲腹地。 732 年，阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼领导一 支军队翻越了比利牛斯山，他的目 标是征服法兰克人。 这一年爆发的战役将成为一个历 史转折点。 但这是另一个故事了。 好了，我希望你们喜欢这次课程并 且希望了解更多，如果你们想的 话，我会给你们一些推荐。 我的推荐是 Dr. Ryan Reeves 的

paragraphs.en: channel by Dr. Ryan Reeves, an Associate Professor of Historical Theology at Gordon-Conwell Theological Seminary. He offers clear explanations of Christian History, and helps his audience better understand things like theArian controversy or early Christian Europe. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: Youtube 频道，他是戈登康威尔神 学院的历史神学助教。 他给出了对于基督教历史的清晰 解读，并且帮助他的观众能够更好 地了解很多事，例如阿里乌派争端 以及早期的基督教欧洲。 谢谢你们。

paragraphs.en: Welcome back to our lecture series on World History, today we will learn about the Carolingian dynasty. This lecture will focus on the years between 411 and 870AD. As we've already learned when the Western Roman Empire fell, there were three major Germanic tribes in the west: The Goths, The Franks and the Vandals. So far we've talked about the Goths and Vandals, but haven't said much about the Franks. In the 300s, when the Roman Empire was still strong and the Franks were Roman allies who lived on the other side of the Rhine river. Their main enemies were the
paragraphs.zh: 欢迎回到我们的世界历史系列讲 座，今天我们将了解到的是加洛林 王朝。 这一讲将聚焦于公元 411 年到 870 年。 正如我们已经在西罗马帝国陷落 时了解过的，当时在西部存在三个 主要的日耳曼部落：哥特人，法兰 克人和汪达尔人。 到目前为止，我们已经谈及过哥特 人和汪达尔人，但还没有过多涉及 法兰克人。 在公元四世纪，罗马帝国尚且强 盛，法兰克人还是生活在莱茵河对 岸的罗马的盟友。 他们的主要敌人是撒克逊人，他们

paragraphs.en: Saxons and their capital city was Cologne. Cologne was actually a Roman settlement on the Frankish side of the Rhine river. The Romans gave the city to the Franks as part of the deal that made them allies. But then the Western Roman Empire collapsed between 395 to 476.At first the Franks were loyal to the Romans, but they eventually joined the migration into former Roman territory. To help you see what a big change happened in the 400s, here is Roman Gaul in 400AD, and here it is in 500AD. During this period, the Franks were led by a great King named Merovech. He is a partly historical
paragraphs.zh: 的首都是科隆。 科隆实际上是罗马在莱茵河法兰 克人占据的一岸的定居点。作为缔 结联盟的协议的一部分，罗马人将 这个城市送给了法兰克人。 但在 395 年到 476 年，西罗马帝国 崩溃了，起先法兰克人忠于罗马 人，但最终他们加入了向罗马国土 移民的行列。 为了帮助你们明白在公元五世纪 发生的是多么大的变化，这里是公 元 400 年时的罗马治下的高卢，而 这里是公元 500 年它的样子。 在这段时间里，法兰克人被一位名 叫墨洛维的伟大国王所统治，他是 一位半历史、半传说的人物。

paragraphs.en: and partly legendary figure. Merovech became the ancestor to a line of Frankish Kings called the Merovingians. He had a son named Childreic and a grandson named Clovis. Clovis led the Franks to victory against the Goths at the Battle of Vouille and drove them out of Gaul. Here in Green you can see a map of the Kingdom that Clovis built. Clovis was the leader who really built the Kingdom of the Franks. Clovis was also mainly a war leader, to maintain power he had to fight wars every year, win battles, and provide his followers with plunder. But Clovis is also important because he had a Nicene Catholic wife named Clotilde.And Clotilde tried
paragraphs.zh: 墨洛维是一系法兰克国王的祖先， 他们被称作墨洛温王朝。他有一个 儿子名叫希尔德里克，还有一个孙 子名叫克洛维。 克洛维在武伊勒战役中领导法兰 克人击败了哥特人，将后者赶出了 高卢。 在这里的绿色部分你可以看到克 洛维所建立的王国的疆域。克洛维 是法兰克王国的真正创立者。 克洛维大部分时间也是一位战争 领导人，他每年都在进行战争，赢 得战役，并向他的追随者提供战利 品。 不过，克洛维的重要性还在于他有 一位笃信尼西亚天主教的妻子，名 叫克洛蒂尔德。克洛蒂尔德试图让

paragraphs.en: to get her husband to convert to Christianity. Eventually Clotilde succeeded and Clovis accepted baptism on Christmas Day 496AD. Here is that scene depicted in an ivory carving. And here is that same scene in a painting. The baptism of Clovis is a major event in Christian history, because it meant that the Franks would be Christians from now on. But the Franks also had a tradition that hurt their power again and again. They practiced "progeniture." This meant that when a man died, his land and possessions were divided equally among his sons. And when Clovis died, he had 4 sons. One named Theuderic from his first marriage .And three other
