paragraphs.zh: 她的丈夫归信基督教。 最终克洛蒂尔德成功了，克洛维在 公元 496 年的圣诞日接受了洗礼， 这是一幅象牙版画，描绘了当时的 情景。 这是描绘同一场景的一副画作，克 洛维的受洗是基督教历史上一个 重要事件，因为它意味着法兰克人 从此将成为基督徒。 但法兰克人也有一项一再削弱了 他们自己力量的传统，他们执行诸 子均分制。 这意味着，当一个人死了，他的土 地和财产将被均等地分给他的三 个儿子。 当克洛维去世的时候，他共有四个 儿子。其中一个名叫提乌德里克， 是他的第一次婚姻所生，另外三个

paragraphs.en: sons from his marriage to Clotilde. So when Clovis died in 511, his Kingdom was divided into 4 pieces, which fragmented Frankish power. Fortunately for the Franks, three sons died without heir, and Chlothar was able to reunify the Kingdom in 558. But the problem repeated itself, Chlothar also had 4 sons. So the empire was once again divided into 4 pieces and each son (Chiperic, Charibert, Sigebert, and Guntram) got their own piece. As you can see, this practice of Progeniture frequently divided the Frankish Kingdoms. So about 1000AD, the practice began to change, and the Western
paragraphs.zh: 儿子则是他与克洛蒂尔德所生。 所以，当公元 511 年克洛维去世的 时候，他的王国被分成了四块，这 削弱了法兰克人的力量。 不过法兰克人还算走运，其中的三 个儿子死时都没有留下继承人，克 洛泰尔由此得以在558年重新统一 了王国。 但问题重演了，克洛泰尔也有四个 儿子。 所以帝国再次被分成了四个部分， 每一个儿子（希尔佩里克，查理贝 尔特，西吉贝尔特和贡特朗）分别 得到了他们各自的部分。 正如你所见，诸子均分制频繁地分 裂着法兰克诸王国。 所以，在公元 1000 年左右，事情 开始起了变化，西欧人开始实行长

paragraphs.en: Europeans practiced Primogeniture instead. In this system, the oldest surviving son got all the land, and his younger brothers got nothing. For Kings, Primogenture meant that the crown was always passed down along the line of oldest sons, while the younger sons became members of the nobility instead. In Europe in the MiddleAges, the Nobility were usually cousins of the King, and almost all of them had a King as an ancestor. That's what made them nobility. The King is the descendant of the oldest son of the King. And the Nobility are the descendants of the younger sons.
paragraphs.zh: 子继承制。 在这一系统下，存活下来的最年长 的儿子继承全部土地，他的弟弟们 则不会得到任何东西。 对于国王们，长子继承制意味着王 位将总是传给最为年长的儿子，而 小儿子们则成为贵族成员。 在中世纪欧洲，贵族通常是国王的 表兄弟，他们几乎所有人都有一个 共同的国王祖先，这是导致他们成 为贵族的原因。 国王是那位国王最年长的儿子的 后代。 而贵族是那些比较年轻的儿子们 的后代。

paragraphs.en: Gradually three social classes or estates developed in Europe. The First Estate was the church, and everyone who worked for it. The first estate was those who pray. The Second Estate was the nobility. The second estate was those who fight. And the Third Estate was everyone else, the commoners. The third estate was those who work. This system reached its height in the years between 1000 and 1500AD. And for those centuries, members of the nobility valued two things above all else. Your bloodline or ancestral connection to the King's family. And your skill in battle or
paragraphs.zh: 逐渐地，欧洲发展出了三个阶级， 或者说三个社会阶层。 第一阶层是教会，以及所有为教会 工作的人，这一阶层是那些负责祈 祷的人。 第二阶层是贵族，是那些负责战斗 的人。 第三阶层是剩下的所有人，普通人 们。第三阶层是那些负责工作的 人。 这一系统在公元 1000 到 1500 年达 到了其巅峰。在这几个世纪里，贵 族阶层成员把两件事看得比其他 一切都要重要。 你与国王家族的血脉或者祖先方 面的联系。 以及你在搏斗中的技巧，或者说

paragraphs.en: "prowess". But now lets return to the Franks. The Merovingian dynasty ruled the Franks for almost 300 years across 11 generations. But by the 600s, the Kings were losing power, they stopped leading their men in battle and left the business of government to their Majordomo, or "Mayor of the Palace". By the late 600s, real power shifted from the Kings to the Majordomos. One of the first really powerful Majordomos was Pepin of Herstal. Technically Theodoric III was the King, but he was a puppet King. Pepin was the real leader. In 688, Pepin had a son named Charles Martel who became Major
paragraphs.zh: “英勇”。 但现在让我们回到法兰克人。墨洛 维王朝统治法兰克人近三百年，历 经 11 代。 但到公元七世纪，国王已经失掉了 权柄，他们不再在战斗中身先士 卒，也将朝政事务丢给了他们的宫 相，或者叫“宫廷的主事”。 到七世纪晚期，真正的权力已经从 国王转移到了宫相的手中。 其中最早也最有权柄的大管家名 叫赫斯塔的丕平。技术上，提乌德 里克三世是国王，但他只是个傀儡 国王，丕平才是真正的领导人。 688 年，丕平有了一个名叫查理·马 特的儿子，他在公元 714 年丕平死

paragraphs.en: Domo after Pepin died in 714AD. As you can imagine, the Franks had been once again divided by progeniture. There were three Frankish Kingdoms. And Charles Martel was only the Majordomo of the one in the northeast. So for the next 4 years, Charles Martel fought a war and conquered the Kingdom in the Middle. By 718, there were only 2 Frankish Kingdoms. The one in the north ruled by Charles Martel and the one in the south ruled by Odo the Great. Of course these two men were rivals at first. But during this time a more dangerous enemy was emerging in
paragraphs.zh: 后接任了宫相一职。 正如你所能想象到的，法兰克人再 次因为诸子均分制而被分裂了，出 现了三个法兰克王国。 而查理·马特只是其中位于东北的 那个王国的宫相。 所以，在接下来的四年里，查理·马 特发动了一次战争，征服了位于中 部的王国。 到公元 718 年，只剩下两个法兰克 王国了。北边的那个由查理·马特 统治，南边的由厄德大帝统治。 当然，他们起初是对手。 但正当这个时候，南方出现了更危 险的敌人。

paragraphs.en: the south. Because in 710 the Ummayyads crossed into Spain. They defeated the Goths at the Battle of Guadalete. And from 711 to 732 they completed their conquest of Spain. In the 720s they began launching raids into Odo's territory. Suddenly Odo was facing a bigger threat than Charles Martel. So Odo reached out to his old enemy. He offered to accept Charles as his boss if Charles would help him defend against the Ummayyads. Charles Martel agreed. When the Umayyad army invaded Gaul in 732, the Franks were unified and ready to face them.
paragraphs.zh: 因为在 710 年，倭马亚军队进入了 西班牙。 他们在瓜达莱特战役中击败了哥 特人。 从 711 年到 732 年，他们完成了对 西班牙的征服。 八世纪二十年代，他们开始侵袭厄 德的领地。 厄德突然间面临着比查理·马特更 大的威胁。 所以厄德向他的旧日敌人伸出了 手。他提出，他可以承认查理为他 的君主，如果查理帮助他击败倭马 亚人的话。 查理·马特同意了。 公元732年，倭马亚军队入侵高卢， 法兰克人于是团结起来，准备迎 战。

paragraphs.en: On October 10th, 732 they met the Umayyad army at the Battle of Tours. In this battle it was Abdul Rahman al Ghafi with a force of cavalry against Charles Martel, who led an infantry force, and Odo, who led a small cavalry force. The battle was fierce, but the Franks had prepared well and they fought off the Umayyads. The Ummayad army decided to turn south and go back to Spain in order to make it home before winter. The Battle of Tours marks the high point of Muslim expansion into Western Europe. In fact the famous historian of Rome, Edward Gibbon, believes
paragraphs.zh: 732 年 10 月 10 日，他们在图尔战 役中遭遇了倭马亚军队。 在这场战役中，正是阿卜杜勒·拉 赫曼率领着一支骑兵部队，对战由 查理·马特率领的步兵部队和由厄 德率领的一支较小的骑兵部队。 战斗是惨烈的，但法兰克人准备充 分，最终击退了倭马亚人。 倭马亚军队决定转而向南，回到西 班牙，以便在冬天降临之前在那里 安营扎寨。 图尔战役标志着穆斯林在西欧扩 张的巅峰。 事实上，罗马著名的历史学家爱德 华·吉本相信，图尔战役是欧洲历

paragraphs.en: that the Battle of Tours was one of the great turning points in European history. He speculates that the Muslims may have conquered all of Europe if the Franks had lost that battle. Although modern historians are a little more skeptical of that idea. In any case, the Battle of Tours made Charles Martel a hero all across the Christian world, especially to the Pope in Rome, who began to see Charles's family as potential allies. When Charles died, in 741 his position was passed down to his son Pepin the Short. But after being Majordomo for 10 years, Pepin decided that he wanted to be the actual King of the Franks.
paragraphs.zh: 史上最重要的转折点之一。 他怀疑，如果法兰克人输掉那场战 役，穆斯林可能会征服整个欧洲。 尽管现代历史学家们对此有点怀 疑。 无论如何，图尔战役将查理·马特 变成了整个基督教世界的英雄，尤 其是对于罗马教皇来说，后者开始 将查理的家族视为潜在的盟友 在查理于 741 年去世以后，他的位 置被传给了他的儿子，矮子丕平。 但在成为宫相十年以后，丕平决 定，他想要成为法兰克的真正国 王。

paragraphs.en: So he asked Pope Gregory III for permission, and the Pope agreed saying that the actual leader of a people should also be the legal King. So in 751 Pepin overthrew the last Merovingian King, Childeric III and sent him off to a monastery to become a monk. But before doing that, Pepin had the monks cut off Childeric's hair, because to the Franks, long hair was the symbol of Kingship. Then in return for the Pope's support, Pepin invaded Italy and defeated the Lombards, who were threatening Rome. And he gave the Pope some of the Lombard territory in Central Italy as a gift.
paragraphs.zh: 所以他向教皇格里高利三世请求 许可，而教皇认可地说，人民的真 正领导人也应该成为法律上的国 王。 所以在 751 年，丕平推翻了最后一 任墨洛维王朝国王希尔德里克三 世，送他到一处修道院去做了僧 侣。 但在做这些事情之前，丕平先让其 他僧侣剪断了希尔德里克的头发， 因为法兰克人相信，长头发是国王 地位的象征。 随后，为了回报教皇的支持，丕平 入侵了意大利并击败了伦巴底族 人，后者当时威胁着罗马。 然后他将一部分位于意大利中部 的伦巴底领土作为礼物献给了教 皇。

paragraphs.en: This territory was called the Donation of Pippin and it made the Pope not just a spiritual leader, but the political leader of that territory as well. The Donations of Pippin became territory that was directly ruled by the Pope. He collected taxes and enforced laws and even had his own army. This territory became known as the Papal states and they existed until the 1870s! Pepin the Short died in 768 and left his kingdom to his two sons: Carloman and Charlemagne. But Carloman died just 3 years later in 771. He was only 20 years old and had no heir.
paragraphs.zh: 这块领土被称为丕平献土，它使得 教皇不再只是一位精神领袖，也成 了这块领土上的政治领袖。 这块由丕平进献的领土直接归入 了教皇统治下，他在此课税，执法， 甚至有了自己的军队。 这块领土被称为教皇国，它一直存 在到了十九世纪七十年代！ 矮子丕平死于 768 年，将王国留给 了他的两个儿子：卡洛曼和查理 曼。 但卡洛曼仅仅三年后就死了，那是 公元 771 年，年仅二十岁，没有继 承人。

paragraphs.en: So Charlemagne became the sole King of the Franks. Beginning with Charles Martel there was now a new dynasty of Kings who ruled the Franks. Since the founder of this dynasty was Charles Martel, and since the name "Charles" in Latin is "Carolus", historians call this dynasty the "Carolingians". And the greatest of the Carolingians was Charlemagne. In life his real name was "Charles" or "Carolus" in Latin. But he was such a great king that people started calling him "Carolus Magnus" or "Charles the Great" in Latin. Carolus Magnus turned into Charles Magne is French and "Charlemagne" in English.
paragraphs.zh: 所以查理曼成了法兰克人的唯一 的国王。 从查理·马特开始，如今是一个新 的王朝的国王在统治法兰克人了。 由于这个王朝的创始人是查理·马 特，而拉丁文中“查理”拼作“卡 洛斯”，历史学家因此将这个王朝 称为加洛林王朝。 加洛林王朝最伟大的国王是查理 曼。 在生时他真正的名字是“查尔斯” 或者拉丁语“卡罗洛斯”，但他是 一位如此伟大的国王，乃至于人们 开始称呼他“Carolus Magnus”， 这是拉丁语拼写的“查尔斯大帝”。 Carolus Magnus 在 法 语 写 成 Charles Magne，在英语则写成 Charlemagne，即查理曼。

paragraphs.en: Like his ancestors, Charlemagne was also a great war leader.And under him the Franks developed a strong cavalry force. First Charlemagne began a six-year war against the Lombards. After completing his conquest of the Lombard in 774 he moved east and conquered Thuringia. In 778 he moved south and took the Pyrenees mountains away from the Emirate of Cordoba, creating a buffer territory between his Kingdom and the Muslims in Spain. Next he began his longest war, a 20-year campaign against the Saxons in the northeast. While that war was going on, he marched east and conquered
paragraphs.zh: 正如他的祖先一样，查理曼也是一 位伟大的战争领导人。在他的领导 下，法兰克人发展出了一支强大的 骑兵军队。 首先，查理曼发动了一场长达六年 的针对伦巴底人的战争。 在 774 年，在完成了对于伦巴底的 征服以后，查理曼移师向东，征服 了图林根。 778 年，他向南进发，从科尔多巴 酋长国手中夺取了比利牛斯山脉， 在他自己的王国与西班牙的穆斯 林之间创造了一个缓冲地带。 随即他开始了他最漫长的战争，一 场长达二十年的针对东北方撒克 逊人的战事。 随着战争的持续，他挥军向东，于 788 年征服了巴伐利亚与卡林西

paragraphs.en: Bavaria and Carintha in 788. Once the Saxons were defeated Charlemagne moved east and conquered theAvars in 796. Then he defeated the Czechs and Moravians in 805. By his death in 814, Charlemagne had created the greatest Kingdom in Western Europe. And of course the Pope in Rome took notice. The Popes had been allies of Charlemagne's family ever since Charles Martel.And so in 799 the Pope asked Charlemagne for help against his enemies in Rome and also in the Byzantine Empire. And Charlemagne agreed, he went south to Rome and strengthened the Pope's power.
paragraphs.zh: 亚。 一旦撒克逊人被击败，查理曼继续 向东进发，在 796 年征服了阿瓦尔 人。 随后，在 805 年，他又击败了捷克 人和摩拉维亚人。 到他在 814 年去世为止，查理曼建 立了西欧最大的王国。 当然，罗马教皇注意到了这一点。 罗马教皇自查理·马特时代就与查 理曼的家族互为盟友，所以，在 799 年，教皇为击败他在罗马和在拜占 庭帝国内的敌人而向查理曼寻求 帮助。 查理曼同意了，他南下来到罗马， 增强了教皇的权柄。

paragraphs.en: And then on Christmas Day 800 AD, the Pope returned the favor by declared Charlemagne the New Roman Emperor. But unlike the old Roman Emperor's, this emperor would be crowned by the Pope making him the "Holy Roman Emperor". And Charlemagne's Kingdom became known as the Holy Roman Empire. So now there were two Kingdoms claiming to be the Roman Empire. There was Charlemagne's Frankish Empire in the west and the Byzantine Empire in the east. But Charlemagne did more than just conquer, he also attracted scholars to his court from all across Europe. Together Charlemagne and these
paragraphs.zh: 然后，在公元 800 年的圣诞日，教 皇报之以李，宣布查理曼为新一任 罗马皇帝。 但与原本的罗马皇帝不同的是，这 位皇帝将由教皇加冕，使得他成为 “神圣罗马皇帝”。 而查理曼的王国也因此成为神圣 罗马帝国。 所以现在有两个自称罗马帝国的 王国了，分别是西方查理曼的法兰 克帝国与东方的拜占庭帝国。 但查理曼所做的不止是四处征伐， 他也从整个欧洲招揽学者到他的 宫廷。 查理曼与这些饱学之士一起，在西

paragraphs.en: men of learning launched a revival of civilization and learning in Western Europe. And one of these scholarsAlcuin of York became Charlemagne's best friend. These two men, the warrior and the scholar put the Holy Roman Empire on a strong foundation. They set up schools in monasteries all across the empire where the sons of the rich could learn to read and write. This launched a revival of learning and preservation of Latin books that historians call the Carolingian Renaissance. The best evidence of this renaissance is seen in manuscripts. From the years 1-800 we only have
paragraphs.zh: 欧发动了文明和学识的复兴运动。 其中的一位学者，约克的阿尔琴， 成了查理曼最好的朋友。 这两个人，战士和学者，共同为神 圣罗马帝国打下了一个强有力的 基础。 他们在整个帝国的各修道院中建 立了学校，使得富人的儿子们可以 学习读写。 这开始了一场复兴学习以及保存 拉丁书籍的运动，历史学家称之为 加洛林复兴。 这次复兴运动最好的证据可以从 手稿中看到。从公元1年到公元800 年，仅有大约 2000 份拉丁文手稿

paragraphs.en: about 2000 surviving Latin manuscripts. But from the years 800-900 we have 7000. These scholars and copyists , mostly working in Monasteries, also developed a standard style of writing letters called Carolingian miniscule. This is when capital and lower-case letters began to develop, as well as the practice of putting spaces between words. We also have some surviving examples of Carolingian architecture like St. John'sAbbey in Switzerland. Or the LorschAbbey gatehouse in Germany.
paragraphs.zh: 流传给我们。 但从公元 800 到公元 900 年，我们 有 7000 份。 这些学者和抄写员大部分在修道 院中工作，他们同时也发展出了对 于手写字母的一种标准写法，被称 为加洛林体。 这是大小写字母分化的开始，也是 词间空格的开始。 我们还有一些加洛林时代的建筑 留存至今，例如瑞士的圣约翰大修 道院。 以及德国的洛尔施修道院门房。

paragraphs.en: Both of these are UNESCO world heritage sites. The Carolingians also developed beautiful carvings of wood and ivory, usually for book covers. And a tradition of painting developed to accompany the texts that were being copied and preserved. These paintings in books became known as "Illuminated Manuscripts". Charlemagne's scholars also developed a standard money system. They took a pound of silver and divided it into 240 pennies. This Carolingian penny became the basis of western European money
paragraphs.zh: 这两个建筑都是联合国教科文组 织认定的世界遗产。 加洛林王朝还发展出了美丽的牙 雕和木雕，通常用于书籍封面。 也发展出了一种绘画传统，在抄写 和保存文本的时候为其配画。 这些书上的画作被称为“配图手 稿”。 查理曼的学者们还发展出了标准 货币系统。 他们把一磅银分成 240 个银币。 加洛林银币由此成为超过一千年 当中西欧货币的基础。

paragraphs.en: for over 1000 years. It was such a good system that it was adopted by the English King Offa of Mercia. In England the system of 240 pennies to pound remained the standard for English money until 1971! But when Charlemagne died in 814, the old problem of Progeniture re-emerged. Charlemagne had only 1 surviving son, but he had 3 grandsons: Lothair, Louis and Charles. These grandsons divided the Holy Roman Empire into 3 pieces in 840. And then they began fighting each other in 843. The Middle Kingdom was crushed between the eastern and western Kingdoms by 870.And
paragraphs.zh: 这是一个非常不错的系统，乃至于 它甚至被英格兰麦西亚国王奥法 所采纳。 在英格兰，这种 240 枚银币总重一 磅的系统直到 1971 年都还是英国 货币的标准！ 但当查理曼在 814 年逝世，诸子均 分制的老问题又出现了。 查理曼只有一个儿子活下来，但他 有三个孙子：Lothair, Louis 和 这三个孙子在840年把神圣罗马帝 国分成了三块。 然后在843年，他们开始相互争斗， 870 年，地处中央的王国在夹击下 灭亡，在原本查理曼的帝国的领土 上出现了西法兰克和东法兰克。

paragraphs.en: from Charlemagne's Empire emerged West Francia and East Francia. East Francia would eventually evolve into the country of France. And West Francia would eventually become Germany. But those are stories for another day. In 800AD, Charlemagne's Holy Roman Empire was the greatest Power in Western Europe. And for this reason, all later western European countries are connected to it in some way. For that reason, Charlemagne is the ancestor of every Royal Family in Western Europe. That's why Charlemagne is so
paragraphs.zh: 东法兰克最终变成了今天的法国。 而西法兰克最终成了德国。 但这就是属于另一天的故事了。 公元 800 年，查理曼的神圣罗马帝 国是西欧最强大的政权。 也因为这个原因，后来的所有西欧 国家都以某种方式与它有所关联。 因此，查理曼是西欧所有皇室的祖 先。 这就是为什么查理曼如此重要，他

paragraphs.en: important; he is the great ancestor of every Royal Family; he is the "Father of Europe". The British Royal Family are his descendants. The current prince George is 44 generations descended from Charlemagne. In France it is the same story. The Last French King, Louis XVI was a 34th generation descendant of Charlemagne. This is why Charlemagne is so important. European history from 800 until the French Revolution can be simplified to the story of Charlemagne's descendants fighting over power. This is why the French Revolution of 1789 is so important, because it marks a change in European
paragraphs.zh: 是每一个皇室家族的祖先，他是 “欧洲之父”。 英国皇室是他的后代，现在的王子 乔治是查理曼的第 44 代孙。 法国也是一样，最后一任法国国王 路易十六，是查理曼的第 34 代孙。 这就是为什么查理曼如此重要，从 公元 800 年起直到法国大革命，欧 洲历史可以被简化成查理曼的后 代们争夺权力的故事。 这就是为什么 1789 年法国大革命 如此重要，因为它标志着欧洲人思 维的转变。

paragraphs.en: thought. Before 1789 legitimate power came from your ancestral relationship to Charlemagne. After 1789, legitimate power came from the will of the People. This is what separates the Middle Ages and Early Modern Period from the Late Modern Period. But now we will shift our attention north. Because in the later years of Charlemagne's rule, a new terrifying menace began to emerge, one that threatened all Europe from the north. And that is the topic of our next class. I hope you enjoyed that lesson, and want to learn more. If you did, I
paragraphs.zh: 在 1789 年以前，合法权力来自于 你与查理曼大帝的血缘关系。 1789 年以后，合法权力来自人民的 意志。 这也是将中世纪以及现代早期和 现代晚期区别开来的东西。 但现在我们需要将我们的注意力 转向北边，因为在查理曼统治的最 后几年，一个新的可怕威胁开始出 现，它自北而来，威胁着整个欧洲。 这是我们下一次课的主题。 我希望你们享受这次课并且希望 了解更多，如果你们想的话，我有

paragraphs.en: have a recommendation for you. It is the Podcast "In Our Time" by the BBC and their episode on the Carolingian Renaissance. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 一个推荐。 这就是 BBC 的播客“In Our Time”， 以及他们关于加洛林复兴的节目。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第十二讲：诺斯人|你知道 Sunday 是一个舶来词吗？

paragraphs.en: Hello and welcome back to our lecture series on world history! Today we will be learning about the Norse people. More commonly known as the Vikings. We will be focusing on the years between 793 and 1066. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. Our story begins with a monastery in Northern England known as Lindisfarne. Lindisfarne was famous for the beautiful illuminated manuscripts that it produced. But on the morning of January 8th, 793 strange men showed up outside the monastery. They had arrived from the ocean on mysterious long
paragraphs.zh: Sunday 是一个舶来词吗？ 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座!今天我们将学习诺斯 人。更广为人知的是维京人。 我们将把重点放在 793 年到 1066 年之间。在我们的时间轴上是这样 的。 我们的故事从英格兰北部的一个 叫做林迪斯法恩的修道院开始。 林迪斯法恩以其精美的彩绘手稿 而闻名。 但是在 1 月 8 日早上，793 名奇怪 的人出现在寺院外。他们乘坐神秘 的小艇从海上来到这里。

paragraphs.en: ships. These men attacked the monastery without mercy. The stole everything of value, burned the buildings, slaughtered the defenseless monks and took other captive to be sold as slaves. Alcuin, the English scholar who was a close friend of Charlemagne wrote about the attack: Never before has such terror appeared in Britain as we have now suffered from a pagan race ... The heathens poured out the blood of saints around the altar, and trampled on the bodies of saints in the temple of God, like dung in the streets. These men were the Scandinavians, also called Norse-men or Northmen. They had come from Scandinavia.
paragraphs.zh: 这些人毫不留情地袭击了修道院。 他们偷走了所有值钱的东西，烧毁 了建筑，屠杀了手无寸铁的僧侣， 还把其他僧侣抓起来当作奴隶贩 卖。 Alcuin，英国学者，查理曼大帝的 好朋友，这样描述这次袭击: 英国以前从未出现过像我们现在 遭 受 异 教 种 族 之 害 这 样 的 恐 怖……外邦人把圣徒的血倒在圣 坛的四围，在神殿中践踏圣徒的身 体，就像践踏街上的粪土。 这些斯堪的纳维亚人，也被称作诺 斯人或北方人。 他们来自斯堪的纳维亚半岛。包括

paragraphs.en: Which includes the modern countries of Denmark, Norway, Sweden and Finland. Like people everywhere the Norse mainly lived by farming or trade. But some would go off on raids. In the Norse language the word for a raider is "Viking" so that word is also used to describe the Norse of this period. The Norse were not yet Christians, they still worshipped the older Germanic gods. And like many ancient people, the Germanic tribes associated their gods with different planets. There was Odin or Woden, the god of wisdom who was associated with the planet Mercury. Then there was Odin's wife Frigg or
paragraphs.zh: 丹麦、诺斯、瑞典和芬兰等现代国 家。 和世界各地的人们一样，诺斯人主 要以农业或贸易为生。但有些人会 发动突袭。在诺斯语中，“掠袭者” 会用“viking”这个词，所以这个 词也被用来描述这个时期的诺斯 人。 诺斯人还不是基督徒，他们仍然崇 拜古老的日耳曼神。 就像许多古代人类一样，日耳曼部 落将他们的神与不同的星球联系 在一起。 奥丁或沃登是智慧之神，代表水 星。 然后是奥丁的妻子弗莱格(Frigg)

paragraphs.en: Freya, who was associated with the planet Venus. Odin had a son named Thor, who was the thunder god, and was associated with the planet Jupiter. Thor is still popular today among fans of Marvel comic books! And there was also Tyr or Tiw who was associated with the planet Mars. The key to Viking power was their excellent skill as sailors. Their long ship was the fastest ship in the world at that time. It had a top speed of 28 km/hour. No other ship in the world could catch them on the open ocean. From 793 to 865, the Vikings raided the English coast over and over again.
paragraphs.zh: 或弗莱娅(Freya)，代表金星。 奥丁有一个儿子叫托尔，他是雷 神，代表木星。 雷神托尔至今仍深受漫威漫画迷 的喜爱! 还有个神叫做 Tyr 或者 Tiw，代表 火星。 维京人力量的关键是他们作为水 手的出色技能。他们的“龙船”是 当时世界上最快的船，最高时速 28 公里。 世界上没有别的船能在广阔的海 洋上追上它们。 从 793 年到 865 年，北欧海盗一遍 又一遍地袭击英国海岸。

paragraphs.en: But in 865, they came with a large army. This time their goal was conquest, not just simple raiding. For the next 9 years they pushed outward and defeated all of the English kingdoms except for one. The territory that they captured became known as the "Danelaw." Because the English were Christians but the Norse were not, English history refers to this invasion force as the "Great HeathenArmy". Only the King of Wessex,Alfred the Great, was able to defeat the Norse. The rest of the Island fell under Viking rule. This Norse period of English history had a big influence on the English language and culture. Over 2000 modern English words come from
paragraphs.zh: 但是在 865 年，他们带来了一支庞 大的军队。这次他们的目标是征 服，而不是简单的劫掠。 在接下来的9年里，他们向外扩张， 除了一个王国，其他的英国王国全 被打败了。 他们占领的领土被称为“丹麦劳”。 因为英国人是基督徒，而诺斯人不 是，所以英国历史把这支侵略军队 称为“伟大的异教徒军队”。 只有韦塞克斯国王阿尔弗雷德大 帝能够打败诺斯人。岛上的其余部 分都归维京人统治。 这段诺斯人的英国历史对英语语 言和文化有很大的影响。超过 2000 个现代英语单词来自诺斯语。这张 幻灯片上有一些例子。

paragraphs.en: the Norse language. Here on this slide are some examples. The Norse also gave the English language names for its days of the week. The first three are obvious. There is Saturday, named after Saturn; Sunday, named after the Sun; Monday, named after the Moon. But there is also Tuesday, which is Tiw's day, who also represented Mars. There was Wednesday, or Woden's Day, who also represented Mercury. There was Thursday, or Thor's Day, who also represented Jupiter. And there was Friday, or Freyya's Day, who also represented Venus. For four Generations, the English
paragraphs.zh: 诺斯人将自己语言中对星期的名 字应用在了英语中。 前三个很明显。周六，来自于土星； 周日来自于太阳；周一，来自于月 亮。 这里还有周二，Tiw’s day, 也代 表了火星。 还有周三，或者叫做 Woden’s Day, 也代表了水星。 还有周四，或者叫 Thor’s Day， 也代表了木星。 还有周五，或者叫做Freyy‘a s Day， 代表了金星。 韦塞克斯的英国国王与诺斯人争

paragraphs.en: Kings of Wessex contended with the Norse. Alfred the Great was succeeded by his son Edward the Elder. Edward the Elder was followed first by his sonAethelstan and then this other son Edmund. Finally King Eadrid and his brother Eadwig succeeded in pushing the Norse out of England. This is the period in history when the name "England" first emerged. But, the Norse were also settlers as well as raiders and conquerors. In 874, a group of Norse led by a man named IngolfurArnarson became the first people to settle Iceland. Then 108 years later one of the
paragraphs.zh: 斗了四代。 阿尔弗雷德大帝的继位是他的儿 子大爱德华。 大爱德华之后是他的儿子埃塞尔 斯坦，然后是另一个儿子埃德蒙。 最后，国王艾德里德和他的兄弟艾 德威格成功地把诺斯人赶出了英 格兰。 这是历史上“英格兰”一词首次出 现的时期。 但是，除了是掠袭者和征服者，诺 斯人也向其他地方移民。 874 年，一个名叫因戈尔弗·阿纳 尔森(Ingolfur Arnarson)的诺斯 人领导的一群人成为第一批定居 冰岛的人。 108 年后，一位名叫埃里克的冰岛

paragraphs.en: Iceland settlers named Erik the Red became the first person to explore Greenland. Erik and his men explored Greenland for 3 years from 982 to 985, and then they returned to Iceland to recruit settlers. In 985 the Norse established a colony on Greenland which survived for about 400 years. The next year in 986 a Norse explorer named Bjarni Herjólfsson was trying to reach Greenland from Iceland but he got blown off course. As he made his way back to Greenland he sighted a new land that no European had ever set eyes on before. It was Canada. When Bjarni came back to Greenland, he told other people
paragraphs.zh: 移民成为第一个探索格陵兰岛的 人。 从 982 年到 985 年，埃里克和他的 人在格陵兰岛探险了 3 年，然后他 们回到冰岛招募定居者。 985 年，诺斯人在格陵兰岛建立了 一个殖民地，并在那里生存了大约 400 年。 第二年，986 年，一位名叫 Bjarni Herjolfsson 的诺斯探险者试图从 冰岛到达格陵兰岛，但他偏离了航 线。 在返回格陵兰岛的途中，他看到了 一块欧洲人从未见过的新大陆。那 就是加拿大。 当 Bjarni 回到格陵兰岛时，他把 他所看到的告诉了其他人。

paragraphs.en: about what he had seen. These stories interested Erik the Red's son, Leif Erickson. So in the year 1001AD, Leif led another expedition further west and they reached the Canadian island of Newfoundland. This made Leif Erickson's expedition the first Europeans to reach theAmericas, in the summer of 1001AD. On the island of Newfoundland they found lots of cranberries and squash berries which they used to make wine. So when Leif returned to Greenland he called this new country "Vine-land". Intrigued by the promise of more
paragraphs.zh: 这些故事引起了埃里克的儿子利 夫·埃里克森的兴趣。 所以在公元 1001 年，利夫带领另 一支探险队向西，到达了加拿大的 纽芬兰岛。 这使得赖夫·埃里克森的探险队在 公元 1001 年夏天成为第一批到达 美洲的欧洲人。 在纽芬兰岛上，他们发现了许多小 红莓和压碎的浆果，就是他们用来 酿酒的东西。 所以当 Leif 回到格陵兰岛的时候 他把这个新国家叫做“葡萄园”（文 兰德）。 为了得到更多的土地来定居，两支

paragraphs.en: land for settlement, two expeditions traveled to Vinland to set up a colony, one in 1004AD and another in 1009AD. But unlike Greenland, Vinland was already settled by NativeAmerican tribes. Unfortunately, the relationship between the Norse and NativeAmericans turned violent. Eventually repeated Native American attacks forced the Norse to abandon the settlement. So the Norse explorer Leif Erickson was the first European to reach America, nearly 500 years before Columbus. But unlike Columbus, his discovery had no long-term affect on history. But the Norse did not just expand west, they also expanded east.
paragraphs.zh: 探险队前往文兰德建立殖民地，一 支在公元 1004 年出发，另一支在 公元 1009 年出发。 但与格陵兰岛不同的是，文兰岛已 经被美洲土著部落所定居。不幸的 是，诺斯人和美洲原住民之间的关 系变得暴力。 最终，印第安人的多次袭击迫使诺 斯人放弃了这个定居点。 因此，诺斯探险家利夫·埃里克森 是第一个到达美洲的欧洲人，比哥 伦布早了近 500 年。但与哥伦布不 同，他的发现对历史没有长期影 响。 但诺斯人不仅向西扩张，还向东扩 张。

paragraphs.en: In the early 800s the Norse discovered trade routes that could connect the Baltic Sea to the Black sea along the Dnieper, Don and Volga rivers. The Norse began to take advantage of this trade and even reached the city of Constantinople in the early 800s. The Byzantines noticed these new-comers and called them the Varangians. These river trade routes also connected the Norse to theAbassids. So the Norse began trading with the Byzantines andAbassids. They traded furs, amber, ivory, and most terribly slaves, some captured from as far away as Ireland.
paragraphs.zh: 在 8 世纪早期，诺斯人发现了可以 沿着第聂伯河、顿河和伏尔加河连 接波罗的海和黑海的贸易路线。 诺斯人开始利用这个贸易路线，甚 至在公元800年代初到达君士坦丁 堡。 拜占庭人注意到了这些新来者，称 他们为瓦朗日人。 这些河流贸易路线也连接了诺斯 人和阿巴斯。 因此，诺斯人开始与拜占庭人和阿 巴斯人进行贸易。 他们交换毛皮、琥珀、象牙和更可 怕的是，他们还交换奴隶，有些奴 隶甚至是从遥远的爱尔兰被抓来 的。

paragraphs.en: The Norse also raided the local Slavic people along these rivers and took them as captives who they sold into slavery in the great Greek and Arab empires. The Norse sent so many slavic people into slavery down south, that word "slave" actually comes from the word "slav". But the Norse also traded and intermarried with the local slavs, and captured the city of Kiev which became their capital. Over time the Norse and Slavs mixed together and formed the state called "The Kievan Rus". This is the earliest origin of what would later become Russia. We will hear more about the Kievan
paragraphs.zh: 诺斯人还袭击了沿河的斯拉夫人， 把他们当作俘虏，卖给伟大的希腊 和阿拉伯帝国做奴隶。 诺斯人把很多斯拉夫人送到南方 当奴隶，奴隶这个词实际上来自 “斯拉夫”slav。 但诺斯人也与当地的斯拉夫人进 行贸易和通婚，并占领了基辅市， 成为他们的首都。 随着时间的推移，诺斯人和斯拉夫 人融合在一起，形成了一个名为 “基辅罗斯”的国家 这就是后来成为俄罗斯的最早起 源。 我们将在后面的课程中听到更多

paragraphs.en: Rus in latter lectures. Finally, the Norse also had a big impact on French history. In the early 800s the Norse launched Viking raids against cities and Monasteries on the French coast. After Charlemagne's son, Louis the Pious died, he left his empire to his three sons. We discussed this last time. Those three sons started to fight each other, weakening the empire. They even hired the Norse to act as mercenaries, which was a bad idea, because it made the Norse war leaders richer and more familiar with the Frankish countryside. In 845 the Norse even launched a raid all the way down the Seine
paragraphs.zh: 关于基辅罗斯的内容。 最后，诺斯语也对法兰西历史产生 了重大影响。 在 8 世纪早期，诺斯人对法兰西海 岸的城市和修道院发动了海盗袭 击。 查理曼的儿子，虔诚的路易死后， 他把帝国留给了三个儿子。我们上 次讨论过。 这三个儿子开始互相争斗，削弱了 帝国。 他们甚至雇佣诺斯人充当雇佣兵， 这是一个坏主意，因为这使得诺斯 战争的领导人更加富有，也更加熟 悉法兰克人的乡村。 845 年，诺斯人甚至对塞纳河发起 突袭，将巴黎围困。

paragraphs.en: river and put Paris under siege. Eventually, the Frankish king Charles the Bald was forced to pay off the Norse commander Ragnar Lodbrock to get him to leave. But this was just a temporary solution and the Norse kept coming back. The Norse attacked Paris again in the 860s. In 880 they captured the Carolingian capital ofAachen, and again the Frankish king had to pay to get them to leave. And in 885 the Norse came back to Paris again. This army in 885 was led by the Norse commander Rollo. But after 40 years of being kicked
paragraphs.zh: 最后，法兰克国王，秃头查理，被 迫向诺斯指挥官罗布罗克付钱，让 他离开。 但这只是一个暂时的解决办法，诺 斯人还是不断地回来。 诺斯人在 860 年代再次袭击巴黎。 880 年，他们占领了加洛林王朝的 首都亚琛，法兰克国王不得不再次 付钱让他们离开。 885 年，诺斯人又回到了巴黎。 885 年，这支军队由诺斯指挥官罗 洛率领。 但是在被诺斯人鞭笞 40 年之后，

paragraphs.en: around by the Norse, the Franks were better prepared. But the siege dragged on, so Charles the Fat was forced to pay Rollo to leave. Twenty-six years later In 911, Rollo attacked again. But the Franks had a new king named Charles the Simple, and he was more determined to fight. The French defeated Rollo at the battle of Chartres. Then the Frankish King Charles the Simple arrived with a force of mounted horsemen. He drove Rollo's men all the way back to their ships. But instead of trying to finish Rollo off, Charles the Simple decided to use his stronger position to negotiate
paragraphs.zh: 法兰克人有了更好的准备。 但是包围还在继续，所以胖子查理 不得不付钱给罗洛让他离开。 26 年后的 911 年，罗洛再次发动袭 击。 但是法兰克人有了一个新的国王， 名叫查理（糊涂王查理三世），他 更有决心去战斗。法兰西人在沙特 尔战役中击败了罗洛。 法兰克国王查理斯率领骑兵到达。 他把罗洛的人一路赶回船上。 但是，查理决定利用他的强势地位 来谈判，而不是努力了结罗洛。

paragraphs.en: a deal. So Charles the Simple gave Rollo three terms: 1)Convert to Christianity 2)Keep Peace with the Crown 3)Provide troops in war Then Charles would give Rollo the province of Normandy in northwest France. Rollo agreed. This new province became known as "Normandy" after Rollo's Norse who settled it. The other advantage was that Normandy contained the mouth of the Seine river. Since this was now Rollo's territory, and Rollo was now loyal to the King of France, there could be no more Viking raids on
paragraphs.zh: 所以查理给了罗洛三个条件: 皈依基督教 与国王保持和平 在战争中提供部队 然后查理将法兰西西北部的诺曼 底省交给罗洛。 罗洛同意了。 这个新建的省份在罗洛的诺斯人 定居后被称为“诺曼底”。 另一个好处是诺曼底有塞纳河的 入海口。因为这里现在是罗洛的领 土，罗洛现在效忠于法兰西国王， 所以海盗再也不能袭击巴黎了。

paragraphs.en: Paris. These Norse lords took Frankish wives and so within a few generations, their children had assimilated into French culture. Within two generations, Rollo's descendants all spoke French and were Catholics. But they were still proud of their Norse heritage on their father's side. Rollo's Norse Vikings became the ancestors the Norman people. And Rollo was the great-great-great-grandfather of another famous King, William the Conqueror. But that is a story for our next class! Join me next time as we learn about the Normans!
paragraphs.zh: 这些诺斯贵族娶了法兰克人的妻 子，因此几代人之后，他们的孩子 就融入了法兰西文化。 在两代人的时间里，罗洛的后代都 说法语，都是天主教徒。但他们仍 然为自己的诺斯血统感到自豪，站 在父亲这一边。 罗洛的北欧海盗成为了诺曼底人 的祖先。 罗洛是另一位著名国王——征服 者威廉的曾曾曾祖父。 但那是我们下节课的故事! 下次和我一起学习诺曼底人吧!

paragraphs.en: Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lecture and want to learn more. If so I have a recommendation for you. My recommendation is the Youtube channel Extra Credit, they have an excellent series on the Viking expansion and its effect on England, France, Russia and Ireland.
paragraphs.zh: 好了，各位，我希望你们喜欢这堂 课，并且希望你们愿意了解更多。 如果是的话，我会给您们推荐： Youtube 频道的 Extra Credit，他 们有一个非常优秀的系列讲座，关 于维京扩展和它对英国，法兰西， 俄罗斯和爱尔兰的影响。

titleZh: 第十三讲：诺曼底人与法兰西人

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we will discuss the Normans and French. Our lesson will focus on the years between 911 and 1066. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. In our last lesson we saw how Charles the Simple defeated the Norse King Rollo at the Battle of Chartes. Charles used mounted cavalry to trap Rollo's retreating men against the river, but instead of trying to finish Rollo off, he offered him a deal. He gave Rollo the land around the mouth of the Seine if Rollo would
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到世界历史系列讲 座。今天我们会讨论诺曼底人和法 兰西人。 我们的课程将集中在 911 到 1066 年间。这是这段时间在时间轴上的 样子。 在上节课中，我们学习了查理如何 在查理战役中击败了诺斯国王罗 洛。 查理用骑兵把罗洛的后撤士兵困 在河边，但他没有把罗洛干掉，而 是提出了一个条件。 他把塞纳河河口附近的土地给了 罗洛，前提是罗洛能维持和平，皈

paragraphs.en: keep the peace, convert to Christianity and provide the French King with troops during wars. Rollo agreed to these terms and the Duchy of Normandy was born. The Norse men married French Catholic wives, and as a result their children grew up speaking French and were baptized as Christians. The Normans basically became French, but with some elements of their original Norse culture, especially in their love of war. Then Rollo and his descendants ruled the Duchy of Normandy for 7 generations from the early 900s into the early 1100s. During this time, some major changes were happening in France.
paragraphs.zh: 信基督教，并在战争期间为法国国 王提供军队。 罗洛同意了这些条件，诺曼底公国 就诞生了。 诺斯男人娶了法兰西天主教的妻 子，结果他们的孩子长大后说法 语，并受洗成为基督徒。 诺斯人基本上变成了法兰西人，但 他们也保留了一些原始诺斯文化 的元素，尤其是他们对战争的热 爱。 从 10 世纪初到 12 世纪初，罗洛及 其后裔统治诺曼底公国达 7 代之 久。 在此期间，法国发生了一些重大变 化。

paragraphs.en: The first change was the invention of the stirrup. It’s a very simple invention, but it didn't appear in Europe until the Early MiddleAges. When it did appear, it changed warfare in a big way. The stirrup was first developed in CentralAsia and reached the Byzantine Empire by around 600 AD. It then spread into the rest of Europe over the next 100 years. Here are two illustrations of early Frankish cavalry, as you can see the men had to keep their balance without stirrups. This limited the amount of armor that the horsemen could wear.
paragraphs.zh: 第一个变化是马镫的发明。 这是一个非常简单的发明，但它直 到中世纪早期才出现在欧洲。 当它出现的时候，它在很大程度上 改变了战争。 马镫最早出现在中亚，公元 600 年 左右传到拜占庭帝国。 然后在接下来的100年里传播到欧 洲其他地区。 这里有两幅早期法兰克骑兵的插 图，你可以看到他们必须在没有马 镫的情况下保持平衡。 这就限制了骑兵的装甲量。

paragraphs.en: And early on, horsemen only played a limited role in Frankish wars. But in the early 700s the Franks came up against the Umayyad cavalry who did posses stirrups. At the Battle of Tours Charles Martel's army was mostly infantry, but he learned from theArabs during the war. And during the reign of Charles Martel, Pepin the Short and Charlemagne, horsemen with stirrups became increasingly important. By the 9th and 10th century, the armored horseman became a major part of the Carolingian army. Charles the Simple was able to use this type of army to defeat Rollo at the Battle of Chartes in 911.
paragraphs.zh: 早期，骑士在法兰克战争中只扮演 了有限的角色。 但是在 7 世纪早期，法兰克人遇到 了拥有马镫的倭马亚骑兵。 在图尔战役中，查理·马特尔的军 队主要是步兵，但他在战争中向阿 拉伯人学习。 在查理·马特尔统治时期，佩宾和 查理曼大帝的马镫骑士变得越来 越重要。 到了 9 世纪和 10 世纪，披甲骑士 成为卡洛林王朝军队的重要组成 部分。 查理一世在911 年的Chartes 战役 中使用这种军队打败了 Rollo。

paragraphs.en: The greater emphasis on cavalry also changed other things. In the post-Roman period most warriors used round wooden shield with a metal dome in the center call a "shield boss", this is where your knuckles would be as you held the shield. This reconstruction shows you how such shields were held. But from the 10th to 13th century, this began to change and a new type of Kite-shaped shield was developed. Akite shield was held by looping your arms through straps, which left your shield hand free to hold the reins of a horse. So by 1050, a type of mounted warrior developed who used
paragraphs.zh: 对骑兵的重视也改变了其他一些 事情。在后罗马时代，大多数战士 使用中间有金属圆顶的木制圆形 盾牌，称为“盾牌栓套”。 这是你拿盾牌时指节的位置。 这个复原图展示了这些盾牌是如 何被持握的。 但从 10 世纪到 13 世纪，这种情况 开始改变，一种新型的风筝形盾牌 被开发出来。 风筝形护盾是用带子把你的手臂 套住，这样你控制护盾的手就可以 自由地拉住马的缰绳。 到 1050 年，一种骑在马上的武士 开始使用马镫，更重的盔甲和风筝

paragraphs.en: stirrups, heavier armor and the kite shield. This was the origin of the Medieval Knight. Over time, knights become more and more heavily armored, as you can see in these illustrations. Until by the 13th, 14th and 15th century you see the appearance of fully armored knights, which are such an iconic symbol of the Middle Ages. And you also see giant battles between heavily armored men, such as the Battle of Grunwald in 1410. Of course, a full suit of armor was quite expensive and heavy, and people needed to breed giant horses called "Destriers" who could support that much weight. Horses of
paragraphs.zh: 盾。 这就是中世纪骑士的起源。 随着时间的推移，骑士的装甲越来 越重，你可以在这些插图中看到。 直到 13 14 15 世纪，全副武装的 骑士才出现，这是中世纪的标志性 象征。 你也可以看到全副武装的士兵之 间的激烈战斗，比如 1410 年的格 伦瓦尔德战役。 当然，一套完整的盔甲是相当昂贵 和沉重的，人们需要培育巨大的马 称为“Destriers”，只有它可以支 持这么多的重量。这种体型的马保 养起来也很昂贵。

paragraphs.en: that size were also expensive to care for. And it also took years of training before you could fight effectively as a knight. So only the rich could afford to fight as armored knights. Common soldiers had to fight with much worse equipment. The cost of becoming a knight is another reason why the idea developed that it was the job of the nobility to fight. This helped develop the Medieval ideal of the three estates of society. But while all of these military changes were happening, the Carolingian empire was entering a period of crisis.
paragraphs.zh: 在你成为一名骑士之前，你还需要 多年的训练。 所以只有富人才有能力像盔甲骑 士那样战斗。普通士兵不得不使用 更差的装备作战。 成为骑士的高贵代价是“贵族的工 作就是战斗”这种想法形成的另一 个原因。 这有助于发展中世纪把社会划分 为三个阶层的思想。 但是当所有这些军事变革都在发 生的时候，卡洛林王朝却进入了一 个危机时期。

paragraphs.en: For over 70 years the empire was strong and stable, under the rule of Charlemagne and his son Louis the Pious. But when Louis the Pious died in 840, the empire was split between his three sons. From 843 - 870, these three kings and their sons fought frequently with each other. Apeace treaty was reached in 870. After that there were periods of peace and war, as well as some efforts to reunify the empire. Charlemagne's great-grandson, Charles the Fat, was even able to briefly reunify the Carolingian Empire for four years. But after his death in 888AD, the empire broke into 5 pieces and
paragraphs.zh: 70 多年来，在查理曼和他的儿子虔 诚路易的统治下，帝国强大而稳 定。 但当虔诚的路易于 840 年去世时， 帝国分裂给了他的三个儿子。 从 843 年到 870 年，这三位国王和 他们的儿子经常交战。 870 年达成了和平条约。在那之后 有一段时间的和平和战争，也做了 一些试图重新统一帝国的努力。 查理曼的曾孙，胖子查理，甚至在 四年的时间里短暂地统一了加洛 林王朝。 但在他于公元 888 年去世后，帝国 分裂成 5 块，再也没有统一过。

paragraphs.en: would never again be unified. The biggest piece of the empire was West Francia, which was inherited by Charlemagne's great-great-grandson Charles the Simple in 898. Charles ruled west Francia for 24 years from 898 - 922. And it was this Charles who defeated Rollo and the Battle of Chartes in 911. And it was this Charles who created the Duchy of Normandy through a deal with Rollo. But Charles the Simple was overthrown in 922 and spent the last seven years of his life in captivity. After Charles lost power, West Francia entered a period of civil war
paragraphs.zh: 帝国最大的一块领土是西法兰克， 它在 898 年由查理曼的曾曾孙“糊 涂王”查理三世继承。 查尔斯从 898 年至922 年统治西法 兰克 24 年。 正是这位查理在911年打败了罗洛 和沙特尔斯战役。 正是这位查理通过与罗洛的条约 建立了诺曼底公国。 但是，公元 922 年，“糊涂王”查 理 三世被推翻，在狱中度过了生 命的最后七年。 查尔斯失去权力后，西法兰克进入 了内战和权力斗争时期。

paragraphs.en: and power struggles. During this time the Kingdom had 7 Kings in 66 years. Eventually a member of the nobility named Hugh Capet seized power in 987. Historians consider Hugh Capet to be not a "King of the Franks" but the "First King of France" Capet was also the great-great-great-great-great-grands on of Charlemagne. I've included his ancestry above. When Hugh Capet came to power in 987, the King of France only controlled a tiny fraction of the country. On this map his territory is colored blue. As you can see, it was mainly
paragraphs.zh: 在这期间，王国在 66 年里有 7 位 国王。 最终，一个名叫于格卡佩的贵族成 员在 987 年夺取了政权。 历史学家认为于格卡佩不是“法兰 克国王”，而是“法国的第一位国 王” 卡佩也是查理曼大帝的曾曾曾曾 孙。我已经把他的祖先罗列在上面 了。 当于格卡佩在 987 年掌权时，法兰 克国王只控制了这个国家的一小 部分。在这张地图上，蓝色的部分 是他的领土。 如你所见，它主要局限于奥尔良，

paragraphs.en: limited to the land around the cities of Orleans, Poissy and Paris. There were 9 kings of France in-between Charlemagne and Hugh Capet during the years from 814 to 987, and as the map on the last slide made clear, the power of the King collapsed during that time, and the power of the Nobles grew. And so the nobles often became the real rulers in a place and fought wars among themselves. These nobles also were the ones who had to deal with outside raiders like the Vikings. So beginning in the 900s, these nobles began building fortresses to protect their territory. Sometimes they fortified old Roman villas, a practice which had been
paragraphs.zh: 普瓦西和巴黎周围的土地。 从 814 年到 987 年，介于查理曼和 于格卡佩之间，有 9 位国王。最后 一张幻灯片上的地图显示，国王的 权力在这段时间内瓦解了，贵族的 权力增加了。 所以贵族们经常成为一个地方真 正的统治者，他们之间还会进行战 争。这些贵族还必须对付像维京人 这样的外来入侵者。 所以从 9 世纪开始，贵族们开始建 造堡垒来保护他们的领土。 有时他们会加固古罗马别墅，这种 做法从 3 世纪就开始了。

paragraphs.en: ongoing since the 3rd century. But these nobles now started building new types of fortresses. First they would construct a man-made hill called a "motte" and then they would build a wooden tower on top of it called a "keep". Then a pallisade wall would connect the motte to a walled courtyard with buildings for the Lord's workers, this was called a "bailey". These wooden "Motte and Bailey" fortresses became the first medieval castles. Here is an artist's depiction of a Motte and Bailey castle. The wooden structures have all decomposed, but we find these mottes all across Europe, as you can
paragraphs.zh: 但是这些贵族现在开始建造新的 堡垒。 首先，他们会建造一座被称为“莫 特”的人工山，然后他们会在山顶 建造一座被称为“要塞”的木塔。 然后一堵栅栏墙将莫特和一个有 围墙的庭院连接起来，庭院里有为 领主的工人建造的建筑，这被称为 “贝利”。 这些木制的“莫特和贝利”堡垒成 为第一批中世纪城堡。 这是一位艺术家对 “莫特和贝利 城堡”的描绘。 木结构都已经腐烂了，但是我们在 整个欧洲都能找到莫特，你可以从 这两张荷兰和苏格兰的照片中看

paragraphs.en: see in these two pictures from the Netherlands and Scotland. Later on the “keep” might be rebuilt in stone if the Lord became wealthier. Some of these stone motte & bailey castles have survived until the present day, like Launceston Castle in England, which was built in 1068. After the keep, the curtain walls around the bailey might also be upgraded into stone walls. Towers might be built around the gate to create a gatehouse, since the gate is the weakest part of the wall. And eventually, by the 11th, 12th and 13th century the Medieval castle reached its mature phase. So when Hugh Capet became King
paragraphs.zh: 到。 如果领地主人往后富裕了，还会再 用石头重新建造要塞。 其中一些石头城堡一直保存到今 天，比如建于 1068 年的英国朗塞 斯顿城堡。 保护完成后，围城周围的幕墙也可 升级为石墙。 城楼可以建在大门周围，形成一个 门楼，因为大门是城墙最薄弱的部 分。 最终，在 11、12 和 13 世纪，这座 中世纪城堡达到了它的成熟期。 当于格卡佩在 987 年成为国王时，

paragraphs.en: in 987, that was the problem he faced. Lords all across France were building castles to assert their independent power. But Hugh Capet was a competent ruler, and he founded a dynasty of 15 Kings which would rule France for almost 350 years: The Capetians. In French history, the Capetians are the great kings of the MiddleAges. But when Charles V, the 15th Capetian King, died without an heir. This set up a power struggle which eventually led to the Hundred Years War, the last great war of the Middle Ages. But we are getting ahead of ourselves, lets return to Hugh Capet in 987. As I mentioned, the French lords
paragraphs.zh: 这就是他面临的问题。法国各地的 贵族都在建造城堡以维护他们的 独立权力。 但于格卡佩是个称职的统治者，他 建立了一个由 15 位国王组成的王 朝，统治法国近 350 年:卡佩王朝。 在法国历史上，卡佩家族在中世纪 诞生了一系列伟大的国王。 但 15 世纪，卡佩国王查理五世死 后没有继承人这就引发了一场权 力斗争最终导致了百年战争，中世 纪的最后一场伟大的战争。 我们聊的有点远了，让我们回到卡 佩在 987 年的作品中。 正如我提到的，法兰克领主在当时

paragraphs.en: were quite powerful at that time, and one of the most powerful was the Duke of Normandy. In theAutumn of 1066, William the Duke of Normandy launched an invasion of England. He faced the English King Harold Godwinson at the Battle of Hastings on October 14th, 1066. Although the English were outnumbered and had no cavalry, they held out well against William's army for most of the day. But then Harold was killed by an arrow to the eye, and his army panicked once they saw their king die. The English turned and ran, and the Norman cavalry chased them down and slaughtered them.
paragraphs.zh: 非常强大，其中最强大的是诺曼底 公爵。 1066 年秋，诺曼底公爵威廉入侵英 国。 1066 年 10 月 14 日，他在黑斯廷斯 战役中面对英格兰国王哈罗德·戈 德温森。 尽管英军寡不敌众，也没有骑兵， 但他们在大部分时间里都与威廉 的军队顽强抵抗。 但是后来哈罗德被箭射中了眼睛， 他的军队一看到他们的国王死去 就惊慌失措。 英军转身逃跑，诺曼骑兵乘胜追击 并开始了一场大屠杀。

paragraphs.en: Then he spent the autumn of 1066 securing control of England. And on Christmas Day, 1066 he was crowned King of England. William became known as William the Conqueror, and his descendants have ruled England until the present day. The current English monarch, Queen Elizabeth, is a 28th generation descendant of William the Conqueror. But for now, let us leave the Normans and French for now. For just as the Nations of England and France were beginning to form, the Muslim world was coming apart. And that is the subject of our next class.
paragraphs.zh: 1066 年秋，他成功控制了英格兰。 1066 年圣诞节，他被加冕为英格兰 国王。 威廉被称为“征服者威廉”，他的 后裔一直统治着英国直到今天。 英国现任君主伊丽莎白女王是征 服者威廉的第 28 代后裔。 但是现在，让我们暂时离开诺曼底 人和法兰西人。因为正当英法两国 开始形成时，穆斯林世界正在分崩 离析。 这是我们下节课的主题。

paragraphs.en: Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If so I have some recommendations for you. The first is the Bayeux Tapestry Museum in Bayeux France. Here you can see the original Bayeux Tapestry. This is an important artifact because it documents the Norman Conquest of England. It was commissioned by Odo of Bayeux, the half brother of William the Conqueror sometime around 1070, and it had survived until the present. It is such an important artifact that the French built a special museum for its display. Here is a close-up photo of a scene from the tapestry. But the entire
paragraphs.zh: 好了，各位，我希望你们喜欢这堂 课，并希望学到更多。如果是的话， 我有一些推荐给你。 第一个是法国贝叶的贝叶挂毯博 物馆。 在这里你可以看到原始的贝叶挂 毯。 这是一件重要的文物，因为它记载 了诺曼底人征服英格兰的历史。它 是 1070 年左右由征服者威廉同父 异母的兄弟贝叶共同建造的，一直 保存到现在。 它是如此重要的文物，以至于法国 人专门为它建立了一个博物馆。 这是挂毯上一个场景的特写照片。 但是整个作品有 70 米长，所以这

paragraphs.en: work is 70 meters long, so this is just one scene from the whole story. If you ever get a chance to go to northern France, go see the tapestry! The other remarkable site that I recommend you visit is Guedelon Castle in Treigny, France. Starting in 1998, a group of historians and archaeologists led by Michel Guyot, with support from the French government, began a project of experimental archaeology. Their goal is to build a real castle using only Medieval techniques over 25 years. By building the castle in the 21st century, we can learn by doing and better understand the past. Here is the castle in 2000. In 2005
paragraphs.zh: 只是整个故事中的一个场景。 如果你有机会去法国北部，去看看 挂毯吧! 另一个值得注意的景点是法国 从 1998 年开始，由米歇尔·圭约 (Michel Guyot)领导的一群历史学 家和考古学家，在法国政府的支持 下，开始了一项实验考古学的项 目。他们的目标是在 25 年内只用 中世纪的技术建造一座真正的城 堡。 通过建造 21 世纪的城堡，我们可 以在实践中学习，更好地了解过 去。 这是 2000 年的城堡。 在 2005 年的样子。

paragraphs.en: In 2009 In 2011 And here it is in 2015.As you can see they have made great progress, and the tourism to the site has allowed them to continue to fund the project. If you want to learn more about Guedelon castle, I recommend the BBC series, Secrets of the Castle. Finally, I once again recommend you visit the Metropolitan Museum ofArt, if ever you are in New York City. Because their Arms and Armor hall has a superb collection of suits of armor. That's all for today, thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 2009 年 2011 年 这是 2015 年。正如你所看到的， 他们已经取得了很大的进步，而且 游客使他们能够继续资助这个项 目。 如果你想了解更多关于 Guedelon 城堡的信息，我推荐 BBC 的《城堡 的秘密》。 最后，如果你有机会到纽约的话， 我再次建议你去参观大都会艺术 博物馆 。 因为他们的武器和盔甲厅有一套 极好的盔甲收藏。 今天就到这里，非常感谢!

titleZh: 第十四讲：陷入分裂|哈里发攻打哈里发

paragraphs.en: Hello, and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we will learn about the collapse of theAbbasid caliphate. We will focus on the years between 750 and 970AD. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. In 1919, the Irish poet William Butler Yeats wrote a poem that described the sense of gloom that he felt about the state of the world. Here is the first part: Turning and turning in the widening gyre The falcon cannot hear the falconer; Things fall apart; the center cannot hold; Mere anarchy is loosed upon the
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。今天我们将了解阿巴斯 哈里发的灭亡。 我们将把重点放在公元 750 年到 970 年之间。这是它在时间轴上的 样子。 1919 年，爱尔兰诗人威廉·巴特 写了一首诗，描述了他对世界现状 的悲观情绪。 这里是第一部分： 再度降临 飞转飞转翱翔在宽阔的旋涡中 猎鹰再也听不见主人的呼唤； 一切皆溃散；零散不堪； 全然混乱横流人世间； 阴红的血水在横流弥漫

paragraphs.en: world, The blood-dimmed tide is loosed, and everywhere The ceremony of innocence is drowned; The best lack all conviction, while the worst Are full of passionate intensity. This poem is one of the most quoted and referenced in English literature, and I use it here because those lines. "Things fall apart; the center cannot hold" does an excellent job of describing what happens to all empires eventually. That's because an empire is a system of extracting resources from many different people on the periphery and moving those resources to the
paragraphs.zh: 为无辜者的祭仪已沉陷； 上进者再无信念，堕落者更加卑劣 满腔激情在贲张。 这首诗是英国文学中被引用和引 用最多的诗之一，我在这里用它是 因为那些诗句。 一切皆溃散；零散不堪； 出色地描述了所有帝国最终的结 局。 这是因为帝国是一种这样的系统， 他从周边的不同人群中抽取资源 然后将这些资源转移到帝国中心 的首都和宫廷。

paragraphs.en: capital city and the court at the center of the empire. But the people on the periphery generally want to be free of this resource extraction.And the court at the center of the empire becomes filled with people plotting and scheming and backstabbing each other in order to control that treasure. So empires require energetic and powerful emperors if they are going to hold together. But the extreme wealth at the center often makes the emperors lazy and more concerned with luxury than with maintaining power. Additionally, the power gets passed down through families, so while the founder of an empire is often a brilliant, strong and capable person,
paragraphs.zh: 但处于边缘的人们通常希望摆脱 这种资源被攫取的状态。帝国中心 的宫廷充满了阴谋诡计的人，他们 为了控制这些资源，互相争斗。 因此，如果帝国要团结在一起，那 么他们需要精力充沛和强大的皇 帝。 但中央的巨大财富往往会让皇帝 变得懒惰，他们更关心奢侈，而不 是维护权力。 此外，权力是通过家族传承的，因 此，虽然一个帝国的创始人往往是 一个才华横溢、强大而有能力的 人，但他的子孙可能没有他的才

paragraphs.en: his sons and grandsons might not have his talents. Eventually these forces cause all empires to fragment. This same problem happened to the Abbasid empire. The firstAbbasid leader was As-Saffah, and he was the one who overthrew the Umayyad dynasty. As-Saffah was followed by Al-Mansur who was the greatest of theAbbasid caliphs. Al-Mansur moved the capital city to Baghdad and began theAbbasid golden age. TheAbbasid golden age continued for the next several caliphs. Al-Mahdi was followed by his sons Al-Hadi and then Harun al-Rashid.
paragraphs.zh: 能。 最终，这些原因导致了所有帝国的 分崩离析。 同样的问题也发生在阿巴斯帝国。 第 一 个 阿 巴 斯 领 导 人 是 As-Saffah，他推翻了倭马亚王朝。 As-Saffah 之后是阿巴斯王朝最伟 大的哈里发曼苏尔。 曼苏尔将首都迁往巴格达，开始了 阿巴斯的黄金时代。 阿巴斯王朝的黄金时代在后来的 几个哈里发时期得到了延续。 紧随其后的是他的儿子哈迪和拉 希德。

paragraphs.en: And during the reign of al-Rashid theAbbasids reached the height of their power. But the center cannot hold, and after Al-Rashid died, his sons al-Amin and al-Ma'mun fought each other in a civil war which lasted from 811 - 813. al-Ma'mun won the civil war, but small rebellions continued until the 830s. After al-Ma'mun died, power passed into the hands of his brother Al-Mu'tasim. Al-Mutasim was a powerful caliph, but he made the mistake of creating an army of Turkish soldiers to help him defeat his rivals. This Turkish army would make trouble for theAbbasids after his
paragraphs.zh: 在拉希德统治期间，阿巴斯王朝达 到了顶峰。 但却无法维持，拉希德死后，他的 儿子阿明和马蒙在811 年至813 年 进行了一场内战。 马蒙赢得了内战，但小规模的叛乱 一直持续到 830 年代。 在马蒙死后，权力移交给了他的兄 弟穆塔西姆。 穆塔西姆是一个强大的哈里发，但 他犯了一个错误，他创建了一支土 耳其士兵的军队来帮助他击败对 手。 这支土耳其军队在他死后会给阿 巴斯家族制造麻烦。

paragraphs.en: death. These new Turkish soldiers caused problems in the capital city, so Al-Mutasim set up a new capital city called Samarra just upriver from Baghdad. Here is a picture of the Great Mosque of Samarra today. Al-Mutasim's sonAl-Wathiq continued the policies of his father and not much changed under his rule. But afterAl-Wathiq died his brother Al-Mutawakkil came to power. Al-Mutawakkil had to rely more and more on the Turkish guard to put down rebellions and fight wars. But eventually his Turkish soldiers assassinated him.
paragraphs.zh: 这些新的土耳其士兵在首都制造 很多麻烦，所以穆塔西姆在巴格达 上游建立了一个新的首都萨马拉。 这是今天萨马拉大清真寺的照片。 穆塔西姆之子在位期间一直延续 了父亲的政策。 但在 Al-Wathiq 死后，他的兄弟 Al-Mutawakkil 不得不越来越多地 依靠土耳其卫队镇压叛乱和打仗。 但最终他的土耳其士兵暗杀了他。

paragraphs.en: After the assassination of Al-Mutawakkil, theAbbasids went through 5 caliphs in the 9 years from 861-870, as different military factions supported or assassinated different Caliphs, all of whom were brothers or cousins. This period in history is known as the "Anarchy at Samarra" and it greatly weakened theAbbasids. During theAnarchy, a second crisis further weakened theAbbasids. You see in the 7th, 8th and 9th century, theArabs in Mesopotamia had begun growing sugar. Originally sugar was an Indian crop, this is when it moved west to Iraq. Southern Mesopotamia is very marshy and warm.
paragraphs.zh: 在 Al-Mutawakkil 被暗杀之后，阿 巴斯在861-870 年的9年里经历了 5 个哈里发，不同的军事派别支持 或暗杀了不同的哈里发，他们都是 兄弟或表亲。 这一时期在历史上被称为“萨马拉 无政府状态”，它极大地削弱了阿 巴斯。 在无政府状态期间，第二次危机进 一步削弱了阿巴斯。 在 7、8、9 世纪，美索不达米亚的 阿拉伯人开始种植糖（甘蔗）。 糖（甘蔗 sugarcane）最初是印度 的一种作物，这是它西移到伊拉 克。 美索不达米亚南部是非常潮湿和 温暖的

paragraphs.en: And this is an excellent environment for growing sugar. But working on a sugar plantation is tough, dangerous and brutal work. So the Ummayads andAbbasids began importing slaves to work in these sugar plantations. These slaves came mainly from east Africa. TheArabs called theseAfrican slaves "the Zanj". The Quran permits the enslavement of non-Muslims. But you are supposed to free your slave if he or she converts to Islam. This rule however was widely ignored because the slave trade was too valuable. So more and more east Africans
paragraphs.zh: 这是种糖（甘蔗）的绝佳环境。 但是在甘蔗种植园工作是一项艰 苦、危险和残酷的工作。 于是倭马亚和阿巴斯开始进口奴 隶到蔗糖种植园工作。 这些奴隶主要来自东非。 阿拉伯人称这些非洲奴隶为" 《古兰经》允许奴役非穆斯林。但 是如果你的奴隶皈依伊斯兰教，你 就应该解放他/她。 然而，这条规则被广泛忽视，因为 奴隶贸易太有价值了。 因此，越来越多的东非人被奴役，

paragraphs.en: were enslaved and shipped to southern Mesopotamia to work in the sugar fields. There they lived terrible lives working in brutal conditions. Eventually, however, the Zanj rebelled, and this rebellion lasted from 869 until 883. The Zanj rebellion was eventually crushed, but it came right at the end of theAnarchy at Samarra. Together these crises meant that the empire was unstable for 22 years. TheAbbasid Caliphate survived both crises, but was permanently weakened. As a result of these internal conflicts, the edges of the empire began to break away.
paragraphs.zh: 并被运送到美索不达米亚南部的 蔗糖园工作。 他们在那里过着可怕的生活，在恶 劣的条件下工作。 然而，最终 Zanj 叛乱了，这场叛 乱从 869 年持续到 883 年。 Zanj 的叛乱最终被镇压，但它正好 发生在萨马拉无政府状态的末期。 这些危机加在一起，意味着帝国有 22 年不稳定的时期。 阿巴斯哈里发在两次危机中都幸 存了下来，但遭到了永久性的削 弱。 由于这些内部冲突，帝国的边疆开 始慢慢脱离。

paragraphs.en: First came Spain which broke away from theAbbasids during the transition from the Umayyad to Abbasid rule. As I explained in a previous lecture, the Umayyad princeAbdul Rahman I fled from Damascus to Spain and eventually became the leader of the Muslims in Spain. Under him the Umayyad territory in Spain became the Emirate of Cordoba. This was the first fracturing of the caliphate. Next came Morocco. In 788, the great-great-grandson ofAli, Idriss I arrived in Morocco. Under his leadership the Moroccans broke away from theAbbasids and formed the Shi'ite Idrisid dynasty. Next came Persia.
paragraphs.zh: 首先是西班牙，在倭马亚统治到阿 巴斯统治的过渡时期，西班牙脱离 了阿巴斯。 正如我在前一讲中所解释的那样， 阿卜杜勒·拉赫曼一世(Abdul Rahman I)王子从大马士革逃往西 班牙，最终成为西班牙穆斯林的领 袖。 在他的统治下，西班牙的倭马亚领 地成为科尔多瓦酋长国。这是哈里 发国的第一次分裂。 接下来是摩洛哥。788 年，阿里的 曾曾孙伊德里斯一世到达摩洛哥。 在他的领导下，摩洛哥人脱离了阿 巴斯，建立了什叶派伊迪里德王 朝。 接下来是波斯。

paragraphs.en: During the Civil Wars between the brothers al-Amin and al-Ma'um, the general Tahir ibn Husayn became a powerful ally of al-Ma'um and was appointed governor of Persia. Seeing that theAbbasids were growing weak from Civil war, Tahir declared independence in 822. Tahir died that same year, and the Abassids forced his son Talha to rejoin the empire, but his family the Tahirids ruled Persia from 822 - 873 and became increasingly independent from Baghdad. From 820 until 1186, numerous dynasties rose and fell in Persia. And theArabs lost control of the region. This period in history is called the Iranian Intermezzo and there were at
paragraphs.zh: 在阿明和马蒙兄弟内战期间，塔希 尔将军成为马蒙的强大盟友，并被 任命为波斯总督。 由于阿巴斯王朝在内战中日渐衰 弱，塔希尔于 822 年宣布独立。 同年塔希尔去世，阿巴斯家族强迫 他的儿子塔尔哈重回帝国的怀抱， 但是他所属的家族塔西里兹家族 从 822 年到 873 年继续统治波斯， 并逐渐脱离阿巴斯王朝的控制。 从 820 年到 1186 年，波斯有许多 朝代起落。阿拉伯人失去了对该地 区的控制。 这段时期在历史上被称为伊朗间 隔期。在这段时期至少有 7 个不同

paragraphs.en: least 7 different families that controlled Persian territory during this period. They were: The Tahirids, the Sajids, the Saffarids, the Samanids, the Buyids, the Sallarids and the Ghaznavids. Each of these dynasties rose and fell and fought each other, so we won’t get into the details in this class, but the big story was that an independent Persian-Muslim culture emerged at this time. Persia is one of the great centers of world civilization, but during the late 600s and 700s they were dominated by theArab Muslims. The Persians converted to Islam, but during the Persian Intermezzo, they re-asserted their culture and
paragraphs.zh: 的家族控制着波斯领土。 他们是:塔西里兹人，萨吉人，萨 法里德人，萨曼人，布依人，萨勒 里德人，加兹纳维人。 每一个朝代都有兴衰和斗争，所以 我们不会在这节课上详细讲，但是 这里要讲一个重要的事件就是一 个独立的波斯穆斯林文化在这个 时期出现。 波斯是世界文明的伟大中心之一， 但在六七世纪晚期，波斯被阿拉伯 穆斯林统治。 波斯人改信伊斯兰教，但在波斯间 隔期，他们重新主张他们的文化和 语言与阿拉伯语言和文化分离。

paragraphs.en: language as separate fromArab language and culture. The greatest figure of this Persian cultural resurgence was the poet Ferdowsi. He lived in the Persian city of Tus from 940 to 1020 during the Samanid and Ghaznavid dyansties. Ferdowsi was a Muslim, but he felt great loyalty to Persia's pre-Muslim culture, literature, and language. In order to protect and preserve his native Persian language he created the greatest work of Persian literature, The Shahnameh or "Book of Kings". Ferdowsi was a language patriot, so he made sure to use only Persian words in writing this book and to avoid the use of anyArabic words
paragraphs.zh: 波斯文化复兴的最伟大的人物是 诗人费尔多西。公元 940 年至 1020 年，在萨满和加兹纳维王朝统治时 期，他住在波斯城市图斯 。 费尔多西是穆斯林，但他对波斯前 穆斯林文化、文学和语言非常忠 诚。 为了保护和保存他的母语波斯语， 他创作了最伟大的波斯文学作品 ——《沙纳姆》或者叫“王者之书”。 费尔多西是一个语言爱国者，所以 他在写这本书的时候确保只使用 波斯语词汇，尽可能避免使用阿拉 伯语词汇。

paragraphs.en: when possible. The book tells the history of Persia from the mythical creation of the world until theArab conquests of Persia in the 600s. The book was written in the most beautiful Persian, because Ferdowsi was a genius, and it had a huge impact on Persian literature. It became the National epic of the Persian people and its style and language was studied, copied and revered for generation after generation. And this study and reading of the Shanameh played a large role in the preservation and resurgence of the Persian language. If you took Journey to the West, The Water Margin, Romance of the
paragraphs.zh: 这本书讲述了波斯的历史，从神话 创造的世界，直到七世纪阿拉伯征 服波斯。 这本书是用最美的波斯语写的，因 为费尔多西是个天才作家，它对波 斯文学产生了巨大的影响。 它成为波斯人民的民族史诗，其风 格和语言被一代又一代地研究、复 制和推崇。 这种对沙纳姆的研究和阅读对波 斯语的保存和复兴起了很大的作 用。 如果你把《西游记》、《水浒传》、《三 国演义》和《红楼梦》合为一本书，

paragraphs.en: Three Kingdoms and Dream of Red Mansions and combined them into one book, the importance of that book to the Chinese people would be about equal to the importance of the Shahnameh to the Persian people. In similar way, the reverence that Indian people have for the Ramayana, the Bhagavadgita and the Mahabarata. Or the reverence that Greek people have for the Illiad and the Odyssey. All of these are examples of national epics. But now we must turn our attention back to the disintegratingAbbasid empire. Let's look at how it fell apart, piece by piece. First Spain broke away in 756 to
paragraphs.zh: 那本书对中国人的重要性就相当 于《沙纳姆》对波斯人的重要性。 它类似于印度人对罗摩衍那、薄伽 梵歌和摩诃婆罗多的崇敬。 或者是希腊人对伊利亚德和奥德 赛的崇敬。 所有这些都是来自不同国家的史 诗。 但现在我们必须把注意力转回正 在瓦解的阿巴斯帝国。让我们看看 它是如何一点一点地分解的。 首先，西班牙于 756 年独立，成立

paragraphs.en: form the Emirate of Cordoba. Then Morocco broke off in 788 to form the Idrisid dynasty. NorthAfrica became autonomous under theAghlabid family in 800. Then came the civil war of 811-813, theAnarchy in Samarra from 861-870 and the Zanj revolt from 869-883. Persia became increasingly autonomous from Baghdad under the Tahirid Dynasty. And then Persia split away completely under the Saffarids in 876. Then in 893 a Shi'ite preacher named AbuAbdullah lead a rebellion among the Kautama Berbers of NorthAfrica.
paragraphs.zh: 了科尔多瓦酋长国。 后来摩洛哥在 788 年分裂，成立了 伊德里斯王朝。 800 年，北非在阿格拉布德家族的 统治下实现了自治。 然后是811-813年的内战，861-870 年萨马拉的无政府状态，869-883 年 Zanj 的叛乱。 在塔希里德王朝统治下，波斯逐渐 从巴格达独立出来。 然后波斯在876年在萨法里完全分 裂了。 然后在 893 年，一个名叫阿布·阿 卜杜拉的什叶派传教士在北非的 考塔马柏柏尔人中领导了一场叛 乱。

paragraphs.en: AbuAbdullah was joined by another leader named al-Mahdi Billah, who was a descendant ofAli. TogetherAbuAbdullah and al-Mahdi Billah founded the Fatimid dynasty, since al-Mahdi Billah claimed descent from Muhammad's daughter Fatima. Once al-Mahdi Billah was in control in NorthAfrica, he declared himself the caliph. In 929 the Emir of Cordoba,Abdul Rahman III also declared himself the Caliph. So by 929 there were 3 caliphs, in Spain, NorthAfrica and Mesopotamia. The Fatimids were quite successful and their conquest ofAbassid territory continued through the 900s.
paragraphs.zh: 和阿布·阿卜杜拉一起的还有阿里 的后代，另一位领导人马哈迪·比 拉。 阿布·阿卜杜拉和马哈迪·比拉一 起创建了法蒂玛王朝，因为马哈 迪·比拉自称是穆罕穆德女儿法蒂 玛的后代。 一旦马哈迪·比拉控制了北非，他 就宣布自己是哈里发。 公元 929 年，科尔多瓦的埃米尔阿 卜杜勒·拉赫曼三世也宣布自己为 哈里发。 到 929 年，在西班牙，北非和美索 不达米亚有了 3 个哈里发。 法蒂玛王朝非常成功，他们对阿巴 斯领土的征服一直持续到10世纪。

paragraphs.en: The Fatimids conquered North Africa by 909AD. They were in control of Sicily by 950. They conquered Egypt by 969AD. And then Palestine, Jordan and Syria fell the next year in 970. Most of these conquests occurred during the reign of the fourth Fatimid caliph: al-Mu‘izz. In 969AD, he founded a new city in Egypt called Cairo. This became the capital of the Fatimids until their collapse in 1250. Afterwards it became the capital of the Mamluk sultanate. Later it was the provincial capital under the Ottoman Turks. And then when Egypt declared
paragraphs.zh: 法蒂玛王朝在公元909年征服了北 非。 950 年，他们控制了西西里岛。 他们在公元 969 年征服了埃及。 第二年，巴勒斯坦，约旦和叙利亚 在 970 年沦陷。 这些征服大多发生在第四法蒂玛 哈里发统治期间:穆·伊兹。 公元 969 年，他在埃及建立了一座 名为开罗的新城市。直到 1250 年 法蒂玛王朝灭亡之前，这里一直是 法蒂玛王朝的首都。 后来它成为马穆鲁克苏丹国的首 都。它成为奥斯曼土耳其人统治下 的省会。 1805 年，埃及从土耳其人手中宣布

paragraphs.en: independence from the Turks in 1805 it became the capital of the country, which it remains to this day. So as you can see theAbbasid caliphate disintegrated from 811 to 969. And then in 945, a Persian family called the Buyids took control of Baghdad, and they selected the Caliph after that. So let's look at the big picture. This is what the Muslim world looked like in 750, united under the Umayyad Caliphate. But 220 years later in 970AD, the Muslim world had fractured into many different kingdoms. Divisions between Arabs, Berbers and Persians and divisions between
paragraphs.zh: 独立，成为土耳其的首都，一直延 续到今天。 大家可以看到，阿巴斯王朝从 811 年到 969 年瓦解。 945 年，一个叫布依德的波斯家族 控制了巴格达，在那之后的哈里发 基本都由他们推选。 让我们来看看整体情况。这是公元 750 年穆斯林世界的样子，在倭玛 亚王朝的统治下统一。 但 220 年后的公元 970 年，穆斯林 世界分裂成许多不同的王国。 阿拉伯人、柏柏尔人和波斯人之间 的分歧，逊尼派和什叶派之间的分

paragraphs.en: Sunni and Shiite, and finally competition for power and wealth had divided the Muslim World. Islamic civilization of course continued up until the present day, but the Muslim World, just like the Christian world, would be divided into many different countries and kingdoms. So now we have studied the disintegration of the Carolingians and theAbbasid empires. What about the other great Empire of Western Eurasia, the Byzantines, what happened to them in the time between the 7th and 11th centuries? Well please join me next time for that story. Okay that concludes today's lecture; if you want to learn more, I have a
paragraphs.zh: 歧，以及最终对权力和财富的争 夺，使穆斯林社会出现了分裂。 伊斯兰文明当然一直延续到今天， 但是穆斯林世界，就像基督教世界 一样，会被分成许多不同的国家和 王国。 现在我们研究了卡洛林王朝和阿 巴斯王朝的瓦解。 欧亚大陆西部的另一个帝国，拜占 庭，在 7 世纪到 11 世纪发生了什 么? 那么，下次请和我一起来听这个故 事。 好,今天的演讲到此结束;如果你 想了解更多，我有一个推荐给你。

paragraphs.en: recommendation for you. And that is get yourself a copy of Firdowsi's Shahnameh and read it for yourself. Like the Illiad and Odyssey, it is one of those great classics of World Literature. Okay that's all for now. Thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 那就是给你自己弄一份费尔多西 的《沙纳玛》来读。就像《伊利亚 德》和《奥德赛》一样，它也是世 界文学的经典之一。 好了，到此为止。非常感谢!

titleZh: 第十五讲：希腊人与俄罗斯人|是什么永久地伤害了基督教？

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, welcome back to our lecture series on World History, today we will learn about the Greeks and Russians. Our lesson will focus on the years from 867 to 1071. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. In our last class we saw how the Abbasid caliphate began falling apart until it had broken up into different Muslim kingdoms in Spain, NorthAfrica, Egypt, Mesopotamia and Persia. Never again would the entire Muslim world be unified into a single political state. This disintegration ofAbbasid power was a good thing for their
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座，今天我们将了解到的是 希腊人和俄罗斯人。 我们的课程将聚焦于 867 年至 1071 年，这是这段时间在我们时间 线上的位置。 在我们的上一讲中我们看到了阿 拔斯王朝是如何分崩离析，直到最 终变成散落在西班牙、北非、埃及、 美索不达米亚和波斯的诸多穆斯 林王国的。 穆斯林世界再未完全统一过。 阿拔斯王朝的解体对于他们的主 要敌人——拜占庭帝国——来说

paragraphs.en: chief enemy, the Byzantine Empire. As we saw in previous classes, the Byzantine Empire grew out of the Eastern Roman Empire, which survived the 5th century. And then the reign of Justinian the Great began in 527AD. Under Emperor Justinian, the Byzantines almost succeeded in re-uniting the old Roman Empire. Certainly by this time they were the greatest power in the Mediterranean World. But after Justinian, the empire grew weak from disease, new nomadic invaders and war with Persia. By 630 the Byzantines had lost some of the territory captured by Justinian.
paragraphs.zh: 是件好事。 正如我们已经在此前的课程中看 到过的，拜占庭帝国是在东罗马帝 国的基础上建立起来的，后者维持 到了公元五世纪。 查士丁尼大帝的统治从公元527年 开始。 在皇帝查士丁尼的统治下，拜占庭 几乎成功地重新统一了从前的罗 马帝国。 当然，在那个时候，他们是地中海 世界中最强大的力量。 但在查士丁尼之后，帝国因疫病、 新的游牧民族入侵者以及与波斯 的战争而不断衰弱下去。 到公元 630 年，拜占庭已经失去了 当时由查士丁尼夺取的大部分领 土。

paragraphs.en: But then theArab Muslim invasions of the 7th century really damaged Byzantine Power and caused them to lose half of their empire. To understand this better, look at a map of the Byzantine Empire in 550 as compared to 717. Over the 150 years from 717 to 867 the Byzantine Empire continued to shrink, as they lost control of Sicily, Corsica, Sardinia and almost all their Italian territory.Although they did strengthen their control of Greece at this time. But around 867, the situation started changing. TheAbbasids were weakening from rebellions, the Anarchy at Samarra and the Zanj Revolt. In 867 a man named Basil I became
paragraphs.zh: 但最后，公元七世纪阿拉伯穆斯林 的入侵真正重创了拜占庭的权柄， 导致他们失去了帝国的一半土地。 为了更好的理解这一点，来看看拜 占庭帝国在公元 550 年与公元 717 年版图的对比。 从 717 年到 867 年的 150 年间，拜 占庭帝国持续沉沦，先后失去了对 于西西里、科西嘉、撒丁岛以及几 乎全部意大利领土的控制。尽管同 时他们也增强了他们对于希腊的 控制。 但到867年左右，形势出现了变化。 阿巴斯王朝因内乱而衰弱下去，先 后发生了萨马拉无政府时期和津 芝叛乱。 867 年，巴希尔一世登基成为拜占

paragraphs.en: the emperor of the Byzantine empire. Basil was born a simple peasant, but rose up through Byzantine society as a horse groom and a wrestler. He came to the attention of powerful families in Constantinople. Eventually Basil became close to the Emperor, Michael III and was eventually made Michael's co-emperor. But when Michael began to pay attention to a new favorite, Basil overthrew Michael and became the Emperor himself. Basil founded a dynasty of emperors that historians call the Macedonian Dynasty. There were 17 emperors and empresses in this dynasty and they ruled the Byzantine Empire
paragraphs.zh: 庭帝国皇帝。 巴希尔生在一个普通的农民家庭， 但在拜占庭社会中以一个马夫和 摔角手的身份迅速升迁。他很快得 到了君士坦丁堡有权势的家族的 注意。 最终，巴希尔与皇帝米海尔三世变 得十分亲密，并最后成了与米海尔 共治的皇帝。 但当米海尔开始将注意力转向一 个新的受到宠幸的廷臣的时候，巴 希尔推翻了米海尔，自己做了皇 帝。 巴希尔创立了一个新的皇帝世系， 被历史学家称为马其顿王朝。这一 王朝共有 17 位皇帝及女皇，他们 从 867 年开始，统治拜占庭帝国直 到 1056 年。

paragraphs.en: from 867 to 1056. The Macedonian Dynasty was the strongest dynasty during the Middle Ages period of Byzantine history and they produced some of the greatest leaders. Under the Macedonians the Byzantine empire underwent a resurgence in power and culture. The borders of the empire expanded from this in 867 . To this in 1025 at the peak of the Macedonian Dynasty. The Greatest of the Macedonian Rulers was Basil II, we will return to him shortly.... ... But first we have to look at what was happening north of the Byzantines at this same time period.
paragraphs.zh: 马其顿王朝是拜占庭历史上中世 纪时期最强盛的王朝，其间诞生了 多位最伟大的君主。 在马其顿王朝的统治下，拜占庭帝 国发生了一次权力和文化方面的 复兴。 帝国疆界从 867 年起开始扩张。 这是 1025 年，马其顿王朝的巅峰 时期。 马其顿皇帝中最伟大的一位是巴 希尔二世，我们很快将说回到他。 不过首先我们需要先来看看同一 时期在拜占庭的北面发生了什么。

paragraphs.en: This area had been the core of the Hunnic empire from the 370s to the 450s. After the Hunnic empire collapsed, that part of the world was occupied by tribes of people speaking an Indo-European language called Slavic. The details of this period are hard to know because the Slavs did not have writing yet, so we have to rely on Roman records from outside of their culture. During this time period, we think the Slavs founded the city of Kiev around 482. So just 6 years after the fall of the Western Roman empire. The Slavic language eventually split into the west, south and east Slavic branches.
paragraphs.zh: 这一地区在370年到450年曾是匈 帝国的核心。 在匈帝国崩溃以后，这一地区被另 一些族群所占据，他们讲一种被称 为斯拉夫语的印欧语言。 有关这段时期的细节难于知晓，因 为当时的斯拉夫人还没有文字，所 以，我们只能依赖于罗马人的记 载，从外部了解他们的文化。 在这段时期，我们认为斯拉夫人在 482 年左右建造了基辅城。这在西 罗马帝国陷落后仅仅六年。 斯拉夫语最终分裂成为西斯拉夫、 南斯拉夫和东斯拉夫三个分支。

paragraphs.en: These different Slavic languages eventually developed into the modern languages of Russian, Ukrainian, Polish, Czech and Serbo-Croatian. The Southern Slavs began pushing into the Byzantine empire during the reign of Justinian. This is also the time that we get some of the earliest references to the Slavs as a people from Greek and Roman sources. Here's a more precise map of their migrations. Eventually they changed that part of the world from a Greek speaking region to a Slavic speaking region. This map shows you where these different groups of Slavs were living from the 600s to the 800s.
paragraphs.zh: 不同的斯拉夫语言最终又发展成 了不同的现代语言，包括俄语、乌 克兰语，波兰语，捷克语和塞尔维 亚-克罗地亚语。 南斯拉夫人在查士丁尼统治期间 开始迁入拜占庭帝国。 也是这段时间里，我们从希腊和罗 马的资源中找到了对于斯拉夫人 作为一个群体的最初提及。 这是一张对于他们迁徙的更加精 确的地图。 最终，他们改变了这个地区，把它 从一个说希腊语的地方变成了说 斯拉夫语的地方。 这张地图显示了这些不同的斯拉 夫人族群从公元7世纪到公元9世 纪生活的地区。

paragraphs.en: As you can see, from this map the Slavs were the neighbors of the Byzantine empire, and many Slavs lived within the empire’s borders. So in 863, the Byzantine Emperor Michael III sent two monks who were brothers north to convert the Slavs to Christianity. Their names were Cyril and Methodius. As Cyril and Methodius began their missionary work among the Slavs, they realized that their work would be more effective if the Bible was translated into Slavic. But the Slavs did not yet have a writing system for their language. So Cyril and Methodius created an alphabet for them.
paragraphs.zh: 正如你从这张地图上所看到的，斯 拉夫人是拜占庭帝国的邻居，还有 许多斯拉夫人生活在拜占庭帝国 境内。 所以，在 863 年，拜占庭帝国皇帝 米海尔三世派遣两名僧侣向北，去 向斯拉夫人传播基督教。 这两名僧侣是兄弟，他们是西里尔 和墨索迪乌斯。 西里尔和墨索迪乌斯开始在斯拉 夫人中间开始他们的传道工作，他 们意识到，如果圣经能够被翻译成 斯拉夫语，他们的工作效率将大大 提高。 但斯拉夫人还没有为他们的语言 建立起书写系统。 所以，西里尔和墨索迪乌斯为他们 创立了一个字母表。

paragraphs.en: They based this alphabet on the Greek alphabet, but made modifications so it would better suit the sounds of the Slavic languages. Today we call this alphabet the Cyrillic alphabet in honor of Cyril. The next period of Slavic history begins with the arrival of the Vikings in the 800s. As we learned in a previous lesson, the Norse Vikings had discovered a trade route that connected the Baltic Sea to the Black Sea via the rivers of Russia. These Norse came to control these rivers and set up settlements.As they reached the Black Sea, the Greek speaking Byzantines became aware of them and started calling them "the Varangians".
paragraphs.zh: 他们以希腊字母表为基础，但做了 一些调整，以使它能够更好地符合 斯拉夫语的发音。 今天我们将之称为西里尔字母，以 纪念西里尔。 斯拉夫人历史的下一个时期开始 于 9 世纪，维京人抵达。 正如我们已经在上一次课中了解 到的，诺斯维京人发现了一条商 路，能够从波罗的海经过俄罗斯境 内河流通往黑海。 这些诺斯人控制了这些河流并在 沿岸定居下来，当他们抵达黑海的 时候，说希腊语的拜占庭人发现了 他们，并开始称呼他们“瓦良格 人”。

paragraphs.en: The details of the Varangians are poorly understood, but we do know that they launched an attack on Constantinople in 860. The Theodosian Walls protected the main part of the city, but these Varangians did great damage to the suburbs of the city. Also around 860, groups of Slavic tribes living far to the north asked another Varangian leader named Rurik to be their King. Rurik accepted and became king of a country called the "Rus". Rurik chose the settlement of Novgorod as his capital, and this is the period when that city first enters the historical record. Rurik died in 879 and his throne passed to his son Igor.
paragraphs.zh: 有关瓦良格人的细节罕为人知，不 过我们知道，他们在 860 年开始攻 击君士坦丁堡。 狄奥多西城墙保护了城市的主要 部分，但这些瓦良格人给城市郊区 造成了重创。 也是在 860 年左右，生活在极北地 区的斯拉夫部落将另一位名叫留 里克的瓦良格领导人推举为自己 的国王。 留里克接受了，成为了这个被称为 “罗斯”的国家的国王。 留里克选择了诺夫哥罗德作为自 己的首都，这也是这个城市第一次 进入历史记载。 留里克死于 879 年，他的王位传给 了他的儿子伊戈尔。

paragraphs.en: But Igor was still very young so Igor's uncle Oleg became the leader of the Rus people from 879 to 912. Oleg led the Rus army south and the conquered the city of Kiev around 882. Kiev became the new capital of the Rus, and so the country that developed is called the "Kievan Rus" by historians. The Kievan Rus is important, because it would eventually become the country of Russia. In fact, Rurik founded a dynasty of Kings that historians call the "Rurikids" and they ruled Russia for 21 generations, from 862 until 1610. In this way, Rurik is to Russia what Charlemagne is to western Europe:
paragraphs.zh: 但当时伊戈尔还非常年轻，所以他 的叔叔奥列格在879年到912年成 为罗斯人的领导。 奥列格领导了罗斯军队挥军向南， 在 882 年左右征服了基辅城。 基辅成为新的罗斯首都，因此这个 国家被历史学家称为“基辅罗斯”。 基辅罗斯十分重要，因为它最终成 为了俄罗斯。 事实上，留里克创建了一个国王世 系，历史学家称为“留里克王朝”， 他们统治俄罗斯达到了 21 代，从 862 年直到 1610 年。 在这一意义上，留里克之于俄罗斯 正如查理曼之于西欧：伟大的始祖

paragraphs.en: the great ancestor king. Now that you know about Rurikids in The Kievan Rus and the Macedonian Dynasty in the Byzantine Empire, we can tie both stories together. In 976, Basil II became the Byzantine Emperor. He was the great-great grandson of Basil I, the founder of the Macedonian Dynasty. Here is Basil II's ancestry. I've also included Basil's younger sister Anna, because she is very important to this story. Meanwhile Vladimir I of Kiev was the ruler of the Kievan Rus, he was the great-grandson of Rurik. Here is Vladimir I's ancestry. At first Basil II and Vladimir were
paragraphs.zh: 君王。 现在你已经知道了基辅罗斯的留 里克王朝与拜占庭帝国的马其顿 王朝，现在我们可以把两边联系起 来了。 976 年，巴希尔二世成了拜占庭皇 帝，他是马其顿王朝创始人巴希尔 一世的重孙。 这是巴希尔二世的世系。我还把他 的妹妹安娜包括了进去，因为她对 于这个故事非常重要。 与此同时，基辅大公弗拉基米尔一 世正统治着基辅罗斯，他是留里克 的重孙。 这是弗拉基米尔一世的世系。 起初巴希尔二世与弗拉基米尔是

paragraphs.en: enemies, but both Kings admired things about the other. Basil admired the military strength of the Kievan Rus, and the Vladimir admired the culture and wealth of the Byzantines. In 987, one of Basil's generals named Bardas Phokas rebelled. Basil needed help defeating this rebellion. So he asked Vladimir for troops. Vladimir agreed, but he asked to marry Basil's sisterAnna as the price of his help. This seemed an impossible request, because never before had a barbarian king married a Byzantine princess. But Basil was smart enough not to
paragraphs.zh: 敌人，但互相敬仰。 巴希尔羡慕基辅罗斯军力强盛，弗 拉基米尔则倾慕拜占庭的文化和 财富。 987 年，巴希尔手下一位名叫 Bardas Phokas 的将军起兵叛乱。 巴希尔需要帮助镇压叛乱，因此他 向弗拉基米尔请求军队支援。 弗拉基米尔同意了，但作为他的帮 助的代价，他要求迎娶巴希尔的妹 妹安娜。 这看上去是个不可能的要求，因为 此前从未有蛮族国王迎娶过拜占 庭公主。 但巴希尔足够聪明，没有让骄傲阻

paragraphs.en: let pride get in the way of what needed to be done. He told Vladimir that he would accept the marriage proposal if Vladimir would convert to Christianity. Vladimir agreed. And he was baptized that year in 988AD. He marriedAnna immediately afterwards. Then Valdimir returned to Kiev and had all of his people convert to Christianity as part of a mass-baptism in the Dnieper river. This marks the moment in history when the Russians became Christian. It also accelerated the conversion of all the
paragraphs.zh: 碍他完成真正需要做的事。 他告诉弗拉基米尔，如果后者皈依 基督教，他就同意这门婚事。 弗拉基米尔同意了。 他在 988 年接受了洗礼。 他随后立即与安娜结了婚。 随后弗拉基米尔返回基辅，并要求 他的全部臣民皈依基督教，他们在 第聂伯河畔组织了大型洗礼。 这标志着俄罗斯人从此成为基督 徒的历史时刻。这也加速了整个说 斯拉夫语的族群皈依基督教的过 程，这一过程开始于 120 年以前西

paragraphs.en: Slavic-speaking people to the Christian religion, which had begun with the work of Cyril and Methodius 120 years before. Vladimir sent Basil 6,000 soldiers who formed a special unit called the Varangian guard. With their help Basil was able to crush Phocas's rebellion in 988. The Varangian guard became an elite unit within the Byzantine empire and would serve the emperor for centuries. This support from the Kievan Rus also allowed Basil to focus his attention on the Bulgarian Kingdom to his north. This war lasted from 1000 to 1018. Basil was a good general and he completely defeated the Bulgarians.
paragraphs.zh: 里尔和墨索迪乌斯当年的工作。 弗拉基米尔向巴希尔派出了六千 士兵，他们构成了一支特种部队， 称为瓦良格卫士。在他们的帮助 下，巴希尔得以在 988 年镇压了 Phocas 的叛乱。 在未来的几百年里，瓦良格卫士成 了拜占庭帝国内一支精英部队，一 直服务于皇帝。 来自基辅罗斯的支持还使得巴希 尔得以将注意力集中在他北方的 保加利亚王国之上。双方的战争从 1000 年持续到 1018 年。 巴希尔是一位良将，他彻底击败了 保加利亚人。

paragraphs.en: For these accomplishments, Basil II is revered by modern Greeks as a national hero, but despised by modern Bulgarians. By 1025 Basil II had pushed the frontiers of the Byzantine empire all the way to the Danube River for the first time in 400 years. Basil II died on December 15th, 1025, his own assessment of his rule can be summarized with the words on his tomb: "From the day that the King of Heaven called upon me to become the Emperor, the great overlord of the world, no one saw my spear lie idle. I stayed alert throughout my life and protected the children of the New Rome, valiantly campaigning both in the West and at the outposts of the East...O, man, seeing now my
paragraphs.zh: 由于他所取得的这些成就，巴希尔 二世被现代希腊人视为民族英雄， 但被现代保加利亚人所鄙视。 到 1025 年，巴希尔二世四百年来 第一次将拜占庭帝国的边境推到 了多瑙河。 巴希尔二世死于 1025 年 12 月 15 日，他自己对于他统治的评价在他 的墓志铭上得到了体现。 从上帝让我取得伟大帝国的土地 之统治权的那一刻起， 我的生命每时每刻都在保持着警 醒， 以保卫新罗马的孩子们， 勇敢地进军西方， 直捣东部边疆的最远端， 好心的人啊，

paragraphs.en: tomb here, reward me for my campaigns with your prayers" Unfortunately, after Basil II, his successors were much less capable. Under their leadership the empire began to weaken and a succession crisis began. From 1025 - 1055 there were constant power struggles inside the imperial court, which badly weakened the empire. The Byzantines went into decline. To better understand the effect of this decline, look at the Empire's borders in 1025: Compared to 1095. During this chaotic period, the Bishop of Constantinople got into a
paragraphs.zh: 为回报我的征服， 照看我的坟墓，用你们的祈祷报答 我吧！ 不幸的是，在巴希尔二世之后，他 的继承者们软弱无能。在他们的领 导下，帝国开始衰弱，继承危机开 始了。 从 1025 年到 1055 年，帝国宫廷中 发生了持续的权力斗争，这严重削 弱了帝国。 拜占庭走向了衰落。 为了更好地理解这种衰落，来看看 1025 年的帝国疆界。 与 1095 年对比。 在这段混乱时期内，君士坦丁堡大 主教与教皇发生了冲突（后者同时

paragraphs.en: fight with the Pope (who was also the Bishop of Rome). They started fighting about who was the head of the Catholic church. This fight escalated and eventually both men excommunicated each other. This mutual excommunication in 1054 is called "The Great Schism" and it marks the first great permanent division of Christianity. After 1054 the western Latin-speaking churches would be led by the Pope in Rome and become the Roman Catholic Church. The eastern Greek-speaking churches would be led by the Patriarch of Constantinople and become the Eastern Orthodox
paragraphs.zh: 也是罗马大主教）。 他们争夺的是天主教会的领导人 一职。 冲突逐渐升级，最终双方断绝了关 系。 这次发生在 1054 年的断交被称为 “大分裂”，这标志着基督教的第 一次永久分裂。 在 1054 年以后，西方说拉丁语的 教会由教皇所统治，成为罗马天主 教会。 东方说希腊语的教会被君士坦丁 堡牧首所领导，成为东正教会。

paragraphs.en: Church. Here is a map of how the two sides became divided. Since the Slavs had been converted by Byzantine missionaries, they became part of the Eastern Orthodox Church. You can see this religious connection even today in the style of Roman catholic churches versus the style of Russian Orthodox churches and Greek orthodox churches. And you can see it in the dress of Roman catholic priests as compared to Russian Orthodox priests and Greek orthodox priests. The Great Schism hurt the Byzantines because the Pope began opposing them. With the support of the Pope,
paragraphs.zh: 这是双方分裂的示意图。由于斯拉 夫人接受的是拜占庭传道士的传 道，他们成了东正教会的一部分。 直到今天，你还可以在罗马天主教 堂的建筑样式，与俄罗斯东正教堂 以及希腊东正教堂的建筑式样上 看到这种宗教联系。 你们也可以从罗马天主教牧师的 着装上看到这一点，对比一下俄罗 斯东正教牧师与希腊正教牧师。 大分裂伤害了拜占庭，因为教皇开 始反对他们。 在教皇的支持下，诺曼底骑士开始

paragraphs.en: Norman knights began to seize Byzantine territory in southern Italy. Meanwhile, in the east a new powerful enemy emerged: the Seljuk Turks. In 1048 the Seljuk Turks started pushing into Byzantine territory in Anatolia. The power struggles that had followed Basil II's death finally ended in 1068 when a Byzantine Emperor named Romanos IV Diogenes became the ruler. Romanos was determined to stop the Turks from pushing any further into Byzantine territory. But despite his good intentions, Romanos would make mistakes that would lead to the one of the most famous military disasters in
