paragraphs.zh: 在意大利南部抢夺拜占庭的领土。 与此同时，东方出现了新的强大敌 人：塞尔柱土耳其人。 1048 年，塞尔柱土耳其人开始从安 纳托利亚进入拜占庭帝国的领土。 巴希尔二世死后爆发的权力斗争 在 1068 年结束，当时，一位名叫 罗曼诺斯·第欧根尼四世的拜占庭 皇帝成为了统治者。 罗曼诺斯决定要阻止土耳其人进 一步入侵拜占庭领土。 但尽管他的意图是好的，但他犯下 了一系列错误，最终造成了拜占庭 历史上最著名的军事灾难之一。

paragraphs.en: Byzantine History. This disaster would mark the beginning of the end for the Byzantine Empire, and it would have other effects that would change Europe forever. So please join me next time for that lesson. Thank you very much. Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If you do, I have two recommendations for you. The first is the 12 Byzantine Rulers podcast by Lars Brownworth. This is a great summary of the entire history of the Byzantine Empire through the lives of 12 of its most important emperors.
paragraphs.zh: 这场灾难标志着拜占庭帝国末日 的开端，它的其他影响还将永久地 改变欧洲。 所以请与我一起开始下一次课程。 谢谢。 好了，我希望你们喜欢这次课程并 希望了解更多，如果你们愿意的 话，我有两个推荐的资源。 首先是 Lars Brownworth 的播客 《拜占庭的 12 位统治者》(非官方 翻译)。这是通过 12 位拜占庭最重 要的皇帝的一生对整个拜占庭历 史做出的非常不错的总结。

paragraphs.en: My second recommendation is the much more detailed "The History of Byzantium" Podcast by Robin Pierson. 12 Byzantine Rulers is great if you just want a summary, and The History of Byzantium is good if you want to become an expert. That's all for now, thank you very much!
paragraphs.zh: 我的第二项推荐中有更多的细节， 这就是 Robin Pierson 的《拜占庭 帝国历史》 如果你们想看总结，《拜占庭的 12 位统治者》非常不错，如果你们想 要成为专家，《拜占庭帝国历史》 很好。 今天的课到此为止，非常感谢你 们！

titleZh: 第十六讲：第一次十字军东征|打开潘多拉魔盒

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we will learn about the First Crusade. The focus of this lesson will be on the 28 years from 1071 to 1099. This is what that looks like on our timeline. At the beginning of the 11th century, as the Muslim world was splintering apart a new power arose in the land just east of the Caspian sea. These were a group of Turks who had recently converted to Islam. They were led by a man named Seljuk Beg, and would name themselves after him, becoming known to history as the Seljuk Turks.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。今天我们将会了解到第 一次十字军东征。 这一讲我们将聚焦于 1071 到 1099 的 28 年，这是它在我们时间线上的 样子。 在 11 世纪初，当穆斯林世界分崩离 析之际，里海之东出现了一支新的 力量。 这就是新近皈依伊斯兰教的一个土 耳其部族，他们的领导人名叫塞尔 柱·贝格。未来他们领导人的名字 被用于给部落命名，因此在历史上 他们就被称为塞尔柱土耳其人。

paragraphs.en: Seljuk had a son named Mikail Beg, and Mikail had a son named Tughril Beg. The divided Muslim world was vulnerable to a conqueror like Tugril beg, and he led his people in a series of successful wars from 1038 -1063. This map shows you Seljuk territory in 1038. And here it is in 1063, the year Tughril died. In that same year, Tughril was succeeded by his nephew Alp Arslan. In 1068 a new Byzantine Emperor named Romanos IV Diogenes gained power. Romanos was determined to stop the Turks from pushing any further into Byzantine
paragraphs.zh: 塞尔柱的儿子名叫米哈伊尔·贝格， 米哈伊尔又有一个儿子，名叫图赫 里勒·贝格。 分裂的穆斯林世界无力抵抗如同图 赫里勒·贝格这样的征服者，他领 导着他的军队在 1038 到 1063 年间 取得了一系列战争的胜利。 这张地图向你们描绘了 1038 年的 塞尔柱土耳其人的领土。 这是 1063 年时，这一年图赫里勒去 世了。 同一年，图赫里勒的侄子阿尔普·阿 尔斯兰继承了他的王位。 1068 年，新的拜占庭皇帝罗曼诺 斯·第欧根尼四世赢得了权柄，罗 曼诺斯决定制止土耳其人进一步入 侵拜占庭领土。

paragraphs.en: territory. So Romanos organized a large army and met AlpArslan in battle near the town of Manzikert in 1071AD. But the battle was an utter disaster for the Byzantines. Their army was destroyed and Romanos himself was captured. After Manzikert, Byzantine power inAnatolia collapsed, and the heartland of the empire was conquered by the Turks. To understand the impact of this defeat, look at this map. This was the Byzantine Empire in 1068 before Manzikert. Six years after Manzikert, this was the borders of the Empire. The Byzantines lost control of
paragraphs.zh: 所以罗曼诺斯组织了一支庞大军 队，在公元 1071 年于曼齐克尔特附 近迎战了阿尔普·阿尔斯兰。 但这场战斗对于拜占庭人来说是一 场彻头彻尾的灾难，他们的军队被 摧毁了，罗曼诺斯本人也被俘。 在曼齐克尔特之战以后，拜占庭帝 国在安纳托利亚的控制权崩溃了， 他们帝国的心脏地带也被土耳其人 所征服。 为了理解这次战败的影响，来看看 这张地图，这是拜占庭帝国在 1068 年曼齐克尔特之战以前的版图。 曼齐克尔特之战以后六年，它的疆 界变成了这样。 拜占庭人失去了对安纳托利亚的控

paragraphs.en: Anatolia which had been the heartland of the empire for over 1000 years. The Battle of Manzikert is one of history's great turning points. Ever since Alexander the Great's conquests in 329 BC,Anatolia had been part of the Greek world. Its inhabitants spoke Greek and were part of Greek culture. In the Hellenistic and Roman era, Greek was the language that people spoke inAnatolia especially in the western parts. This map shows you where Greek was the dominant language in those years, as you can see, western Anatolia is a big part of that area. This map shows secondary languages of the Roman Empire
paragraphs.zh: 制，过去超过一千年里这一直是帝 国的心脏。 曼齐克尔特战役是历史的重大转折 点之一。 自从公元前 329 年被亚历山大大帝 征服以来，安纳托利亚一直是希腊 世界的一部分，它的居民说希腊语， 也是希腊文化的一部分。 在希腊化以及罗马时代，希腊语是 安纳托利亚，尤其是其西部，人们 使用的主要语言。 这张地图显示了希腊语在那些年里 的哪些地区拥有主导地位，正如你 所见，西安纳托利亚在其中占有很 大比重。 这张地图显示了罗马帝国内第二语 言的分布情况（除了拉丁语），如果

paragraphs.en: (aside from Latin) and if you look closely you can see that Greek was still the main language of Western Anatolia. AndAnatolia was the heartland of the Greek-speaking Byzantine Empire from the 300s until the 1070s, as you can see from these four maps. The empire expanded and it shrunk, butAnatolia was always the heartland. But now it was gone. After Manzikert,Anatolia became the heart of the Seljuk Turkish Empire. And of the later Ottoman Turkish Empire. As a result, the main language of
paragraphs.zh: 你细看的话，会发现希腊语仍然是 西安纳托利亚的主要语言。 从公元4世纪直到11世纪70年代， 安纳托利亚一直是拜占庭帝国希腊 语地区的心脏，正如你从这四张地 图上所见。 帝国扩张，帝国衰落，但安纳托利 亚始终位于心脏地带。 但现在这都过去了。 在曼齐克尔特以后，安纳托利亚成 了塞尔柱土耳其帝国的心脏。 也是随后奥斯曼土耳其帝国的核心 地带。 结果之一就是今天安纳托利亚的主

paragraphs.en: Anatolia today is Turkish. And today,Anatolia is the heartland of the country of Turkey. AlpArslan was succeeded by his son Malik-Shah I. Malik was Sultan for 20 years from 1072-1092. By the time he died, the Seljuk Empire was at the peak of its power, controlling most ofAnatolia, Canaan, Mesopotamia, Persia, parts ofArabia, and a large part of Central Asia. Manzikert was a disaster for the Byzantines, but to make matters worse, the Byzantines were also being attacked in the west by their fellow Christians. In 1000AD the Byzantines still controlled southern Italy.
paragraphs.zh: 要语言是土耳其语。 今天，安纳托利亚是土耳其这个国 家的心脏。 阿尔普·阿尔斯兰的继承人是他的 儿子马利克沙一世。 马利克做了二十年苏丹，从 1072 年直到 1092 年。到他死时，塞尔柱 帝国正在其巅峰期，控制了安纳托 利亚、迦南、美索不达米亚和波斯 的大部分，一部分阿拉伯和很大一 块中亚地区。 曼齐克尔特对拜占庭人来说是一场 灾难，使得事情更糟糕的是，他们 同时也在西边被他们的基督徒教友 所攻击。 公元 1000 年，拜占庭仍控制着意大 利南部。

paragraphs.en: But by 1084, all of this Italian territory had been captured by the Normans. In 1081, the Byzantines got their first effective emperor since Basil II, his name was Alexios I Komnenos. Alexios would need all of his intelligence and skill, because the empire was a mess. This map shows you the border of the Empire whenAlexios came to power in 1081. In 1095,Alexios sent a group of Ambassadors to meet with the Pope in Rome. They held a meeting at the Council of Piacenza from March 1st - March 7th.
paragraphs.zh: 但到 1084 年，全部意大利领土都落 入了诺曼人之手。 1081 年，拜占庭人迎来了他们自巴 希尔二世以来的首位有为君主，他 的名字是阿莱克修斯·科穆宁一世。 阿莱克修斯需要竭尽所能，因为帝 国如今一片混乱。 这张地图显示了阿莱克修斯 1081 年接掌皇位时的帝国疆界。这张地 图显示了阿莱克修斯 1081 年接掌 皇位时的帝国疆界。 1095 年，阿莱克修斯派遣了一个使 节团前往会见罗马教皇。 他们从 3 月 1 日到 7 日在皮亚琴察 议会举行了会见。

paragraphs.en: Alexios's message was simple: He asked why Christians were attacking his Empire? The Byzantine's were the only thing standing between the Muslim world and the rest of Christendom! InsteadAlexios asked the Pope, Urban II, for help retaking Byzantine lands inAnatolia. Alexios was hoping for some money or some mercenaries, but his request got the Pope thinking about something else entirely. So Pope Urban II called a great council at the French town of Clermont from the 18th to the 28th of November, 1095. On November 27th, he delivered a speech that would change the world forever.
paragraphs.zh: 阿莱克修斯的讯息十分简单：他要 问，为什么基督徒会攻击他的帝 国？拜占庭如今是穆斯林世界与其 他基督教地区之间唯一的屏障了！ 阿莱克修斯反过来向教皇厄本二世 要求援助，以重新夺回安纳托利亚 的前拜占庭领土。 阿莱克修斯希望得到一些钱和兵力 援助，但他的要求反而使得教皇开 始思考一些其他的东西。 所以，教皇厄本二世在法国城镇克 莱芒召集了一场盛大的宗教会议， 时间是从 1095 年 11 月 18 日到 28 日。 11 月 27 日，他发表了一次演讲， 这永远地改变了世界。

paragraphs.en: The Pope said these words. He called on Christian soldiers to go east. To aid their brother Christians and free Jerusalem from Muslim rule, and he offered a unique spiritual incentive. He said: "Whoever, for devotion alone, not to gain honor and money, goes to Jerusalem to liberate the Church of God, can substitute this journey for all penance." This was a brand new idea in Christianity. If you read the Bible, Jesus tell Christians again and again to be peaceful, to love their enemies, and to avoid violence. But by 1095, a doctrine had developed in the Church giving the Pope the power to forgive sins.
paragraphs.zh: 教皇说了这样一些话。他呼吁基督 徒战士们去东方，去援助他们的基 督徒兄弟们，将耶路撒冷从穆斯林 统治下解放出来，他还为此提供了 特殊的精神激励。他说： “任何人，只为了虔诚而不是为了 赢得荣誉或钱财的，前往耶路撒冷 去解放上帝的教会的，都可以用这 趟旅程取代所有的忏悔。” 这是基督教中的一个标志性的新提 法。 如果你读圣经的话，耶稣曾一再告 诉基督徒们要和平行事，要爱他们 的敌人，要避免暴力。 但到 1095 年，教会发展出了一条教 义，给予教皇以赦免人的罪的权力。

paragraphs.en: And so the Pope used this power to create a new concept in Christianity, a Holy War. The Pope thought this war would have 4 positive effects. 1) Instead of knights and lords fighting each other in Europe and causing destruction, he could get these violent men out of the continent and throw them against the Muslims. 2) Such a war might heal the great--schism between the Roman Catholic Church and Eastern Orthodox Church. 3) This war would increase the power of the Pope if people responded to his call. 4) Jerusalem had a powerful hold on the medieval Christian imagination.
paragraphs.zh: 所以，教皇使用这种权力创造出了 基督教中的一个新的概念：圣战。 教皇认为这场战争将有四个积极影 响。 1）让骑士与领主不再在欧洲彼此争 斗并造成损失，他现在可以让这些 暴力的人远离欧洲大陆，让他们去 攻打穆斯林。 2）这场战争将弥合罗马天主教会和 东正教会之间的大裂痕。 3）如果人们都响应他的召唤，战争 将增加教皇的权力。 4）耶路撒冷将成为中世纪基督徒关 注的焦点。

paragraphs.en: But even the Pope had no idea what an effect this pronouncement would have. When the Pope made his offer, his audience was exhilarated. They stood up and began cheering and shouting, Deus le volt, Deus le Volt, which is Latin for "God wills it". And then in a fit of passion people began ripping up their clothes and making crosses which they could sew onto their garments. Thousands of Lords, Knights and ordinary people vowed to travel to the Holy Land and fight for Christ. They identified themselves by wearing a Cross, and became known as the "Cruces Signatus" which is Latin for "Cross Sign" or "Those who wear the Cross".
paragraphs.zh: 但即使是教皇，也没有料到这次声 明会造成的影响。 在教皇发出他的邀请以后，他的观 众们狂喜不已。他们站在一起，开 始庆祝和高喊，Deus le volt, Deus le Volt，这是拉丁语的“上帝的旨 意”。 然后，在充满激情的情况下，人们 开始撕扯他们的衣服并制作他们可 以缝制在衣服上的十字架。 成千上万的地主、骑士和普通人发 誓要前往圣地，为基督而战。 他们通过穿戴十字架来辨识自己， 并因此被称为“Cruces Signatus”， 这是拉丁语版的“十字标记”或者 是“穿戴十字架的人”。

paragraphs.en: This is the origin of the English word "Crusader". The call to crusade was answered by several lords the next year in 1096. One group came from Northern France and Flanders, led by: Godfrey of Bouillon and his brother Baldwin Hugh of Vermandois (younger brother of the French King) Count Robert of Flanders Duke Robert of Normandy (son of William the Conqueror) and Stephen of Blois From Southern France Came Count Raymond of Toulouse and the Crusades spiritual leader the bishop Adhemar of Le Puy.
paragraphs.zh: 这就是英语词汇“十字军”的起源。 呼吁东征的号召在第二年，也就是 1096 年，得到了许多地主的响应。 其中的一个群体来自法国北部和法 兰德斯，它由布永的戈弗雷以及他 的兄弟鲍尔德温、法王弟弟韦尔芒 杜瓦伯爵于格一世、法兰德斯伯爵 罗伯特二世、“征服者威廉”的儿子 诺曼底公爵罗贝尔二世，以及布卢 瓦伯爵斯蒂芬二世等贵族领导。 从法兰西南部来的是图卢兹的雷蒙 特伯爵，和十字军的精神领袖勒皮 主教阿希马尔。

paragraphs.en: And from Southern Italy came Bohemond of Taranto and his nephew Tancred. This map shows where each Crusader prince came from in Europe. Together they led 7 different Crusader armies. Notice that the all came from French or Norman territory. These 7 armies travelled across Europe and met up at Constantinople in the summer of 1096. This was not what the Byzantine Emperor had wanted at all. Alexios Komeinos wanted some mercenaries and money to fight the Turks, instead he was now facing an
paragraphs.zh: 从意大利南部来的则是塔兰托的博 希蒙德和他的侄子坦克雷德。 这张地图显示了每一位十字军王公 们是从欧洲的哪个部分赶来的。 现在他们共领导着 7 支不同的十字 军军队。 请注意，所有人都来自法兰西或者 是诺曼人的领土。 这 7 支军队穿越欧洲，在 1096 年的 夏天于君士坦丁堡集结。 这完全不是拜占庭皇帝想要的东 西。 阿莱克修斯·科穆宁想要的是一些 雇佣兵和资金支持，以击退土耳其 人，而现在他面对的是一支六万人

paragraphs.en: army of 60,000 men, the largest force that Europe had assembled since the Roman Empire. But these armies were all led by their own princes. What if they changed their mind and attacked him instead of the Turks? Many of the Crusaders were Normans, and the Normans had been fighting the Byzantines in southern Italy for decades. The situation in Constantinople was tense, but Alexios eventually persuaded all of the princes to swear an oath of allegiance to him in 1096. In return he provided them with food and supplies. They did this reluctantly, and with mutual suspicion, but it was enough, andAlexios gave them
paragraphs.zh: 的军队，这是自罗马帝国以来，欧 洲曾集结起来的最庞大的军队。 但这些军队各为其主。如果他们改 变了心意，转而攻击他，而不是土 耳其人呢？ 十字军中的很多人是诺曼底人，而 诺曼底人在南意大利与拜占庭人已 经战斗了几十年。 君士坦丁堡形势紧张，但阿莱克修 斯最终劝服了所有王公，让他们在 1096 年宣誓与他结盟。作为回报， 他向他们提供了食物和补给。 后者不情愿地照做了，双方都对对 方充满怀疑，但这已经足够了，阿 莱克修斯允许他们通过水路进入安

paragraphs.en: transportation across the water into Anatolia. The Turks were not ready for the First Crusade, because the Seljuk Empire had splintered apart after the death of Malik Shah I in 1092. Here is what it looked like in 1092. And after that it broke apart into different regions governed by different Turkish princes called "Atabegs". Malik Shah's son KilijArslan I took theAnatolian territory. His nephew Radwan had taken Aleppo and another nephew Duqaq had taken Damascus. Another son Mahmud I took Persia. Still another son,Ahmad Sanjar
paragraphs.zh: 纳托利亚。 土耳其人对第一次十字军东征准备 不足，因为塞尔柱帝国在 1092 年马 利克沙一世死后陷入了分裂。 这是它 1092 年的样子。 在它分裂以后，不同的地区分由不 同的土耳其王公统治，它们被称为 马利克沙的儿子克里奇·阿尔斯兰 一世接管了安纳托利亚领土。 他的侄子拉德温接管了阿勒颇，另 一个侄子杜达克接管了大马士革。 另一个儿子马穆德一世接管了波 斯。 还有一个儿子，艾哈迈德·桑贾尔

paragraphs.en: took the land just south of the Caspian sea. While two other sons divided Mesopotamia. Barkiyaruk took the southern part and, Muhammad I took the northern part. So the Seljuks became divided just as the first Crusade began. As a result, the war began successfully with the Byzantines and Crusaders working together to re-capture the city of Nicea in the summer of 1097. The Crusaders wanted to push on towards Jerusalem, but the Byzantines wanted to stay behind and consolidate the victory, so the Byzantines and Crusaders split up.
paragraphs.zh: 接管了里海以南的土地。 另两个儿子则分割了美索不达米 亚。 巴尔基雅鲁克占领了南部，而穆哈 迈德一世占领了北部。 所以，塞尔柱人在第一次十字军东 征时已经四分五裂。 结果就是拜占庭人和十字军战士并 肩作战，在战争的开始时段，1097 年的夏天，重新夺回了尼西亚城。 十字军战士希望进一步推进到耶路 撒冷，但拜占庭人希望留在后方巩 固其胜利，拜占庭人和十字军于是 分道扬镳。

paragraphs.en: The Crusaders began their march acrossAnatolia. And they were surprised by Kilij Arslan's Turkish army at the Battle of Doryleum. It was a tough battle, but the Crusaders won. After the victory at Dorlyeum, the Crusaders pushed onwards. The rest of the march through Anatolia was difficult. After the Battle of Dorylaeum the Crusader army split up. Baldwin and his brother Tancred went off on their own and travelled east, while the main Crusader army struggled through the mountains of Cappadocia. Tancred rejoined the main army after that, but Baldwin continued
paragraphs.zh: 十字军开始其横穿安纳托利亚的行 军。 在多利留姆之战中，他们被克里 奇·阿尔斯兰的土耳其军队突袭了。 这是一场艰苦的战斗，但十字军赢 了。 在多利留姆的胜利以后，十字军继 续向前。 继续穿过安纳托利亚的行军十分困 难。 在多利留姆之战以后，十字军分裂 了。鲍尔德温和他的兄弟坦克雷德 自行向东，而十字军大部队继续艰 苦地横穿卡帕多西亚群山。 坦克雷德在之后重新汇入了大部队 之内，但鲍尔德温继续向东进入亚

paragraphs.en: east intoArmenia where the King of Edessa adopted him as his son, and Baldwin was made the count of Edessa, the first crusader state. Baldwin was more interested in ruling his new kingdom so he dropped out of the crusade. In October of 1097, the rest of the Crusader army reached Antioch, the last major stronghold between the Crusaders and Jerusalem. Here they put the city under siege. The siege was long and difficult and lasted from October of 1097 until June of 1098. Finally, in the middle of the night on June 3rd, 1098, Bohemond and 60 of his knights scaled a wall and opened the city gates as dawn broke. The Crusaders poured into the city
paragraphs.zh: 美尼亚，当地的埃德萨国王将他收 为养子，鲍尔德温做了埃德萨伯爵， 这就是第一个建立起来的十字军王 国。 鲍尔德温对于统治他的新王国兴趣 更大，所以他退出了十字军东征。 1097 年 10 月，十字军残部抵达安 提俄克，这是十字军与耶路撒冷之 间的最后一个主要堡垒。他们围困 了这座城。 围城漫长而艰苦，从 1097 年 10 月 持续到了 1098 年 6 月。 最终，在 1098 年 6 月 3 日午夜，博 希蒙德和他的 60 位骑士翻过了城 墙，在破晓时分打开了城门。 十字军涌入城内，屠杀了城中的穆

paragraphs.en: and massacred the Muslim population. The only survivors were those who could make it to the citadel. Then, on June 28th, the Crusaders fought a battle against a larger Turkish army sent to relieve Antioch. Although outnumbered the crusaders defeated the Turks. After their victory atAntioch, the crusaders continued their march south. Local rulers made peace with them instead of fighting, so they faced little opposition, until they reached Jerusalem on June 7th, 1099. Many of the men broke into tears upon seeing the city they had journeyed so long to reach.
paragraphs.zh: 斯林人口，仅有的幸存者是那些筑 起了堡垒的人。 然后，6 月 28 日，十字军与一支被 派来解救安提俄克的土耳其大军爆 发了战斗。 尽管对方人数远超己方，但十字军 战士击败了土耳其人。 在安提俄克大捷以后，十字军继续 挥军南下。地方统治者们选择了与 他们讲和，而不是作战，所以他们 没有遭遇多少抵抗，直到他们在 1099 年 6 月 7 日抵达了耶路撒冷。 在看到这座城市的时候许多人激动 得留下了泪水，他们经过了如此漫 长的旅程才得以抵达此处。

paragraphs.en: This map shows the path of the First Crusade as they approached Jerusalem. This is a good map, but it is a little inaccurate. It shows Jerusalem as belonging to the Seljuk Turks, but it had actually been recaptured by the Shiite Fatimids the year before in 1098. So it was the Fatimids, not the Turks who defended Jerusalem. The Siege of Jerusalem lasted from June 7th to July 15th, 1099. Afirst assault on June 13th had failed, and during the siege that followed the Crusaders ran low on food and water. But when resupply ships from Italy arrived they were able to use the wood from those ships to build
paragraphs.zh: 这张地图显示了第一次十字军东征 至耶路撒冷的路线。 这是一张很不错的地图，但稍有不 确之处。它显示耶路撒冷属于塞尔 柱土耳其人，但实际上，它在 1098 年以前已经被什叶派法蒂玛人重新 夺回。 所以，是法蒂玛人，而不是土耳其 人在包围耶路撒冷。 对耶路撒冷的围困从1099年6月7 日持续到 7 月 15 日。 6 月 13 日发起的第一次强攻失败 了，在围城期间，十字军战士的粮 食和饮水逐渐告罄。 但当补给船只从意大利抵达，他们 得以使用造船的木料来搭建围城的 高塔。

paragraphs.en: siege towers. And then they attacked Jerusalem from the south and northwest on July 14th. The southern tower was destroyed by the defenders, but the northwestern tower, led by Duke Godfrey, broke through. Once they captured the city, the crusaders committed a terrible massacre of the Jews and Muslims who had defended the city. We know this killing happened because of the accounts of contemporary Christian chroniclers who write openly about it. Here are two such quotes describing the killing around the Temple Mount. This Christian slaughter of Muslims and Jews have made the Crusades a
paragraphs.zh: 7 月 14 日，他们从城市南部和西北 部再次攻击耶路撒冷。 南方的高塔被守城战士摧毁，但由 戈弗雷公爵建造的西北方的塔突破 了防守。 一旦他们夺取了城市，十字军立即 对城中守城的犹太人与穆斯林发起 了残酷的大屠杀。 我们知道这些杀戮曾经发生过，要 感谢当时基督教编年史的撰写者们 公开写到了它。这是两段引文，描 述了圣殿山附近发生的杀戮。 基督徒对穆斯林和犹太人的屠杀， 致使十字军在超过九百年中一直是

paragraphs.en: source of historical bitterness from a Jewish and Muslim point of view for over 900 years. Additionally, this killing was done in the name of a religion whose founder preached non-violence and love of enemies. For this reason, the crusades have been a deeply controversial topic among Muslims, Jews and Christians ever since. I present this history in a matter of fact way, but honestly speaking the controversy around the crusades deserves more discussion than I have given it here. After the capture of Jerusalem, the Fatimids sent a relief army to re-capture Jerusalem, but the crusader scouts located them first
paragraphs.zh: 犹太人和穆斯林历史苦难的源头之 一。 除此之外，这些杀戮还是在宗教的 名义下进行的，这一宗教的创始人 崇尚的却是非暴力和对敌人的爱。 因此，自那时起十字军在穆斯林、 犹太人和基督徒之间一直是一个极 具争议的话题。 我是以一种事实方式来描述这段历 史的，但是诚实地说，围绕着十字 军的种种争议值得比我在这里所给 出的更多的讨论。 在夺取了耶路撒冷以后，法蒂玛王 朝派出了一支军队试图夺回耶路撒 冷，但是十字军探子首先发现了他 们，十字军因而得以出其不意地在

paragraphs.en: and the crusaders were able to catch this army by surprise and defeat it at the Battle ofAscalon onAugust 12th, 1099. After the victory at Ascalon most of the Crusaders returned home to Europe, having achieved their goals. They left behind them 4 crusader states, which survived for several decades. These were as follows: The County of Edessa, which lasted until 1150. The Principality ofAntioch which lasted until 1268. The County of Tripoli, which lasted until 1289. The Kingdom of Jerusalem which lasted until 1291. The Muslims would eventually
paragraphs.zh: 1099 年 8 月 12 日的阿什凯隆战役 中截击并击败了他们。 在阿什凯隆的胜利以后，目标已经 达到，大多数十字军战士返回了欧 洲的家乡。他们留下了四个十字军 王国，后者持续存在了几个世纪。 名单如下： 埃德萨伯国，延续至 1150 年。 安条克公国，延续至 1268 年。 的黎波里伯国，延续至 1289 年。 耶路撒冷王国，延续至 1291 年。 穆斯林最终征服了这些十字军王

paragraphs.en: conquer these Crusader Kingdoms, which in turn would prompt calls for a second, third and fourth crusade. The crusaders would also bring back knowledge of the Middle East and expose Western Europe to things like spices and sugar. Europe was changing, and entering a new phase of Medieval history which historians call "The High MiddleAges" and which includes the period from 1000 to 1300AD. Please join me next time as we learn all about that, and find out what happened to the crusader states. Thank you! I hope you enjoyed that lecture. If you did I have three recommendations for you.
paragraphs.zh: 国，这反过来引发了对于第二次、 第三次和第四次十字军东征的号 召。 十字军也向中东带去了知识，并使 得西欧得以接触到香料和糖。 欧洲正在发生变化，并进入中世纪 历史的一个新阶段，即历史学家所 谓“中世纪中期”，这包括了从公元 1000 年到 1300 年的时期。 请下一次继续和我一起，去了解这 段时间，了解在十字军王国当中发 生了些什么。 谢谢你们! 我希望你们喜欢这次课程。如果你 们喜欢的话，我有三个推荐的资源。

paragraphs.en: The first is the series on the first crusade by the Youtube channel, Extra Credit. The second is the series on the first crusade by the Youtube channel, Epic History TV. And the third is the book The First Crusade: 1096 - 1099 by David Nicolle and Christa Hook. I think illustrated picture books can make history really come alive, and this is a good one. Thank you all very much.
paragraphs.zh: 首先是关于第一次十字军东征的 Youtube 频道 Extra Credit 制作的 系列节目。 其次是 Youtube 频道 Epic History TV 制作的关于第一次十字军东征 的节目。 第三是一本书，题为《第一次十字 军东征：1096-1099 年》，作者是 我认为插图书籍能帮助历史真正地 鲜活起来，而且这是一部杰作。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第十七讲：中世纪鼎盛时期|英法世仇的起源

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lecture series on World History. Today we are going to talk about the High MiddleAges in Europe. This period of time was centered on the years between 1100 and 1300 AD. During these two centuries, Western Europe began to flourish. The chaos of the centuries that followed the Carolingian collapse had begun to ebb. The Viking Invasions from the north,Arab raids from the south, and Magyar raids from the east had all passed. Additionally, the climate
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。今天我们将讨论的是欧 洲中世纪中期。 这段时间是在公元 1100 年到 1300 年。 在这两个年份之间，西欧开始繁荣。 在加洛林王朝崩溃以后，数百年的 混乱时期开始走向终结。 维京人自北方发起的入侵，阿拉伯 人自南方发动的劫掠，以及马扎尔 人从东方进行的抢劫，都已经成为 过去。 除此之外，气候也在温度上发生了

paragraphs.en: experienced a minor shift in temperature and was about half a degree warmer than it had been in the centuries immediately after the fall of Rome. Scholars call this time the Medieval Warm Period. The winters were much more mild than they had been in the DarkAge cold period or the Little IceAge that followed. This made both farming and daily life easier all across Europe. At the same time, new farming methods increased productivity even more. During the DarkAges and Carolingian period, farmers had practiced a type of farming called slash and burn agriculture. First they would clear an area of
paragraphs.zh: 小小的变化，比之罗马陷落后的那 几个世纪大约温暖半摄氏度。 学者们讲这段时间称为中世纪温暖 时期。比起黑暗时代寒冷期以及接 下来的小冰河期，冬季变得温和许 多。 这使得在全欧洲范围内，农业和日 常生活都变得更容易了。 与此同时，新的农业方法也显著提 高了生产力。 在黑暗时期和加洛林时代，农民们 使用的是一种被称为刀耕火种的农 业方式。 首先，他们会在茂盛的多年生林中

paragraphs.en: old-growth forest. Here is a graphic I made to illustrate the point. First an area of forest would be chopped down and burned to clear the ground. Then this cleared land would be farmed, year after year. Eventually, this would exhaust the nutrients in the soil. So then the farmers would clear a new patch of forest and abandon the exhausted land. While they were farming that land, plants would begin growing back in the old field. Then once the second field was exhausted, a third field would be cleared.
paragraphs.zh: 清出一片区域。 这是一张我做的示意图，用来说明 这种情形。 首先，有一块林地会被砍倒，然后 点燃，以清理出地面。 然后这块被清理干净的土地会被种 上植物，年复一年。 最后，这将耗尽土壤中的养分。 所以，之后农民又会清理出一块新 的林地，放弃之前耗尽肥力的土地。 在他们耕种这块土地的时候，植物 就会在旧的土地上重新开始生长。 然后，当第二块土地肥力耗尽，就 再清理出第三块土地。

paragraphs.en: And that land would be farmed while the first and second fields recovered. Slash and Burn agriculture is simple and cheap, but it requires a lot of land and is very destructive for forests. Its also not very productive because it exhausts the soil quickly. In developing countries slash and burn agriculture is still a major cause of deforestation. After about 1000AD, a new better type of farming began to develop in Europe and replace slash and burn agriculture. This type is called the "Three Field System". In this system the farmers would grow wheat in one field, peas in another, and they would let a third field lie fallow and let their animals
paragraphs.zh: 在这块土地被耕种的时候，前两块 土地恢复肥力。 刀耕火种式农业简单便宜，但它要 求有很多土地，并且非常毁坏森林。 并且，它的生产效率也不太高，因 为它很快就会使土地耗尽肥力。 在发展中国家，刀耕火种式农业仍 然是森林毁坏的主要原因。 在大约公元 1000 年以后，一种新的 更好的农业方式开始在欧洲出现， 代替了刀耕火种式农业。这就是“三 田系统”。 在这一系统当中，农民会在一块田 地中种植小麦，另一块种豌豆，第 三块地休耕，并在上面放牧他们的 家畜。

paragraphs.en: graze on it. Then they would rotate their fields the next year. The peas would go to the field that had grown the wheat. Since peas are a legume they add nitrogen into the soil, this helps restore the nitrogen that the wheat had taken out. And then they would rotate again. After growing peas the field would lie fallow and the grazing animals would help replenish its fertility with their manure. This way the soil fertility would be restored by the time the farmer wanted to grow wheat there again. By the fourth year the cycle began again. This new system of farming doubled the amount of food that a farmer
paragraphs.zh: 然后，他们在第二年轮换土地。用 过去种小麦的田地种豌豆，豌豆是 一种豆类，它们会向土壤里排出氮， 这能帮助恢复土壤中被小麦抽干的 氮。 随后他们再次轮换土地。种植过豌 豆的土地休耕，上面放牧的家畜的 排泄物将帮助土地恢复肥力。 这样土壤将会在农民在上面再次种 植小麦之前恢复肥力。 第四年，循环再次开始。 新的农业系统使一个农民能够获得 的食物产量翻了一番，但这需要犁

paragraphs.en: could produce, but it required more plowing. Fortunately this was helped by the invention of the horse collar, which allowed Europeans to use horses to pull a plow. Since horses can pull a plow faster than oxen, they gradually replaced oxen across France and later across England. Windmills and water wheels were built all across Europe at this time. These machines were often used for labor-intensive tasks like grinding grain. As a result of these innovations and improvements in agriculture, the population and wealth of Europe increased in the years from 1000 to 1300. This led to the flourishing of the
paragraphs.zh: 更多的地。幸运的是，马颈轭的发 明帮助解决了这个问题，它使得欧 洲人可以用马拉犁。 因为马拉犁比牛快，他们逐渐用马 取代了牛，首先在法国，随后在英 国。 这一时间，整个欧洲都在建造风磨 和水车。 这些机器通常用于劳动密集型任 务，比如磨谷。 这些农业方面的创新和改进，使得 公元 1000 年到 1300 年期间，欧洲 人口和财富均有增长。 这带来了经济的繁荣，因此成为“中

paragraphs.en: economy that caused the "High MiddleAges". The population of Europe grew steadily in this period. It rose by 1/3 from around 60 million people in 1050 to nearly 80 million people by 1300. However, the organization of government and the economy was still unsophisticated by today's standards. The High MiddleAges marks the high-point of Feudalism. Under this system all land was theoretically owned by the King. He would distribute this land to his Barons, who would then distribute their land among their knights. The King, Barons, and Knights were all the landowners.At the bottom of the system were the peasants who did the actual work.
paragraphs.zh: 世纪中期”。 这一时期，欧洲人口稳定增长，从 1050 年的 6000 万人口增加到了 1300 年的 8000 万人口，增长了三 分之一。 然而以今天的标准看来，政府组织 和经济仍然如此粗放。中世纪中期 标志着封建制的高峰。 在这一系统下，理论上全部土地都 归属国王。他将他的土地分封给他 的王公们，后者再将他们的土地分 给他们的骑士。 国王、王公和骑士都是地主。在系 统的最下层是农民，他们从事着实 际工作。

paragraphs.en: This helps explain why Europeans thought of society as divided into those who pray, those who fight and those who work. Freed from having to actually work the land themselves, the Kings, Barons and Knight could collect the surplus from their peasants and focus their energy on politics and war. The entire system of Feudalism was held together by a network of relationships. In each case the relationship was defined by the relationship between a Lord and a Vassal. In life you could be involved in both sorts of relationships. For example, the Barons would be the Vassals of the Kings, but they
paragraphs.zh: 这帮助解释了为什么欧洲人会认为 社会分为教士、骑士和劳动者三个 阶层。 免于土地上的实际劳役以后，国王、 王公和骑士得以从他们的农民身上 收取租税，将他们的精力集中在政 治和战争上。 封建制的整个系统通过一个关系网 络而聚合在一起。在其中的每一个 例子当中，关系都被定义为一个领 主和一个臣仆。 一个人一辈子可能同时担任这两个 角色。 比如说，王公是国王的臣仆，但他 们是自己骑士的领主。

paragraphs.en: would be the Lords for their own Knights. And a Knight would be a Vassal of a Baron, but he would be the lord of the Peasants who worked his land. Theoretically, the Lord owned all the land and provided the land to his vassals. The lord also protected his vassals from attack by outside enemies. In return the vassals would provide the lord with resources such as soldiers, money, food or equipment. At the center of this system were promises that men swore to one another as either vassals or lords. These promises were called an "Oath of Fealty". The vassal would kneel in front of
paragraphs.zh: 骑士是王公的臣仆，但他同时也可 能是自己土地上为他耕作的那些农 民的领主。 理论上，领主拥有所有土地并向他 的臣仆提供土地，领主还保护他的 臣仆免受外敌攻击。 反过来，臣仆将向领主提供资源， 例如士兵、金钱、食物或设备。 这一系统的中心是一个人向另一个 人发下的誓言，要么作为臣仆，要 么作为领主。 这些承诺被称为“效忠誓言”。 臣仆将跪在他的领主面前，低下头，

paragraphs.en: his lord, bow his head and hold his hands together and present them to his lord. Then the lord would clasp the hands of his vassal in-between his own, and both men would swear a sacred oath. The lord would swear to protect his vassal and the vassal would swear to protect his lord. In fact, since Christians refer to God as "the Lord" this position of kneeling with clasped hands in front of you became the standard pose for Christian prayer in the MiddleAges. Earlier Christians in the Roman era had prayed with their hands held wide, palms outwards, in the "Oran Prayer Pose". Two men who we've already learned
paragraphs.zh: 双手合十，向他的领主举起手。 然后领主将他的臣仆之手扣在他自 己的双手之间，两个人都会发神圣 的誓言。 领主将发誓保护他的臣仆，臣仆也 发誓保护他的领主。 事实上，由于基督徒将上帝称为“我 主”，这种跪在他人面前双手合十的 姿势因此成为中世纪基督徒的标准 姿势。 更早之前，罗马时代的基督徒在祈 祷时使用的是双手分开、手掌向外 的姿势，所谓的“奥兰祈祷姿势”。 两个我们已经有所了解的人也处在

paragraphs.en: about were connected with a Lord and Vassal relationship, Charles the Simple and Rollo the Viking. In 911 Rollo agreed to become Charles' vassal in exchange for the Land of Normandy. This made the King of France the Lord to the Duke of Normandy. As we've already learned, Charles the Simple was overthrown 11 years later in 922. And so from 911 to 1066, there were 10 different Kings of France. During this same time, there were 7 Dukes of Normandy. By 1066, the King of France was Philip I and the Duke of Normandy was William I. But both men were bound to the
paragraphs.zh: 一组领主-臣仆关系当中，这就是糊 涂王查理和维京人罗洛。 911 年，罗洛同意成为查理的臣仆， 以交换诺曼底土地。 这使得法兰西国王成为了诺曼底公 爵的领主。 正如我们已经知道的，糊涂王查理 在 11 年后的 922 年被推翻了。 所以，从 911 年到 1066 年，共有 10 位法兰西国王。 与此同时，共有 7 位诺曼底公爵。 到 1066 年，法兰西国王是菲利普一 世，诺曼底公爵则是威廉一世。 但这两个人都被他们的先辈在 150

paragraphs.en: oath that their predecessors had made 150 years before: The Duke of Normandy was still the Vassal of the King of France. But in 1066, William I invaded England, as we've already learned. And he defeated the English King Harold Godwinson at the Battle of Hastings. And on Christmas Day, 1066, William was crowned King of England, and now history knows him as "William the Conqueror". And his direct descendants have been the Kings and Queens of England ever since. In the meantime, the power of the French King had been falling. The first Capetian King, Hugh Capet, had only controlled a tiny portion of
paragraphs.zh: 年前发下的誓言所约束：诺曼底公 爵依然是法兰西国王的臣仆。 但在 1066 年，正如我们已经知道的 那样，威廉一世入侵英格兰。 然后他在哈斯廷战役中击败了英格 兰国王哈罗德·葛温森。 在 1066 年圣诞节，威廉加冕为英格 兰国王，现在他被称为“征服者威 廉”。 他的直系后代自此成为英国的国王 和女王。 与此同时，法兰西国王的权力不断 削弱。第一任卡佩王朝国王于格·卡 佩，只控制了这个国家的一个很小 的部分。

paragraphs.en: the country. On this map you see that he only controlled the areas in blue. He was less powerful than many other French lords at the time. Four generations later the Capetians had increased their territory by a little, but now William the Conqueror controlled Normandy AND England. His power far exceeded that Phillip I, even though Phillip was his theoretical Lord. This connection and competition between the English and French Kings would lead to centuries of warfare. Now let's keep something else in mind. The Norman Conquest and the First Crusade happened at about the same time in history. In fact,
paragraphs.zh: 在这张地图上，你可以看见他仅有 的控制区被用蓝色标出。他比同一 时间其他法兰西领主都要虚弱。 四代以后，卡佩王朝控制的领地稍 有增加，但现在征服者威廉控制了 诺曼底与英格兰，他的权力远远超 过了菲利普一世，尽管理论上菲利 普仍是他的领主。 英王和法王之间的联系和竞争关系 将导致长达数百年的战争。 现在让我们记住这一点，对诺曼底 的征服和第一次十字军东征发生在 同一个历史时期，事实上，它们之 间只隔一代。

paragraphs.en: these two events were only a generation apart. And three of the Crusaders had very close connection to the Norman and French royal families: Stephen of Blois, Duke Robert and Hugh of Vermandois. Duke Robert was William the Conqueror's oldest son, and Stephen of Blois was married to his daughter Adela. Meanwhile, Hugh of Vermandois was the younger brother of the French King, Philip I. Hugh's motherAnne is interesting because she was actually Russian. Her grandfather was Vladimir the Great: the Russian King who converted the Kievan Rus to Christianity.
paragraphs.zh: 有三位十字军领袖与诺曼底以及法 兰西王族有非常密切的关系：布卢 瓦的斯蒂芬，罗伯特公爵和韦尔芒 杜瓦的于格。 罗伯特公爵是征服者威廉的长子， 布卢瓦的斯蒂芬则与他的女儿阿黛 拉结了婚。 与此同时，韦尔芒杜瓦的于格是法 王菲利普一世的弟弟。 于格的母亲安妮是个很有趣的人 物，因为她实际上是俄罗斯人。她 的祖父是弗拉基米尔大公，那位带 领基辅罗斯皈依基督教的俄罗斯国 王。

paragraphs.en: Anyway, this meant that the Crusaders had a strong influence on the English-Norman and French courts, because their own family members went to fight. So when, the Seljuk general Zengi attacked the Crusader state of Edessa and conquered it in 1144, the Kings of Europe were paying attention. In response, the Pope Eugene III called for a Second Crusade to re-take Edessa. This time two Kings responded to the call: Louis VII of France and Conrad III of Germany. In 1147 they launched the Second Crusade and travelled to the Holy Land. There they fought a series of battles,
paragraphs.zh: 无论如何，这意味着十字军对于英 格兰-诺曼底以及法兰西宫廷拥有 很强的影响力，因为正是他们自己 的家族成员在参与战斗。 所以，当塞尔柱将军赞吉在 1144 年攻击十字军王国之一埃德萨伯 国，并征服了它的时候，欧洲国王 们开始密切关注。 作为回应，教皇尤金三世号召了第 二次十字军东征，以夺回埃德萨。 这一次，有两位国王响应了召唤： 法王路易七世，和德意志国王康拉 德三世。 1147 年，他们发动了第二次十字军 东征，向圣地进发。 在那里他们进行了一系列战斗，但

paragraphs.en: but had less success than the first crusade. The Second Crusade ended in failure and both Kings had to go home in defeat. By the time the Crusaders arrived, Zengi had already died, so the Turkish forces were led by his son, Nur ad-Din. Once the crusaders had gone home, Nur ad Din turned his attention to Fatimid Egypt. Fatimid power was in steep decline at this point and Nur ad Din saw an opportunity, so he sent an army to invade Egypt in 1164. This army was led by the Seljuk general Shirkuh and his nephew, Saladin.
paragraphs.zh: 远不如第一次东征那样成功。 第二次十字军东征最终以两位国王 都败北回师结束。 在十字军抵达的时候，赞吉已经去 世，所以土耳其军队是由他的儿子 Nur ad-Din 领导的。 一旦十字军撤军，努尔丁就将注意 力转向了法蒂玛埃及。 法蒂玛王朝的权力在这个时候已经 被严重削弱，努尔丁发现了机会， 所以他在 1164 年派遣军队前去入 侵埃及。 军队由塞尔柱将军西里古和他的侄 子萨拉丁领导。

paragraphs.en: By 1167 Shirkuh and Saladin had defeated the Fatimids. Shirkuh died soon after, and Saladin seized control. He then abolished the Fatimid caliphate and founded theAyyubid Sultanate. In 1174 Saladin's formed lord, Nur ad-Din died, and in response Saladin invaded Syria. By 1181 Saladin had consolidated control of the Muslim territories in the Near East. He then turned his attention to the Crusader states. From 1182 until 1187 he fought a series of campaigns against the crusader states and defeated them. On July 4th, 1187 Saladin won a major battle against the Crusader army at the Battle of Hattin. He outmaneuvered the crusaders and
paragraphs.zh: 到 1167 年，西里古和萨拉丁击败了 法蒂玛王朝军队。 西里古不久后就去世了，萨拉丁接 过了权柄。他随即废除了法蒂玛哈 里发国，建立了阿尤布苏丹国。 1174 年，萨拉丁的旧领主努尔丁去 世了，作为回应，萨拉丁入侵了叙 利亚。 到 1181 年，萨拉丁已经巩固了对于 近东穆斯林领土的控制。他由此将 注意力转向了十字军各王国。 从 1182 到 1187 年，他发动了一系 列针对十字军王国的攻势，并击败 了它们。 1187 年 7 月 4 日，萨拉丁在哈丁战 役中赢得了对十字军军队的一次关 键胜利。他用谋略胜过了十字军并 切断了他们的水源供应。他的军队

paragraphs.en: cut off their water supply. His troops had water and the crusaders did not. It was a huge victory for Saladin. After the battle, Saladin accepted the surrender of the Crusader leaders and spared the life of their King, Guy of Lusignan. Then Saladin moved on Jerusalem and accepted its surrender on October 2nd, 1187. Unlike the Crusaders, his army committed no massacres when they captured the city. This combination of military skill and humanity has made Saladin something of a folk hero, especially among Muslims, and I think he is a very admirable historical figure. When word reached Europe that Jerusalem had fallen, the new Pope, Gregory VII called for a Third
paragraphs.zh: 有饮水，而十字军没有。这是萨拉 丁的一次巨大胜利。 在这次战役以后，萨拉丁接受了十 字军领导人的投降，并饶恕了其国 王、吕西尼昂的居伊的性命。 然后萨拉丁前往耶路撒冷，并在 1187 年 10 月 2 日接受了这座城市 的投降。与十字军不同，他的军队 在攻占这座城市时没有进行屠杀。 军事才能与人性慈悲的结合使得萨 拉丁成了一位民间英雄，尤其是在 穆斯林当中，我也认为他是一位非 常值得仰慕的历史人物。 当耶路撒冷陷落的消息传到欧洲， 新任教皇格里高利七世号召了第三 次十字军东征，意欲夺回耶路撒冷。

paragraphs.en: Crusade to take back Jerusalem. This time three European Kings answered the call: They were Richard I of England, Phillip II of France, and Frederick Barbarossa. But Frederick Barbarossa died on the way- he had a heart attack while crossing a river in his armor, fell off his horse and drowned. So command was left to Richard and Phillip who were rivals with each other. This discord between the leader probably doomed the efforts. By this point Saladin had conquered most of the Crusader states except for Tripoli andAntioch. The third Crusade began with the capture of Acre. But after that both the Muslims and Christians began to have serious difficulty. Philip left
paragraphs.zh: 这一次，三位欧洲国王响应了号召： 英王理查一世，法王菲利普二世， 以及红胡子腓特烈（神圣罗马帝国 皇帝腓特烈一世）。 但红胡子腓特烈在路上去世了—— 他在军队渡过一条河时突发心脏病 而摔下了马背，淹死了。 所以，指挥权落入了理查一世和菲 利普二世之手，二人互相为敌，领 导人之间的不和可能将毁灭这次行 动。 在当时，萨拉丁已经征服了十字军 王国的大部分地区，只除了的黎波 里和安提俄克。第三次十字军东征 在夺回阿克后开始，但在那以后， 穆斯林和基督徒都开始面临严重困 难。菲利普在夺回阿克以后离开， 只留下理查独自面对萨拉丁。

paragraphs.en: after the capture ofAcre leaving Richard alone to face Saladin. Eventually Richard and Saladin agreed to terms. The Muslims gave back some Crusader territory, but kept Jerusalem. However Christian pilgrims would be allowed to visit, and both sides agreed to a 3-year truce. Richard at this point was sick with scurvy and his brothers were taken advantage of his absence. So he returned to England soon after. And Saladin lived until 1193, and at his death, hisAyyubid Kingdom had become one of the greatest in the Muslim world, as well as the keepers of the Holy Cities of Mecca and Medina. Saladin was born in 1137 and when
paragraphs.zh: 最终，理查与萨拉丁达成了协议。 穆斯林归还了一些十字军的领土， 但保留了耶路撒冷。不过，基督徒 朝圣者们被允许拜访，双方还同意 了三年的休战期。 当时，理查患了坏血病，而他的兄 弟们利用了他不在国内的机会。所 以，他很快就返回英格兰了。 萨拉丁在 1193 年逝世，在他死时， 他的阿尤布王国已经成为穆斯林世 界中最大的国家，也是圣城麦加和 麦地那的控制者。 萨拉丁出生于 1137 年，死于 1193

paragraphs.en: he died in 1193, he was just 56 years old. But just as this great leader was leaving the historical stage, another leader was making his entrance. Because when Saladin was 25 years old, a baby was born thousands of miles to the east, in a land that neither he nor the crusaders knew much about. And yet this baby would grow up into the greatest conqueror in world history.And these people would be the greatest danger that the Christians and Muslims had ever faced. That baby was given the name Temujin at his birth in 1162, but history knows him better as Genghis Khan.
paragraphs.zh: 年，享年 56 岁。 不过，正当这位伟大的领导人离开 历史舞台之际，另一位领导人正在 登场。 这是因为，在萨拉丁 25 岁的那年， 几千英里以外的东方诞生了一个婴 儿，在当时，无论是萨拉丁还是十 字军，都还对那里知之甚少。 这个婴儿未来将长成世界历史上最 伟大的征服者。生活在那里的人也 将成为基督徒和穆斯林曾经面对过 的最大威胁。 在 1162 年他出生时，这个婴儿被取 名为铁木真，但历史将铭记他另一 个名字：成吉思汗。

paragraphs.en: So please join me next time as we learn about him, and about the Mongol Empire he created. Thank you all very much. I hope you enjoyed this lesson, and if you want to learn more I have some recommendations for you. The first are more books on the Crusades by Osprey publishing. The author in all cases is David Nicolle and the illustrators of Christa and Richard Hook. I also recommend this wonderful website which contains excellent maps of the ancient and medieval Mediterranean world. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 所以，请在下一次课时继续与我一 起去了解有关他以及他所建立的蒙 古帝国的事情。谢谢大家。 我希望你们喜欢这次课程，如果你 们想要了解更多，我这里有一些推 荐。 首先是 Osprey 出版社出版的许多 关于十字军东征的书，所有这些书 的作者都是 David Nicolle，插图 作者则是 Christa Hook 和 Richard 我也推荐这个超赞的网址，里面有 很多古代和中世纪地中海世界的非 常好的地图。 谢谢大家。

titleZh: 第十八讲：蒙古人|人类历史上最伟大的征服者

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone and welcome back to our lectures series on world history. Today we will be learning about the Mongol Empire. We will be focusing on the years between 1206 and 1294. Here is what that looks like on our timeline. In 1162 a baby boy was born to a Mongolian woman named Hoelun and a Mongol chief named Yesugei. This boy's name was Temujin. Over the next 44 years Temujin grew into a great leader and united the Mongolian tribes. In 1206 the last of his rivals was defeated and a great meeting of all the clans was held known as a "Kurultai."
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。今天我们将了解的是蒙 古帝国。 我们将聚焦于 1206 年到 1294 年之 间的这段时期。这是它在我们时间 线上的样子。 1162 年，一位名叫诃额仑的蒙古女 性诞下了一个男婴，孩子的父亲是 一位蒙古首领，名叫也速该。这个 男孩被取名为铁木真。 在接下来的 44 年里，铁木真将成长 为伟大的领袖，并统一蒙古诸部。 1206 年，他的最后一个对手也被击 败，各部召集了一次大会，这被称 为“忽林勒塔”。

paragraphs.en: At the 1206 Kurultai gave him a new title, Genghis (Chingus) Khan, which means "Universal Ruler". Like the Huns before them, the Mongolian skill with horses and archery made them the most powerful army in the world when united. Now Genghis would turn this army outwards and expand the borders of his empire. Here is a blank physical map of Eurasia. Now let's add the countries that existed in 1206. In 1206 the Mongols had two immediate neighbors. To the west were the Western Xia founded by the Tangut people.. To the east and south was the Jin Dynasty, founded by Manchurian
paragraphs.zh: 1206 年，这一大会授予了他一个新 的头衔，成吉思汗，意思是“天可 汗”。 正如之前的匈人一样，蒙古人的骑 射技能让他们一旦统一便拥有了全 世界最强大的军队。 现在，成吉思汗将挥军向外，扩张 其帝国。 这是一张欧亚大陆的地形图，现在 我们将会把 1206 年时存在的国家 添上去。 1206 年，蒙古人有两个近邻，西方 是党项人建立的西夏。 东南则是满族女真人建立的金朝。

paragraphs.en: Jurchens. South of the Jin was the Southern Song dynasty, the true Han China, and probably the most advanced, populous and wealthy country in the world. For now, Genghis planned to focus his efforts on the Western Xia and Jin dynasties. Genghis began with raids into Xia territory in from 1205 to 1209. When these went well, he launched a full-scale invasion of the Xia in 1209. The Xia emperor, LiAnquan, asked the Jin for help, but the Jin emperor, Wanyan Yongji, foolishly refused. The Mongols easily destroyed every Xia army sent against them in open
paragraphs.zh: 金朝以南是南宋王朝，纯正的汉人 中国，可能也是当时世界上最先进、 人口最多也最富有的国家。 到目前为止，成吉思汗决定将精力 集中在西夏和金朝上。 成吉思汗从 1205 年到 1209 年开始 侵袭西夏领土。 这些劫掠屡屡得手，他于是在 1209 年发动了对西夏的全面入侵。 西夏皇帝李安全向金朝求援，但金 国皇帝完颜永济愚蠢地拒绝了。 蒙古人轻易地在公开战斗中摧毁了 所有被派去阻击他们的西夏军队，

paragraphs.en: battle, but, like many nomadic people before them, they had difficulty in sieges. The Mongols had a hard time during the siege of Yinchuan and could not capture that city. However, the Mongols still controlled the Xia countryside, so the next year the two sides came to terms and the Xia agreed to become the vassals of the Mongols, and send them annual tribute. This was Genghis Khans first victory over a foreign power, and it taught him many lessons. Most importantly his army began to learn about siege-craft by capturing and hiring Chinese engineers, and using them to build siege engines. The next year the new Jin Emperor
paragraphs.zh: 不过，就像此前的许多游牧民族一 样，他们在围城战方面遭遇了一些 困难。 蒙古人在银川围城战中度过了一段 艰难时期，并且未能攻克此城。 然而，蒙古人仍然控制了西夏的边 陲地区，第二年，双方开始谈判， 西夏同意做蒙古人的臣仆，并每年 向他们纳贡。 这是成吉思汗对外部势力取得的第 一次胜利，这教会了他很多。 最重要的是，他的军队开始通过俘 虏或雇佣汉族工程师来学习围城技 巧，并利用他们来建造围城工具。 第二年，新的金朝皇帝完颜永济愚

paragraphs.en: Wanyan Yongji stupidly sent a delegation to the Mongols demanding their submission. His attitude can be summed up in a quote of his. "Our Empire is like the sea; yours is but a handful of sand ... How can we fear you?" This was a fatal mistake. Upon receiving the order to submit, Genghis turned south, spat on the ground, and rode north on his horse, leaving the Jin ambassadors in the dust. Then Genghis held another Kurultai in 1211 on the Kherlen River and the decision was made to invade the Jin. Genghis went off to pray to the God
paragraphs.zh: 蠢地向蒙古人派出了一个使团，要 求他们臣服。 他的态度可以在以下引文中得到总 结。 “我们的国家犹如大海，你们的不 过一握沙，我们怎么会怕你？” 这是一个致命的错误。 在收到要求臣服的命令以后，成吉 思汗“遽南面唾，即乘马北去”，将 金国使节留在了尘土之中。 成吉思汗在 1211 年的克鲁伦河畔 举行了另一次诸部大会，决定入侵 金国。 成吉思汗前往向长生天祈祷，在四

paragraphs.en: of the Eternal Sky, and after four days of prayer he returned to tell his people that God had promised them victory. In 1211 the Mongols invaded the Jin. From 1211 to 1214, the Mongols smashed the Jin army and pillaged the entire north China plain. This created hundreds of thousands of desperate refugees who began fleeing the Mongol armies. These refugees crowded the roads and made it even harder for the Jin army to maneuver. In 1214, the Jin general Hushahu murdered Emperor Wanyan Yongji and enthroned his nephew Xuanzong of Jin. When the Mongols reached
paragraphs.zh: 天的祈祷之后，他返回告诉他的部 下，长生天已经承诺了他们的胜利。 1211 年，蒙古侵金。 从 1211 年到 1214 年，蒙古人碾压 了金军并劫掠了整个中国北方平 原。由于试图逃离蒙古军队，制造 了成千上万流离失所的难民。 这些难民集聚路途，导致金军更难 机动调遣。 1214 年，金军将领胡沙虎刺杀了皇 帝完颜永济，并将自己的侄子立为 金宣宗。 在蒙古人抵达中都（今北京）之时，

paragraphs.en: Zhongdu (Which is now Beijing) the Jin presented Genghis with a large gift, and a Jurchen princess and agreed to become a tributary state of the Mongols. Genghis accepted this peace offering and withdrew. But then in 1215, the Jin court moved the capital from Zhongdu to Kaifeng, in order to get farther away from the Mongols. This was a clear sign that they were planning to continue the war, which enraged Genghis. So in 1215 the war resumed and the Jin were on the defensive for the rest of the conflict. Zhongdu fell to the Mongols on June 1st, 1215. The city was destroyed and its people were killed
paragraphs.zh: 金朝为成吉思汗送上了一份大礼 ----一位女真公主，并同意成为蒙 古的属国。 成吉思汗接受了这一求和的礼物， 退了兵。 但随后在 1215 年，为了更加远离蒙 古人，金朝都城自中都迁至开封。 这是一个明确信号，他们打算继续 战争，这惹怒了成吉思汗。 所以，在 1215 年，战火重燃，金朝 在余下的战争中都处于守势。 中都在1215年6月1日陷落于蒙古 人之手，城市遭到摧毁，居民要么 被杀，要么成为奴隶。随后蒙古人

paragraphs.en: or enslaved. Then the Mongols pushed further south. The war would last another 20 years, but it would end with the total destruction of the Jin in 1234. After the fall of Zhongdu, many Chinese were taken prisoners, and one was very important to history: Yelu Chucai. Yelu was a Chinese Confucian scholar who had worked for the Jin. He persuaded Genghis that he should tax the people not just kill them. Yelu argued that China can be conquered from the saddle, but it cannot be governed from the saddle. Yelu pointed out that with taxation and government, the Mongols would make more money over the
paragraphs.zh: 继续向南推进。 战争将持续二十年之久，并以金朝 在 1234 年的彻底覆灭为结局。 在中都陷落以后，许多中国人被掳 为囚犯，其中的一个将在历史上举 足轻重：耶律楚材。 耶律楚材是一位在金朝任职的中国 儒家学者，他劝说成吉思汗要对人 民课税，而不是杀掉他们。 耶律楚材说，中国可由马上得之， 但不可由马上治之。 耶律楚材指出，通过课税和建立政 府，蒙古人将能够得到更多钱财， 以供给远征军需。

paragraphs.en: long run. Genghis liked this new idea of taxation, and so Yelu may have saved millions of lives by changing his mind on the matter. In 1213, an old enemy of Genghis, the Naiman Khan Kuchlug fled west to the Qara Khitai empire, and then seized the throne through treachery. From his new power-base, Kuchlug began to organize against the Mongols. So in 1218, Genghis decided to destroy Kuchlug once and for all, and he sent his general Jebe west to do this. Jebe won a decisive victory against Kuchlug. And this expanded Mongol territory
paragraphs.zh: 成吉思汗很欣赏这个课税的新主 意，因此，耶律楚材通过在这个问 题上促使他改变心意而拯救了数百 万人的性命。 1213 年，成吉思汗的一位宿敌，乃 蛮可汗屈出律逃到西辽，然后通过 政变取得了皇位。 从他的新权力基础出发，屈出律开 始组织抵抗蒙古人。 所以，在 1218 年，成吉思汗决定一 举摧毁屈出律，他派出了他的将军 哲别为此西征。 哲别赢得了一场针对屈出律的决定 性胜利。 在各个方向上向外扩张的蒙古领土

paragraphs.en: all the way to the borders of the Khwarezmian Empire. The Khwarezmians were a Persian empire that had taken over Central Asia from the preceding Seljuk and Ghurid Empires. They were led by a King named Ala ad-Din Muhammad, who was a great conqueror in his own right. The Persians lived in great cities and became wealthy off of trade. Their cities had wonderful markets. So in 1218 Genghis Khan sent a convoy to the Khwarezmians looking for trade opportunities. The ambassadors were looking to establish a treaty of peace with the Persians. But the local Khwarezmian
paragraphs.zh: 已经触及了花剌子模帝国的边境。 花剌子模是一个波斯帝国，它从之 前的塞尔柱和古尔帝国手中接管了 中亚。 领导他们的国王名叫阿拉·丁·穆 罕默德，他本身就是一位伟大的征 服者。 波斯人生活在大城市当中并通过贸 易变得富有，他们的城中有极好的 市场。 所以，在 1218 年，成吉思汗派遣了 一个使团前往花剌子模，寻求贸易 机会。 使节试图与波斯人签订一个和平条 约。 但当地的花剌子模统治者相信，这

paragraphs.en: governor believed these Mongol traders to be spies, so he killed them and seized their goods. This killing of his emissaries enraged Genghis Khan who viewed it as a violation of the sacred law of hospitality. So in 1219 the Mongols invaded Persia. The Persians in 1219 were a highly urbanized people. They lived in great cities like Samarkand, Balkh, Bukhara, Merv and Nishapur. Normally the strong walls of these cities kept them safe from nomadic armies. But the Mongols were different. Ten years of war with the Chinese had taught them how to capture walled cities.
paragraphs.zh: 些蒙古商人是间谍，所以他杀掉了 他们，并没收了他们的货物。 使节被杀一事触怒了成吉思汗，他 将此视为对于好客的神圣法则的一 种触犯。 所以，在 1219 年，蒙古人入侵了波 斯。 1219 年的波斯人是一个高度城市 化的族群，他们生活在撒马尔罕， 巴尔赫，布哈拉，梅尔夫和尼沙布 尔等大城市当中。 通常，这些城市所拥有的坚固城墙 能够帮助他们抵御住游牧民族的军 队。 但蒙古人有所不同。与中国人的十 年战争教会了他们如何攻陷有高墙 的城池。

paragraphs.en: This war was devastating to Persian civilization. One by one their great cities were destroyed by the Mongols, and their people were massacred. The Mongols did more damage to Persian culture and civilization than any other conqueror in their history. The Mongols may have killed 1.7 million people, nearly a quarter of the whole population! By 1220 the Kwarezmian Empire had been destroyed and Persia was now part of the Mongol Empire. But the Western Xia had not sent troops to help in this war. So Genghis decided to return east and punish the Western Xia for not helping him. But at this point, his general Subatai
paragraphs.zh: 这次战争对于波斯文明是毁灭性 的，他们的大城市一个接一个地被 蒙古人摧毁，他们的人民则遭到屠 杀。 蒙古人对于波斯文化和文明所造成 的伤害比历史上任何其他征服者都 要更甚。 蒙古人可能杀掉了 170 万人，这是 当时他们总人口的接近四分之一！ 到 1220 年，花剌子模帝国已经被摧 毁，波斯如今成了蒙古帝国的一部 分，但西夏在这场战争当中仍然没 有派出军队帮忙。 所以，成吉思汗决定转向西方，为 西夏没有前来帮助他而惩罚他们。 但在那时，他的将军速不台建议他

paragraphs.en: suggested that they divide the army in half. Genghis would take one army back through centralAsia while Subatai would explore the land north of the Caspian sea. Genghis liked this idea and agreed. Genghis travelled through Afghanistan and Northern India on his way home, pillaging and conquering and adding that territory to the empire. In the meantime, his best generals, Subatai and Jebe, went north into Russia. Here's another map of all these campaigns. First Subatai's army moved into the land between the Black Sea and Caspain Sea.
paragraphs.zh: 们分兵为二，成吉思汗带领一支军 队途径中亚返回，速不台继续探索 里海以北。 成吉思汗喜欢这个提议，同意了。 成吉思汗取道阿富汗和北印度返 回，征伐并将这些领土并入了帝国 之内。 与此同时，他最好的将军速不台与 哲别北征俄罗斯。 这是关于这些军事行动的另一个地 图。 首先，速不台的军队进入了黑海和 里海之间的地区。

paragraphs.en: They defeated the Georgian army at the Battle of Khunan in September of 1222. Then Subatai moved into Russia. He defeated a large Russian army at the Battle of the Kalka River on May 31st, 1223. Even though he was outnumbered 4 to 1, Subatai used superior strategy and the skill of his troops to destroy the Russian forces. And he tortured the captured Russian princes by crushing them to death. Then Subutai went home. His mission was scouting not conquest. In 1226, the army had reunited, and Genghis destroyed the Xia as punishment for not helping him in his war against Persia.
paragraphs.zh: 他们在1222年9月的库南战役中击 败了格鲁吉亚军队。 然后，速不台进入俄罗斯。他在 1223 年 5 月 31 日的卡尔卡河战役 中击败了一支俄罗斯大军。 尽管敌人的兵力四倍于他，速不台 还是运用高超的策略和军事技巧摧 毁了俄罗斯军队。 然后，他残酷折磨了俘获的俄罗斯 王公，直到他们死去。 而后速不台返回了，他的任务是侦 察，不是征服。 1226 年，军队重新会合，成吉思汗 已经摧毁了西夏，作为他们没有在 他针对波斯的战争中给予援助的惩 罚。

paragraphs.en: Then inAugust of 1227, Genghis died. He had united the Mongols, created the most powerful army on earth, and built the world's largest empire. But like all men he was mortal. Genghis had four legitimate sons with his wives, but he did not choose the oldest son, Jochi, as his successor. Instead he chose his third son Ogedei, who he thought would do the best job. (To save space, I've left out the women on this family tree, except for Tolui's wife Sorkatani, because Tolui died young, and she guided her four sons to power later on.) The choice of Ogedei was Genghis Khan's final accomplishment, and there was a smooth transfer of power from Genghis to Ogedei.
paragraphs.zh: 然后在1227年8月，成吉思汗去世。 他统一了蒙古，创立了地球上最强 大的军队，然后建成了世界上最大 的帝国。但他也是凡人，凡人都会 死去。 成吉思汗与他的妻子们共有四个合 法的儿子，但他没有选择长子术赤 作为他的继承人，相反地，他选择 了他的第三个儿子窝阔台，认为他 最为能干。 （为了节省空间，我没有在家族树 上列入女性，除了托雷的夫人唆鲁 禾帖尼，因为托雷早夭，是她引领 着她的四个儿子在之后获得了权 力。） 选择窝阔台是成吉思汗最后的一项 功绩，从成吉思汗到窝阔台的权力 交接十分平顺。

paragraphs.en: Ogedei Khan ruled from 1229 to 1241, and under his rule the Mongols completed the conquest of the Xia and Jin, and then moved west, this time to conquer. Ogedei sent his two best generals, Jebe and Subatai, his son Guyuk and his nephew Batu west to lead that army. The Mongols then conquered the rest of Perisa, Georgia, the Caucasus, the Kipchaks and the Russians from 1227 to 1241. The war against Russia lasted from 1235 to 1241. The Russians fought bravely, but they never stood a chance. As you can see from this picture, the walls of their major cities like Kiev were made of wood.
paragraphs.zh: 窝阔台汗的统治从 1229 年持续到 了 1241 年，在他的统治下蒙古人完 成了对西夏和金的征服，并再次向 西，这一次是为了征服西方。 窝阔台将他的两位最好的将军哲别 和速不台，他的儿子贵由和他的侄 子巴图派去领导西征的军队。 蒙古人由此在 1227 年到 1241 年间 征服了波斯的剩余地方、格鲁吉亚、 高加索人、钦察人和俄罗斯人。 对俄罗斯的战争从 1235 年持续到 了 1241 年。 俄罗斯人英勇奋战，但他们丝毫没 有机会，正如你在这张图上所看到 的，他们的主要城池，比如基辅， 是木结构的。

paragraphs.en: But the Mongols had learned how to destroy the great stone walls of China and Persia. Their siege engines made short work of Russian defenses. The Greatest Russian city, Kiev, was itself destroyed in December of 1240. Next the Mongols pushed into Eastern Europe. They destroyed a Polish army at the Battle of Legnica onApril 9th, 1241. And then destroyed the Hungarian army two days later at the Battle of Mohi. After that, the Mongols planned to invade Western Europe the next year in 1242.
paragraphs.zh: 但蒙古人现在已经知道如何摧毁中 国和波斯的宏伟的石头城墙了。 他们的攻城工具使得俄罗斯守卫相 形见绌。 伟大的罗斯城市基辅在 1240 年 12 月被摧毁了。 接下来，蒙古人推进到了东欧。 他们在1241年4月9日的列格尼卡 战役中摧毁了一支波兰军队。 然后又在两天以后的莫西战役中摧 毁了匈牙利军队。 在那以后，蒙古人计划在 1242 年入 侵西欧。

paragraphs.en: The goal was to make it all the way to theAtlantic Ocean. But then word arrived that Ogedei had died. He was an alcoholic and he drank himself to death. So the invasion of Europe was called off. Subutai and Jebe west east to attend the Kurultai and choose the next great Khan. This took a long time, but after 4 years, the Mongols chose Ogedei's son Guyuk as his successor on August 24th, 1246. This was the empire when Guyuk became Khan. But Guyuk's reign was short, he died onApril 20th, 1248. It took three more years, but
paragraphs.zh: 他们的目标是竭尽全力前往大西 洋。 但随即消息传来，窝阔台死了。 他有酗酒的毛病，醉死了自己。 因此，对欧洲的入侵行动被叫停。 速不台和哲别转回东方，以参加诸 部大会并选出下一位大汗。 这花了很长时间，但在四年以后， 1246 年 8 月 24 日，蒙古人选择了 窝阔台的儿子贵由作为他的继承 人。 这是贵由成为可汗时的帝国。 但贵由的统治是短暂的，他死于 1248 年 4 月 20 日。 这又花了三年，但最终，蒙古人在

paragraphs.en: eventually the Mongols chose Mongke Khan as their leader in 1251. Mongke was the son of Tolui, Genghis's youngest son. Under Mongke, the Mongol war machine was able to get going again. But Mongke decided to attack the Middle East instead of Europe. In 1258 the Mongols captured Baghdad and destroyed the city, killing tens of thousands. The Mongols also destroyed the House of Wisdom and Baghdad's libraries, this is why the sack of Baghdad in 1258 marks the end of the Islamic golden age. And they killed the lastAbbasid
paragraphs.zh: 1251 年选择了蒙哥作为他们的领 导。 蒙哥是成吉思汗幼子托雷的儿子。 在蒙哥的领导下，蒙古战车得以再 次转起来。 但是蒙哥决定，要攻打中东，而不 是欧洲。 1258 年，蒙古人攻占巴格达，并摧 毁了这个城市，杀掉了成千上万的 人。 蒙古人还摧毁了智慧之殿和巴格达 图书馆，这就是为什么 1258 年巴格 达的陷落成了伊斯兰黄金时代终结 的标志。 他们还杀了阿巴斯王朝的最后一个

paragraphs.en: caliph. The Mongols also destroyed the Ayyubid Sultanate that Saladin had founded and made it as far as Gaza, the border of Egypt. But then Hulagu received word that Mongke had died onAugust 11th, 1259. So Hulagu left a small force behind and returned to Mongolia to attend the Kurultai. The next year an Egyptian army turned up in force and defeated the Mongols at the Battle of 'Ain Jalut on November 6th, 1260. The victorious Egyptians then overthrew the last remnants of the Ayyubids and founded the Mamluk Sultanate.
paragraphs.zh: 哈里发。 蒙古人还摧毁了萨拉丁所创建、一 度远及埃及边境加沙的阿尤布苏丹 国。 但随后，旭烈兀收到了蒙哥在 1259 年 8 月 11 日已经死亡的消息。 所以，旭烈兀将一小支部队留了下 来，自己返回蒙古以参加诸部大会。 第二年，一支埃及军队起兵，在 1260 年 11 月 6 日的阿音札鲁特战 役中击败了蒙古人。 取得了胜利的埃及人随后推翻了阿 尤布王朝的剩余部分，并创立了马 穆鲁克苏丹国。

paragraphs.en: Meanwhile back in Mongolia, the Kurultai selected Kublai Khan in May of 1260. Kublai was also a son of Tolui and the younger brother of Mongke. Kublai was less interested in expanding west into Egypt or Europe, for him the really important prize was the richest country in the world, the Southern Song. The Mongols and Song had been fighting each other since 1235: this was their hardest war and required the most troops. This Mongol-Song war is also significant because it is probably the first war in history with gunpowder on both sides. This final stage of the Mongol Song War lasted from 1260 to 1276, and
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，让我们回到蒙古，诸部 大会在 1260 年 5 月选举了忽必烈 汗。 忽必烈也是托雷的儿子，是蒙哥的 弟弟。 忽必烈对于西进埃及或者欧洲殊少 兴趣，对他来说，真正重要的目标 是世界上最富庶的国家，南宋。 蒙古和南宋自 1235 年起就在交战， 这是他们最为艰苦的战争，需要最 多的军队。 蒙古与宋的战争的重要性还在于它 也可能是历史上第一次双方都使用 火枪的战争。 蒙宋战争的最后一个阶段从 1260 年持续到了 1276 年，仅它自己就值

paragraphs.en: deserves it own series of lessons. But in short, there are two key battles to know about: The first battle was the siege of Xiangyang which controlled access the the Yangtze river. (Today it is the city of Xiangyang in Hubei province.) Xiangyang was a mighty fortress-city and the siege lasted for 6 years from 1267 to 1273, but the Mongols took it in the end. Once Xiangyang fell, the rest of the Song Empire quickly collapsed. The final battle was the Battle of Yamen on March 19th, 1279. Yamen, is located near modern-day Jiangmen city in Guangdong province.
paragraphs.zh: 得用一个系列的课程来讲。 但简而言之，有两场战争值得一说： 首先是襄阳围城战，这座城池控制 了长江水路。（也就是今天的湖北省 襄阳市。）(音频更正，今天还是叫 襄阳) 襄阳是全副武装的堡垒城市，围城 持续了六年之久，从1267年到1273 年，但最终蒙古人夺下了这个城市。 在襄阳陷落以后，宋王朝的其余部 分很快崩溃了。 最后一战是 1279 年 3 月 19 日的崖 门海战。 崖门位于今天广东省江门市。

paragraphs.en: It was a giant naval battle on the Pearl River, a heroic last stand by the Song forces trying to protect the boy emperor Zhao Bing. The Song lost this battle and Zhao Bing, drowned during the fighting. His death marks the end of the Song Dynasty. After the fall of the Song, Kublai founded a new Chinese dynasty called the Yuan, with himself as its first emperor. But just as the Mongols won their greatest victory, their empire was beginning to fragment. It was just too big for one man to control. Although the Mongols all acknowledged Kublai as the supreme Khan, in reality his cousins had each taken a piece of it for
paragraphs.zh: 这是一场发生在珠江上的大型水 战，是宋军为了保护娃娃皇帝赵昺 而展开的英勇抵抗。 宋朝输掉了这场战役，赵昺则在战 斗中落水淹死。 他的死标志着宋王朝的终结。 在宋朝覆灭以后，忽必烈创立了一 个新的中国王朝，号为元，他自己 为第一位皇帝。 但就在蒙古人赢得他们最大的胜利 之际，他们的帝国开始分崩离析。 它已经变得太大了，不可能由一个 人实现控制。 尽管蒙古人承认忽必烈是最高的可 汗，事实上他的表兄弟们各自据有 自己的领地。在 1294 年忽必烈死 后，分裂正式发生了。

paragraphs.en: themselves. This division became more formal with Kublai's death in 1294. The empire was divided into 4 pieces. Kublai ruled EastAsia, including Mongolia, China and Korea. This part is known to history as the Yuan dynasty. CentralAsia was known as the Chagatai Khanate. Persia and the Middle East became known as the Ilkhanate. And the Russian territory became known as the Golden Horde. Now before we end let's take a moment to appreciate the magnitude of the Mongol conquests in the 1200s.
paragraphs.zh: 帝国被分成了四个部分。 忽必烈统治包括蒙古、中国和朝鲜 在内的东亚，这个部分史称元朝。 中亚被称为察合台汗国。 波斯和中东成为伊尔汗国。 俄罗斯领土则变成了金帐汗国。 现在，在我们结束之前，我们花一 点时间来看看 13 世纪蒙古人所征 服领土的规模。

paragraphs.en: Here is the empire in 1206 when Genghis became Great Khan. And 1296, the year of Kublai's death. Okay everyone, that's all for now. Please join me next time as we turn our attention back to Europe for our next lesson: "ACentury of Woe". I hope you enjoyed that lesson. If so, here is a recommendation for you. It is the podcast series Wrath of the Khans by Dan Carlin. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 这是 1206 年的帝国，当时成吉思汗 做了大汗。 1296 年，这是忽必烈去世的那一 年。 好了，这就是今天的全部内容，下 一次请继续和我一起，将注意力转 回到欧洲，就是我们的下一次课程： 灾难的世纪。 我希望你们喜欢这次课。如果我猜 对了的话，这是给你们的一个推荐。 这就是 Dan Carlin 的系列播客《大 汗的怒火》(非官方翻译)。谢谢大 家。

paragraphs.en: Lecture19:A Century Hello everyone and welcome back to our series on World History. Today we will focus on England and France and the calamitous 14th century. Our focus will be on the 14th century, but we will do a quick review of the 250 years leading up to it. Our story begins with William the Conqueror, the first Norman King of England. William ruled England from 1066 to 1087 and he had four children who are important to our story. The eldest was named Robert and he should have been the next King. But he got into a big fight with his father in 1077 and was disinherited.
paragraphs.zh: 大家好，欢迎回到我们的世界历史 系列讲座。今天我们会关注到英格 兰和法兰西以及多灾多难的 14 世 纪。 我们的重点是 14 世纪，但我们会 快速回顾一下它之前的 250 年。 我们的故事从“征服者威廉”开始， 他是英国第一个诺曼国王。威廉从 1066 年到 1087 年统治英国，他有 四个孩子，他们对我们的故事很重 要。 长子名叫罗伯特，他本应成为下一 任国王。但 1077 年，他和父亲大 吵了一架，被剥夺了继承权。

paragraphs.en: So when William died in 1087, the crown passed to his second son, William II. William didn't marry and onAugust 2nd, 1100 he was killed in an accident while out hunting with his brother Henry. (Although it might not have really been an accident.) So the crown passed on to Henry. Henry married a Scottish princess named Matilda in late 1100 and they had two healthy children, a daughter also named Matilda and a son also named Henry. So the succession looked secure. But then Henry drowned in a shipwreck called the White Ship tragedy on November 25th, 1120. And Henry I had no more sons.
paragraphs.zh: 所以当威廉于 1087 年去世时，王 位传给了他的第二个儿子，威廉二 世。 威廉当时未婚，1100 年 8 月 2 日， 他和弟弟亨利外出打猎时发生意 外身亡。 (尽管这可能不是真正的意外。) 于是王位传给了亨利。 1100 年末，亨利迎娶了一位名叫玛 蒂尔达的苏格兰公主，他们生了两 个健康的孩子，一个女儿也叫玛蒂 尔达，一个儿子也叫亨利。王位看 起来后继有人。 但是后来亨利王子在 1120 年 11 月 25 日的一场名为“白船悲剧”的海 难中溺水身亡。然而亨利一世没有 其他儿子。

paragraphs.en: So when King Henry died, there was a power struggle between his nephew Stephen and his Daughter Matilda. This led to an 18-year civil war known to history as theAnarchy. Eventually a peace deal was reached. Matilda would accept Stephen as King if Stephen would name her son as his heir.And Stephen agreed. So when Stephen died the next year in 1154, the crown passed to Matilda's son, Henry II. Henry II married an extremely wealthy French Duchess named Eleanor ofAquitaine. Eleanor owned the southwestern part of France. Henry already ruled the
paragraphs.zh: 所以当亨利国王去世时，他的侄子 斯蒂芬和女儿玛蒂尔达之间发生 了一场权力斗争。 这导致了长达 18 年的内战，历史 上称之为无政府状态。 最终双方达成了和平协议。玛蒂尔 达愿意接受斯蒂芬为国王，只要斯 蒂芬愿意指定她的儿子为继承人。 斯蒂芬同意了。 因此，当斯蒂芬于次年 1154 年去 世时，王位传给了玛蒂尔达的儿子 亨利二世。 亨利二世娶了一位非常富有的法 国公爵夫人，名叫埃莉诺。埃莉诺 拥有整个法国西南部。 亨利已经统治了西北部和英格兰，

paragraphs.en: northwestern part and England, so put together they created the Angevin Empire. And at this point Henry II actually ruled more of France than the French King! Henry and Eleanor had four sons. Henry the Young, Richard, Geoffrey and John. So the succession looked secure. But when Henry died in 1189 two of his sons: Henry the Young and Geoffrey were already dead. So the crown passed to his second son, Richard I. Richard I went on the Third Crusade. And it was this Richard who fought against Saladin.
paragraphs.zh: 所以他们一起建立了安杰文帝国。 这时，亨利二世实际上统治了比法 国国王还要多的法国国土。 亨利和埃莉诺有四个儿子。小亨 利，理查，杰弗里和约翰。王位又 是看起来后继有人。 但是当亨利二世 1189 年去世时， 他的两个儿子:小亨利和杰弗里已 经死了。于是王位传给了他的二儿 子，理查一世。 理查一世参加了第三次十字军东 征。 正是这位理查与萨拉丁作战。

paragraphs.en: But Richard had no legitimate children, so when he died in 1199 the crown passed to his younger brother John. Unfortunately for Richard and John, France at this point was ruled by one of its greatest Kings, Phillip II, and he thought that the English owned too much of his country. When Phillip II came to power in 1180 the English Kings controlled much more of France than the French Kings. That should be clear if you look at this map. But Phillip II was really good at war, and by the end of his life in 1223, he had conquered most of the Angevin Empire in France. And Phillip had even conquered
paragraphs.zh: 但是理查没有合法的孩子，所以当 他 1199 年去世时，王位传给了他 的弟弟约翰。 对理查和约翰来说不幸的是，当时 法国的统治者是法兰西历史上最 伟大的国王之一的菲利普二世，他 认为英国人占据了太多法国的土 地。 当菲利普二世在 1180 年掌权时， 英国国王控制的法国国土比法国 国王要多。 如果你看这张地图就会明白。 但是菲利普二世非常擅长战争，在 他 1223 年生命的最后，他征服了 安杰文帝国在法国的大部分国土。 菲利普甚至征服了诺曼底，英国王

paragraphs.en: Normandy, the ancestral home of the English Kings! John of course kept trying to stop Phillip and that meant lots of expensive wars in France paid for by high English taxes. The English Barons got tired of paying these Taxes eventually. And so this created a power struggle between the Barons and King. And this led to the first Baron's War in 1215. King John lost the first Barons war and he was forced to sign a document called the Magna Carta or Great Charter. The Magna Carta placed limits on the power of the King, and created certain legal protections for English rights. Mainly it required the King
paragraphs.zh: 室的起源地! 当然，约翰一直试图阻止菲利普， 这意味着在法国的许多昂贵的战 争都是由英国的高税收支付的。英 国的贵族们最终厌倦了纳税。 这就造成了贵族和国王之间的权 力斗争。 这导致了 1215 年的第一次“王爵 之战”。 约翰国王在第一次“王爵之战”中 战败，他被迫签署了一份名为《大 宪章》的文件。 《大宪章》对国王的权力进行了限 制，并为英国人的权利建立了一定 的法律保护。主要是要求国王在向 地主征税之前先征得他们的同意。

paragraphs.en: to get the permission of landowners before he taxed them. This led to the creation of the English Parliament. So the Magna Carta is the beginning of English democracy. In many ways John was a bad king, but he did successfully produce an heir, Henry III. So when John died in 1216, the crown passed to Henry, who reaffirmed the Magna Carta. From 1216 to 1327 there was a smooth succession from father to son for four generations. By the time we get to Edward III, the English story and French story get hopelessly mixed up, because Edward the III was the son of Isabelle of France.
paragraphs.zh: 这导致了英国议会的产生。所以大 宪章是英国民主的开端。 在很多方面，约翰是一个糟糕的国 王，但他确实成功留下了后嗣：亨 利三世。 因此，当约翰于 1216 年去世时， 王位传给了亨利三世，亨利重申了 《大宪章》。 从 1216 年到 1327 年，父子相传权 力顺利地过渡了四代。 当我们讲到爱德华三世的时候，英 国历史和法国历史无可救药地被 搅在了一起，因为爱德华三世是法 国伊莎贝尔公主的儿子。

paragraphs.en: Isabelle had a bad relationship with Edward II, so she overthrew him in 1326 and replaced him with their son Edward III. Isabelle was the great-great-great-granddaughter of Phillip II. And her father Phillip IV was one of France's strongest Kings, who greatly increased the power of the monarchy. It was not strange for Edward III to have a French Princess for a mother because all the Kings from William I to Edward III had spoken French as their first language. And their Norman ancestry combined with English territories in France had made them feel that France really belonged to them.
paragraphs.zh: 伊莎贝尔和爱德华二世关系不好， 所以她在 1326 年推翻了爱德华二 世，代之以他们的儿子爱德华三 世。 伊莎贝尔是菲利普二世的曾曾曾 孙女。 她的父亲菲利普四世是法国最强 大的国王之一，他极大地增强了君 主制的力量。 爱德华三世有一位法国公主做母 亲并不奇怪，因为从威廉一世到爱 德华三世，所有的国王都把法语作 为第一语言。 他们的诺曼血统加上英国在法国 的拥有的领土让他们觉得法国真 的属于他们。

paragraphs.en: But this way of thinking created a real problem during Isabelle's generation. Phillip IV had four children: Louis X, Philip V, Charles IV and Isabella. So the succession should have been secure. At first things went well. When Phillip IV died in 1314, the crown passed to his son Louis X. But then Louis X died in 1316 without a son, so the crown went to his younger brother Philip V. But then Phillip died without a son and the crown passed to Charles IV in 1322. And then Charles IV died in 1328, also without producing a son.
paragraphs.zh: 但是这种思考方式在伊莎贝尔这 一代产生了一个真正的问题。 菲利普四世有四个孩子:路易十 世、菲利普五世、查尔斯四世和伊 莎贝拉。 所以法国王位是后继有人的。 起初一切顺利。菲利普四世死于 1314 年，王位传给了他的儿子路易 十世。 但是 1316 年路易十世去世时没有 儿子，所以王位传给了他的弟弟菲 利普五世。 但菲利普死后没有儿子，1322 年王 位传给了查理四世。 1328 年查理四世去世，也没有生儿 子。

paragraphs.en: At this point Isabella was the only living child of Phillip IV. So she claimed the throne for her 16 year old son, Edward III. But the French nobles rejected that claim. They argued that the crown could only pass through men, and they wanted a French King. As you can see from this family tree Philip IV had many grandchildren, but no unbroken male line, just daughters of sons and the son of a daughter. So the crown went up a generation to his father Phillip III and then passed down through Phillip IV's younger brother Charles of Valois. And that's how Phillip VI became King of France.
paragraphs.zh: 此时伊莎贝拉是菲利普四世唯一 活着的孩子。 因此，她为 16 岁的儿子爱德华三 世继承了王位。 但法国贵族拒绝了。他们认为王位 只能由男人继承，他们想要一个法 国国王。 从家谱中可以看出，菲利普四世有 很多孙子，但没有完整的父系，只 有儿子的女儿和女儿的儿子。 所以王位传给了他的父亲菲利普 三世，然后传给了菲利普四世的弟 弟瓦卢瓦的查尔斯。 这就是菲利普六世成为法国国王 的原因。

paragraphs.en: Edward III accepted this at first, but nine years later in 1337 he got into a fight with Phillip VI. Now at this point Edward still held some land in southwest France from his ancestors. And so Philip VI seized that land as part of their fight. This enraged Edward. So in 1340 he decided to renew his claim to the French throne, and a war began. Although neither king knew it at the time, they had just begun the 100-years war: the last great war of the MiddleAges. Edward trained his army well, and the English had been developing a powerful new weapon: the longbow.
paragraphs.zh: 爱德华三世一开始接受了这一点， 但 9 年后的 1337 年，他与菲利普 六世吵了一架。 此时，爱德华仍然拥有法国西南部 的一些土地。 于是菲利普六世作为他们斗争的 一部分，夺取了那片土地。这激怒 了爱德华。 1340 年，他决定重新继承法国王 位，于是一场战争爆发了。 尽管当时国王和王后都不知道，但 他们刚刚开始了百年战争:中世纪 的最后一场伟大战争。 爱德华把他的军队训练得很好，英 国人一直在研制一种威力强大的 新武器:长弓。

paragraphs.en: Which was first demonstrated in the naval battle of Sluys. In that battle the French used War Galley's like this one. The English didn't really have a navy, so they used Merchant Cogs like this one. Which they modified by adding fighting platforms. The English longbow outranged the French bows, and allowed them to win the battle, giving the English control of the seas. The next major battle came 6 years later near the town of Crecy. At this Battle, the French outnumbered the English 2 to 1, so it should have been an easy French victory.
paragraphs.zh: 这在斯路易斯海战中得到了首次 展示。 在那次战役中，法国人使用了这样 的战船。 英国人并没有真正的海军，所以他 们使用像这样的商船。 他们对商船进行了改造，加上的作 战平台。 英国人的长弓比法国人的弓厉害， 所以英国人赢得了战斗，于是就控 制了海洋。 6 年后，在克雷西 镇附近又发生了 一场大战。 在这场战役中，法军的人数是英军 的两倍，所以这本来应该是一场轻 松的胜利。

paragraphs.en: But Edward's disciplined army made good use of their longbows. And the French were poorly led and disorganized. The English also had a few cannons at this battle. Crecy is the first battle in western Europe with gunpowder weapons. The cannons were not very good, but they made a loud noise which scared the French soldiers and horses. And the English won a decisive victory at Crecy which gave them control of the city of Calais. The war would have kept going but the next year France and England were both struck by a terrible sickness.
paragraphs.zh: 但爱德华训练有素的军队很好地 利用了他们的长弓。 法国人领导不力，组织混乱。 英军在这场战役中还有几门大炮。 克雷西之战是西欧第一场使用火 药武器的战争。 他们的炮并不是很有威力，但是却 能发出很大的声音，把法国士兵和 他们的战马都吓坏了。 英格兰人在克雷西取得了决定性 的胜利，控制了加莱市。 战争本应继续下去，但第二年，一 种可怕的疾病席卷了法国和英国。

paragraphs.en: It was the Black Death. The disease was caused by the Yersina Pestis bacteria and spread across Europe in the years from 1347 to 1353. Millions died. The sufferers developed swollen lymph nodes called buboes, and in the final stages, limbs, fingers and toes would turn black with gangrene. Millions died. The population of England fell from 5 million to 2.5 million.All across Europe similar death rates were seen. The Italian Humanist Giovanni Boccaccio was an eyewitness to the black death and said these words about it.
paragraphs.zh: 那就是黑死病。 这种疾病是由耶尔西纳鼠疫菌引 起的，并在 1347 年至 1353 年间传 遍欧洲。 数百万人因此死亡。 患者的淋巴结肿大，称为 buboes， 在最后阶段，四肢、手指和脚趾会 变黑并伴有坏疽。 数百万人死亡，英格兰人口从 500 万下降到 250 万。在整个欧洲，也 发现了类似的死亡率。 意大利人文主义者乔瓦尼·薄伽丘 是黑死病的目击者，他说：

paragraphs.en: And yet despite such horrible suffering, the war continued as soon as the plague let up. The French had a new King at this point: John II. In 1355 Edward's son, Edward the Black Prince ravaged southern France. He killed, burned, and pillaged his way across the countryside.And the French army did nothing to stop him. When the Black Prince attacked again in 1356, the French decided to stop him at the Battle of Poitiers. The French King himself led the army.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，尽管遭受了如此可怕的苦 难，瘟疫一平息，战争就继续了。 此时法国有了一个新国王: 约翰 二世。 1355 年，爱德华的儿子，黑王子爱 德华洗劫了法国南部。 他在乡间杀人、放火、抢劫。法国 军队没有阻止他。 当黑王子在 1356 年再次进攻时， 法国人决定在普瓦捷战役中阻止 他。法国国王亲自率领军队。

paragraphs.en: Again the French outnumbered the English, and again poor leadership led to their defeat. The French army was destroyed at Poitiers. And even worse, the French King was captured! The English demanded 85,000 kilograms of silver as a ransom for his release. And so the French were forced to pay high taxes to pay that ransom. And they were forced to give up huge tracts of land. For the common people of France, the 1300s were a century of plague, terrible war, and crushing taxation: a century of woe. And France's bad luck kept going.
paragraphs.zh: 法国人又一次在人数上超过了英 国人，但也是又一次因领导不力导 致了他们的失败。 法国军队在普瓦捷被摧毁。 更糟的是，法国国王自己也被俘虏 了! 英国人索要八万五千公斤白银作 为释放他的赎金。 因此法国人被迫支付高额税款来 支付赎金。 他们被迫放弃了大片的土地。 对于普通的法国人来说，13 世纪是 一个充满瘟疫、战争和超高税负的 世纪:苦难的世纪。 法国的厄运还在继续。百年战争又

paragraphs.en: The Hundred Year‘s War lasted for another four generations, and the third French King, Charles VI was mentally ill. This led to disaster after disaster, and by 1420, the English King Henry V controlled most of northern France. And in 1420 he forced the French to sign the Treaty of Troyes. In this treaty Charles VI disinherited his own son and named Henry V his heir. In 1422, both Henry V and Charles VI died, but the treaty came into effect and the French crown passed to Henry's 1 year-old son, Henry VI. But Charles VII kept fighting, so the English began to re-conquer the rest of France. By 1429, the last major
paragraphs.zh: 四个世代，雪上加霜的是，第三代 法王查理六世还患有精神疾病。 这导致了一场又一场的灾难，到 1420 年，英国国王亨利五世控制了 法国北部的大部分地区。 1420 年，他强迫法国人签署特罗耶 条约。 在这个条约中，查理六世剥夺了他 儿子的继承权，并指定亨利五世为 他的继承人。 1422 年，亨利五世和查理六世都去 世了，但条约生效，法国王位传给 了亨利五世一岁的儿子亨利六世。 但是法国王太子查理七世继续战 斗，所以英国人开始重新征服那些 未并入英国治下的法国土地。到

paragraphs.en: obstacle to this was the city of Orleans. So the English put it under siege in October of 1428. By 1429, the French had basically lost the war. The English now ruled their country. The French people cried out for a hero who would save France. And just then, at their hour of greatest need, this hero appeared. But this person was not a King, or a Lord, or a Knight… …she was a 17-year old shepherd girl named Joan ofArc. When Joan was 13 she started seeing visions that she believed were from God. In 1429 the visions told her to travel to the town of Valcouers.
paragraphs.zh: 1429 年，最后一个主要障碍是奥尔 良市。于是英国人在 1428 年 10 月 包围了它。 到 1429 年，法国基本上输掉了战 争。英国人现在统治着他们的国 家。法国人哀嚎着祈求一个能拯救 他们于水火的英雄。 就在他们最需要的时候，这位英雄 出现了。 但这个人不是国王，不是领主，也 不是骑士…… 她是一个 17 岁的牧羊女，贞德。 当贞德 13 岁时，她开始看到她相 信来自上帝的异象。 1429 年，异象告诉她去瓦尔库尔 城。

paragraphs.en: There she convinced the local garrison commander, Robert de Baudricourt to help her by predicting the outcome of a battle ahead of time. Joan cut her hair, put on a man's clothes and crossed enemy territory to reach Charles' court in Chinon. On March 9th she impressed Charles VII by identifying him while he was wearing a disguise. When they talked alone she made a strong impression on him, and he began to believe in her. Joan told the court that God had sent her to liberate Orleans from the English siege. The French court was desperate and decided to give it a shot. They sent her to Orleans with a horse, a suit of
paragraphs.zh: 在那里，她说服当地守军指挥官罗 伯特·德·鲍德里考特(Robert de Baudricourt)提前预测一场战斗 的结果，从而帮助她。 贞德剪了头发，穿上男人的衣服， 穿过敌人的领地来到了王太子查 理在奇农的宫廷。 3 月 9 日，她认出了乔装打扮的查 理七世，因此给他留下了深刻的印 象。 当他们单独谈话时，她给他留下了 深刻的印象，他开始相信她。 贞德告诉法庭，上帝派她来把奥尔 良从英国的包围中解放出来。 法国法院当时很绝望，决定试一 试。他们派她带着一匹马，一套盔 甲和一支小部队去奥尔良。

paragraphs.en: armor and a small force. Joan and this small army arrived to reinforce the French forces already trying to break the siege.And as she travelled Joan began to inspire the soldiers. And when Joan reached Orleans on April 29th, things began to change, she gave the French soldiers new courage and confidence. The English had captured several fortresses around Orleans. To break the siege and save the city, the French needed to retake those fortresses. Once Joan arrived, the French soldiers began to win. During the fighting, Joan was wounded by an arrow, but returned to the fight and grabbed a ladder
paragraphs.zh: 贞德和这支小军队来增援已经试 图突破包围的法国军队。行军途 中，贞德开始激励士兵们。 当贞德在 4 月 29 日到达奥尔良时， 情况开始改变，她给了法国士兵新 的勇气和信心。 英国人占领了奥尔良周围的几个 要塞，为了打破包围并拯救这座城 市，法国人需要夺回这些要塞。 贞德一到，法国士兵就开始取胜。 在此期间，贞德被箭射伤，但她回 到战场上，自己抓起梯子去攻击最 后的英国堡垒。

paragraphs.en: herself to attack the final English fortress. Encouraged by her example the French forces surged forward.And the English retreated. Joan had done it! Orleans had been liberated. Words spread quickly and the common people flocked around her, eager to see this Holy maid who had saved them. Soon words spread around France. God had sent them a saint to save the country! The French felt hope and courage once again. And all across France, thousands of soldiers joined Joan's army, eager to fight for this miraculous woman. Over the next 10 weeks, the French
paragraphs.zh: 在她的激励下，法国士兵们也奋勇 往前冲。英国人撤退了。 贞德做到了!奥尔良解放了。消息 很快传开了，老百姓都聚集在她的 周围，渴望见到这位救了他们的圣 女。 消息很快传遍了法国。上帝派了一 位圣人来拯救这个国家! 法国人又一次感到了希望和勇气。 在法国各地，成千上万的士兵加入 了琼的军队，渴望为这个神奇的女 人而战。 在接下来的 10 个星期里，法国军

paragraphs.en: army won victory after victory and recaptured the city of Rheims. And there Charles VII was crowned King of France with Joan by his side. Now with a King determined to fight, and new faith in their cause, the French began winning again. Joan had turned the tide of the war. For the next year Joan ofArc fought with the army, inspiring the troops. She served as the focal point for a surging feeling of French patriotism and fighting spirit. But then, on May 22nd, 1430, she was captured by the Burgundians, allies of the English. And then was sold to the English in December of 1430.
paragraphs.zh: 队取得了一次又一次的胜利，重新 夺回了莱茵市。 查理七世在那里被加冕为法国国 王，贞德侍立在旁。 现在，有了决心战斗的国王和对他 们事业的新信念，法国人又开始胜 利了。琼扭转了战争的局势。 在接下来的一年里，圣女贞德与军 队并肩作战，鼓舞了军队。 她是法国人爱国主义和战斗精神 高涨的中心人物。 但在 1430 年 5 月 22 日，她被英国 的盟友勃艮第人俘虏。 然后在 1430 年 12 月卖给了英国。

paragraphs.en: The English put her on trial for heresy. The outcome of the trial was never in doubt. The English had to find her guilty. Otherwise it would mean that God opposed England! After a deeply unfair trial, Joan was found guilty of heresy by the English judges and sentenced to die. And she was burned at the stake on May 30th, 1431. She was just 19 years old. But something had changed. Joan had given the French people hope again, and crowned a King determined to win. And the English starting having their own problems back in England. Over the next 19 years the French began winning battle after battle and
paragraphs.zh: 英国人以异端罪审判她。审判的结 果是毫无疑问的。英国人不得不判 她有罪。否则就意味着上帝反对英 格兰! 在一次极不公平的审判后，英国法 官判定贞德犯有异端罪，判处她死 刑。 1431 年 5 月 30 日，她被烧死在火 刑柱上。当时她只有 19 岁。 但是有些事情已经改变了。贞德又 给了法国人民希望，给一个决心获 胜的国王加冕。 英国人在他们自己国家也开始产 生问题。 在接下来的 19 年里，法国人开始 在一场又一场的战役中获胜，并把

paragraphs.en: pushing the English out of their country. And by 1453 they had driven the English out of all of France except for the city of Calais. The French had won! And Joan ofArc continued on in the hearts of her countrymen as a French hero for all time. The Catholic Church even made her a Saint in 1920. So the Hundred Year’s War ended in 1453. Because in 1453 the mighty walls of Constantinople, the walls which had protected that city for over a thousand years, finally fell.And in that year, the last vestige of the Roman Empire came to an end.
paragraphs.zh: 英国人赶出了他们的国家。 到了 1453 年，他们把英格兰人赶 出了法国除了加莱城。 法国人赢了! 圣女贞德作为一个法国英雄一直 活在国人的心中。1920 年，天主教 会甚至将她封为圣徒。 百年战争于 1453 年结束。 因为在 1453 年，君士坦丁堡坚固 的城墙——保护这座城市一千多 年的城墙，最终倒塌了。在那一年， 罗马帝国的最后余晖也消失了。

paragraphs.en: And in 1453 the MiddleAges ended, and the Modern Era began. We do, however, need to learn a bit more about that final siege of Constantinople, and the final fall of Roman Empire. But that's a story for our last class in this series. Join me next time for that! Okay everyone, I hope you enjoyed that lesson and want to learn more. If so I have a recommendation for you. It is the book A Distant Mirror: The Calamitous Fourteenth Century by Barbara Tuchman. Thank you very much.
paragraphs.zh: 1453 年，中世纪结束，现代世界开 始。 但我们确实应该了解下君士坦丁 堡之困以及罗马帝国的最终落幕。 这是本系列最后一节课的内容。欢 迎下次收听! 好了，各位，我希望你们喜欢这堂 课，并希望学到更多。如果是的话， 我有一个建议给你。 这是芭芭拉·塔奇曼的《遥远的镜 子:14 世纪的灾难》一书。 非常感谢。

titleZh: 第二十讲：奥斯曼土耳其|众城市的女王之陨落

paragraphs.en: Hello everyone, and welcome back to our final lecture in our series on the MiddleAges. Today we are going to learn about the Ottoman Turks.
